- AC: FlywheelStats schema and interface include required top-level awaitingCloseOut: number; decodeFlywheelStats rejects payloads missing it
- AC: flywheel.ts (FlywheelStatus/FlywheelHeadline) is unchanged
- AC: Contracts test suite passes with updated fixtures

title: pan-1767: Add shared lib helper listing issues awaiting close-out
description:
Create src/lib/awaiting-close-out.ts exporting listAwaitingCloseOut(statuses?: Record<string, ReviewStatus>): Array<{ issueId: string; prUrl?: string; updatedAt: string }> and countAwaitingCloseOut(): number. The list filters review statuses to mergeStatus === 'merged', sorted oldest-first by updatedAt. Default data source is loadReviewStatuses() from src/lib/review-status.ts (sync SQLite via getAllReviewStatusesFromDb — the established pattern; no execSync, no network). The optional statuses parameter exists for tests and for callers that already hold the map. This helper is THE definition of 'awaiting close-out' — every other bead consumes it or mirrors its predicate.
- AC: listAwaitingCloseOut returns exactly the review-status entries with mergeStatus === 'merged', sorted ascending by updatedAt
- AC: Entries with mergeStatus pending/queued/merging/verifying/failed or absent are excluded
- AC: Unit tests cover empty store, mixed statuses, and sort order; suite passes via vitest
- AC: No execSync and no async network calls in the helper; safe to import from both CLI commands and dashboard server code

title: pan-1709: Exclude candidates with review-pipeline presence in the candidate filter
description:
Add getReviewStatusForIssue?: (issueId: string) => <ReviewStatus pick> | null to FindOrphanProposedOptions, defaulting to getReviewStatusSync from src/lib/review-status.ts. In findOrphanProposedSpecsForReconciler, skip any spec whose review_status row satisfies hasReviewPipelinePresence(), logging a logReconcilerDiagnostic('candidate-excluded', { issueId, reason: 'review-pipeline-presence' }) console diagnostic (never an activity-feed entry). Add the issue-mandated unit test: spec status 'proposed', beads == plan items, no tmux session, agent state stopped (in-review-with-stopped-work-agent), review_status { reviewStatus: 'passed', prNumber } → no candidate and no spawn through reconcileOrphanProposedSpecs. Also test that an all-pending row still yields a candidate.
- AC: Unit test: issue with spec 'proposed', stopped work agent, and review_status reviewStatus 'passed' + prNumber produces no candidate and reconcileOrphanProposedSpecs attempts no spawn (the issue's required in-review-with-stopped-work-agent case)
- AC: Unit test: an issue with a review_status row whose fields are all pending/absent is still returned as a candidate (row existence alone does not exclude)
- AC: Default wiring uses getReviewStatusSync when no getReviewStatusForIssue option is supplied; exclusion emits only a console diagnostic, no emitActivityEntrySync call (PAN-1626 discipline asserted in test)
close_reason: Added review_status candidate exclusion with injectable reader, default getReviewStatusSync wiring, and tests for in-review exclusion, all-pending eligibility, and feed discipline.

title: pan-1709: Open-PR gate before spawn attempt in reconcileOrphanProposedSpecs
description:
Add hasOpenPrForBranch?: (projectPath: string, issueId: string) => Promise<boolean> to ReconcileOrphanProposedOptions. Default implementation runs `gh pr list --head feature/<issue-lowercase> --state open --json number --limit 1` with cwd=projectPath using promisified exec (execAsync — NEVER execSync; this code is reachable from the dashboard server). In reconcileOrphanProposedSpecs, after the cooldown gate and before the spawn attempt, call it: true → skip with logReconcilerDiagnostic('spawn-skipped', { reason: 'open-pr' }) and push a 'Skipped … open-pr' action, no spawn, no activity-feed entry; gh error → fail-open (log diagnostic, proceed). Set the cooldown timestamp on open-PR skips as on attempts so gh is called at most once per issue per cooldown window. Unit tests mock hasOpenPrForBranch — no real gh calls, no real timers.
- AC: Unit test: candidate with hasOpenPrForBranch resolving true is skipped — spawnWorkAgent is never called and no activity-feed entry is emitted
- AC: Unit test: hasOpenPrForBranch rejecting (gh failure) fails open — the spawn attempt proceeds
- AC: Default implementation uses promisified exec with cwd=projectPath and the feature/<issue-lowercase> head branch; no execSync anywhere in the change
close_reason: Added post-cooldown open-PR spawn gate with default gh pr list check via promisified exec, fail-open diagnostics, and unit tests for skip, rejection fail-open, and command/cwd behavior.

title: pan-1709: Widen tmux exclusion to live pipeline sessions (agent-<issue>-* prefix)
description:
In findOrphanProposedSpecsForReconciler, replace the exact tmuxSessionNames.includes(agentId) check with: exclude when any session equals agentId OR starts with `${agentId}-` (trailing hyphen). This covers live review convoy sessions (agent-<issue>-review-<subRole>, per review-monitor.ts), test, and ship sessions. Add unit tests for both the positive and the prefix-collision-negative case.
- AC: Unit test: a live session named agent-<issue>-review-security excludes the issue from candidates
- AC: Unit test: a session for a longer issue number (e.g. agent-pan-30011) does NOT exclude PAN-3001 — trailing-hyphen prefix match prevents issue-number collisions
close_reason: Added exact-or-trailing-hyphen issue-scoped tmux session exclusion and tests for review-session exclusion plus longer-issue-number collision safety.

title: pan-1709: Add hasReviewPipelinePresence() predicate with unit tests
description:
In src/lib/cloister/orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts, add an exported pure function hasReviewPipelinePresence(status) accepting a Pick of ReviewStatus (reviewStatus, testStatus, mergeStatus, readyForMerge, prNumber, prUrl) | null. Return true when reviewStatus !== 'pending', testStatus !== 'pending', mergeStatus is set and !== 'pending', readyForMerge === true, or prNumber/prUrl is present; false for null or an all-pending row. Add unit tests in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/orphan-proposed-reconciler.test.ts covering every branch.
- AC: Predicate returns false for null and for a row with reviewStatus 'pending', testStatus 'pending', no mergeStatus, readyForMerge false, no prNumber/prUrl
- AC: Predicate returns true for each progressed field in isolation: reviewStatus beyond pending, testStatus beyond pending (including 'skipped'), mergeStatus beyond pending, readyForMerge true, prNumber set, prUrl set — each covered by a unit test
close_reason: Added exported hasReviewPipelinePresence predicate and unit tests for null/all-pending false plus each progressed review/test/merge/ready/pr signal true.

title: pan-1747: Reconcile ship-role docs and stale in-code comments to the post-removal reality
description:
Update docs and stale in-code comments to match the post-removal reality. In docs/ROLES.md remove the `ship | roles/ship.md` row (~lines 17-34) and the ship entry in the new-role checklist context (~138); state that shipping is server-side (rebaseFeatureBranch + PAN-1650 event-driven readyForMerge) with no spawned ship role, and that 'ship' survives only as the merge-specialist identity. In CLAUDE.md update the role-taxonomy table (~line 112) to drop roles/ship.md as the ship instruction source. Add a one-line note in docs/MERGE-WORKFLOW.md that the reactive 'shipping' state no longer spawns any agent. Update stale in-code comments that describe ship as a spawned 'final pipeline stage': src/dashboard/server/routes/projects.ts:320, src/dashboard/server/routes/command-deck.ts:558-561, src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:1083 and :720.
- AC: docs/ROLES.md no longer lists roles/ship.md as an existing instruction source; the ship row reflects server-side shipping + merge-specialist identity.
- AC: CLAUDE.md role-taxonomy table no longer cites roles/ship.md.
- AC: docs/MERGE-WORKFLOW.md notes that the reactive shipping state spawns no agent.
- AC: Stale in-code comments in projects.ts, command-deck.ts, and specialists.ts no longer describe ship as a spawned pipeline stage.
- AC: npm run lint (incl. scripts/lint-skills.sh) still passes -- no doc/skill drift introduced.
close_reason: Updated role/merge/spawn docs, planning taxonomy, and stale code comments to reflect server-side shipping with no spawned ship role; lint passed.

title: pan-1747: Make agent spawn honest: verify a role definition file exists before passing --agent
description:
In src/lib/agents.ts getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand (~464-503), before emitting `--agent <definitionPath>`, verify the resolved role definition file exists on disk (resolve relative to the path claude loads --agent against -- the workspace/project root). When the file is absent, take the no-definition branch: apply the global Claude permission flags (getClaudePermissionFlagsStringSync), skip bypassPrefixForAgentFlagSync, omit --agent, and log a single warning. Keep this generic (an existence check for any role) rather than special-casing ship to null, so no future role can silently point --agent at a missing file. This is the issue's third explicit requirement. Add a unit test.
- AC: When the resolved role definition file is missing, getRoleRuntimeBaseCommand emits NO --agent flag and includes the global permission flags (no-definition branch).
- AC: When the file exists, behaviour is unchanged: --agent <path> is emitted, permissionFlags is empty, and the bypass prefix is applied.
- AC: A single warning is logged when a role definition file is missing (the failure is no longer silent).
- AC: A unit test covers both the missing-file and present-file branches.
close_reason: Added generic role-definition existence guard before --agent; missing definitions now warn and use global permission flags, with tests.

title: pan-1747: Remove the deacon checkUndispatchedShip safety-net patrol
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts remove the checkUndispatchedShip() function (~3097-3160), the SHIP_DISPATCH_STALENESS_MS / SHIP_DISPATCH_COOLDOWN_MS constants (~3066, 3070), the shipDispatchCooldowns / shipDispatchCounts maps (~3071-3072), and the patrol-loop call site + action aggregation (5174-5176). Delete the dedicated test src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-undispatched-ship.test.ts -- the behaviour it guards is gone. Leave checkReadyForMergeStuck (PAN-344) and checkFailedMergeRetry intact: those are the real post-readyForMerge merge-trigger safety nets and are unrelated to ship dispatch.
- AC: checkUndispatchedShip and its SHIP_DISPATCH_* constants and cooldown/count maps are removed; grep finds no remaining references.
- AC: The deacon patrol loop no longer calls checkUndispatchedShip (lines 5174-5176 removed); typecheck and lint pass with no dead vars/imports.
- AC: deacon-undispatched-ship.test.ts is removed; the full test suite is green.
- AC: checkReadyForMergeStuck and checkFailedMergeRetry remain unchanged.
close_reason: Removed the deacon undispatched-ship patrol, its state, loop aggregation, and obsolete dedicated test; related cloister tests pass.

title: pan-1747: Stop the reactive scheduler from spawning a ship agent (ROLE_RUN_STATES.shipping -> null)
description:
In src/lib/cloister/service.ts, change ROLE_RUN_STATES.shipping from 'ship' to null so stateToRole('shipping') returns null and onIssueStateChange('shipping') becomes a no-op (no ship agent spawned). Remove 'ship' from the spawnable-role list in roleFromAgentId (~line 179). Keep the test.passed -> 'shipping' state transition in issueStateChangeFromDomainEvent unchanged -- only the role spawn is removed; the shipping STATE remains for frontend phase display and flywheel MERGE_GATE_VERBS. Update src/lib/cloister/__tests__/reactive-scheduler.test.ts to assert no ship agent is spawned on test.passed.
- AC: stateToRole('shipping') returns null (ROLE_RUN_STATES.shipping === null).
- AC: A test.passed domain event no longer spawns an agent-<issue>-ship session; onIssueStateChange('shipping') logs 'no role for issue state shipping' and returns.
- AC: roleFromAgentId no longer lists 'ship' among spawnable roles.
- AC: reactive-scheduler.test.ts updated to assert shipping produces no spawn; the suite is green.
- AC: The shipping STATE transition still occurs (issueStateChangeFromDomainEvent unchanged); frontend 'Shipping' phase and flywheel MERGE_GATE_VERBS are unaffected.
close_reason: Stopped reactive shipping from spawning a ship role; shipping remains a lifecycle state and reactive scheduler tests pass.

title: pan-1744: Replace clearStuckForks blanket-fail with a boot recovery pass (dispatch by stage + retry guard)
description:
Replace the blanket clearStuckForks() call in src/dashboard/server/main.ts boot sequence with a recovery pass. Implement recoverStuckForks() (near runForkPipeline so it can call it) that, for each conversation from getStuckForks(): (a) if fork_retry_count >= 2, mark failed with the current/standard message and skip; (b) otherwise call incrementForkRetryCount BEFORE attempting (so a crash during recovery still burns a retry and cannot loop); (c) parse fork_request — if missing/unparseable (legacy fork), mark failed and skip; (d) dispatch by observed state: if the tmux session is alive AND the runtime mirror reports active/waiting-on-human, the fork actually finished — markConversationActive + updateForkStatus(name, null) and do NOT kill it; otherwise re-enter the resumable runForkPipeline reconstructed from fork_request (re-resolving the parent conversation by name). Schedule the pass a few seconds after boot via setTimeout (not inline) so tmux/runtime-mirror/lifecycle services have settled; wrap each fork's recovery in try/catch so one failure cannot abort the others. Keep clearStuckForks only if still needed as the narrow terminal fail-marker, otherwise remove it and update/remove its test.
- AC: Boot no longer blanket-marks in-flight forks failed; recoverStuckForks() runs on a short post-boot delay and processes each conversation returned by getStuckForks().
- AC: Tmux-alive AND runtime-mirror-active fork is marked active with fork_status cleared and is never killed (test: simulate live session+mirror -> stays active).
- AC: A fork with a handoff doc already on disk is recovered by re-entering runForkPipeline, which reuses the doc and re-spawns/re-injects rather than re-authoring (test asserts no re-author).
- AC: A pre-doc (authoring-stage) fork is recovered by re-running the pipeline from scratch (test: in-flight with no doc and no tmux -> pipeline re-runs).
- AC: fork_retry_count is incremented BEFORE the attempt; a fork at retry_count >= 2 is marked failed and not re-attempted (test: third boot for the same fork yields failed, not a fourth attempt).
- AC: A legacy in-flight fork with no fork_request is marked failed (graceful fallback, no crash).
- AC: One fork's recovery throwing does not prevent the others from being processed (per-fork try/catch).
close_reason: Replaced startup blanket fork failure with delayed recoverStuckForks dispatch, retry guarding, legacy fallback, and tests.

title: pan-1744: handoff.ts and fork.ts: print 'in progress — watch <url>' instead of false success on timeout/in-flight
description:
Fix the false-success print in both CLI consumers of forkConversationViaServer. In src/cli/commands/handoff.ts (~line 100-117) and src/cli/commands/fork.ts (~line 72-81), after the existing forkStatus==='failed' branch, add a branch: if the result timedOut (or forkStatus is non-null and not 'failed'), print a clearly-distinct message — e.g. yellow 'Fork/handoff still in progress — watch https://pan.localhost/conv/<id>' with the conv id — and exit non-zero, instead of falling through to the green 'forked/handoff succeeded' block. Only print the green success block when forkStatus is null (truly complete).
- AC: handoff.ts prints an 'in progress — watch <url>' message (not the green success block) when the result is timedOut/in-flight, and includes the conv id + dashboard URL.
- AC: fork.ts applies the same honest output for the same timedOut/in-flight condition.
- AC: The green success block is printed ONLY when forkStatus is null (genuine completion); the 'failed' branch is unchanged.
- AC: Both commands exit with a non-zero code on the in-progress/timeout path so scripts/automation do not treat a not-yet-live fork as success.
close_reason: Updated fork and handoff CLI output to report in-progress fork pipelines with watch URLs and non-zero exit status.

title: pan-1744: Persist the full fork request at creation in the summary-fork route
description:
In the POST /api/conversations/:name/summary-fork route in src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, immediately after the fork conversation row is created (createConversation, ~line 3779) and before/at the runForkPipeline launch (~line 3799), serialize the exact arguments passed to runForkPipeline into a ForkRequest object and persist it via setForkRequest(newConv.name, JSON.stringify(request)). The persisted request must include the parent conversation name (not the in-memory object), sessionId, and all fork-mode/summary/handoff parameters, so a later process can reconstruct the call. This makes the in-flight fork re-enterable; it does not change the live happy path.
- AC: Creating a fork (any mode) writes a fork_request JSON row whose fields, when parsed, exactly reconstruct the arguments runForkPipeline was launched with, including parent conv name and sessionId.
- AC: fork_retry_count is initialized to 0 for a newly created fork.
- AC: The live happy-path fork still completes exactly as before (no behavior change observable to a fork that does not get interrupted); existing summary-fork route tests pass.
close_reason: Persisted the complete ForkRequest JSON immediately after fork conversation creation and added route helper coverage.

title: pan-1744: Give in-flight fork pipelines a bounded grace window on shutdown
description:
Reduce how often recovery is even needed by letting in-flight forks finish before the process exits. Track in-flight fork pipelines (e.g. a module-level Set of in-flight runForkPipeline promises/names registered at launch and cleared on settle) and, in the handleShutdownSignal handler in src/dashboard/server/main.ts (SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP), await their completion with a bounded timeout (e.g. up to ~10s) before process.exit. The wait MUST be bounded — if a fork hangs past the window, log it and exit anyway so a restart is never blocked indefinitely (boot recovery then catches that fork). This is the common-case complement: a pan reload during fork authoring lets the fork finish instead of relying on recovery.
- AC: In-flight fork pipelines are registered when launched and de-registered when they settle (success or failure).
- AC: On SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP the shutdown handler awaits in-flight forks up to a bounded timeout before exiting.
- AC: The wait is bounded: a fork that does not finish within the window is logged and the process exits anyway (test: a never-settling in-flight fork does not hang shutdown past the cap).
- AC: With no in-flight forks, shutdown is not measurably delayed (the grace path is a no-op when the in-flight set is empty).
close_reason: Tracked in-flight fork pipelines and added a bounded 10s shutdown wait with fake-timer tests.

title: pan-1744: fork-client: distinguish deadline-expiry-while-in-flight from completion
description:
In src/cli/commands/fork-client.ts, change forkConversationViaServer so callers can tell a deadline-expiry-while-still-in-flight apart from a genuine completion. Today the poll loop returns the latest state whether it broke because forkStatus cleared/failed OR because the deadline expired with forkStatus still in flight. Add an explicit signal — e.g. a `timedOut: boolean` field on the returned ForkResultConv — set true only when the loop exits via the deadline with a non-null, non-failed forkStatus. Completion (forkStatus null) and failure (forkStatus 'failed') leave timedOut false.
- AC: ForkResultConv carries a timedOut boolean; it is true only when the poll deadline expires with forkStatus still in-flight (non-null, non-'failed').
- AC: A completed fork (forkStatus becomes null) returns timedOut=false; a failed fork (forkStatus 'failed') returns timedOut=false.
- AC: Unit test with a fake/stubbed server response simulates a timeout-while-spawning and asserts timedOut=true with the last in-flight forkStatus preserved.
close_reason: Added ForkResultConv.timedOut and fake-timer tests for in-flight, completed, and failed fork polling outcomes.

title: pan-1744: Make runForkPipeline resume-safe (reuse existing handoff doc, skip spawn when tmux already alive)
description:
Make runForkPipeline in src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts safe to call a second time for an already-in-flight fork, so boot recovery can re-enter it without re-authoring or double-spawning. Two idempotency guards in the handoff/summary path: (1) if the fork row already has handoff_doc_path set AND the file exists on disk, reuse that doc text instead of re-authoring (skip authorHandoffExternal / requestHandoffFromAgent); (2) before spawnConversationSession, if the tmux session already exists (sessionExists), skip the spawn and proceed straight to injection + markConversationActive. The normal first-run path (no doc yet, no tmux) must behave exactly as today. Keep the change confined to re-entrancy; do not alter authoring, fallback, or delivery semantics for the first run.
- AC: Re-entering runForkPipeline for a handoff whose handoff_doc_path is set and whose doc file exists reuses the existing doc text and does NOT call the authoring path again.
- AC: Re-entering runForkPipeline when the tmux session already exists skips spawnConversationSession and proceeds to injection + mark-active (no second tmux session is created).
- AC: First-run behavior is unchanged: with no doc and no tmux session, runForkPipeline authors, spawns, injects, and clears fork_status exactly as before (existing fork tests pass).
- AC: A unit/integration test exercises a second invocation of runForkPipeline for the same fork and asserts no re-author and no double-spawn.
close_reason: Made runForkPipeline reuse persisted handoff docs and existing tmux sessions; added route helper coverage.

title: pan-1744: Add fork_request and fork_retry_count columns + DB accessors to the conversations table
description:
Extend the conversations schema so an in-flight fork carries enough durable state to be re-entered after the dashboard process dies. Add two columns in src/lib/database/schema.ts — `fork_request TEXT` (a JSON blob of the runForkPipeline parameters) and `fork_retry_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` — to both the CREATE TABLE conversations block (for fresh databases) and a new additive migration step v52 -> v53 (bump SCHEMA_VERSION to 53; wrap each ALTER TABLE in try/catch matching the existing pattern). In src/lib/database/conversations-db.ts: read both columns into mapRow/the Conversation type (forkRequest: string|null, forkRetryCount: number), and add accessors — getStuckForks() returning conversations where fork_status IS NOT NULL AND fork_status != 'failed', setForkRequest(name, json), and incrementForkRetryCount(name) returning the new count. Define a single ForkRequest TypeScript shape capturing every runForkPipeline argument needed to re-enter (forkMode, summaryModel, localSummaryOnly, includeThinkingInSummary, summaryHarness, handoffFocus, handoffAuthor, handoffAuthorModel, handoffAuthorHarness, parent conversation name, sessionId) and export it for the persist and recovery beads to share. NO behavior change yet — this bead only lays the schema + accessor + shared-type foundation.
- AC: CREATE TABLE conversations includes fork_request TEXT and fork_retry_count INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0; a fresh DB created from schema has both columns.
- AC: Migration v52->v53 adds both columns via try/catch ALTER TABLE; SCHEMA_VERSION is 53; running migrations twice on an existing DB is idempotent (no throw).
- AC: A shared exported ForkRequest type enumerates every parameter runForkPipeline needs to re-enter (all summary/handoff params + parent conv name + sessionId).
- AC: getStuckForks(), setForkRequest(name, json), and incrementForkRetryCount(name) exist; a unit test round-trips: set a request, increment retry twice (returns 1 then 2), and getStuckForks returns only non-null, non-failed in-flight forks.
- AC: mapRow exposes forkRequest and forkRetryCount on the Conversation type; existing conversation reads/writes are unaffected (typecheck + existing conversations-db tests pass).
close_reason: Added fork_request/fork_retry_count schema + v52→v53 migration, ForkRequest type, DB accessors, row mapping, and database/migration tests.

title: pan-1737: Quality gates + Playwright visual pass of the three card states
description:
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all green in the workspace. Playwright (isolated browser profile) screenshots of the card in steady/assembling/empty states against the dev dashboard; verify no overflow/truncation at rail width and that labels match the approved mockup language.
- AC: typecheck, lint, and the full test suite pass in the workspace; delay-based tests use fake timers
- AC: Screenshots of the three states exist and match the approved mockup's structure (zones, labels, dialogs) with no overflow at rail width
close_reason: Done in 22bc98062: typecheck+lint+full suite green (5757 root + frontend); Playwright screenshots of steady/promote-confirm/empty states in docs/design/pan-1737-card-*.png, no overflow at rail width

title: pan-1737: Frontend: UAT batches card per approved mockup (batches, checklists, honest dialogs, escape hatch)
description:
Rebuild MergeQueueCard.tsx as the UAT batches card per docs/design/pan-1737-uat-batch-trains.html: plain-language intro; batches newest-first (ready: Open UAT frontend + Merge batch (N) to main + rebuild; assembling: live status line incl. conflict-resolution progress; superseded: still testable/promotable; held-out chips with reasons); What-to-UAT section grouped per member issue from the API's ACs with explicit verify-the-touchpoint items for resolutions; ready-features reference rows (order, IdLink, monospace branch, PR link, status chips); escape hatch 'Merge one feature to main…' via useConfirm naming exact semantics; promote confirm dialog naming every member and 'lands exactly the tree you tested'. All confirms via DialogProvider useConfirm. The string 'Ship batch' must not appear anywhere in the frontend.
- AC: The card renders the three mockup states from API data: steady (ready + superseded), assembling-in-background, and empty queue with the explanatory empty state — never an error on missing data
- AC: Promote and escape-hatch actions open useConfirm dialogs that name the exact members/branches and consequences; confirming fires the API call, cancelling fires nothing
- AC: grep -r 'Ship batch' over src/dashboard/frontend returns nothing; What-to-UAT renders per-member ACs with a 'No UAT steps in plan' fallback and touchpoint items for resolved conflicts
- AC: Open-UAT-frontend button reflects stack status (start-with-progress when absent, plain link when running) and component tests cover the state branches
close_reason: Done in abc5063b6: UAT batches card per approved mockup — batches/checklist/rows/escape-hatch zones, useConfirm gating, no 'Ship batch' string in frontend; 6 component tests

title: pan-1737: API: uat-generations chain (with per-member ACs), stack ensure, promote, force-rebuild
description:
routes/flywheel.ts: GET /api/flywheel/uat-generations returns the chain newest-first — per generation: name, branch, status, createdAt, members (issueId, title, branch, prUrl), heldOut with reasons, resolutions, stack {status, frontendUrl}, and per-member acceptance criteria resolved server-side via findSpecByIssue + extractACFromDocument from the shared src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts (NO new vBRIEF parser). POST /api/flywheel/uat-generations/:name/stack ensures the stack; POST /api/flywheel/uat-generations/:name/promote promotes; POST /api/flywheel/assemble-uat repurposed to force-reassembly via the reconciler. GET /api/flywheel/merge-queue keeps the branchName/prUrl enrichment; POST /merge-next unchanged (escape hatch).
- AC: GET /api/flywheel/uat-generations returns the documented shape including per-member ACs from the shared extractor, with graceful [] when no generations or no flywheel run exist
- AC: Stack and promote POSTs return structured success/error payloads (stale-base promote returns the reassemble guidance); assemble-uat now force-rebuilds and reports the new generation
- AC: No second vBRIEF-parsing path is introduced (verifiable from the diff: only acceptance-criteria.ts/extractACFromDocument is used); merge-queue response is unchanged apart from the existing enrichment
close_reason: Done in 001c0badb: GET /api/flywheel/uat-generations (per-member ACs via shared extractor), POST :name/stack, POST :name/promote, assemble-uat repurposed as forced reconcile; 60s reconciler service in main.ts

title: pan-1737: On-demand live UAT stack per generation: max 2 concurrent, mandatory teardown
description:
src/lib/cloister/uat-stack.ts: ensureUatStack(generation) renders the devcontainer on the generation worktree (ensureDevcontainerSync — folder uat-<codename>-<mmdd> yields host uat-<codename>-<mmdd>.pan.localhost via the FEATURE_FOLDER template) and runs docker compose up -d --build via async exec; records stackStartedAt. Enforce max 2 running UAT stacks: starting a third tears down the oldest first. teardownUatStack(generation) = compose down -v --remove-orphans + clear stack state; invoked on invalidation, promotion, and generation cleanup. Status probe for the API (running/starting/absent + frontendUrl).
- AC: Ensuring a stack on a ready generation yields a reachable frontend at uat-<codename>-<mmdd>.pan.localhost serving that generation's tree; the API reports its status and URL
- AC: A third concurrent stack request tears down the oldest running UAT stack first — at no point do more than 2 UAT stacks run (Docker network pool protection)
- AC: Invalidation and promotion always tear the generation's stack down (compose down -v --remove-orphans); no orphaned uat networks/containers accumulate; all exec is async
close_reason: Done in 3f273c145: ensureUatStack/teardownUatStack/probeUatStack — FEATURE_FOLDER Traefik host, HARD max 2 with oldest eviction, mandatory teardown; 13 tests

title: pan-1737: Promote: merge the tested batch to main, per-member post-merge exactly once
description:
src/lib/cloister/uat-promote.ts: precondition generation.status in (ready|superseded) AND baseSha == origin/main HEAD (else reject with a 'stale — reassemble' error). Merge --no-ff the uat branch into main in a temporary worktree (NEVER the primary checkout), push main, then for each member issue run the existing per-issue post-merge lifecycle through the PAN-328 in-flight guard exactly once (GitHub auto-marks the per-feature PRs merged because their commits become reachable from main). Mark the generation promoted, tear down its stack, invalidate all other live generations (main moved), let the reconciler rebuild from the remaining queue.
- AC: Promoting a ready generation whose baseSha matches origin/main produces one no-ff merge commit on main whose tree equals the uat branch tree, and pushes it
- AC: Every member issue goes through the post-merge lifecycle exactly once (in-flight guard respected; tests/unit/lib/cloister/in-flight-guard.test.ts stays green)
- AC: Promoting with a stale baseSha is rejected with an explanatory error and no git mutation; promoting an older superseded generation works when its baseSha still matches
- AC: After promotion the generation is promoted, its stack is torn down, other live generations are invalidated, and unit tests with fake deps cover success/stale/partial-failure reporting
close_reason: Done in 7fdb90373: promoteUatGeneration — stale-base hard check, no-ff merge in temp worktree, per-member post-merge exactly once via PAN-328 guard (guard suite green); 9 tests

title: pan-1737: Reconciler: auto-assemble on ready-set change, invalidate stale generations
description:
src/lib/cloister/uat-reconciler.ts + interval wiring in the dashboard server (60s, gated per-tick on isMergeTrainEnabled, single-flight). Each tick: compute the ready set from the flywheel merge queue (null-safe when no run is active — no-op); compare against the newest live generation (members ∪ heldOut issue sets, member branch headShas, baseSha vs origin/main). On difference: assemble the next generation (one assembly in flight at a time). Mark ready/superseded generations invalidated when baseSha no longer matches origin/main or a member left the queue / changed head; invalidation tears down that generation's stack. Newest ready generation supersedes older ready ones. Also export a force-reassemble entry for the API.
- AC: With merge_train_enabled on and >=1 ready feature, a generation exists within one tick with zero human action; with the flag off or no active flywheel run the tick is a no-op
- AC: A new ready feature triggers background assembly of a superset generation while the current generation row stays ready (status only flips to superseded when the new one reaches ready)
- AC: main advancing past baseSha, a member leaving the queue, or a member branch gaining commits marks affected generations invalidated and tears down their stacks; reassembly follows automatically
- AC: Reconciler unit tests with fake deps cover: no-op tick, first assembly, superset assembly, invalidation cascade; interval tests use fake timers
close_reason: Done in 4efffa7c1: reconcileUatGenerations — auto-assembly, staleness invalidation w/ stack teardown, stuck-row unwedge, failure backoff, single-flight; 18 tests

title: pan-1737: Assembly-agent conflict resolution: timeboxed headless run in the generation worktree
description:
Implement the resolveConflict hook as a headless agent run (claude -p, async execFile with timeout/AbortSignal, never execSync): cwd = generation worktree mid-conflict, mission = resolve THIS merge only (no behavior changes beyond resolution), then stage and commit with a 'uat-assembly: resolve <issueId> <-> <conflictingIds>' subject. Post-run verification in code: no unmerged paths, merge concluded with a commit. Timebox (default 5 min) or verification failure -> git merge --abort -> held out. Record resolution files+commit in the generation row.
- AC: A resolvable conflict between two member branches yields a resolution commit on the uat branch with the uat-assembly: marker, and the resolution (issueIds, files, commitSha) is recorded on the generation
- AC: Agent timeout or leftover unmerged paths results in merge --abort and held-out — the assembly never wedges and the server event loop is never blocked
- AC: Tests use fake timers / fake runner for the timebox path (no real multi-second waits) per the fake-timers rule
close_reason: Done in a11fbd557: buildConflictAgentHook — headless claude -p acceptEdits, 5min timebox, in-code verify+commit, never wedges; 8 tests (fake timers)

title: pan-1737: Assembly engine: persistent worktree, ordered merges, held-out fallback, generation recording
description:
Build src/lib/cloister/uat-generation-engine.ts (pure orchestrator with injected deps, modeled on uat-assemble.ts) + uat-generation-deps.ts (real git). Given the ready set (ordered members with branches), assemble a new generation: unique codename via makeUatCandidateName seeded per generation (not per day), persistent worktree at <projectRoot>/workspaces/uat-<codename>-<mmdd>/ on branch uat/<codename>-<mmdd> off origin/main, merge each feature branch in order with --no-edit, on conflict call the injected resolveConflict hook, on hook failure git merge --abort and record the feature as held out with reason, push -u origin at the end, record the generation row (members with headShas, resolutions, heldOut, baseSha). All git via async exec. Old uat worktrees/branches beyond the newest 3 non-promoted generations are removed (worktree remove + branch delete local+origin).
- AC: Assembling with zero conflicts produces a pushed uat/<codename>-<mmdd> branch off origin/main whose tree contains every member branch merge, in queue order, and a ready generation row with baseSha and member headShas
- AC: A conflicting member invokes the resolveConflict hook; hook failure aborts that merge only — the member lands in heldOut with a human-readable reason and assembly continues with remaining members
- AC: Generation codenames never collide with existing rows (isTaken consults the store); worktree folder name matches the branch (slash -> dash) so the Traefik host pattern applies
- AC: Cleanup keeps at most the newest 3 non-promoted generations' worktrees/branches; engine unit tests with fake deps cover happy path, conflict-resolved, held-out, and cleanup
close_reason: Done in 18123ada7: assembleUatGeneration + cleanupUatGenerations (keep-3), persistent worktrees, held-out fallback, 12 unit tests green

title: pan-1737: SQLite generation store: uat_generations table + CRUD module
description:
Add a uat_generations table to ~/.panopticon/panopticon.db via the schema.ts migration chain (bump schema version): name (PK, e.g. uat/calm-otter-0610), branch, worktreePath, baseSha, members JSON (issueId, title, branch, headSha, mergeOrder), heldOut JSON (issueId, reason), resolutions JSON (issueIds, files, commitSha), status (assembling|ready|superseded|invalidated|promoted|failed), stackStartedAt nullable, createdAt/updatedAt. Build src/lib/database/uat-generations-db.ts following the app-settings.ts / review-status-db.ts sync-wrapper pattern: insert, getByName, listChain (newest first), updateStatus, setStackStartedAt, clearStack. Unit tests for CRUD + chain ordering.
- AC: Schema migration creates uat_generations with the fields above; migration is idempotent and existing DBs upgrade cleanly
- AC: CRUD module exposes sync insert/getByName/listChain/updateStatus/stack-state functions in the established database module style; no execSync anywhere
- AC: Unit tests cover insert -> listChain ordering (newest first), status transitions, and JSON round-trip of members/heldOut/resolutions
close_reason: Done in d342e1887: schema v51 uat_generations + sync CRUD module, 7 unit tests green

title: pan-1700: Deacon backstop: re-send the continue prompt to a work agent stalled since resume with zero user records
description:
Add a new deacon patrol in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts (model it on nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads, deacon.ts:5899) and register it in runPatrol (deacon.ts:4818). For each running work agent with a live tmux session that is idle (isAgentIdleForNudge), detect a stalled resume: zero user records appended to the session JSONL since the last resume (via the shared transcript-landing helper, keyed off the resume entry the resume path already appends to continue state, or the agent's resume timestamp). When stalled, re-send the continue prompt (the default 'You are resuming work on <ISSUE>…' message, or the redelivered kickoff if kickoffDelivered===false), bounded by a per-agent cooldown file (mirror the .last-bead-nudge pattern). Do not fire for orphans (handled by recoverOrphanedAgents), closed issues, paused/troubled agents, or agents that already have user records since resume.
- AC: A new patrol identifies a stalled resume: status=running, role=work, live tmux, isAgentIdleForNudge, and ZERO user records in the session JSONL since the agent's last resume.
- AC: On detection it re-sends the continue prompt (not a generic ping), using the redelivered kickoff prompt when kickoffDelivered===false, otherwise the default resume continue message.
- AC: A per-agent cooldown (own marker file) prevents re-sending more than once per cooldown window; an agent with ANY user record since resume is skipped.
- AC: Orphans (no live tmux), closed issues, and paused/troubled agents are skipped — consistent with the existing patrol's gating.
- AC: The patrol is registered in runPatrol and its actions are logged like the sibling patrols.
- AC: Tests assert: a zero-user-record idle resumed agent is re-sent once then cooled down; an agent with a user record since resume is never re-sent; an orphan/closed/paused agent is skipped. typecheck + lint pass.
close_reason: Added stalled-resume deacon patrol with lastResumeAt tracking, zero-user-record detection, cooldown, prompt resend, runPatrol registration, and tests.

title: pan-1700: resumeAgent: verify the auto-continue prompt landed and redeliver once before flagging success
description:
In resumeAgent (src/lib/agents.ts, the non-pi/non-codex Claude branch at ~4406-4416), take a transcript snapshot (transcript-landing-helper) just before delivering the auto-continue prompt. After deliverAgentMessage returns, poll within a bounded window for a new user record to appear in the session JSONL. If none appears, redeliver the same effectiveMessage once and poll again. Only set messageDelivered=true and call markKickoffRedelivered AFTER landing is confirmed; if it never lands, set messageDelivered=false and log so the deacon backstop / existing kickoff machinery re-sends — never record a phantom delivery (the PAN-1491 failure). Keep the existing waitForReadySignal gate and the pi/codex branches unchanged.
- AC: Before delivering the auto-continue prompt on resume, a 'before' transcript snapshot is captured via the shared helper.
- AC: After delivery, the code polls (bounded) for a new user record; if none lands, it redelivers the same message exactly once and polls again.
- AC: messageDelivered=true and markKickoffRedelivered() are set ONLY after a landed user record is confirmed; if landing never confirms, messageDelivered=false and the failure is logged (no phantom success).
- AC: The pi and codex resume branches and the existing waitForReadySignal gate are unchanged.
- AC: Tests (using vi.useFakeTimers for the bounded poll/redelivery) assert: landing-on-first-delivery → no redelivery + messageDelivered true; never-lands → exactly one redelivery + messageDelivered false; lands-after-redelivery → messageDelivered true and markKickoffRedelivered called once when redeliveringKickoff.
- AC: typecheck, lint, and the resume tests pass.
close_reason: resumeAgent now verifies a user JSONL record lands after auto-continue delivery, redelivers once on no-land, and only marks delivered/kickoff redelivered after confirmation.

title: pan-1700: Shared helper: detect whether a user record landed in a session JSONL after a snapshot point
description:
Add a small, pure, well-unit-tested helper that answers 'has a user record been appended to this session JSONL since snapshot S?'. It resolves the JSONL via sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId) (src/lib/paths.ts:230), reads the file tail tolerantly (ignore unparseable trailing lines, mirroring waitForReadySignal's JSON.parse try/catch), and returns enough to take a 'before' snapshot and compare 'after' (e.g. a count of user-type records, or the timestamp/uuid of the last user record). It must never throw on a missing/empty/mid-write file — return a safe default. This is consumed by both resume-verify-redeliver and deacon-backstop-patrol; keep it free of dashboard-server imports so it does not bloat the agents.ts/deacon.ts import graph.
- AC: A helper resolves the Claude session JSONL from (workspace, sessionId) via sessionFilePath and returns a snapshot of user-record presence (count or last-user-record identity).
- AC: Given a 'before' snapshot, the helper reports whether a NEW user record was appended after it; an assistant-only or tool-only append does not count as a landed user message.
- AC: The helper never throws on a missing, empty, or mid-write JSONL (malformed trailing line) — it returns a safe default and is exercised by a unit test for each of those cases.
- AC: The helper has no import dependency on dashboard-server services (verified by it living in a lib module importable from both agents.ts and deacon.ts without cycles).
- AC: Unit tests cover: no new record, a new user record, an assistant-only append, and the three not-throwing cases; typecheck + lint pass.
close_reason: Added shared transcript landing snapshot helper with tolerant JSONL parsing and unit coverage for missing, malformed, user, assistant, and tool-only records.

title: pan-1700: Supervisor echo-confirmation before Enter (shared root-cause fix; also resolves #1697)
description:
In src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts, change injectPtyMessage so that after writing the message content it observes the child PTY output stream (child.onData) and confirms a normalized prefix of the message echoed in the input box BEFORE writing the standalone \r. Strip ANSI and normalize whitespace before matching; match a bounded prefix (mirror the tmux verifyLine slice(0,40) approach in src/lib/tmux.ts:790-858). Poll up to a bounded window (~1.5s) × 2 attempts, re-writing the content once on the first miss. On confirmation success, proceed exactly as today (echo-to-stdout transcript mirror unchanged, send \r, log). On final confirmation failure, make createPtySupervisorServer return a non-2xx response so postUnixSocketJson (src/lib/agents.ts:1303-1309) rejects and deliverAgentMessage records an honest supervisor failure (falling through to the tmux path in auto mode). Reconcile the caller side: raise the supervisor POST timeout in deliverAgentMessage (src/lib/agents.ts:1375, currently 2000ms) to exceed the confirmation budget so a legitimate confirmation is not cut short. Preserve all behavior for the success path; do not change the payload schema or the stdout transcript mirror.
- AC: After writing content, injectPtyMessage waits for a normalized (ANSI-stripped, whitespace-collapsed) prefix of the message to appear in the child PTY output before sending \r.
- AC: On confirmation timeout the content write is retried exactly once; on a second timeout the POST returns a non-2xx status (not 200).
- AC: When confirmation succeeds, the success path is byte-for-byte unchanged: content written, ~300ms settle, standalone \r sent, message echoed to stdout exactly once, socket_write logged.
- AC: deliverAgentMessage's supervisor POST timeout is raised to exceed the supervisor confirmation window so a confirmed delivery returns { ok:true, path:'supervisor' } within budget (no spurious tmux fallback).
- AC: Subprocess tests (extending src/lib/channels/__tests__/pty-supervisor.test.ts) cover both cases: a child that echoes its input → POST 200 and the message is delivered; a child whose PTY output never reflects the input → POST non-2xx after one retry, within budget.
- AC: deliver-agent-message tests assert that a non-2xx supervisor response is surfaced as a supervisor failure (strict 'supervisor' method throws; 'auto' falls through to tmux).
- AC: npm run build, typecheck, lint, and the supervisor + delivery test suites pass.
close_reason: Addressed inspection feedback by replacing real delay waits with fake-timer coverage and keeping subprocess tests as fast smoke coverage.

title: pan-1712: Point conv-lookup skill at `pan conv jsonl`; remove duplicated Python resolution
description:
In `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/scripts/conv-find.py`, rewrite `resolve_session_file(info)` to shell out to `pan conv jsonl --json <id>` (via subprocess), parse the returned `{ status, path }`, and return `(path, status)`. Delete the now-duplicated `encode_claude_project_dir()` and the inline derive+glob logic that the CLI now owns. Handle the failure modes cleanly: if `pan` is not on PATH (FileNotFoundError) or returns malformed/empty JSON, print a single clear stderr line and treat as 'unknown' — do NOT print a Python traceback and do NOT silently re-implement the removed derivation. Update `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/SKILL.md` to document `pan conv jsonl <id>` / `pan conv transcript <id>` as the canonical resolver, note that conv-find.py delegates to it, and note the --json status-vs-exit-code contract. Edit ONLY the sync-sources copy (the `.claude/skills/` copy is a gitignored sync target). Verify by running `python3 scripts/conv-find.py --jsonl <id>` against a live conversation (prints path, exit 0) and a known-expired one (reports expired, exit 1).
- AC: conv-find.py's resolve_session_file() resolves the path by calling `pan conv jsonl --json <id>`; the inline encode_claude_project_dir + derive + glob duplication is removed.
- AC: conv-find.py degrades gracefully when `pan` is missing or returns non-JSON/non-zero output: a single clear stderr line, no traceback, no silent re-implementation of the removed logic.
- AC: SKILL.md documents `pan conv jsonl` / `pan conv transcript` as the canonical resolver, states conv-find.py delegates to it, and explains the --json status-in-payload contract.
- AC: Only `sync-sources/skills/conv-lookup/` files are edited (not the gitignored `.claude/skills/` copy); `python3 scripts/conv-find.py --jsonl <id>` still prints the resolved path (exit 0) for a live conv and reports expired/unknown the same way.
close_reason: Implemented conv-lookup delegation to pan conv jsonl and verified live/expired/failure paths

title: pan-1712: Add `pan conv jsonl <conv-id>` command (alias `transcript`) + registration + tests
description:
Add `src/cli/commands/conversations/jsonl.ts` exporting `jsonlAction(convId: string, opts: { json?: boolean })`. Parse convId as an integer (exit 1 with a clear stderr error if non-numeric); look up the conversation with `getConversationById()` (exit 1 'Conversation <N> not found' if missing); call `resolveConversationTranscript(conv.cwd, conv.claudeSessionId)`. Plain mode: on 'ok' print the absolute path to stdout and exit 0; on 'expired' print a Claude-Code-retention message to stderr and exit 1; on 'unknown' print a 'no claude_session_id recorded for this conversation' message to stderr and exit 1. `--json` mode: print one JSON object `{ status, path, conversationId, claudeSessionId, cwd }` to stdout and exit 0 regardless of status. Register the subcommand in `src/cli/commands/conversations/index.ts`: `.command('jsonl <conv-id>').alias('transcript').description(...).option('--json', ...)`, with a description that states the argument is the conversation id (the number in pan.localhost/conv/<N>) — distinct from `pan conv show`. Add `src/cli/commands/conversations/__tests__/jsonl.test.ts` following the show.test.ts harness (seed a conversation row, mock process.exit/console, assert exit codes + output for ok / expired / unknown / not-found across plain and --json modes).
- AC: `pan conv jsonl <conv-id>` and its alias `pan conv transcript <conv-id>` are registered under the conversations/conv group with a `--json` option; the description states the argument is the conversation id (the /conv/<N> number).
- AC: On status 'ok', prints only the absolute JSONL path to stdout and exits 0.
- AC: On 'expired' prints a retention-explaining message to stderr and exits 1; on 'unknown' prints a no-claude_session_id message to stderr and exits 1.
- AC: Non-numeric conv-id and not-found conversation each exit 1 with a clear stderr error.
- AC: `--json` prints a single object `{ status, path, conversationId, claudeSessionId, cwd }` to stdout and exits 0 for ok/expired/unknown alike (status conveyed in the payload).
- AC: jsonl.test.ts covers ok / expired / unknown / not-found in both plain and --json modes (exit codes + output); `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and the test suite pass.
close_reason: Added pan conv jsonl/transcript command with JSON/plain output contracts and tests

title: pan-1712: Add status-aware resolveConversationTranscript() helper + tests
description:
Create `src/lib/conversations/transcript-path.ts` exporting `resolveConversationTranscript(cwd: string | null | undefined, claudeSessionId: string | null | undefined): { path: string | null; status: 'ok' | 'expired' | 'unknown' }`. Derive the path with the existing `sessionFilePath(cwd, claudeSessionId)` from src/lib/paths.ts. Resolution order, mirroring conv-find.py's resolve_session_file(): (1) if claudeSessionId is falsy → { path: null, status: 'unknown' }; (2) compute derived = sessionFilePath(cwd, sessionId); if it exists on disk → { path: derived, status: 'ok' }; (3) else glob `~/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` (one level only) and return the first match as { path: match, status: 'ok' }; (4) else → { path: derived, status: 'expired' }. Add a unit test file under src/lib/conversations covering all four outcomes with a temp HOME / ~/.claude/projects fixture. This helper is the single source of truth for status-aware transcript resolution that the CLI command and (transitively) the skill depend on — keep its status taxonomy and return shape stable.
- AC: Exported `resolveConversationTranscript(cwd, claudeSessionId)` returns `{ path: string | null, status: 'ok' | 'expired' | 'unknown' }` and derives via sessionFilePath() from src/lib/paths.ts.
- AC: Returns status 'ok' with the absolute derived path when `sessionFilePath(cwd, sessionId)` exists on disk.
- AC: When the derived path is absent, returns status 'ok' with the first `~/.claude/projects/*/<sessionId>.jsonl` glob match (one directory level), preserving conv-find.py's fallback for cwd-encoding drift.
- AC: Returns 'expired' (path = the expected derived path) when claudeSessionId is set but no JSONL exists on disk or via glob; returns 'unknown' (path = null) when claudeSessionId is null/empty.
- AC: Unit tests cover all four outcomes (ok-derived, ok-glob, expired, unknown) using a temp ~/.claude/projects fixture; `npm run typecheck` and the new test pass.
close_reason: Fixed inspection blockers: canonical spec is clean; pipeline runtime status no longer writes metadata into .pan/specs

title: pan-1706: Add MCP browser_close cleanup phase to the pipeline UAT prompt
description:
In src/lib/cloister/prompts/uat-agent.md, add a final cleanup step that calls the Playwright MCP `browser_close` tool after Phase 4 (Console & Network Audit) and on any early termination. Also clarify near the existing 'Handling Playwright' code snippet that `await browser.close()` is the Playwright *library* API; an agent driving Playwright via the MCP server must call the `browser_close` MCP tool instead. Keep the change to prose/instructions only — no code.
- AC: src/lib/cloister/prompts/uat-agent.md includes a final cleanup step that calls the `browser_close` MCP tool after the four phases and on early termination.
- AC: The prompt clarifies that the existing `await browser.close()` snippet is the Playwright library API and that the agent must call the MCP `browser_close` tool (so the snippet is no longer the only/misleading close guidance).

title: pan-1706: Add browser_close boundary to the pan-uat-agent subagent definition
description:
In sync-sources/agents/pan-uat-agent.md, add a Boundaries/cleanup line requiring the subagent to call the Playwright MCP `browser_close` tool before it emits its UAT PASSED / UAT FAILED sentinel, and also when it aborts early. Edit ONLY the sync-source — .claude/agents/pan-uat-agent.md is a gitignored sync target and must not be edited or committed (hazard H1).
- AC: sync-sources/agents/pan-uat-agent.md requires calling the `browser_close` MCP tool before/when emitting the final UAT PASSED|FAILED sentinel and on early abort.
- AC: Only sync-sources/agents/pan-uat-agent.md is changed in the diff; .claude/agents/pan-uat-agent.md (gitignored sync target) is not modified or committed.

title: pan-1706: Add browser_close cleanup to the test role's Browser UAT contract
description:
In roles/test.md, under the 'Browser UAT Contract' section (around lines 84-93), add an explicit cleanup requirement: once browser UAT finishes — whether the verdict is TESTS PASSED, TESTS FAILED, or the role exits early — the role MUST call the Playwright MCP `browser_close` tool to release the headless browser. Add a one-line rationale: a left-open headless page keeps running the full dashboard at full poll rate, multiplying server load (PAN-1706).
- AC: roles/test.md instructs the test role to call the Playwright MCP `browser_close` tool after emitting the UAT verdict AND on any early exit/failure.
- AC: The added instruction names the `browser_close` MCP tool explicitly (not just 'close the browser') and gives the one-line rationale (a left-open headless page keeps polling the dashboard at full rate).

title: pan-1705: Wrap pre-gate bun install and contracts build_command in the low-priority helper
description:
In src/lib/cloister/verification-runner.ts, the pre-gate dependency install (`installCmd`, ~line 322) and the contracts package `build_command` (~line 334) run via execAsync at NORMAL priority before runQualityGates is reached, so they bypass the validation.ts nice wrapper. Wrap each of these commands with the same wrapLowPriority helper from the previous bead so the CPU- and I/O-heavy install/build cannot starve the dashboard event loop during a verification-gate run. Keep the existing cwd, env, encoding, and per-command timeouts (install 60s, build 30s) intact; do not change git fetch/merge calls.
- AC: On Linux, the pre-gate install command and the contracts build command are each executed through the wrapLowPriority helper (nice + ionice prefix present in the spawned command string).
- AC: Non-zero exit codes from install/build still reject (failure still aborts the verification run) and the existing per-command timeouts are preserved — verified by tests/cloister/verification-runner.test.ts (extended if needed).
- AC: git fetch/merge and any non-install/non-build execAsync calls in verification-runner.ts are left untouched.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test (cloister suite) pass.

title: pan-1705: Set MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2 on the dashboard server child (RSS-fragmentation experiment)
description:
Add MALLOC_ARENA_MAX:'2' to the environment of every spawn site that launches dist/dashboard/server.js (the supervised API child in src/cli/commands/dev.ts:366-369 env block, plus any production `pan up` server spawn site — grep for spawn(node22,[bundledServer]) / server.js spawn). This caps glibc malloc arenas to curb the RSS fragmentation observed in the incident (1.3 GB RSS vs 236 MB V8 heap) caused by constant child spawning. This is the experiment the issue explicitly requests; it does not affect the V8 heap or correctness, only resident memory.
- AC: Every spawn of dist/dashboard/server.js includes MALLOC_ARENA_MAX='2' in its env block.
- AC: The dashboard still starts cleanly under `pan up` / `pan dev` (server boots, /ws/terminal works) — no behavioural regression from the env var.
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass.

title: pan-1705: cancelQueries before invalidateQueries on panopticon:reconnected so restart-stalled fetches recover instantly
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx, the panopticon:reconnected handler (~lines 1030-1037) currently calls only queryClient.invalidateQueries(). Because React Query dedupes against an in-flight promise, a messages fetch that hung across a server restart blocks the refetch until the 20s fetchWithTimeout fires. Update the handler to call queryClient.cancelQueries() (abort all in-flight queries) BEFORE queryClient.invalidateQueries() (refetch active ones), so a conversation click that was loading when the server restarted resolves on reconnect in ~0s instead of ~20s. Keep the existing console.log; the handler may become async or fire-and-forget the cancel (void queryClient.cancelQueries()).
- AC: The panopticon:reconnected handler calls queryClient.cancelQueries() before queryClient.invalidateQueries().
- AC: Manual/observed: with a conversation panel stuck on Loading… because a server restart killed an in-flight messages fetch, dispatching panopticon:reconnected (transport reconnect) clears the spinner and shows messages without waiting the full 20s timeout and without a page reload.
- AC: Frontend typecheck/lint pass; no other reconnect listeners (EventRouter, NoResumeBanner) are altered.

title: pan-1705: Shared low-priority command wrapper (nice + ionice) applied to the local quality-gate runner
description:
Add a shared helper (e.g. exported `wrapLowPriority(command: string): string` in src/lib/cloister/validation.ts or a small src/lib/process-priority.ts) that prefixes a command with `nice -n 19 ionice -c2 -n7 sh -c '<escaped>'` on Linux, `nice -n 19 sh -c '<escaped>'` on other POSIX platforms (no ionice on darwin), and returns the bare command on win32. Replace the existing inline nice-only wrapper at src/lib/cloister/validation.ts:423-432 with a call to this helper. Preserve the existing single-quote escaping (command.replace(/'/g, "'\\''")) byte-for-byte — only the fixed-literal priority prefix changes. Do NOT touch the SSH or docker-exec branches.
- AC: On Linux, the local (non-remote, non-container) gate command is wrapped as `nice -n 19 ionice -c2 -n7 sh -c '<escaped>'`; the escaped inner command is byte-identical to the pre-change output.
- AC: On darwin the wrapper is `nice -n 19 sh -c '<escaped>'` (no ionice); on win32 it is the bare command — verified by a unit test that stubs process.platform for all three branches.
- AC: The SSH (fly) and docker-exec gate branches in validation.ts are unchanged; existing tests/cloister/verification-gate.test.ts still passes.
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass.

title: pan-1704: Route-level regression test: 206k-token same-harness switch appends no compact_boundary
description:
Add a route/service-level regression test (extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/conversation-compaction.test.ts or add a sibling switch-model test) for the exact observed incident: a session JSONL whose latest assistant usage totals ~206000 tokens with an observed ceiling > 200000, switching between two same-routing Anthropic models. Assert the session file gains NO new compact_boundary entry across the switch decision (compaction is skipped). Cover the contrast case (context > effectiveTargetWindow*0.8 → a compact_boundary IS appended) so the gate is proven in both directions. Use fixtures/mocks consistent with the existing conversation-compaction tests; no real wall-clock delays (fake timers if any retry/delay is involved).
- AC: Test: 206k-token, same-routing Anthropic switch with inferred 1M effective window asserts no compact_boundary is appended to the JSONL (AC#5 of the issue)
- AC: Contrast test: a context that exceeds effectiveTargetWindow*0.8 DOES append a compact_boundary, proving the gate fires when the window forces it
- AC: The new test(s) pass under npm test and use no real-wall-clock sleeps
close_reason: Added route-level regression coverage proving a 206k-token same-harness Anthropic model switch does not append a compact_boundary.

title: pan-1704: Implement Tier 1 — lossless in-session /model switch via the PTY supervisor with statusline verification and Tier 2 fallback
description:
In the switch-model route, when strategy.tier === 1 (useModelCommand): do NOT kill the session and do NOT markRespawnPending. Persist the new model in the DB (setConversationModel), then deliver `/model <targetModel>` via deliverAgentMessage(conv.tmuxSession, '/model <targetModel>', 'switch-model-tier1', resolveConversationDeliveryMethod(conv)). Verify acceptance: poll capturePane(conv.tmuxSession) for the target model's displayName (getModelCapabilitySync(...).displayName) in the statusline within a bounded timeout (~5s). On success return active with the new model (sessionAlive:true, no respawn). On delivery error, timeout, or missed echo, fall back to the Tier 2 path (kill + respawn --resume, no compaction) so the switch always completes. Confirm the exact `/model` argument form Claude Code accepts (full id vs alias) during implementation and encode it.
- AC: Anthropic→Anthropic switch with context < target window performs Tier 1: the tmux session is NOT killed, no respawn occurs, no compact_boundary is appended, and full history remains in context (AC#1 of the issue)
- AC: Terminal scrollback is preserved across a Tier 1 switch (the blank-terminal symptom does not occur because the pane/TUI is never respawned)
- AC: When the /model delivery fails or the statusline echo is not observed within the timeout, the route falls back to Tier 2 (kill + respawn --resume, no compaction) and still returns a live session with the new model — never a wedged or half-switched state
- AC: Tier 1 persists the new model to the DB before delivery so a fallback respawn uses the correct model
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass
close_reason: Implemented safe Tier 1 in-session /model switching with statusline verification and respawn fallback.

title: pan-1704: Refactor switch-model route to be tier-aware (defer kill) and replace maybeCompactBeforeRespawn's modelChanged force-flag with a window-fit shouldCompact gate
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts (postConversationSwitchModelRoute, ~2401-2551): compute the tier BEFORE killing the session. Gather inputs — currentModel=conv.model, targetModel=model, harnessChanged, providerRoutingChanged = getProviderExportsForModel(currentModel) !== getProviderExportsForModel(targetModel), contextUsage = computeContextUsage(sessionFile, conv.model) to get contextTokens (estimatedTokens) and currentObservedCeiling (maxObservedInputTokens), cliproxyRouted = getProviderForModelSync(targetModel).name==='openai' && await getProviderAuthMode(targetModel)==='subscription', registryWindow = getModelCapabilitySync(resolveModelIdSync(targetModel)).contextWindow → effectiveTargetWindow via getEffectiveTargetWindow → strategy = decideSwitchStrategy(...). Wire Tier 4 (harnessChanged: existing converter branch, verbatim), Tier 3 (kill+markRespawnPending → compact via the updated maybeCompactBeforeRespawn with shouldCompact:true → respawn --resume), Tier 2 (kill+markRespawnPending → respawn --resume, shouldCompact:false → no compaction). Leave Tier 1 to the next bead: for now a Tier-1-eligible decision should fall through to the Tier 2 path (kill+respawn --resume, no compaction) so this bead is independently correct and mergeable. ALSO update maybeCompactBeforeRespawn (conversation-compaction.ts:176) and MaybeCompactBeforeRespawnOptions: replace `modelChanged: boolean` with `shouldCompact: boolean`; drop the internal COMPACT_TOKEN_THRESHOLD/estimateContextTokens gate; keep the isBackgroundFeatureEnabled('summaryFork') early-return; compact only when shouldCompact && summaryFork enabled. Update the single caller accordingly.
- AC: The session kill (formerly conversations.ts:2458) is moved to run only on Tier 2/3/4; markRespawnPending is set only on respawn tiers
- AC: Tier 2 (cross-provider same-harness, context fits window): respawn with --resume and NO compaction; the session JSONL gains no compact_boundary (AC#2 of the issue)
- AC: Tier 3 (context > effectiveTargetWindow*0.8): compaction runs and the summary references the correct pre-switch token count; respawn --resume follows (AC#3 of the issue)
- AC: Tier 4 (harness change): behavior is byte-for-byte the existing convertConversationTranscript flow; native compaction never runs for a harness change (AC#4 of the issue)
- AC: maybeCompactBeforeRespawn takes shouldCompact (not modelChanged), preserves the summaryFork early-return, and compacts only when shouldCompact && summaryFork enabled; the lone caller and MaybeCompactBeforeRespawnOptions are updated; no orphaned imports/constants remain
- AC: providerRoutingChanged is derived from getProviderExportsForModel string equality; two Anthropic models compare equal, claude vs gpt compare unequal
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass; the existing conversation-compaction route/service tests still pass
close_reason: Moved switch-model window/tier decision before session kill and replaced unconditional model-change compaction with a shouldCompact gate.

title: pan-1704: Add pure tier-decision module (getEffectiveTargetWindow + decideSwitchStrategy) with unit tests
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/switch-strategy.ts with two PURE, side-effect-free functions plus a SAFETY_FACTOR = 0.8 constant. (1) getEffectiveTargetWindow({ registryWindow, currentObservedCeiling, cliproxyRouted, providerRoutingChanged }): number — returns CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW (150000) when cliproxyRouted; else max(1_000_000, registryWindow) when !providerRoutingChanged && currentObservedCeiling > registryWindow (mirrors the extended-context inference in conversation-service.ts:1148-1152); else registryWindow. (2) decideSwitchStrategy({ harnessChanged, providerRoutingChanged, contextTokens, effectiveTargetWindow }): { tier: 1|2|3|4, compact: boolean, useModelCommand: boolean } — Tier 4 first when harnessChanged; else Tier 3 (compact:true) when contextTokens > effectiveTargetWindow * SAFETY_FACTOR; else Tier 1 (useModelCommand:true) when !providerRoutingChanged; else Tier 2. The route (next bead) computes the async inputs and feeds these. Ship src/lib/conversations/__tests__/switch-strategy.test.ts covering every branch including the regression cases.
- AC: getEffectiveTargetWindow returns 150000 (CLIPROXY_CODEX_CONTEXT_WINDOW) for cliproxyRouted targets regardless of observed ceiling
- AC: getEffectiveTargetWindow lifts to 1_000_000 when !providerRoutingChanged and currentObservedCeiling (e.g. 206000) > registryWindow (200000); returns registryWindow when ceiling <= window
- AC: getEffectiveTargetWindow does NOT lift the window for a cross-provider (providerRoutingChanged) non-cliproxy target even if the source ceiling exceeds the registry window (H6 guard)
- AC: decideSwitchStrategy returns tier 4 when harnessChanged regardless of all other inputs
- AC: decideSwitchStrategy returns tier 1 (useModelCommand:true, compact:false) when !harnessChanged && !providerRoutingChanged && contextTokens <= effectiveTargetWindow*0.8
- AC: decideSwitchStrategy returns tier 2 (useModelCommand:false, compact:false) when providerRoutingChanged && contextTokens fits the window
- AC: decideSwitchStrategy returns tier 3 (compact:true) when contextTokens > effectiveTargetWindow*0.8, for both same- and cross-routing
- AC: Regression: 206000 tokens, same-routing Anthropic switch, effective window inferred to 1_000_000 → decideSwitchStrategy returns tier 1, compact:false (the exact observed incident at the decision layer)
- AC: Module is pure: no fs/network/process imports; unit tests run without mocks for the decision functions; npm run typecheck and the new test file pass
close_reason: Added pure switch strategy module with effective window and tier decision logic plus unit tests.

title: pan-1629: Regression test: N concurrent beads operations all succeed (reproduces the RUN-14 failure)
description:
Add a test that reproduces the original failure and proves the fix. Fire N (>=5) concurrent operations that exercise the start beads path (gate query + dedup/create) against a shared temp Dolt DB (or a deterministic mock injecting transient lock errors for the first attempts). Assert: no operation aborts with 'dedup failed: list failed', none emits the spurious 'No beads tasks found … Planning must create a task breakdown', and all N resolve beads successfully. Use vi.useFakeTimers for any backoff. This is the regression lock for the whole issue.
- AC: Test fires >=5 concurrent start-path beads operations and all succeed (no 'list failed', no spurious 'no beads')
- AC: Test injects transient lock errors on early attempts and asserts retry recovers (deterministic, fake timers)
- AC: Test fails against the pre-fix code path (guards the regression) and passes after the fix
close_reason: Added deterministic concurrent start-gate retry regression test

title: pan-1629: Verify `pan start --host --yes` returns promptly after spawn (the Related note)
description:
Confirm the secondary symptom is resolved. `pan start` returns after spawnAgent creates a detached tmux session (it does not attach), so the prior slow return was bd calls blocking on the Dolt lock. Establish that with the lock+retry work, each `pan start --host --yes` returns within a bounded time even under parallel launch (bd blocking is now bounded by the lock acquisition wait + retry bound, not the agent's lifetime). If a genuine separate attach/block bug is found, fix it; otherwise document that the prompt-return behavior is intended and the prior slowness was lock blocking. Provide a documented repro (or test) firing N parallel `--host --yes` and confirming all N return and all N agents spawn.
- AC: N parallel `pan start --host --yes` invocations each return within a bounded time and all N work agents are spawned
- AC: The cause of any residual delay is identified as bd-lock blocking (now bounded) — or a separate bug is fixed; the finding is documented
close_reason: Verified and documented that pan start --host --yes exits after spawn without attaching

title: pan-1629: Never report 'planning must create beads' on a transient bd failure; emit an honest locked-retry message
description:
In src/cli/commands/start.ts, classify the recovery/gate failure (via isTransientBdError). When the underlying failure is a transient/lock error — or when the issue's beads are known to exist (live count seen previously OR non-empty JSONL) — do NOT print 'No beads tasks found … Planning must create a task breakdown' and do NOT trigger destructive vBRIEF re-materialization. Instead print an honest message (e.g. 'beads database was temporarily locked by a concurrent operation; retried N times — re-run `pan start <id>` shortly') and exit with a distinct, non-zero, retryable status code. The 'planning must create beads' guidance must only appear when beads are genuinely absent (live query succeeded and returned empty AND JSONL empty).
- AC: A transient/lock failure during gate or recovery prints a 'temporarily locked — retry' message, NOT 'Planning must create a task breakdown'
- AC: A transient read failure does NOT trigger destructive vBRIEF re-materialization (no bead deletion on a lock error)
- AC: 'planning must create beads' guidance still appears only when beads are genuinely absent (live query returned empty AND JSONL empty)
- AC: The transient-failure exit code is distinct from the genuine-no-beads exit code so callers/Flywheel can distinguish retryable from re-plan
close_reason: Added honest retryable messaging for transient bd lock failures

title: pan-1629: Route the `pan start` beads-gate query and `beads-query.ts` through lock + retry (get the LIVE count, not stale JSONL)
description:
Make the beads-resolution gate reliably reflect live Dolt under contention. In src/cli/commands/start.ts: the gate (`hasBeadsTasks`/`countBeadsTasks` at lines 541/575, used only within start.ts) and `validateBeadsMatchPlan` must obtain the live count via the lock+retry helper before falling back to the JSONL snapshot — so a transient `bd list` failure retries and succeeds instead of spuriously returning 0 and triggering recovery. Prefer a bounded retry around the existing call (sync retry loop) or an async start-path helper; enumerate callers (all in start.ts) before changing signatures. In src/lib/beads-query.ts, wrap `queryBeadsForIssuePromise`'s `bd list` in retry too (the dashboard start-agent path). The JSONL fallback remains only as a genuine last resort.
- AC: Under simulated lock contention, the start gate retries `bd list` and returns the true live count (>0 when beads exist) instead of falling back to a stale/empty JSONL
- AC: queryBeadsForIssuePromise in beads-query.ts retries on transient lock errors before the JSONL fallback
- AC: No signature change leaks beyond start.ts callers; existing start.ts tests stay green
close_reason: Routed pan start beads gate and beads-query live reads through runBdWithRetry

title: pan-1629: Route create-beads/dedup (`clearBeadsForIssue` → `bd list`) through the cross-process lock + retry
description:
In src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts, make the dedup path resilient: route `listBeadsForIssue` and `clearBeadsForIssue`'s bd invocations through runBdWithRetry, and make the whole `createBeadsFromVBriefPromise` critical section additionally hold the cross-process lock (compose with the existing in-process withBdMutexPromise — do not replace it). The 'dedup failed: list failed:' abort (beads.ts:254) must no longer fire on transient lock contention; it should only fire on a genuine, retries-exhausted failure. Keep the existing bd-ping/doctor recovery and the create logic intact.
- AC: listBeadsForIssue/clearBeadsForIssue retry on transient lock errors instead of immediately returning 'list failed'
- AC: createBeadsFromVBrief holds the cross-process lock for its bd-touching section, composed with (not replacing) the existing in-process mutex
- AC: Existing src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts stays green (dedup-failure-on-genuine-error cases still abort)
close_reason: Routed create-beads dedup through bd process lock and retry

title: pan-1629: Add a cross-process bd lock + bounded-retry wrapper (mirroring restart-lock.ts)
description:
Create src/lib/bd-process-lock.ts providing `withBdProcessLock(caller, fn, opts?)` and a `runBdWithRetry` helper. The lock is an atomic O_EXCL lockfile under `getPanopticonHome()/locks/bd-<slug-of-resolved-shared-beads-dir>.lock`, mirroring src/lib/restart-lock.ts: holder = {pid, ts, caller}; stale-reclaim when holder PID is dead OR age > bound; bounded acquisition wait with backoff+jitter; ALWAYS release in finally. Key the lockfile on the RESOLVED shared Dolt `.beads` dir (resolve `.beads/redirect` → main repo) so all worktrees of one project share one lock. `runBdWithRetry` wraps a bd invocation: acquire the process lock, run, and on isTransientBdError (from the classifier bead) retry with bounded exponential backoff+jitter; surface a typed/contextful failure when retries are exhausted. Provide both a Promise API and an Effect variant consistent with restart-lock.ts. This is the concurrency foundation the wiring beads build on — get the lock KEY (shared DB, not workspace) and stale-reclaim right or the bug persists / deadlocks.
- AC: withBdProcessLock serializes across SEPARATE processes (two concurrent acquirers do not both hold it); proven by a test that forks/simulates two holders
- AC: Lockfile path is derived from the resolved shared Dolt `.beads` dir so two worktrees of the same project share one lock
- AC: A stale lock (dead PID or age beyond bound) is reclaimed; a live holder is respected; the lock is always released in finally
- AC: runBdWithRetry retries on isTransientBdError with bounded backoff and stops after the bound, surfacing a typed transient failure (tests use vi.useFakeTimers — no real backoff sleeps)
- AC: Acquisition wait is bounded so a wedged holder degrades to a typed transient failure rather than hanging forever
close_reason: Added cross-process bd lock and bounded retry helper

title: pan-1629: Add a conservative transient/lock bd-error classifier
description:
Add a pure predicate `isTransientBdError(error): boolean` (e.g. in a new src/lib/bd-process-lock.ts or src/lib/bd-mutex.ts) that returns true ONLY for Dolt/beads lock-contention signatures observed when a concurrent process holds the DB lock — and false for everything else, especially 'genuine empty result' (which is exit 0 + `[]`, never an error).
