First confirm the real Dolt lock stderr text by running concurrent `bd list` against a throwaway temp DB, then match those signatures conservatively (lock held / database is locked / resource temporarily unavailable / could not acquire). Cover the error shapes execFile/execFileSync produce (error.stderr, error.message, error.code). Default to NON-transient when uncertain.
- AC: isTransientBdError returns true for confirmed Dolt lock-contention stderr signatures
- AC: isTransientBdError returns false for a successful-but-empty bd list result and for genuine fatal errors (corrupt db, command-not-found)
- AC: Unit tests cover execFile error shapes (stderr string, message, code) and the empty-result false case
- AC: The confirmed Dolt lock stderr text is documented in a code comment citing how it was reproduced
close_reason: Added conservative transient bd lock classifier and unit tests

title: pan-1658: Unit-test the testStatus-from-green-CI reconciler
description:
Add a unit test (e.g. tests/unit/lib/cloister/test-status-reconciler.test.ts) for the new reconciler, mocking the GitHub PR/CI lookups and the review-status store so no network is required (follow the dependency-injection / module-mock pattern used by existing cloister reconciler tests such as merge-train-reconciler.test.ts and the github-app PR-state tests). Cover the happy path and every guard path so the reconciler can never mask a red build.
- AC: Test: reviewStatus=passed + testStatus=pending + non-terminal mergeStatus + OPEN PR + green CI check-runs ⇒ reconciler sets testStatus=passed (with testNotes) and reports one action.
- AC: Test: CI still pending ⇒ testStatus unchanged, no action; Test: CI failed/red ⇒ testStatus unchanged, no action (the reconciler never masks a red build).
- AC: Test: non-candidate states are untouched — testStatus='testing', testStatus already 'passed'/'skipped'/'failed', reviewStatus!='passed', and terminal mergeStatus (merged/merging/queued/verifying) each produce no mutation.
- AC: Test: greenness is judged on the CURRENT HEAD SHA (a stale/pre-rebase SHA is not used), and the per-issue cooldown suppresses repeat GitHub lookups within the throttle window on pending-CI / API-error outcomes.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass with the new test included.
close_reason: Added unit coverage for the green-CI test-status reconciler across happy path, pending/red CI, guard paths, current HEAD, cooldown, and API-error throttling.

title: pan-1658: Add a deacon reconciler that flips stuck testStatus=pending → passed from green CI, and wire it into the patrol
description:
Add a reconciler function (e.g. reconcileTestStatusFromGreenCi(): Promise<string[]>) in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts, modeled on reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge (deacon.ts:3333). Guard on isGitHubAppConfigured(). For each entry in loadReviewStatuses() where reviewStatus==='passed' AND testStatus==='pending' (exactly) AND mergeStatus is non-terminal (pending/undefined/null — never merged/merging/queued/verifying) AND a parseable GitHub PR URL exists: resolve the current PR HEAD (getPullRequestState → headSha, the same primitive reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge uses), skip if the PR is not OPEN or is merged, then evaluate the bead-1 CI greenness signal on that HEAD SHA. If green, call setReviewStatusSync(issueId, { testStatus: 'passed', testNotes: '<cite the CI run + short SHA>' }) — do NOT set readyForMerge or any other gate; it re-derives. Maintain a per-issue cooldown Map (set it on 'CI still pending' and on API error, like closedPrReadyReconcileCooldowns) so transient states don't burn the GitHub rate budget each patrol. Push a human-readable action string per reconciled issue and console.log it, matching the sibling reconcilers. Finally, wire the reconciler into the deacon patrol loop alongside the other reconcilers (deacon.ts ~5028-5041): call it, actions.push(...) the result, and addLog each action with state.patrolCycle.
- AC: For an issue with reviewStatus='passed', testStatus='pending', non-terminal mergeStatus, an OPEN unmerged PR, and green CI check-runs on the current HEAD, the reconciler sets testStatus='passed' with testNotes citing the CI run and the HEAD short SHA.
- AC: The reconciler never marks testStatus='passed' when CI on the current HEAD is pending/incomplete or failed/red; in those cases testStatus is left unchanged.
- AC: The candidate filter excludes testStatus values other than 'pending' (so a running 'testing' role, or already 'passed'/'skipped'/'failed', is never disturbed) and excludes terminal mergeStatus values (merged/merging/queued/verifying). The reconciler mutates only testStatus/testNotes — uatStatus, verificationStatus, reviewStatus, and readyForMerge are never written by it.
- AC: After the reconciler flips testStatus='passed', the existing event-driven derivation makes readyForMerge become true (assuming no other blocker/uat gate), so triggerMerge no longer returns 'Cannot merge: review and tests have not passed yet'.
- AC: The reconciler is guarded by isGitHubAppConfigured(), uses a per-issue cooldown on pending-CI and API-error outcomes to throttle GitHub calls, and returns string[] action descriptions consistent with the sibling reconcilers.
- AC: The reconciler is invoked once per deacon patrol cycle from the patrol loop, with its actions pushed into the patrol actions list and addLog'd, placed alongside reconcileMergedButReviewing / reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge.
close_reason: Added reconcileTestStatusFromGreenCi, wired it into patrol, and verified with targeted eslint plus typecheck.

title: pan-1658: Add a check-runs-driven CI greenness signal for a commit SHA in github-app.ts
description:
Expose a reusable function in src/lib/github-app.ts that reports whether GitHub Actions CI is green on a given (owner, repo, sha), computed from the /repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{sha}/check-runs endpoint ONLY. It must NOT depend on the combined commit-status (/status) endpoint, because that folds in the self-reported panopticon/review and panopticon/test contexts that are stuck/absent after a rebase (see continue.json hazard H1). The existing private getCommitCheckState already fetches check-runs; refactor/extend it (or add a sibling like getCiCheckRunsState) so the reconciler can ask a single, unambiguous question: 'are the CI check-runs on this exact SHA complete and all-successful?'. Return enough detail to distinguish green (≥1 success, 0 pending, 0 failed), not-yet (any pending/incomplete), and red (any failed). Empirically confirm against a real rebased PR that an absent/empty combined commit-status does not contaminate the signal before finalizing.
- AC: An exported function in github-app.ts returns a green verdict for a SHA only when there is ≥1 successful CI check-run AND zero pending/incomplete check-runs AND zero failed check-runs.
- AC: The greenness verdict is derived from the /commits/{sha}/check-runs endpoint only; it does NOT block on, or require, the panopticon/review or panopticon/test self-reported commit-statuses (a commit missing those statuses can still be judged green if its CI check-runs are green).
- AC: Any pending/incomplete check-run yields a 'not yet green' verdict (distinct from 'failed'); any failed/timed-out/cancelled check-run yields a 'red' verdict; a commit with zero CI check-runs is NOT green.
- AC: Existing callers of getCommitCheckState / getPullRequestState (checksPending/checksFailed behaviour and reconcileClosedPrReadyForMerge) are unchanged — typecheck, lint, and the existing github-app/PR-state tests stay green.
close_reason: Added getCiCheckRunsState check-runs-only CI verdict helper with unit coverage, typecheck/lint verification, and empirical check-runs/status confirmation.

title: pan-1647: Guard tests: normal-flow spec reads still resolve correctly after the resolver change
description:
Add test-only coverage protecting the blast radius of the projectRootFromWorkspace change (which flips read resolution from <workspace>/.pan/specs/ to <mainRoot>/.pan/specs/ for ALL workspaces). Add two cases in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/io.test.ts (reuse the worktree-shaped temp fixtures): (1) PRE-PROMOTION — when only the workspace draft <workspace>/.pan/spec.vbrief.json exists and no spec is in <root>/.pan/specs/, findPlanSync(workspace)/readWorkspacePlanSync(workspace) still resolve the draft via the findWorkspaceDraftPlanSync fallback (planning-in-progress window unaffected). (2) POST-PROMOTION — when a canonical spec for the issue exists in <root>/.pan/specs/ (the single-spec-on-main location), findPlanSync(workspace) resolves THAT main-side spec (its path is under <root>/.pan/specs/, not <workspace>/.pan/specs/). Then run the full vbrief + dashboard planning suite to confirm no regression: src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/{io,create-beads,beads,lifecycle-io}.test.ts and src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/{complete-planning,auto-promote-chain.integration}.test.ts. This bead is blocked by fix-resolver (the tests assert the corrected resolver behavior).
- AC: Pre-promotion test: with only <workspace>/.pan/spec.vbrief.json present (no <root>/.pan/specs/ entry for the issue), findPlanSync(workspace) and readWorkspacePlanSync(workspace) resolve the workspace draft via the fallback — confirming the planning-in-progress window is unaffected by the resolver change.
- AC: Post-promotion test: with a canonical spec for the issue present in <root>/.pan/specs/, findPlanSync(workspace) resolves the main-side spec (returned path is under <root>/.pan/specs/, not <workspace>/.pan/specs/) — confirming the intended single-spec-on-main resolution.
- AC: The full vbrief unit suite and the dashboard complete-planning + auto-promote-chain integration tests pass (npm test green), demonstrating no regression in normal planned-work spec resolution.
close_reason: Added explicit git-worktree guard tests for pre-promotion draft fallback and post-promotion main-side spec resolution; required vBRIEF/dashboard planning tests pass.

title: pan-1647: Correct projectRootFromWorkspace to resolve a workspace to its true project root (+ round-trip regression test)
description:
In src/lib/vbrief/io.ts, fix projectRootFromWorkspace (lines 146-151) so a workspace path resolves to its true project root. Remove the `if (existsSync(join(workspacePath,'.git')) && existsSync(join(workspacePath, PAN_DIRNAME,'specs'))) return workspacePath;` short-circuit branch — a git worktree always satisfies it (.git is a gitdir file; .pan/specs/ is checked out from main), which is exactly why reads were resolving to <workspace>/.pan/specs/ instead of the main root where writeAutoStartVBrief wrote the spec. The function should return resolve(workspacePath,'..','..') for the reachable case. This is safe because both callers (sync findPlanSync io.ts:189 and async findPlan io.ts:489) already return null earlier unless issueIdFromWorkspacePath(workspacePath) is non-null, i.e. the path is a genuine <projectRoot>/workspaces/feature-<id>/ worktree two levels below its project root. Removing the branch may leave the existsSync/join imports unused locally — clean up only imports YOUR change orphans. Then add a regression test (in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/, e.g. io.test.ts or a new auto-start-roundtrip.test.ts) that reproduces the WORKTREE shape: a temp <root>/workspaces/feature-pan-<n>/ with a `.git` file and a `.pan/specs/` directory (mirror the fixtures in create-beads.test.ts), call writeAutoStartVBrief(root, workspace, issue), then assert readWorkspacePlanSync(workspace) (and findPlanSync(workspace)) returns the synthesized plan (non-null, with the expected item). With execFile/bd mocked per create-beads.test.ts, extend the test through createBeadsFromVBrief(workspace) and assert created.length >= 1. The test MUST fail before the fix and pass after.
- AC: projectRootFromWorkspace(workspace) returns resolve(workspacePath,'..','..') (the main project root) for a feature-<id> worktree that has a .git file and a checked-out .pan/specs/ directory — it no longer returns the workspace itself.
- AC: A regression test reproduces the worktree shape (<root>/workspaces/feature-pan-<n>/ with a .git file and a .pan/specs/ dir) and asserts that after writeAutoStartVBrief(root, workspace, issue), readWorkspacePlanSync(workspace) and findPlanSync(workspace) resolve the synthesized plan (non-null). The test fails on the pre-fix code and passes on the post-fix code.
- AC: With execFile/bd mocked (per create-beads.test.ts), the round-trip writeAutoStartVBrief → createBeadsFromVBrief(workspace) returns success with created.length >= 1 — proving the 0-beads rollback no longer occurs.
- AC: Any import (existsSync/join) left unused in io.ts solely by removing the short-circuit branch is cleaned up; typecheck and lint pass.
close_reason: Fixed projectRootFromWorkspace to resolve workspaces to project root and added auto-start worktree round-trip regression covering createBeadsFromVBrief.

Thread the per-row result (and the row's rs.uatNotes from the store) into AwaitingMergeRow via new optional props. No rendering yet — just data plumbing and types. title: pan-1686: Unit-test the uat-context assembly and fallbacks
description:
Add a vitest covering the endpoint's assembly logic: AC extraction prefers metadata.kind==='acceptance_criterion' and falls back to all subItems; deliverables derive from item title + narrative.Action; changedFiles map correctly; and the graceful path (no vBRIEF / git failure) returns empty arrays with the right source descriptor rather than throwing. Prefer testing a pure assembly helper extracted from the route handler so the test does not need a live git repo; if testing the route directly, stub git via the exported helpers.

title: pan-1686: Add GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/uat-context endpoint
description:
Add a new route GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/uat-context in src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts. Resolve project/workspace exactly like the sibling state-md route (parseIssueIdSync + extractPrefixSync + getProjectPath + getWorkspacePathForIssue). Resolve the vBRIEF via the existing resolvePlanLocation() helper. From the vBRIEF doc, build: acceptanceCriteria (flatten subItems where metadata.kind==='acceptance_criterion'; if an item has subItems but none AC-tagged, include all of that item's subItems) and deliverables (one entry per item: { title, action: narrative.Action }), plus proposal (narratives.Proposal). Compute changedFiles + diffStat by calling the exported review-context helpers against the workspace path (getDiffBase then getChangedFiles/getDiffStat), mapping ChangedFile to { path, status, additions, deletions }, truncated/sorted as already done by the helper. Wrap git work in try/catch: on any failure return changedFiles:[] and a null/empty diffStat. Return a JSON payload { issueId, acceptanceCriteria, deliverables, proposal, changedFiles, diffStat, source: { plan: 'vbrief'|'none', files: 'git'|'none' } }. Register the route in the workspaces router export list alongside the other routes. Never throw to the client — return 200 with empty fields when data is unavailable (the card must never blank).
- AC: GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/uat-context returns 200 with acceptanceCriteria[], deliverables[], proposal, changedFiles[], diffStat, and a source descriptor.
- AC: acceptanceCriteria are sourced from vBRIEF subItems with metadata.kind==='acceptance_criterion', with the documented fallback to all subItems of an item; deliverables list item title + Action narrative.
- AC: changedFiles come from git diff base...HEAD in the workspace (reusing the exported review-context helpers); on git failure or missing workspace the route returns changedFiles:[] and source.files:'none' without erroring.
- AC: Project/workspace/vBRIEF resolution is tracker-agnostic (works for PAN/MIN/KRUX/AUR/... via parseIssueIdSync/extractPrefixSync), and an invalid issue id returns 400.
- AC: All git calls are async (execAsync); no execSync is introduced; the existing /plan route is unchanged.
close_reason: Added GET /api/workspaces/:issueId/uat-context with vBRIEF AC/deliverable assembly, async git changed-file summary reuse, graceful fallbacks, and route registration; typecheck passes

title: pan-1686: Export git changed-files helpers from review-context.ts for reuse
description:
In src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts, add the `export` keyword to getDiffBase(cwd), getChangedFiles(cwd, base), and getDiffStat(cwd, base) so they can be reused by the new UAT-context route without duplicating git-diff logic. The ChangedFile interface is already exported. Do not change their behavior, signatures, or the existing review-context flow.
- AC: getDiffBase, getChangedFiles, and getDiffStat are exported from review-context.ts with unchanged signatures/behavior.
- AC: Existing review-context behavior and its consumers (buildReviewContextPromise, formatTier1Summary) are unaffected; typecheck and existing review-context tests still pass.

title: pan-1681: Add checkCompletedButUnsignaledTests deacon failsafe (mirrors checkCompletedButUnsignaledReviews)
description:
Add an exported async function checkCompletedButUnsignaledTests() in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts, structurally mirroring checkCompletedButUnsignaledReviews (deacon.ts:2441): a SETTLE window (reuse the 5-minute constant pattern), a module-level dedup Map (mirror unsignaledReviewNudges) so each test cycle is nudged at most once, and the same guard discipline. For each issue with reviewStatus==='passed' and testStatus in {'testing','pending'}: resolve the workspace, locate .pan/test/result.json, and recover the verdict. When the test session agent-<id>-test is dead and the artifact exists → auto-complete via setReviewStatusSync({ testStatus: <status>, testNotes: <notes or default> }). When the session is alive but isAgentIdleForNudge reports idle past the settle window: on first pass nudge once (pan admin specialists done test <id> --status <status> [--notes ...], mirroring deacon.ts:2525); on a subsequent pass with the verdict still unsignaled, auto-complete from the artifact. When the artifact is ABSENT, never auto-complete to passed/failed — at most nudge once to write+POST. Skip closed issues (isIssueClosed) and stuck/ignored issues. Wire the function into the patrol loop in deacon.ts right after checkCompletedButUnsignaledReviews (~line 4790), pushing+logging its actions like the neighbours. Use vi.useFakeTimers in tests per the project's retry/delay test rule.
- AC: checkCompletedButUnsignaledTests() is exported from deacon.ts with settle-window + dedup-Map structure mirroring checkCompletedButUnsignaledReviews
- AC: Only acts on issues with reviewStatus==='passed' && testStatus∈{'testing','pending'}; skips closed (isIssueClosed) and stuck/ignored issues; gates on isAgentIdleForNudge(agent-<id>-test) + settle window
- AC: Dead session + verdict artifact present → setReviewStatusSync auto-completes testStatus from the file; alive+idle → nudge once then auto-complete on the next pass
- AC: Absent verdict artifact never produces a guessed passed/failed; at most one scoped nudge per cycle (deduplicated)
- AC: Function is invoked in the patrol loop right after checkCompletedButUnsignaledReviews, with actions pushed and addLog'd
- AC: Unit tests (vi.useFakeTimers) cover: passed-file+dead→auto-complete passed; failed-file→auto-complete failed; idle-alive+file→nudge-once-then-complete; missing-file→no passed/failed mutation

title: pan-1681: Stop burning the 3-retry budget on a live idle test agent; surface the strand instead of silently capping
description:
Fix checkPendingTestDispatch (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:2205) so it no longer wastes the retry budget on an idle-but-alive test agent and no longer strands silently. (1) Only re-dispatch a fresh test agent AND increment testRetryCount when there is NO live test session (agent-<id>-test) for the issue; when a live session exists, do not spawn and do not increment — defer recovery to checkCompletedButUnsignaledTests. (2) When testRetryCount >= 3 and testStatus is still pending/dispatch_failed, replace the silent `if (retryCount >= 3) continue;` with a ONE-TIME visible stuck marker: setReviewStatusSync({ stuck: true, stuckReason: 'test_signal_strand', stuckAt: <iso>, testNotes: 'Test stranded at pending after 3 re-dispatches — test agent never signaled. See agent-<id>-test.' }) plus a pushed/logged action, set only once (don't re-stamp every patrol). Keep the existing PAN-1665 tryReserveAdvancingSlot() reservation and PAN-1496/PAN-1613 isIssueClosed() guards intact (hazard H5).
- AC: checkPendingTestDispatch re-dispatches + increments testRetryCount ONLY when no live agent-<id>-test session exists; a live session is left for the failsafe (no spawn, no counter bump)
- AC: retryCount>=3 with testStatus still pending/dispatch_failed sets a one-time stuck marker (stuck:true, stuckReason:'test_signal_strand', actionable testNotes) instead of a silent continue, and does not re-stamp on every patrol
- AC: Existing PAN-1665 advancing-slot reservation and PAN-1496/PAN-1613 closed-issue guard are preserved
- AC: Unit test asserts: retryCount>=3 → stuck marker set (not silent); a live idle test session is neither re-dispatched nor counted

title: pan-1681: Test role writes a deterministic verdict artifact (.pan/test/result.json) and clears it on dispatch
description:
Establish the verdict-artifact contract that the deacon failsafe will read. In buildTestRolePrompt (src/lib/cloister/test-agent-queue.ts), add a REQUIRED step instructing the test agent to write the workspace file .pan/test/result.json with shape {"status":"passed"|"failed","notes":"..."} immediately after it decides the verdict and BEFORE the existing curl POST to /api/review/:id/status. Clear any stale .pan/test/result.json at every test-dispatch site so a previous cycle's verdict can never be misread: in dispatchTestAgentAndNotifyPromise / the Effect dispatchTestAgentAndNotify (test-agent-queue.ts) and in the re-dispatch branch of checkPendingTestDispatch (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts ~2262), mirroring how reviewer report/signal files are cleared at review-agent.ts:240-242. Do NOT touch launcher-generator.ts or the reviewSignal path (hazard H1).
- AC: buildTestRolePrompt output contains a REQUIRED step to write .pan/test/result.json as {status,notes}, placed before the curl POST step
- AC: Stale .pan/test/result.json is deleted before spawning a test agent at BOTH dispatch sites (test-agent-queue dispatch and deacon checkPendingTestDispatch re-dispatch)
- AC: A unit test asserts buildTestRolePrompt includes the .pan/test/result.json write instruction and its JSON shape
- AC: launcher-generator.ts and the reviewSignal/buildReviewSubRoleCommand path are NOT modified

title: pan-1675: Unit test: deacon fresh-overflow tier uses native compaction and falls back to /clear
description:
Add a Vitest test (alongside the existing deacon/context-overflow tests) that drives checkApiErrorAgents with a mocked agent-* session whose tmux tail contains a context-overflow error. Mock resumeAgent and sendKeys. Assert: (1) fresh overflow → resumeAgent called with { compact:true } and NO sendKeys('/compact'); (2) when resumeAgent resolves { success:false }, sendKeys('/clear') + reseed is sent in the same pass; (3) when resumeAgent resolves { success:true }, no /clear is sent and contextOverflowRecoveryState records phase 'compact'. Use fake timers if any settle/cooldown windows are involved (per the fake-timers rule).
- AC: Test: fresh agent-* overflow triggers resumeAgent({compact:true}) and never sendKeys('/compact').
- AC: Test: resumeAgent failure → /clear + reseed sent in the same patrol pass.
- AC: Test: resumeAgent success → no /clear; recovery state phase is 'compact'. Suite uses fake timers for any delay windows and passes under npm test.

title: pan-1675: Add 'Resume (compact)' action to IssueAgentCard
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/IssueAgentCard.tsx extend the resumeAgent() fetch helper to accept an optional { compact?: boolean } and include it in the POST body. Add a secondary 'Resume (compact)' control next to the existing Resume button (or a menu item) that calls the mutation with compact:true. Show it for resumable agents; a tooltip should explain it recovers a context-window-wedged agent via out-of-band compaction. Reuse the existing resume mutation's loading/error handling (showAlert on failure).
- AC: IssueAgentCard renders a 'Resume (compact)' action for resumable work agents that POSTs /api/agents/:id/resume with { compact:true }.
- AC: The plain Resume button still POSTs without compact; both share loading/error handling.
- AC: Frontend typecheck/lint pass and the action has an accessible label/tooltip describing the out-of-band recovery.

title: pan-1675: Deacon: use Panopticon-side compaction for the fresh-overflow recovery tier (fallback to /clear)
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents(), the FRESH-overflow branch for agent-* sessions (currently `await sendKeys(sessionName, '/compact')` followed by setting contextOverflowRecoveryState phase 'compact', around lines 1052-1074) is replaced: call resumeAgent(sessionName, undefined, { compact:true }). On result.success, set contextOverflowRecoveryState phase 'compact' (so the existing 'previous tier cleared the overflow' branch nudges the resumed agent to continue) and emit a 'recovering via Panopticon-side compaction' activity entry; push an action string. On failure (result.success false), DO NOT record a compact attempt — fall straight through to the existing /clear + reseed tier in the SAME patrol pass (so a failed native compaction immediately escalates to /clear, matching the issue's fallback semantics). The /clear tier, the MAX_CONTEXT_CLEAR_ATTEMPTS ceiling, the markWorkspaceStuck escalation, and the contextSaturatedAt bookkeeping are all unchanged. Restrict native compaction to agent-* sessions (specialists/planning keep the old /compact behaviour). Leave a code comment at maybeProactivelyCompactContext noting the proactive 85% tier still uses harness /compact by design (PAN-1675, H6).
- AC: On a fresh context-overflow for an agent-* session, the deacon calls resumeAgent(session, undefined, { compact:true }) instead of sendKeys('/compact').
- AC: When resumeAgent({compact:true}) fails, the same patrol pass falls through to the existing /clear + reseed tier (no compact attempt recorded, /clear sent against the still-live session).
- AC: On success, contextOverflowRecoveryState records phase 'compact' so the next patrol's 'overflow cleared' branch nudges the resumed agent; an activity entry naming Panopticon-side compaction is emitted.
- AC: The /clear ceiling, markWorkspaceStuck escalation, contextSaturatedAt bookkeeping, and the proactive 85% tier are unchanged; non-agent (specialist/planning) sessions keep the old /compact path.

title: pan-1675: Thread `compact` through POST /api/agents/:id/resume
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts, postAgentResumeRoute: read `compact` from the JSON body alongside message/model/harness, and include it in resumeOpts passed to resumeAgent (resumeOpts = { ...model, ...harness, ...(compact ? { compact:true } : {}) }). Keep the existing lifecycle gate and event emission unchanged.

- AC: Body without compact resumes exactly as before (compact undefined).
- AC: npm run typecheck passes; npm run build of the server succeeds.

title: pan-1675: Add `--compact` flag to `pan resume`
description:
In src/cli/index.ts add `.option('--compact', 'Run Panopticon-side out-of-band compaction on the agent JSONL before resuming (recovers context-window-wedged agents)')` to the `resume <id>` command. In src/cli/commands/resume.ts add `compact?: boolean` to ResumeOptions and pass it through to resumeAgent(id, undefined, { ..., compact: options.compact === true }). On a compaction failure, surface the returned error and exit non-zero. No pan-resume wrapper skill exists, so no skill needs updating.
- AC: `pan resume <id> --compact` calls resumeAgent with { compact:true }; `pan resume <id>` (no flag) calls it without compact.
- AC: `pan resume --help` lists the --compact flag with a description.

title: pan-1675: Unit test: compactAgentSession resolves the right JSONL and returns a non-throwing result
description:
Add a Vitest unit test for compactAgentSession that mocks compactConversationNative (the dynamically-imported module) and the agent-state/session-id resolvers. Assert: (1) it calls compactConversationNative with sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId) and no conversationName; (2) it returns { compacted:true } on success; (3) it returns { compacted:false, error } (never throws) when compactConversationNative rejects; (4) it returns { compacted:false } without calling compaction when no sessionId/workspace is resolvable.
- AC: Test asserts compactConversationNative is called with the agent's resolved sessionFilePath and no conversationName.
- AC: Test asserts a rejecting compactConversationNative yields { compacted:false, error } and does not throw.
- AC: Test asserts missing sessionId/workspace short-circuits to { compacted:false } with no compaction call. Test passes under npm test.

title: pan-1675: Add compact option to resumeAgent() — compact JSONL before relaunch, fail-safe before zombie-kill
description:
Extend resumeAgent(agentId, message?, opts?) in src/lib/agents.ts with `compact?: boolean` on opts. When opts.compact is true, at the very START of the try block (BEFORE the existing zombie tmux-session kill at ~line 4188) call compactAgentSession(normalizedId). If it returns { compacted:false, error }, return { success:false, error: `Panopticon-side compaction failed: ${error}` } immediately — do NOT kill the session or relaunch, so the live wedged session survives for a /clear fallback (H2/H3). If compaction succeeds, continue the normal kill + `claude --resume` relaunch unchanged. When opts.compact is falsy, behaviour is byte-for-byte identical to today.
- AC: resumeAgent opts accepts compact?: boolean; passing { compact:true } invokes compactAgentSession before the zombie-session kill.
- AC: When compaction fails, resumeAgent returns { success:false, error } WITHOUT killing the tmux session or building a launcher (the wedged session is left live for /clear fallback).
- AC: When opts.compact is omitted/false, the resume path is unchanged (no compaction call).
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass.

title: pan-1675: Document `resume --compact` and the deacon native-compaction recovery tier
description:
Document the new recovery path where the existing context-overflow / compaction behaviour is described (the doc that covers the deacon overflow tiers and conversation-compaction — locate via grep for 'panopticon-native' / 'context-window overflow' in docs/). Add a short section: `pan resume <id> --compact` and the dashboard 'Resume (compact)' action run out-of-band native compaction (compactConversationNative via compactAgentSession) on the agent JSONL before `claude --resume`; the deacon's fresh-overflow recovery tier now uses this instead of harness /compact, falling back to /clear only if Panopticon-side compaction fails; the proactive 85% tier still uses harness /compact by design. No code behaviour changes in this bead.
- AC: The relevant existing doc gains a section describing pan resume --compact, the dashboard action, and the deacon recovery-tier change with the /clear fallback.
- AC: The doc states the proactive 85% tier intentionally still uses harness /compact (scope note).

title: pan-1675: Add compactAgentSession(agentId) primitive — out-of-band native compaction of a work agent's JSONL
description:
In src/lib/agents.ts add and export `compactAgentSession(agentId: string): Promise<{ compacted: boolean; error?: string }>`. It resolves the agent state (getAgentStateSync) and saved Claude session id (getLatestSessionIdSync), derives the JSONL path with sessionFilePath(workspace, sessionId), and runs compaction by DYNAMICALLY importing compactConversationNative from ../dashboard/server/services/conversation-compaction.js (mirrors the deacon's dynamic-import pattern to avoid the known circular dependency — see hazard H1). Call compactConversationNative(sessionFile) WITHOUT a conversationName so no conversation.compacting events fire for a work-agent JSONL (H5). Catch all errors and return { compacted:false, error } — never throw. If no workspace or no saved sessionId, return { compacted:false, error } without attempting compaction (H4).
- AC: compactAgentSession is exported from src/lib/agents.ts with signature (agentId) => Promise<{ compacted: boolean; error?: string }> and never throws (all failure paths return { compacted:false, error }).
- AC: It resolves the SAME session file the harness resumes from: sessionFilePath(agentState.workspace, getLatestSessionIdSync(agentId)); when sessionId or workspace is missing it returns { compacted:false } without calling compaction.
- AC: compactConversationNative is loaded via dynamic import (no new top-level import of conversation-compaction in agents.ts) and called WITHOUT a conversationName argument.
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass.

title: pan-1395: CommandPalette: Conversations group above Memory
description:
Render a Conversations result group in CommandPalette.tsx, ordered above Memory, using the existing excerpt-segment renderer for excerptSegments. Group is hidden when conversations[] is empty/feature disabled.
- AC: Typing a phrase shows a Conversations group above Memory with highlighted excerpts
- AC: No Conversations group renders when there are no conversation hits
close_reason: Implemented PAN-1395 conversation search feature and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: Unit tests: FTS + vector RRF ranker
description:
Add unit tests for the ranker: RRF fusion of two known rank lists produces the expected order, and excerpt/excerptSegments are emitted with the ⦇…⦈ marker. Use a deterministic stub embedding provider.
- AC: RRF fusion of two seeded lists yields the expected fused order
- AC: Excerpt segments are produced with correct match flags

Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/palette.ts to populate the already-reserved conversations array with { sessionId, conversationId, projectId, role, ts, displayContent, excerpt, excerptSegments, rank } from the ranker. Additive only — commands/memory/observations/issues groups are untouched. Return an empty conversations[] when the feature is disabled.
- AC: Response conversations[] contains the documented fields, ranked, when the feature is enabled
- AC: conversations[] is empty and existing groups are unchanged when the feature is disabled

title: pan-1395: Live JSONL watcher feeding the incremental indexer
description:
Add a chokidar watcher (started only when feature enabled) on the JSONL session directories; debounce change events and call the indexer's index-one-file entry for changed/added files. Stop the watcher cleanly on shutdown / when the feature is disabled at runtime.
- AC: Appending to a watched JSONL file triggers incremental indexing of just the new bytes
- AC: Watcher only runs while the feature is enabled and stops cleanly on shutdown
close_reason: Implemented PAN-1395 conversation search feature and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: Reindex cost guard (>$1 requires explicit confirm)
description:
Before a full reindex, estimate token cost via provider.estimateCost and show it; require an explicit confirmation when the estimate exceeds $1 before any embedding API calls are made.
- AC: Estimated reindex cost is shown before the reindex runs
- AC: Reindex estimated over $1 blocks until the user explicitly confirms
close_reason: Implemented reindex estimate/confirm flow and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: Hybrid BM25 + vector ranker fused with RRF, with ⦇…⦈ excerpts
description:
Implement query-time ranking: embed the query, get vector top-k from sqlite-vec and BM25 top-k from FTS5, fuse with Reciprocal Rank Fusion, and return ranked chunk rows. Build the excerpt around the matched region using the ⦇…⦈ marker convention and produce excerptSegments {text, match}[] (reuse the memory tokenizer pattern).
- AC: Returns hits whose source messages need not contain the exact query words (semantic recall)
- AC: BM25 and vector lists are fused via RRF into a single ranked order
- AC: Each hit carries excerpt (⦇…⦈) and excerptSegments {text, match}[]
close_reason: Implemented PAN-1395 conversation search feature and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: Incremental indexer: scan, chunk, embed, upsert (idempotent)
description:
Orchestrate indexing: enumerate JSONL session files across project dirs, read each file's persisted byteOffset cursor, process only new/appended bytes via the chunker, embed via the provider, upsert into the DB, advance the cursor. Expose a full-reindex entry (clears cursors) and an index-one-file entry (for the watcher). Skip entirely when feature disabled.
- AC: Startup scan embeds only chunks past each file's stored cursor
- AC: Restarting the dashboard does not re-embed already-indexed chunks
- AC: No-ops when conversationSearch.enabled is false
close_reason: Implemented PAN-1395 conversation search feature and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: Unit tests: chunker boundaries + byte-offset across appends
description:
Add unit tests for the chunker: message-boundary correctness, ~512-token window splitting/overlap, and byteOffset accuracy verified before and after the fixture file is appended to.
- AC: Tests assert boundary segmentation and window splitting
- AC: A test appends to the fixture and asserts byteOffset of earlier and new chunks is correct

title: pan-1395: Settings: Conversation Search section
description:
Add a Conversation Search section in dashboard Settings: embedding provider/model/api-key inputs, an enable/disable toggle (OFF by default), the last-indexed timestamp, and a 'Reindex all conversations' button. Persists to the conversationSearch config block.
- AC: Provider/model/api-key and an enable toggle (default off) are editable and persist
- AC: Last-indexed timestamp and a Reindex-all button are shown

title: pan-1395: Pluggable embedding provider (OpenAI default) + token-cost estimator
description:
Add an embedding-provider abstraction reading the config block: default OpenAI text-embedding-3-small via @ai-sdk/openai (already a dep), batched embed of N chunk texts, and a pure estimateCost(textChunks) helper used by the cost guard. Provider is selected by config; unknown/disabled provider yields a clear unavailable error.

- AC: estimateCost(texts[]) returns a USD estimate from token count and model price; unit-tested

title: pan-1395: Resolve sessionId + byteOffset to a message for open-at-message
description:
Add backend support so a (sessionId, byteOffset) pair resolves to the target message identity used by the conversation drawer (extend the conversations-by-id messages handler or add a resolve helper). The frontend uses this to scroll to the matched message.
- AC: Given sessionId + byteOffset, the backend returns the matching message's locator (id/index) for the drawer

title: pan-1395: Embeddings sidecar DB: sqlite-vec + FTS5 schema and init
description:
Create the sidecar DB module at ~/.panopticon/conversations/embeddings.db using better-sqlite3: a chunks table (the chunker record fields + embedding), a sqlite-vec virtual table for cosine ANN, an FTS5 table over chunk text for BM25, and a per-file byteOffset cursor table for incremental idempotency. Load the sqlite-vec extension behind a single guarded init; fail closed (report unavailable) if the extension can't load rather than crashing the dashboard.
- AC: DB initializes with chunks, vec virtual table, FTS5 table, and per-file cursor table at the configured path
- AC: sqlite-vec extension load is isolated and failure degrades the feature to unavailable without crashing the server
- AC: Upsert keyed on (sessionId, byteOffset) is idempotent — re-inserting the same chunk does not duplicate
close_reason: Implemented PAN-1395 conversation search feature and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: JSONL conversation chunker emitting byte-offset-keyed records
description:
Implement a streaming chunker (new lib module) that reads a JSONL session, segments by message boundary, splits long messages on ~512-token windows with overlap, and emits records { sessionId, projectId, role, ts, byteOffset, charLength, text }. byteOffset must point at the message's start in the file and remain stable across appends. This record shape is the cross-cutting contract consumed by the indexer and ranker.
- AC: Streams a JSONL session and emits one+ records per message with correct sessionId/projectId/role/ts
- AC: Long messages split into ~512-token overlapping windows
- AC: byteOffset/charLength accurately locate the source bytes and are stable when the file is later appended to
close_reason: Implemented PAN-1395 conversation search feature and verified with typecheck, lint, and full test suite

title: pan-1395: Add conversationSearch config block (OFF by default)

Add a conversationSearch section to ~/.panopticon/config.yaml schema (src/lib/config-yaml.ts): { enabled: false, provider: 'openai', model: 'text-embedding-3-small', apiKeyRef, dbPath default ~/.panopticon/conversations/embeddings.db }. Default enabled=false. Expose a typed accessor other beads read.

title: pan-1580: Verify in-browser: server restart keeps the app mounted and self-recovers
description:
Browser UAT (isolated Playwright instance/profile — do not depend on another agent's session). Open the dashboard, restart the dashboard server, and observe: the page does NOT go blank, the app stays mounted, the 'Reconnecting…' overlay appears during the outage, and the dashboard recovers (overlay hides, live data resumes) once the server returns — all without a full white/blank document gap. Also verify the half-open case if feasible (kill the socket without server-side error) triggers the watchdog reconnect.
- AC: During a `pan reload`-style server restart the dashboard never shows a blank/white document; it stays mounted and shows the reconnect overlay.
- AC: After the server returns, the overlay hides and live data resumes without a manual refresh.
- AC: UAT runs in an isolated browser profile with no dependency on shared/other-agent Playwright state.
close_reason: Ran isolated Playwright restart UAT against a rebuilt Node 22 dashboard on 127.0.0.1:43111: outage kept app mounted/nonblank with reconnect overlay; restart hid overlay and resumed without manual refresh.

title: pan-1580: Client staleness watchdog for half-open socket detection
description:
Reset a staleness timer on EVERY inbound frame (heartbeat or real event) in the wsTransport.subscribe() listener path / EventRouter. If no frame arrives within ~35s (>= 2 missed beats), force resetTransport() and reconnect. Critically, intercept event.type === 'system.heartbeat' at the very top of EventRouter.handleEvent: reset the watchdog and return immediately so the heartbeat never enters the sequence-based recovery coordinator (classifyDomainEvent) or pendingBatch.
- AC: Any inbound frame (heartbeat or real event) resets a ~35s staleness timer.
- AC: When no frame arrives for the staleness window, the client forces resetTransport() and a fresh reconnect (recovers a silent half-open socket that produces no stream error).
- AC: A 'system.heartbeat' frame resets the watchdog but is NOT passed to classifyDomainEvent/applyEvents and is not pushed to pendingBatch (verified by unit test).
- AC: Watchdog tests use vi.useFakeTimers() (no real-timer delays); one missed beat does not trip it, 2+ missed beats does.
close_reason: Added EventRouter staleness watchdog, heartbeat early-return, reconnect-on-stale behavior, and fake-timer tests; dashboard build passed.

title: pan-1580: Surface the snapshot-fallback cliff instead of giving up silently (low priority)
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/EventRouter.tsx, the fallback poller stops silently after SNAPSHOT_FALLBACK_WINDOW_MS (3 min) without bootstrap success. When that window expires and bootstrap has still not completed, surface the actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' overlay (reuse showOverlay from the overlay-wiring bead) rather than ending in a silent, frozen state. Keep the change minimal — this is explicitly lower priority.
- AC: When the snapshot-fallback window expires without a successful bootstrap, the UI surfaces the 'Server unreachable — Retry' overlay instead of stopping silently.
- AC: If bootstrap succeeds before the window expires, behavior is unchanged (no overlay shown).
close_reason: Snapshot fallback expiry now shows the actionable Server unreachable — Retry overlay, while successful bootstrap remains unchanged; tests and dashboard build pass.

title: pan-1580: Emit a periodic heartbeat on the subscribeDomainEvents stream
description:
In src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts, the WS_METHODS.subscribeDomainEvents handler (around ws-rpc.ts:484) currently returns eventStore.streamEvents.pipe(Stream.map(storedToDomainEvent)). Stream.merge a heartbeat stream into it: Stream.repeatEffect(emit a SystemHeartbeatEvent).schedule(Schedule.spaced('15 seconds')). The heartbeat is synthetic and must NOT be persisted to the event store. Touch ONLY the subscribeDomainEvents handler — leave subscribeIssueEvents and subscribeAgentOutput alone.
- AC: subscribeDomainEvents emits a SystemHeartbeatEvent approximately every 15s in addition to real events; real events continue to flow unchanged.
- AC: No heartbeat is written to the event store / no sequence is consumed for it; subscribeIssueEvents and subscribeAgentOutput handlers are unmodified.
- AC: typecheck and build of the dashboard server pass (npm run build).
close_reason: Merged a synthetic 15s system.heartbeat stream into subscribeDomainEvents only; server build passed.

title: pan-1580: Narrow componentDidCatch: stop full-reloading on generic render crashes (root cause)
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/recovery.tsx, narrow RootErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch (recovery.tsx:161) so a generic (non-module-load) render crash NO LONGER calls waitForServerThenReload()/window.location.reload(). Keep the isModuleLoadError(error) reload path intact (legitimate new-hashed-chunk case). Generic crashes keep the document mounted and fall through to the in-app manual fallback render() (and/or the reconnect overlay). This reverses the 3345c7c11 regression. Remove the now-unused CRASH_RELOAD_KEY/cooldown machinery if narrowing makes it dead (clean up only your own orphans).
- AC: A generic (non-module-load) render crash no longer triggers window.location.reload(); the app shows the in-app fallback while staying mounted.
- AC: A module/asset-load error still self-recovers via waitForServerThenReload() (isModuleLoadError path unchanged); recovery.test.ts updated to assert both behaviors.
- AC: Any CRASH_RELOAD_KEY/cooldown code rendered dead by the narrowing is removed; no unused symbols remain; typecheck/lint pass.
close_reason: Narrowed RootErrorBoundary to auto-reload only module-load errors; generic crashes now stay on in-app fallback with tests and frontend build passing.

title: pan-1580: Wire socket-death to the visible reconnect overlay
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/recovery.tsx, export showOverlay (currently private, recovery.tsx:49) and add a hideOverlay that removes the overlay element. From the transport reconnect lifecycle (wsTransport.ts retry/onExit + EventRouter onReconnect), show 'Reconnecting to the dashboard…' once retries begin and hide it on the next successful reconnect (onReconnect). After N consecutive failed attempts, render an actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' state (button calling resetTransport()/reload) instead of spinning silently. The app stays mounted throughout — no window.location.reload().
- AC: showOverlay is exported and a hideOverlay is added; hideOverlay removes the overlay DOM node and is idempotent.
- AC: On the first reconnect retry the 'Reconnecting to the dashboard…' overlay appears; on the next onReconnect it is hidden — without unmounting the app or reloading the page.
- AC: After N consecutive failed attempts the overlay shows an actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' control instead of an indefinite spinner.
close_reason: Exported overlay controls, wired stream retry/reconnect lifecycle to reconnect/unreachable overlays, and verified tests plus dashboard build.

title: pan-1580: Cap reconnect backoff with exponential delay + jitter
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/wsTransport.ts, replace the fixed reconnect cadence (DEFAULT_RETRY_DELAY = Duration.millis(250) at ~line 113 used by Schedule.fixed(retryDelay) at ~line 215) with a capped exponential + jitter schedule, mirroring linear-client.ts:134. e.g. Schedule.exponential('500 millis').pipe(Schedule.either(Schedule.spaced('10 seconds')), Schedule.jittered). Account for the second reconnect path too: the onExit handler's setTimeout(run, 1000) (wsTransport.ts ~line 223) — align it so the two paths do not fight.
- AC: The Effect.retry schedule uses capped exponential backoff with jitter (cap ~10s), not Schedule.fixed(250ms).
- AC: The onExit/setTimeout reconnect path is reconciled with the new backoff (documented or aligned) so the client does not hammer the server during a long outage.
- AC: Backoff behavior is covered by a fake-timer unit test asserting increasing delays capped at the ceiling.
close_reason: Switched stream retry schedule to capped exponential jitter, aligned fresh-transport reconnect delay, added fake-timer backoff tests, and verified dashboard build.

title: pan-1580: Add SystemHeartbeatEvent to the @panctl/contracts DomainEvent union
description:
In packages/contracts/src/events.ts define a new SystemHeartbeatEvent Schema.Struct (type literal 'system.heartbeat', a timestamp string, and a small payload such as { ts: number }) and add it to the DomainEvent union (events.ts:1049). Rebuild contracts. Confirm the client reducer needs no change — applyEvent's default case (event-reducers.ts:1400) already ignores unknown types. The exact type literal and field shape chosen here are consumed verbatim by the server tick and the client watchdog, so this is the protocol foundation for those beads.
- AC: SystemHeartbeatEvent schema exists in events.ts with type literal 'system.heartbeat' and a timestamp + payload, and is exported (schema + type).
- AC: SystemHeartbeatEvent is added to the DomainEvent Schema.Union; `cd packages/contracts && npm run build` succeeds with no type errors.
- AC: Unit test (packages/contracts/src/events.test.ts) decodes a heartbeat frame through DomainEvent and asserts applyEvent leaves ReadModelState unchanged and does not throw.
close_reason: Removed unrelated .panopticon/projects.yaml changes from the bead diff; heartbeat contract changes remain implemented and verified.

title: pan-1641: Docs: install → pull → configure → run a Pi agent locally (Linux/CUDA + Apple Silicon)
description:
Document the local-model path: extend configuration/harnesses.mdx (and/or a new local-models doc) with the end-to-end flow — install Ollama, `ollama pull <Gemma tag>`, set providers.ollama.base_url and an `ollama:<tag>` work/role model in ~/.panopticon/config.yaml, run a Pi agent locally — with both Linux/CUDA (RTX 3090 Ti) and Apple Silicon (M4 Metal) notes. State the v1 scope (Pi-only) and link the follow-up issue for Codex/Claude Code/dashboard-picker.
- AC: Docs cover the full path: install → pull → configure config.yaml (base_url + ollama: model) → run a Pi agent locally
- AC: Both Linux/CUDA and Apple Silicon (Metal) setups are documented
- AC: v1 scope (Pi-only) is stated and the follow-up issue is linked; docs lint/build passes
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1641: Verify a Pi work agent runs end-to-end on local Gemma with zero cloud calls
description:
Prove the full path: configure a Pi work model of `ollama:<gemma tag>`, ensure the sidecar + model are present, spawn a Pi work agent, and confirm it completes a trivial task end-to-end while making ZERO cloud calls (no Anthropic/OpenAI cloud traffic — only localhost:11434). Capture the verification (network observation + transcript evidence) in a short verification note committed under the workspace. This is the integration ground truth for the pi-openai-env / lazy-ensure work.
- AC: A Pi work agent spawned with an ollama: model completes a trivial task end-to-end
- AC: Evidence shows all model traffic went to localhost:11434 with zero Anthropic/OpenAI cloud calls
- AC: A verification note documenting the run and the zero-cloud evidence is committed in the workspace
close_reason: Pi + Ollama Gemma e2e passed. Added docs/PAN-1641-OLLAMA-VERIFY.md with local Gemma output PAN1641_GEMMA_LOCAL_OK and strace evidence showing only localhost:3011 and localhost:11435 connects (non_local_count 0).

comments.author: panopticon-agent[bot]
comments.text: Blocked on host Ollama version: /usr/local/bin/ollama is 0.19.0, and `ollama pull gemma4:12b` fails with 'The model you are attempting to pull requires a newer version of Ollama.' title: pan-1641: Lazy-ensure Ollama + pre-spawn health check in getProviderEnvForModel
description:
In getProviderEnvForModel (src/lib/agents.ts) add an `ollama` branch mirroring the `nous` branch (lines 570-576): call ensureOllamaServeRunning() and the model-present health check from src/lib/ollama.ts BEFORE returning the OpenAI-compatible env from pi-openai-env. On failure, surface the distinct actionable errors ('Ollama not running' vs 'model <tag> not pulled — run `ollama pull <tag>`'). This is the single pre-spawn gate that guarantees the endpoint is live and the model present before a Pi agent starts.
- AC: Selecting an ollama model triggers ensureOllamaServeRunning() and a model-present check before the agent spawns
- AC: A down endpoint and a missing model produce distinct, actionable error messages (no silent fallback to a cloud provider)
- AC: Non-ollama models are completely unaffected by the new branch; typecheck/lint/test pass
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1641: pan doctor: optional Ollama presence/endpoint check
description:
In src/cli/commands/doctor.ts add an optional Ollama check mirroring the existing Pi check: report OK / not-installed / endpoint-unreachable with a fix message (install command + `ollama serve`). The check is advisory (does not fail doctor when Ollama is simply not installed), consistent with the Moonshine/optional-sidecar checks.
- AC: `pan doctor` reports Ollama status: installed+reachable / installed-but-down / not-installed, with an actionable fix line
- AC: Absent Ollama is advisory only (does not turn doctor red); typecheck/lint/test pass
close_reason: Added advisory pan doctor Ollama status check with installed+reachable, installed-but-down, and not-installed results plus actionable install/serve guidance; verified typecheck/lint/test pass.

title: pan-1641: pan install: detect/guide Ollama (`--skip-ollama`) + optional model pull
description:
In src/cli/commands/install.ts add an Ollama step mirroring the Moonshine/beads steps: a `--skip-ollama` flag; detect via isOllamaInstalled() (`ollama --version`) / GET /api/tags; if missing, guide install per platform (curl … | sh on Linux, brew/dmg on macOS) using the existing detectPlatform branch pattern; then offer to `ollama pull <documented default Gemma 4 12B tag>`. execSync is acceptable here (CLI-only). Verify the default tag against ollama.com/library (hazard H2).
- AC: `pan install` registers a `--skip-ollama` flag and skips the step when passed
- AC: When Ollama is absent, the step prints the correct platform-specific install guidance (Linux curl|sh, macOS brew/dmg)
- AC: When Ollama is present, the step offers to `ollama pull` the documented default Gemma tag
- AC: The skills/CLI lint passes (any new flag is reflected where required); typecheck/lint/test pass
close_reason: Added --skip-ollama, install-time Ollama detection/guidance, optional gemma4:12b pull, platform guidance tests, and verified typecheck/lint/test pass.

title: pan-1641: Add optional providers.ollama.base_url to config.yaml
description:
In src/lib/config-yaml.ts add an optional providers.ollama config surface exposing base_url (default http://localhost:11434), resolved/validated through resolveOllamaBaseUrl from src/lib/ollama.ts. No API key is required (Ollama ignores it). Keep it additive — boolean/object normalization consistent with the other providers; absence means 'use the default localhost base'.
- AC: providers.ollama.base_url is readable from ~/.panopticon/config.yaml and defaults to http://localhost:11434 when unset
- AC: A non-localhost base_url is rejected with a clear error (reuses the localhost-only validation from src/lib/ollama.ts)
- AC: Existing config parsing for all other providers is unchanged; typecheck/lint/test pass
close_reason: Added models.providers.ollama.base_url config normalization with localhost-only validation and default http://localhost:11434; verified typecheck/lint/test pass.

title: pan-1641: Emit OpenAI-compatible provider env for Pi + ollama (not Anthropic)
description:
Make the provider-env layer emit the OpenAI-compatible env Pi consumes for ollama models instead of the ANTHROPIC_* shape. Start with a short spike against the installed `pi` binary to confirm the exact env keys/config Pi honors for a localhost OpenAI-compatible endpoint (hazard H1). Then: in getProviderEnvSync (or a dedicated ollama branch) emit OPENAI_BASE_URL=<resolved base>/v1 and a dummy OPENAI_API_KEY (e.g. 'ollama'), with NO ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN; strip the `ollama:` prefix so the bare tag is what Pi/Ollama receive as the model. Add OPENAI_BASE_URL to PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS (agents.ts) so launcher scripts and buildSpawnEnvForModel unset/re-export it cleanly (hazard H4). The model the launcher passes via --model must be the bare tag (prefix stripped).
- AC: For an ollama model, the provider env contains OPENAI_BASE_URL ending in /v1 and a (dummy) OPENAI_API_KEY, and contains NO ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN
- AC: The `ollama:` prefix is stripped before the tag is used as the model id passed to Pi/Ollama
- AC: OPENAI_BASE_URL is added to PROVIDER_ENV_KEYS so it is unset then re-exported by getProviderExportsForModel and stripped by buildSpawnEnvForModel
- AC: The exact Pi wiring mechanism is confirmed against the real `pi` binary and documented in continue.json/decision before finalizing this bead
- AC: Unit tests assert the env shape for an ollama model and that non-ollama providers are unchanged; typecheck/lint/test pass
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1641: Add src/lib/ollama.ts sidecar lifecycle (detect / ensure-serve / health)
description:
Create src/lib/ollama.ts mirroring src/lib/cliproxy.ts's lifecycle shape. Export: shared OLLAMA default base-URL constant + localhost-only validation (SAFE_OLLAMA_HOST_RE pattern); resolveOllamaBaseUrl(config) honoring providers.ollama.base_url with the localhost guard; isOllamaInstalled() (via `ollama --version`); ensureOllamaServeRunning() (idempotently spawn `ollama serve` if GET /api/tags is unreachable); and a health check splitting 'endpoint reachable' from 'model <tag> present' (GET /api/tags). Use async spawn / promisified exec ONLY — never execSync (this module is reachable from the spawn path; see hazard H7). Emit a typed OllamaError. Do not modify the existing embeddings SAFE_OLLAMA_HOST_RE copy.
- AC: src/lib/ollama.ts exports resolveOllamaBaseUrl, isOllamaInstalled, ensureOllamaServeRunning, and a health/model-present check, plus a typed error
- AC: resolveOllamaBaseUrl rejects non-localhost base URLs via the localhost-only regex, defaulting to http://localhost:11434
- AC: ensureOllamaServeRunning is idempotent (no-op when /api/tags already responds) and uses async spawn/exec — zero execSync in this file
- AC: Health check distinguishes 'endpoint unreachable' from 'model not pulled' and returns/throws distinct, actionable messages
- AC: Unit tests cover localhost validation, idempotent ensure (mocked fetch), and the two distinct health failure modes; typecheck/lint/test pass
close_reason: Added src/lib/ollama.ts with localhost-only base URL resolution, async Ollama install detection, idempotent ollama serve startup, endpoint/model health checks, typed OllamaError, and unit tests; verified typecheck/lint/test pass.

title: pan-1641: Register the `ollama` provider and `ollama:` model-routing convention
description:
In src/lib/providers.ts add `'ollama'` to the ProviderName union and a model-agnostic PROVIDERS.ollama entry (models: [], OpenAI-compatible localhost /v1 base, tested: false, description). In getProviderForModelSync, detect any model id beginning with `ollama:` and route it to PROVIDERS.ollama BEFORE the `/`-based OpenRouter heuristic so a bare-tag-with-colon never mis-routes. Confirm (with a test) that the harness policy already allows pi + ollama (provider.name !== 'anthropic') with no harness-policy.ts change. The `ollama:` prefix is a cross-cutting routing token that the env layer, launcher, config and docs all assume — this is the foundation the rest of the plan builds on.
- AC: ProviderName union includes 'ollama' and PROVIDERS.ollama exists, model-agnostic (models: []), OpenAI-compatible localhost base, tested: false
- AC: getProviderForModelSync('ollama:gemma3:12b').name === 'ollama'; getProviderForModelSync('ollama:anything').name === 'ollama'
- AC: Existing routing is unchanged: known bare models (claude-*, gpt-*, qwen/*, minimax-*, etc.) still resolve to their prior providers
- AC: Unit test asserts canUseHarnessSync('pi', 'ollama:gemma3:12b', authMode) is allowed for every authMode
- AC: npm run typecheck, lint and test pass
close_reason: Registered the ollama provider, added ollama: prefix routing before OpenRouter slash routing, covered routing regressions and pi harness policy allowance in tests, and verified typecheck/lint/test pass.

title: pan-1642: Wire Streamdown to the dashboard theme tokens (light/dark)
description:
Streamdown is Tailwind + CSS-custom-property themed. Map its color/border/background custom properties to our existing tokens (--background, --foreground, --border, --accent, --card, etc.) so Streamdown output visually matches the dashboard in both light and dark mode. Add the necessary CSS in command-deck.module.css (or a scoped wrapper class) alongside the existing .chatMarkdown styles.

- AC: With the flag on, Streamdown output matches the dashboard theme in dark mode

When ON, render via Streamdown; when OFF, keep the current ReactMarkdown path unchanged (default off). Verify Streamdown's built-in rehype-harden / urlTransform does not strip file-link chip hrefs (shouldPreserveMarkdownFileLinkHref) and introduces no new XSS surface. Keep the ChatMarkdownErrorBoundary wrapper.

Include a code comment documenting that this is message-level streaming only — the JSONL has no token deltas (decision D4).
