title: Conversation resume reattaches to a dead session instead of respawning
description:
## Problem

`POST /api/conversations/:name/resume` decides reattach-vs-respawn purely on
`tmuxSessionExists(conv.tmuxSession)` (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts:2144).
When the session exists it reattaches and returns immediately; it only respawns
when the session is gone.

But a conversation's launcher.sh ends with a keep-alive loop:

    echo "Conversation session ended. Close this panel or click Resume..."
    while true; do sleep 60; done

So after Claude exits (crash, error, normal end), the **tmux session stays
alive** running that loop. `tmuxSessionExists` returns true, resume "reattaches"
to a corpse, and the dead Claude is never relaunched. The user clicks Resume and
nothing happens.

## Repro (observed 2026-06-06)

7 conversations died on Claude Code 2.1.158 hitting the legacy channels-flag
error (`server:panopticon-bridge · no MCP server configured`). Each left its
tmux session alive in the keep-alive loop. Hitting resume returned
`{"reattached": true}` and did NOT relaunch Claude. The only way to force a
clean respawn was to `tmux -L panopticon kill-session` first, then resume —
which then regenerated the launcher and relaunched correctly.

## Fix direction

Resume must detect "tmux session alive but Claude process dead" and treat it as
respawn, not reattach. Options:
- Check whether the live Claude/pty-supervisor process is actually running in
  the session (not just whether the tmux session name exists).
- Or have the launcher NOT keep the tmux session alive after Claude exits for
  the supervisor path (let the session die so the existing respawn branch fires).

Either way, a dead-Claude session should respawn on resume without manual
kill-session intervention.

## Acceptance
- Resuming a conversation whose Claude has exited (but whose tmux session is
  still in the keep-alive loop) relaunches Claude via spawnConversationSession,
  regenerating launcher.sh from current config.
- `reattached: true` is returned ONLY when Claude is genuinely still running.

title: pan-1617: Integration test: full ghost-agent lifecycle (spawn-fail → stalled → resume re-delivers)
description:
Add an integration-style test that exercises the end-to-end ghost failure mode against the real (mocked-transport) seam: (1) a work agent whose kickoff delivery is forced to fail ends with kickoffDelivered=false and a recorded failure rather than a silent healthy-running state; (2) determineHealthStatus classifies that state.json as 'stalled'; (3) a resume of that agent re-delivers from initial-prompt.md and flips kickoffDelivered=true, after which determineHealthStatus no longer returns 'stalled'. Use fake timers for any grace/backoff waits per the repo's fake-timer rule; do not rely on real wall-clock delays.
- AC: Test reproduces: forced kickoff-delivery failure leaves kickoffDelivered=false + recorded failure (no healthy-running ghost)
- AC: Test asserts determineHealthStatus returns 'stalled' for that state, then 'healthy' (or no longer 'stalled') after resume re-delivery flips kickoffDelivered=true
- AC: Test uses vi.useFakeTimers / advanceTimersByTimeAsync for the grace window — no real setTimeout waits
- AC: npm test, npm run typecheck, and npm run lint all pass
close_reason: Added full mocked ghost lifecycle test; focused tests and typecheck pass

title: pan-1617: Re-deliver kickoff on deacon/agent resume when kickoffDelivered is false
description:
Make work-agent resume re-establish kickoff context for ghosts. In resumeAgent (and the fallback-restart path) in src/lib/agents.ts: when the agent is role==='work' and state.kickoffDelivered===false, after the session is ready, re-deliver the original kickoff read from <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md (instead of, or prepended to, the generic continue prompt), then set state.kickoffDelivered=true on confirmed re-delivery. Make it idempotent: kickoffDelivered===true/undefined keeps today's generic-continue behavior (hazard H4). This closes the autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents → resumeAgent path that the deacon uses after recoverOrphanedAgents marks a ghost running→stopped, so a re-launched ghost actually receives its task rather than a bare TUI + 'continue'.
- AC: Resuming a role=work agent with kickoffDelivered=false re-delivers the kickoff from <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md after the ready signal
- AC: kickoffDelivered is set to true after a confirmed re-delivery on resume
- AC: Resume of an agent with kickoffDelivered=true or undefined is unchanged (no double-delivery) — idempotent
- AC: If initial-prompt.md is missing for a kickoffDelivered=false work agent, resume logs/surfaces the gap rather than silently sending a contextless continue
- AC: Unit test added covering re-delivery on kickoffDelivered=false and no-op on true
close_reason: Resume re-delivers initial-prompt kickoff when kickoffDelivered=false and preserves generic continue otherwise; focused tests and typecheck pass

title: pan-1617: Verify, persist, retry and escalate kickoff delivery at work-agent spawn
description:
In both work-agent spawn paths — spawnAgent (work) and spawnRun when role==='work' in src/lib/agents.ts — make kickoff delivery verified instead of fire-and-forget. Set state.kickoffDelivered=false before delivery and save. Ensure the kickoff is persisted to <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md for later resume re-delivery. After waitForReadySignal: if ready, deliver and inspect the DeliveryResult; on a confirmed path set state.kickoffDelivered=true and save. On ready=false OR an unverified delivery, retry the ready-wait + delivery ONCE. If it still fails, do NOT silently markAgentRunning as a healthy running agent — call recordAgentFailure with reason 'kickoff delivery failed' and emit an activity-level error so the failure surfaces. Set kickoffDelivered=true on the Pi (writePiAgentPrompt) and Codex (inline exec) delivery paths too so those harnesses are never falsely flagged (hazard H5). Per hazard H3, treat tmux-only ok as weak: confirm via the ready signal, and only flip kickoffDelivered=true when the agent reached the prompt and a delivery was issued.
- AC: spawnAgent and spawnRun(work) set kickoffDelivered=false before delivery and flip it to true only after a verified delivery (ready signal observed + delivery issued / supervisor ack)
- AC: On ready=false or failed delivery, the spawn retries once, then on continued failure records an agent failure (recordAgentFailure, reason 'kickoff delivery failed') and emits an error activity entry instead of marking the agent healthy-running with no kickoff
- AC: The kickoff prompt is persisted to <agentDir>/initial-prompt.md for work agents in both spawn paths (enabling resume re-delivery)
- AC: Pi and Codex spawn paths set kickoffDelivered=true after their own delivery step so they are not falsely classified stalled
- AC: Unit test added asserting: successful delivery => kickoffDelivered=true; ready=false twice => failure recorded and kickoffDelivered stays false
close_reason: Verified work-agent kickoff delivery with retry/failure handling; focused tests and typecheck pass

title: pan-1617: Classify kickoff-stalled work agents as 'stalled' in determineHealthStatus
description:
Add an early stall rule to determineHealthStatus in src/dashboard/lib/health-filtering.ts, placed BEFORE the lastActivity-based staleness logic and AFTER the liveness (alive) check. When state.role==='work' && agentStatus==='running' && state.kickoffDelivered===false && (Date.now() - Date.parse(state.startedAt)) > 5*60*1000, return { status: 'stalled', reason: 'Work agent running with no kickoff delivered since spawn' }. The rule must NOT consult lastActivity (the heartbeat masks it — that is the root cause of the misreport). Only fire on explicit ===false, never undefined (hazard H1). Verify the /api/health/agents consumer (src/dashboard/server/routes/misc.ts) and any frontend status switch tolerate the new 'stalled' value; map it to an appropriate severity if a strict union exists (hazard H6).
- AC: determineHealthStatus returns { status: 'stalled' } for role=work, status=running, kickoffDelivered=false, startedAt older than the 5-min grace
- AC: kickoffDelivered=true OR undefined never produces 'stalled'; an agent within the 5-min grace is not flagged
- AC: The rule does not read lastActivity (so a fresh heartbeat cannot mask the stall)
- AC: New unit test src/dashboard/lib/__tests__/health-filtering.test.ts covers stalled-positive, within-grace-negative, kickoffDelivered=true-negative, and undefined-negative cases
- AC: The /api/health/agents route and frontend tolerate the 'stalled' status (no crash / strict-union rejection)
close_reason: Added kickoffDelivered=false stalled health rule, tests, and frontend status handling; targeted tests and typechecks pass

title: pan-1617: deliverAgentMessage returns a structured DeliveryResult
description:
Change deliverAgentMessage's return type from Promise<void> to Promise<DeliveryResult> where DeliveryResult = { ok: boolean; path: 'supervisor'|'channels'|'tmux'|'pi'|'codex'; failure?: string }. Return the appropriate result at every exit (supervisor success => {ok:true,path:'supervisor'}; channels success => channels; auto-mode tmux fallback => {ok:true,path:'tmux'} since sendKeys did run; codex => codex). Strict-mode explicit 'supervisor'/'channels' still THROW on failure (unchanged). Change is additive — existing callers that ignore the return value are unaffected. Update src/lib/__tests__/deliver-agent-message.test.ts to assert the returned result shape per path.
- AC: deliverAgentMessage returns DeliveryResult with correct {ok,path} for supervisor, channels, and tmux-fallback auto-mode paths
- AC: Strict 'supervisor'/'channels' delivery still throws MessageDeliveryFailed on failure (existing behavior preserved)
- AC: Existing callers (tells, feedback, resume) continue to compile and behave unchanged; deliver-agent-message.test.ts asserts the new return shape and passes
close_reason: deliverAgentMessage now returns DeliveryResult for supervisor/channels/tmux/codex paths with tests and typecheck passing

title: pan-1617: Add tri-state kickoffDelivered field to AgentState
description:
Add `kickoffDelivered?: boolean` to the AgentState interface in src/lib/agents.ts with a doc comment defining the tri-state contract (undefined = legacy/pre-feature or non-applicable; false = spawned but kickoff not yet confirmed; true = kickoff confirmed delivered). Add the passthrough in cleanAgentState() so the field is persisted and round-trips through saveAgentState/getAgentState. No behavioral change in this bead — only the schema and its documented semantics.
- AC: AgentState includes kickoffDelivered?: boolean with a doc comment specifying undefined/false/true semantics
- AC: cleanAgentState() carries kickoffDelivered through so it persists in state.json and survives a save/load round-trip
- AC: npm run typecheck passes with the new field
close_reason: Kept kickoffDelivered schema passthrough and removed unrelated projects.yaml diff flagged by inspection

title: pan-1614: Wire recoverStalledReviewConvoys() into runPatrol after checkOrphanedReviewStatuses
description:
In runPatrol() (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts), call recoverStalledReviewConvoys() immediately after the checkOrphanedReviewStatuses() block (around deacon.ts:4341-4344). Push its returned actions into the patrol actions array and addLog each one with state.patrolCycle, following the exact pattern used by the surrounding patrol calls (e.g. orphanActions / missingStatusActions).
- AC: recoverStalledReviewConvoys() is invoked once per patrol cycle, after checkOrphanedReviewStatuses() and after recoverOrphanedAgents().
- AC: Returned actions are appended to the patrol's actions array and each is passed to addLog('action', a, state.patrolCycle), matching the surrounding patrol wiring.
- AC: npm run typecheck, lint, and the full test suite pass; the deacon patrol cycle still completes without regressions.
close_reason: Wired recoverStalledReviewConvoys into runPatrol after checkOrphanedReviewStatuses with action aggregation/logging

title: pan-1614: Add bounded recoverStalledReviewConvoys() patrol (cooldown + attempt cap + escalate-once)
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts add and export async function recoverStalledReviewConvoys(): Promise<string[]>, modeled structurally on recoverUnhealthyTestStack (deacon.ts:1291-1365). Iterate loadReviewStatuses(); for each issue: skip unless reviewStatus is in-flight ('reviewing' or 'pending'); skip if status.stuck or status.deaconIgnored. For the surviving candidates, resolve the canonical tracker state via getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState(issueId) and require it to equal 'in_review'. Call reviewConvoyLiveness(issueId); require anyLive===false AND anyGated===false. For a matching stalled convoy, apply a per-issue in-memory recovery-state Map<string,{lastAttempt:number;attempts:number;escalated:boolean}> with a 15-minute cooldown and a cap of 3 attempts: (a) if attempts>=cap, escalate once (emitActivityEntrySync level:'error' with a 'stalled-review-convoy-unrecoverable' message AND setReviewStatusSync stuck:true with a dedicated stuckReason e.g. 'review_convoy_unrecoverable'), then return; (b) if within cooldown, defer this cycle; (c) otherwise increment attempts, set lastAttempt, resolve workspace via resolveProjectFromIssueSync + findWorkspacePath (skip without consuming an attempt if unresolvable), and force-re-dispatch via Effect.runPromise(spawnReviewRoleForIssue({ issueId, workspace, branch: `feature/<lower>`, force: true })). On a successful dispatch, delete the issue's map entry. Push a descriptive string to the actions array for every branch (re-dispatched / deferred-cooldown / escalated / skipped-unresolvable). Wrap the body in try/catch like checkOrphanedReviewStatuses so one bad issue never aborts the whole patrol.
- AC: Re-dispatches the convoy (spawnReviewRoleForIssue with force:true) exactly when canonical state==='in_review' AND reviewStatus in {'reviewing','pending'} AND reviewConvoyLiveness reports anyLive=false AND anyGated=false AND not stuck AND not deaconIgnored.
- AC: Does NOT re-dispatch when any convoy agent is live (anyLive=true), when any agent is paused/troubled (anyGated=true), when reviewStatus is terminal (passed/failed/blocked), when status.stuck or status.deaconIgnored is true, or when canonical state is anything other than 'in_review' (e.g. verifying_on_main, in_progress, done, canceled, null).
- AC: Within the 15-minute cooldown after an attempt, a subsequent call defers (no second spawnReviewRoleForIssue call) and records a 'deferring — cooldown' action.
- AC: After the attempt cap (3) is reached, the patrol escalates exactly once: emits one emitActivityEntrySync error AND calls setReviewStatusSync with stuck:true and a dedicated stuckReason; subsequent calls neither re-dispatch nor re-escalate (escalated flag prevents duplicate activity entries).
- AC: A successful re-dispatch clears the issue's entry from the in-memory recovery-state map so a future independent stall gets a fresh attempt budget.
- AC: When the workspace/project cannot be resolved, the patrol records a skip action and does NOT consume an attempt from the cap.
- AC: getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState is only consulted for issues whose reviewStatus is already in-flight (candidate set filtered first), and a thrown error from any single issue is caught so the patrol returns its accumulated actions without aborting.
- AC: Unit tests in tests/lib/cloister/ cover the full matrix in ac1-ac6 (recover, each skip reason, cooldown defer, cap exhaustion+escalation+stuck, successful-dispatch map clear, unresolvable-workspace skip), mocking review-status, agents (getAgentStateSync/spawn), spawnReviewRoleForIssue, and getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState. npm run typecheck, lint, and test pass.
close_reason: Implemented bounded stalled-review-convoy recovery patrol with cooldown, attempt cap, escalation, forced re-dispatch, and unit tests

title: pan-1614: Add reviewConvoyLiveness(issueId) helper enumerating work + synthesis + sub-role agents
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts add a helper (e.g. reviewConvoyLiveness(issueId): { anyLive: boolean; anyGated: boolean; agentIds: string[] }) that enumerates the review convoy agents for an issue: the work agent `agent-<id>`, the synthesis agent `agent-<id>-review`, and one `agent-<id>-review-<subRole>` for each subRole in REVIEW_SUB_ROLES (imported from review-monitor.js). For each, read state via getAgentStateSync. anyLive is true if any agent's status is 'running' or 'starting'. anyGated is true if any agent has paused===true or troubled===true. Absent state.json (null) counts as stopped and ungated. Keep it pure/synchronous (no spawning, no status mutation).
- AC: Helper enumerates exactly six agent IDs: agent-<id>, agent-<id>-review, and agent-<id>-review-<subRole> for each of security, correctness, performance, requirements (sourced from REVIEW_SUB_ROLES, not hardcoded literals).
- AC: anyLive === true iff at least one enumerated agent has state.json status 'running' or 'starting'; a null/absent state.json is treated as stopped.
- AC: anyGated === true iff at least one enumerated agent has paused===true OR troubled===true.
- AC: Helper performs no side effects (no spawnRun, no setReviewStatus, no tmux kills) — confirmed by a unit test that asserts no mocked mutators are called.
- AC: Unit tests cover: all stopped → anyLive=false/anyGated=false; one sub-role running → anyLive=true; work agent paused → anyGated=true; a sub-role troubled → anyGated=true; all state.json absent → anyLive=false/anyGated=false. npm run typecheck, lint, and test pass.
close_reason: Implemented reviewConvoyLiveness helper with unit tests covering convoy IDs, live/gated states, absent state, and no side effects

title: pan-1615: Surface the wedged state in the activity feed and document the new health state + recovery ladder
description:
Emit a clear activity-feed event the first time an agent is marked context-saturated (so operators see the wedge instead of a silent 'healthy'), and document the new 'wedged' health state plus the compact→clear→stuck recovery ladder and the proactive trigger in the relevant deacon/agent-health doc. Keep the activity event idempotent per incident (one event per detection, not per cycle).
- AC: An activity-feed event is emitted when contextSaturatedAt is first set for an agent, naming the agent/issue and the context-overflow cause, and is not re-emitted every patrol cycle
- AC: Documentation describes the wedged health state, the compact→clear→stuck recovery ladder, and the proactive trigger
close_reason: Added first-detection wedged activity event and documented wedged health, compact→clear→stuck recovery ladder, and proactive trigger.

title: pan-1615: Cloister health evaluator surfaces the wedged state in summary and attention checks
description:
Make src/lib/cloister/health.ts aware of context saturation so the cloister-side view (getAgentHealth, generateHealthSummary, needsAttention) does not report a wedged agent as 'active'. Expose the contextSaturatedAt marker to the evaluator (extend the runtime accessor it already uses, or read the marker alongside the heartbeat) and surface a distinct wedged state in AgentHealth so the health summary counts it and needsAttention() returns true. Do not route wedged agents into the idle stuck-remediation ladder — context recovery is owned by the deacon's overflow path.
- AC: getAgentHealth returns a distinct wedged/non-active state when contextSaturatedAt is present, even if the heartbeat is recent
- AC: needsAttention() returns true for a wedged agent and generateHealthSummary counts it as non-active
- AC: A non-saturated agent's health classification is unchanged (existing cloister health tests pass)
close_reason: Added wedged cloister health state from contextSaturatedAt, summary/attention handling, and tests.

title: pan-1615: Dashboard /api/health/agents reports a wedged agent as non-healthy
description:
In src/dashboard/lib/health-filtering.ts determineHealthStatus(), when the agent's runtime.json carries contextSaturatedAt, return a distinct non-healthy status (e.g. { status: 'wedged', reason: 'Context window exhausted (400) — recovery in progress' }) that OVERRIDES the lastActivity-based 'healthy'/'warning' path. Read the marker from the runtime.json already loaded for lastActivity — do NOT add a per-agent tmux capture (the route is performance-sensitive). Ensure the health-status type/enum and any consumers tolerate the new value.
- AC: An alive agent with a recent lastActivity AND contextSaturatedAt set returns a non-healthy 'wedged' status, not 'healthy'
- AC: An alive agent without the marker still returns 'healthy'/'warning'/'stuck' exactly as before (no regression)
- AC: No new per-agent tmux/capturePane call is introduced in the /api/health/agents path; the marker is read from the runtime.json already loaded
close_reason: Dashboard health API now reports alive context-saturated agents as wedged using runtime marker, with regression test.

title: pan-1615: Add the /clear + reseed recovery tier to the deacon escalation ladder
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents(), extend the existing context-overflow escalation so it no longer dead-ends at /compact. New ladder: (1) /compact as today (works while under the ceiling); (2) if after CONTEXT_COMPACT_SETTLE_MS the overflow is STILL present (isContextOverflowTail true), send /clear followed by the reseed-message nudge — never re-sending the transcript; track clear attempts separately in contextOverflowRecoveryState; (3) if the overflow persists after the /clear tier's own attempt budget, markWorkspaceStuck(issueId, 'context_overflow', ...) as the terminal state. Preserve the existing settle-window, cooldown, and stuck/deaconIgnored guards. Emit activity-feed events for the /clear tier as for /compact.
- AC: When /compact fails to clear the overflow after the settle window, the deacon sends /clear and then the reseed nudge (in that order) instead of only retrying /compact
- AC: The /clear tier never sends the prior transcript or any 'continue' message that appends context before the /clear has freed tokens
- AC: If the overflow persists after the /clear+reseed attempt budget is exhausted, the workspace is marked stuck with reason 'context_overflow' and the recovery state is cleared
- AC: Existing guards (settle window, recovery cooldown, stuck/deaconIgnored skip) and the /compact-while-under-ceiling path are preserved; tests cover the compact→clear→stuck progression
close_reason: Extended deacon context-overflow ladder from compact to /clear+reseed, with separate clear attempts and stuck escalation tests.

title: pan-1615: Persist a self-clearing contextSaturatedAt marker on the agent runtime.json
description:
Add a contextSaturatedAt?: string field to the agent runtime state (AgentRuntimeState in src/lib/agents.ts and the runtime.json persistence path) and helpers to set/clear it. In the deacon patrol's context-overflow handling, when isContextOverflowTail(tail) is true, set contextSaturatedAt on FIRST detection (idempotent — keep the original timestamp on subsequent cycles); when the tail no longer shows overflow, clear it. The marker is a live mirror of the current tail, NOT a sticky flag. This crystallizes the schema both classifiers read.
- AC: AgentRuntimeState carries an optional contextSaturatedAt timestamp persisted to and read from runtime.json
- AC: The deacon sets contextSaturatedAt the first cycle overflow is detected (preserving the original timestamp across repeated detections) and clears it the first cycle the tail no longer overflows
- AC: Writing/clearing the marker does not clobber other runtime.json fields (lastActivity, claudeSessionId, etc.) — verified by a test that round-trips a populated runtime state
close_reason: Added contextSaturatedAt runtime marker, domain event persistence, deacon set/clear mirroring, and tests for preservation/clearing/non-clobbering.

title: pan-1615: High-water-mark proactive /compact before the hard ceiling
description:
Add a proactive compaction trigger to the deacon patrol: when an agent's server-side context usage (percentUsed from the conversation-service computation, against the conservative ceiling from proactive-verify) crosses a high-water mark (~85%) AND the agent is idle/at-prompt AND still under the hard ceiling (no overflow yet), send /compact to pre-empt the 400. Cooldown-guarded per agent so a wrong threshold cannot spam /compact, and a no-op once an overflow is already present (the recovery ladder owns that case). This makes the wedge preventable, not just recoverable.
- AC: When percentUsed crosses the high-water mark and the agent is idle and not yet overflowing, the deacon sends a single /compact (cooldown-guarded)
- AC: The proactive trigger is a no-op when the agent is already overflowing (recovery ladder owns that) and when within the cooldown window
- AC: Tests cover: crosses-threshold → compact, below-threshold → no-op, already-overflowing → no-op, within-cooldown → no-op
close_reason: Added idle high-water context usage compaction trigger with cooldown and tests for threshold/no-op cases.

title: pan-1615: Verify CLIProxy auto-compaction behavior and establish a conservative ceiling constant
description:
Establish, from code and the CLIProxy/model-capabilities path, (a) whether Claude Code's native auto-compact fires for CLIProxy-routed models (e.g. gpt-5.5) or whether — as deacon.ts comments assert — it never fires because the proxied model's context window is undeterminable, and (b) the real hard ceiling the proxy/backend enforces. Deliver the finding as code: a documented conservative ceiling constant (and/or correction to the model-capabilities contextWindow for proxied models) plus an inline comment citing the evidence, consumed by the proactive trigger bead. Record the conclusion in the PR description.
- AC: The PR/commit documents whether CLIProxy-routed models auto-compact and what their effective hard ceiling is, with file:line evidence
- AC: A documented conservative ceiling constant (or corrected model-capabilities value) is committed for the proactive trigger to consume
close_reason: Established conservative 200k CLIProxy/Codex context ceiling for gpt-5.5, documented auto-compact gap, and updated context usage test.

title: pan-1615: Pure builder for the post-/clear reseed nudge
description:
Add a pure function that builds the reseed nudge text sent after a /clear. The message must instruct the agent to reconstruct its work-in-progress WITHOUT any prior transcript: read .pan/continue.json, run `bd ready`/`bd show <id>` for its open beads, inspect `git status`/`git diff` for uncommitted work on disk (which survives /clear), and continue from there — explicitly NOT start over and NOT expect the prior conversation. Keep it transcript-free by construction (it references durable artifacts, never prior messages).
- AC: A pure function returns a reseed message that names continue.json, beads (bd ready/bd show), and git status/diff as the reconstruction sources
- AC: The message explicitly tells the agent NOT to start over and that its prior conversation was cleared to recover from a context-window overflow
- AC: A unit test asserts the message contains the durable-artifact references and the do-not-restart instruction
close_reason: Added pure post-/clear reseed nudge builder and tests for durable-artifact reconstruction instructions.

title: pan-1615: Extract a shared context-overflow tail predicate with unit tests
description:
Extract the inline context-overflow detection from src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts checkApiErrorAgents() into a single reusable predicate (e.g. isContextOverflowTail(tail: string): boolean) exported from a shared module, preserving the existing behavior exactly: judge only the recent tail (last ~40 lines) against CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_PATTERNS. Refactor checkApiErrorAgents() to call the new predicate (no behavior change there). This is the single canonical detection contract that the marker, the classifiers, and the recovery tier all consume.
- AC: A pure exported predicate (e.g. isContextOverflowTail) returns true for a tail containing 'input exceeds the context window' / 'exceeds the context window of this model' in the last ~40 lines and false otherwise
- AC: checkApiErrorAgents() is refactored to call the predicate and its existing overflow-handling behavior is unchanged (existing deacon tests still pass)
- AC: Unit tests cover a true hard-wedge tail, a benign tail that merely mentions 'context window' outside the recent window, and an empty tail
close_reason: Extracted shared context overflow tail predicate, refactored deacon detection to use it, and added predicate unit tests.

title: pan-1613: Invalidate the tracker-closed cache when an issue is reopened
description:
In the `pan reopen` path (src/cli/commands/reopen.ts and/or the reopen workflow in src/lib/lifecycle/workflows.ts), call clearIssueClosedCache(issueId) after the issue is reopened so the new gates do not wrongly treat the just-reopened issue as closed for up to 5 minutes due to a stale TTL cache entry. This prevents a 'reopened but nothing spawns' confusion window.
- AC: The reopen path calls clearIssueClosedCache(issueId) so a subsequent isIssueClosed(issueId) re-queries fresh state instead of returning a cached 'closed'.
- AC: A test asserts the cache entry for the issue is cleared during reopen (or that isIssueClosed returns false immediately after reopen given fresh tracker state).
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Cleared shared closed-issue cache in reopenWorkspaceState with regression coverage

title: pan-1613: Add a terminal-close teardown reaper that stops running agents on closed issues
description:
Add a new reconciler (e.g. src/lib/cloister/closed-issue-reaper.ts, exporting reconcileClosedIssueAgents()) and wire it into runPatrol() (deacon.ts ~4212, alongside the other reconcilers like reconcileOrphanProposedSpecs). It enumerates running agents via listRunningAgents(), and for each agent whose parent state.issueId resolves isIssueClosed===true, stops it via stopAgent(agentId) (which kills the tmux session, kills the orphan launcher, and marks state stopped — workspace, branches, and state.json are preserved; this is NOT deep-wipe). It must be idempotent (skip agents already stopped) and emit a clear activity log per reaped agent ('[deacon] reaped <agentId> — parent issue <issue> is closed'). To bound cost, dedupe isIssueClosed calls per issueId within a single patrol pass (the helper's 5-min cache already amortizes the live gh fallback across passes).
- AC: reconcileClosedIssueAgents() stops every running agent (any role: work, review, test, ship, inspect) whose parent issue isIssueClosed===true, using stopAgent() — never deep-wipe and never teardownWorkspace.
- AC: It does NOT stop agents on open issues, on verifying_on_main issues (whose tracker issue is still open), or agents already in stopped/error state (idempotent — a second pass is a no-op).
- AC: The reaper is invoked from runPatrol() and is guarded by the same no-resume / globally-paused gates the sibling reconcilers respect (it does not run when the deacon is globally paused).
- AC: A test asserts: a running ship agent on a closed issue is stopped; a running work agent on an open issue is left alone; a stopped agent on a closed issue is not touched; a second invocation reaps nothing new.
- AC: isIssueClosed is evaluated at most once per distinct issueId per patrol pass.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Added closed-issue running-agent reaper wired into patrol with stopAgent and deduped closed checks

title: pan-1613: Gate idle-work-agent nudging (nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads) on isIssueClosed
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads (~deacon.ts:5255), add an isIssueClosed guard so the patrol does NOT nudge a live work/inspect agent whose parent issue is closed. Nudging a zombie keeps it alive and burns tokens; skip closed issues. Place the gate after the role==='work' filter so only closed-issue work agents are skipped.
- AC: nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads does not send a nudge to a work agent when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true; open-issue idle agents are still nudged.
- AC: A test asserts no nudge message is delivered for a closed issue's idle work agent.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Gated idle work-agent nudging on shared isIssueClosed with closed/open coverage

title: pan-1613: Broaden the work-agent auto-resume closed-gate to use shared isIssueClosed
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents (the existing narrow gate at ~deacon.ts:5472-5477 that only checks shadowState?.trackerStatus === 'closed'), replace the inline check with a call to the shared isIssueClosed(state.issueId). This broadens the resume skip to also cover COMPLETED/CANCELLED (targetCanonicalState 'done'|'canceled', shadowStatus==='closed') and the live tracker fallback, closing the gap where a closed-but-not-trackerStatus-closed issue currently slips through and gets its work agent auto-resumed.
- AC: autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents skips auto-resume (continue) when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true, including the COMPLETED/CANCELLED cases that the old trackerStatus-only check missed.
- AC: A test asserts a stopped work agent on an issue with targetCanonicalState==='done' (but trackerStatus not 'closed') is NOT auto-resumed; an open issue's stopped work agent still resumes.
- AC: Existing auto-resume tests (e.g. pan-871-auto-resume.test.ts) still pass.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Broadened stopped work-agent auto-resume gate to shared isIssueClosed helper

title: pan-1613: Gate inspect-agent dispatch (spawnInspectAgentPromise) on isIssueClosed
description:
In src/lib/cloister/inspect-agent.ts spawnInspectAgentPromise (~inspect-agent.ts:142, before the session-exists check), add an isIssueClosed guard that logs '[cloister] <issue>: skipping inspect dispatch — issue is closed' and returns without building the tmux launcher. The inspect path does not use spawnRun, so it needs its own gate.
- AC: spawnInspectAgentPromise returns early without spawning an inspect session when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true.
- AC: A test asserts no inspect tmux launcher is created for a closed issue, and normal inspect dispatch occurs when the issue is open.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Gated inspect dispatch on shared isIssueClosed and surfaced skipped no-op cleanly

title: pan-1613: Gate the deacon review/test safety-net patrols on isIssueClosed
description:
The deacon patrols re-dispatch review/test agents directly, bypassing the reactive chokepoint. Add an isIssueClosed gate so each one skips closed issues: checkOrphanedReviewStatuses per-issue loop (add the gate at the top of the loop alongside the existing status.stuck / status.deaconIgnored continues near deacon.ts:1449-1452 — this single insertion covers BOTH the orphaned-review re-dispatch at ~1615 and the orphaned-test re-dispatch at ~1683); the stuck-review re-dispatch (~deacon.ts:1784); the additional review re-dispatch patrol (~deacon.ts:2331); and the pending/retry-test dispatch (~deacon.ts:1862). Each skip should log '[deacon] <issue>: skipping <role> re-dispatch — issue is closed'. checkUndispatchedShip routes through onIssueStateChange and is already covered by gate-reactive-dispatch — do NOT duplicate a gate there.
- AC: checkOrphanedReviewStatuses skips closed issues for both its review and test re-dispatch branches (verified by reading the diff at the loop guard).
- AC: The stuck-review re-dispatch (~1784), the second review re-dispatch patrol (~2331), and the pending/retry-test dispatch (~1862) each skip when isIssueClosed is true.
- AC: A test asserts each patrol does NOT call spawnReviewRoleForIssue / spawnRun('test') when isIssueClosed is stubbed true, and DOES dispatch normally when false.
- AC: No gate is added to checkUndispatchedShip (it is covered by the reactive chokepoint gate) — confirmed by inspection.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Gated deacon review/test safety-net re-dispatch patrols on shared isIssueClosed with regression coverage

title: pan-1613: Gate the reactive dispatch chokepoint (service.ts onIssueStateChangePromise) on isIssueClosed
description:
In src/lib/cloister/service.ts onIssueStateChangePromise, after the `const role = stateToRole(newState)` null-check (around service.ts:281, before the activeRoleRunExists probe), add an early return when isIssueClosed(normalizedIssueId) is true: log a clear '[cloister] <issue>: skipping <role> dispatch — issue is closed' line, emit an info activity entry, and return without spawning. This single guard covers reactive review/test/ship dispatch AND the undispatched-ship safety-net patrol (which routes through onIssueStateChange('shipping')).
- AC: onIssueStateChangePromise returns early (no spawnReviewRoleForIssue, no dispatchTestAgentAndNotify, no spawnRun) when isIssueClosed(issueId) is true, for review, test, and ship roles.
- AC: When the issue is NOT closed, dispatch behavior is unchanged (existing reactive-dispatch tests still pass).
- AC: A test asserts that onIssueStateChange(issueId,'shipping') on a closed issue does NOT dispatch ship, and the same for 'in_review' and 'testing'. isIssueClosed is stubbed to true.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Gated reactive review/test/ship dispatch on shared isIssueClosed with regression coverage

title: pan-1613: Extract shared isIssueClosed helper into src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts
description:
Move isIssueClosed(issueId, closedIssueIds?) and isTrackerIssueClosed(issueId) plus the trackerClosedCache / TRACKER_CLOSED_CACHE_TTL_MS into a new shared module src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts and export them. Also export a clearIssueClosedCache(issueId?: string) function that deletes a single issue's cache entry (or clears all when no arg). Re-point orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts to import isIssueClosed and the cache-clear from the new module with NO behavior change there (the existing trackerClosedCache.clear() at line 381 should call the new exported clear). This is the foundation contract every downstream gate imports.
- AC: New module src/lib/cloister/issue-closed.ts exports isIssueClosed, isTrackerIssueClosed, and clearIssueClosedCache, preserving the existing semantics (shadow trackerStatus/shadowStatus==='closed', targetCanonicalState 'done'|'canceled', live gh fallback, 5-min TTL cache).
- AC: orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts imports isIssueClosed (and clearIssueClosedCache for its existing reset) from the new module; its own copies are deleted; its existing tests still pass unchanged (no behavior change).
- AC: A unit test for the new module asserts isIssueClosed returns true for each of trackerStatus==='closed', shadowStatus==='closed', targetCanonicalState==='done', targetCanonicalState==='canceled', and via the gh tracker fallback; and false for an open issue. Tracker-poll behavior is mocked (no real gh call).
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass.
close_reason: Extracted shared closed-issue helper, repointed orphan proposed reconciler, and added mocked unit coverage

title: pan-1616: Deacon watchdog: detect hung/dead inspect sessions, fail loud, deliver verdict to parent
description:
Add an inspect-timeout patrol to the Deacon (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts), wired into the existing ~60s patrol cycle alongside checkStuckWorkAgents/orphan/api-error patrols. Each cycle, for every issue whose review status has inspectStatus==='inspecting': compute elapsed = now - inspectStartedAt; trip if (elapsed > INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS) OR (the inspect-<issue>-<bead> tmux session no longer exists). INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS is a documented constant (~12 min, above the inspect-agent.md 10-min budget). On trip, atomically: (1) set inspectStatus='error' with inspectNotes explaining timeout-vs-crash so the next cycle skips it (idempotency guard — never re-trip an 'error'); (2) kill the inspect tmux session if still present (killSession); (3) deliver an 'INSPECTION ERROR for bead <id>: inspection could not complete (timeout/crash) — no verdict was produced. Treat as infrastructure failure: do not silently proceed.' verdict to the PARENT work agent via pan tell using the UPPERCASE issue id (routes to agent-<issue>, the blocked session), mirroring inspect-agent.md's verdict delivery. If the parent session is also gone, log and leave inspectStatus='error' for a human/patrol to surface. Use async tmux primitives only (no sync sendKeys, no execSync).
- AC: Patrol trips when an 'inspecting' status exceeds INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS measured from inspectStartedAt
- AC: Patrol also trips when inspectStatus==='inspecting' but the inspect-<issue>-<bead> tmux session no longer exists (crash-without-verdict)
- AC: On trip: inspectStatus is set to 'error', the inspect tmux session is killed if present, and an INSPECTION ERROR verdict is delivered to the parent work agent via pan tell with the uppercase issue id
- AC: Idempotent: a status already in 'error' is never re-tripped on subsequent patrol cycles (no duplicate verdicts / no oscillation)
- AC: INSPECT_TIMEOUT_MS is a single named constant (~12 min) documented next to the inspect-agent.md 10-minute budget; uses async tmux + no execSync
- AC: A unit test (fake timers) drives a stale 'inspecting' status past the timeout and asserts the trip actions fire exactly once
close_reason: Added Deacon inspect watchdog for timeout/crash, terminal error state, parent verdict delivery, idempotency, patrol wiring, and fake-timer coverage

title: pan-1616: inspect-agent.md: align stated timeout with watchdog and note read-only auto-approval
description:
Update src/lib/cloister/prompts/inspect-agent.md so its 'Timeout: You have 10 minutes' note (around line 144) explicitly states the watchdog enforces this bound (sessions exceeding it are killed and an ERROR verdict is delivered to the parent), and add a one-line note that this inspect session's read-only commands (git diff/branch/rev-parse, file reads, grep) are auto-approved — the agent should never expect or wait on a permission prompt. Keep it to a couple of lines; this is documentation alignment so the enforced timeout and the prompt's stated budget never drift.
- AC: inspect-agent.md states the 10-minute budget is enforced by the Deacon watchdog (kill + ERROR verdict on overrun)
- AC: inspect-agent.md notes read-only inspection commands are auto-approved (no permission prompt expected)
close_reason: Aligned inspect prompt with enforced watchdog timeout and read-only auto-approval expectations

title: pan-1616: work.md: handle INSPECTION ERROR verdict and reflect the guaranteed bounded wait
description:
Update the inspection wait instructions in src/lib/cloister/prompts/work.md (steps around lines 187 and 308). Add an INSPECTION ERROR branch alongside PASSED/BLOCKED: on 'INSPECTION ERROR ...', the agent must treat it as an infrastructure failure — report it to its supervisor via pan tell and STOP advancing to the next bead (do not silently proceed, do not treat it as a normal spec BLOCK fix-loop). Reword the unbounded 'WAIT' guidance to state that a terminal verdict (PASSED, BLOCKED, or ERROR) is GUARANTEED to arrive within the inspection timeout because the Deacon watchdog backstops it — so the agent waits for the next pan tell rather than polling or assuming the inspection silently succeeded. Keep the edits surgical and matched to the surrounding prompt style.
- AC: work.md documents an INSPECTION ERROR verdict branch that escalates via pan tell to the supervisor and halts bead advancement
- AC: The WAIT guidance states a terminal verdict is guaranteed within the inspection timeout (watchdog-backstopped), not an open-ended wait
- AC: Edits are surgical (only the inspection-wait sections change) and the prompt still renders/templates correctly
close_reason: Updated work prompt to handle INSPECTION ERROR and describe watchdog-backed bounded inspection waits

title: pan-1616: Extend inspect status schema: inspectStartedAt, inspectBeadId, and terminal 'error' state
description:
Extend the ReviewStatus inspect fields in src/lib/review-status.ts: add optional inspectStartedAt (ISO 8601 string), optional inspectBeadId (string), and add 'error' to the inspectStatus union ('pending' | 'inspecting' | 'passed' | 'failed' | 'error'). Set inspectStartedAt (current time) and inspectBeadId in spawnInspectAgentPromise (src/lib/cloister/inspect-agent.ts) at the exact point it flips inspectStatus to 'inspecting' (currently inspect-agent.ts:154-157). Persist via the existing setReviewStatusSync path and its DB layer (review-status-db.ts). Audit all readers of inspectStatus (dashboard read-model, src/dashboard/server/routes/specialists.ts:381, frontend components) so the new 'error' member renders/handles gracefully and does not crash a switch. Do NOT build the watchdog here — only the schema + spawn-time population the watchdog will consume.
- AC: ReviewStatus inspect type gains optional inspectStartedAt and inspectBeadId, and inspectStatus union includes 'error'
- AC: spawnInspectAgentPromise sets inspectStartedAt (now) and inspectBeadId at the same point it sets inspectStatus='inspecting'
- AC: The two new fields persist through setReviewStatusSync/review-status-db and survive a read-back
- AC: All existing inspectStatus readers (dashboard read-model, specialists route, frontend) compile and handle 'error' without throwing; typecheck + lint pass
close_reason: Added inspect error state, started/bead metadata, SQLite persistence, spawn-time population, frontend handling, and DB round-trip coverage

title: pan-1616: Regression test: auto-approve-hook grants allow for pipeline prefixes, stays silent otherwise
description:
Add a test that executes sync-sources/hooks/auto-approve-hook with a representative tool-call JSON on stdin under different PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID values and asserts the output. For agent-*, planning-*, flywheel-*, strike-*, and inspect-* the hook must emit hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision === 'allow'. For conv-* and an unset PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID the hook must exit 0 with NO permissionDecision (falls back to normal prompting). Also assert that AskUserQuestion is NOT auto-allowed regardless of prefix (the ask-user-question-hook deny must still win). This locks the class-1 fix (b8960b6dc) against silent regression. Place it alongside existing hook tests (e.g. src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts) or a new dedicated test file; invoke the script via child_process so it tests the real shipped artifact.
- AC: Test asserts permissionDecision==='allow' for each of agent-/planning-/flywheel-/strike-/inspect- prefixed PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID
- AC: Test asserts conv-* and unset PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID produce exit 0 with no permissionDecision (no auto-allow)
- AC: Test asserts AskUserQuestion is not auto-allowed even for an in-scope prefix
- AC: Test runs against the real sync-sources/hooks/auto-approve-hook script (not a reimplementation) and passes under npm test
close_reason: Added real auto-approve-hook regression test for pipeline prefixes, conv/unset silence, and AskUserQuestion pass-through

title: PAN-1410: Implement issue
pan-1547: Verify and capture the reduced install warning set
description:
After speech-optional and transformers-optional land, run the real install probe used during planning — a clean `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` from a temp project (or `npm pack` of the local build installed into a temp dir to validate pre-publish) — and capture the before/after warning sets. Assert glob@10.5.0 and node-domexception@1.0.0 are GONE, and that only the documented residuals (ini@7 on Node <22.22.2; prebuild-install) remain. Record the captured output in the PR description / issue as the evidence the fix meets the issue's 'eliminate transitive noise' bar.
- AC: A captured real-install probe shows glob@10.5.0 and node-domexception@1.0.0 warnings absent.
- AC: The captured output shows only the documented residuals (prebuild-install; ini@7 on Node <22.22.2) and is attached to the PR/issue as evidence.

title: pan-1547: Document Node 22 requirement, opt-in optional features, and expected residual deprecations
description:
Update README install/prerequisites: state the Node 22 requirement clearly and recommend the latest Node 22.x (>=22.22.2) to avoid the upstream effect→ini@7 engine notice; document that Google Cloud STT (`@google-cloud/speech`) and local doc-embedding indexing (`@xenova/transformers`) are now opt-in installs, with the exact commands; and add a short 'known install notices' note explaining the residual `prebuild-install` deprecation (transitive of node-pty/better-sqlite3, expected, harmless) and the ini@7 engine notice on Node <22.22.2. Docs only — no code.
- AC: README states Node 22 is required and recommends the latest 22.x (>=22.22.2) to avoid the upstream ini engine notice.
- AC: README documents that @google-cloud/speech and @xenova/transformers are opt-in installs, with the exact install commands.
- AC: README includes a brief note that the residual prebuild-install deprecation (and ini@7 notice on Node <22.22.2) are expected upstream transitive notices.

title: pan-1547: Make @xenova/transformers a not-auto-installed optional dependency
description:
Apply the same optional-peer-dep pattern established by `speech-optional` to `@xenova/transformers`: remove it from `dependencies`, declare it optional-peer, keep it resolvable for development, and make the single dynamic-import site at src/lib/docs/index-builder.ts (~line 417) emit an actionable error when absent (naming the install command). This removes sharp (and sharp's own deprecations) from the default install. NOTE: it does NOT remove prebuild-install — node-pty and better-sqlite3 still require it; that residual is handled by the docs bead.
- AC: @xenova/transformers is removed from `dependencies` and declared as an optional peer dep; it is NOT present in a fresh `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` resolved tree.
- AC: A real production install probe shows sharp (and its deprecations) no longer pulled via @xenova/transformers.
- AC: When @xenova/transformers is absent at runtime, local-embedding doc indexing emits an actionable error naming the install command.
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm test` pass in the dev workspace.

title: pan-1547: Make @google-cloud/speech a not-auto-installed optional dependency
description:
Remove `@google-cloud/speech` from `dependencies` in the root package.json and declare it as an optional peer dependency (`peerDependencies` + `peerDependenciesMeta['@google-cloud/speech'].optional = true`), keeping it resolvable for development (devDependencies and/or bun install). Update the single dynamic-import site at src/voice/google-cloud-transcription.ts (~line 88) so that a missing module produces a clear, actionable error naming the exact install command (e.g. `npm install -g @google-cloud/speech`) instead of a raw 'Cannot find module'. This establishes the optional-peer-dep pattern that the transformers bead reuses and the verification bead checks. Verify with a real `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` probe (in a temp dir) that glob@10.5.0 AND node-domexception are no longer in the resolved tree, and that `npm run typecheck && npm run build && npm test` still pass in the dev workspace.
- AC: @google-cloud/speech is removed from `dependencies` and declared as an optional peer dep; it is NOT present in a fresh `npm install @panctl/cli --omit=dev` resolved tree.
- AC: A real production install probe shows glob@10.5.0 and node-domexception@1.0.0 deprecation warnings are GONE.
- AC: When @google-cloud/speech is absent at runtime, the Google Cloud transcription path emits an actionable error naming the install command — not a raw module-not-found.
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, and `npm test` pass in the dev workspace (package still resolvable for development).

title: pan-538: Unit test: reload guard catches a skipped frontend bundle
description:
Add a focused unit test (Vitest) that exercises the reload freshness check: server bundle mtime advances but frontend bundle mtime does not → the guard reports the 'Build did not refresh' failure and does not proceed to restart. Follow the repo's existing test style for cli/commands; mock the build and stat/mtime sources rather than running a real build. No real wall-clock delays.
- AC: A new test fails on the pre-fix logic (server-only check passes a stale frontend) and passes after the guard is extended.
- AC: The test uses mocked mtimes (no real build, no real setTimeout/wall-clock delays) and runs in the default `npm test` suite.

title: pan-538: Contract test: build:dashboard:frontend cleans public before building
description:
Add a small test that reads the root package.json and asserts the `build:dashboard:frontend` script removes dist/dashboard/public before the `npm --prefix ./src/dashboard/frontend run build` step (rmrf token precedes the build token). This pins the PAN-538 fix so a future edit cannot silently reintroduce the stale-bundle regression. Place it alongside existing repo-config/contract tests; if none exists, a minimal Vitest file under tests/ that requires package.json is sufficient.
- AC: Test asserts package.json scripts['build:dashboard:frontend'] contains an `fs-helper.mjs rmrf dist/dashboard/public` step that appears before the frontend build invocation.
- AC: Test runs in the default `npm test` suite and passes against the fixed package.json.

title: pan-538: Extend pan reload freshness guard to cover the frontend bundle
description:
Make pan reload's 'Build did not refresh' guard watch the frontend bundle in addition to the server bundle. Add `resolveBundledPublicIndexPath()` to src/cli/commands/restart.ts, derived as join(dirname(resolveBundledServerPath()),'public','index.html'). In src/cli/commands/reload.ts, capture the frontend bundle mtime before and after runBuild() (reusing the existing ENOENT->0 statSync pattern) and fail with the existing 'Build did not refresh … old dashboard left running' error if EITHER the server OR the frontend bundle mtime did not advance.
- AC: restart.ts exports resolveBundledPublicIndexPath() returning dist/dashboard/public/index.html derived from resolveBundledServerPath()'s directory.
- AC: reload.ts captures before/after mtimes for BOTH the server bundle and the frontend bundle, tolerating ENOENT as mtime 0.
- AC: When the frontend bundle mtime does not advance across the build (skipped frontend), reload exits non-zero with the existing 'Build did not refresh … old dashboard left running' message and does NOT restart the dashboard.

title: pan-538: Clean dist/dashboard/public before the frontend build
description:
In the ROOT package.json, change `build:dashboard:frontend` to remove dist/dashboard/public before invoking the frontend build, mirroring `build:contracts`. New value: `node scripts/fs-helper.mjs rmrf dist/dashboard/public && npm --prefix ./src/dashboard/frontend run build`. This ensures a skipped/failed `vite build` (e.g. when the preceding `tsc` fails) leaves NO stale bundle, so pan up / pan reload detect the absence loudly instead of serving old code.
- AC: Root package.json build:dashboard:frontend runs `fs-helper.mjs rmrf dist/dashboard/public` before `npm --prefix ./src/dashboard/frontend run build`, mirroring the build:contracts rmrf pattern.
- AC: A clean `npm run build` still succeeds and produces a fresh dist/dashboard/public/index.html plus hashed assets (no regression on the happy path).
- AC: Reproduce-and-confirm: with a deliberate frontend tsc error in place, `npm run build:dashboard:frontend` leaves dist/dashboard/public EMPTY (not a stale bundle); the deliberate error is removed afterward and the build passes again.

Final verification across runtimes.

title: pan-1579: Create shared SQLite driver adapter (driver.ts) with pragma/transaction shims and runtime selection
description:
Add src/lib/database/driver.ts exporting openDatabase(path: string): SqliteDatabase and the SqliteDatabase / SqliteStatement TypeScript interfaces that all DB code will depend on. Select bun:sqlite when typeof Bun !== 'undefined', else node:sqlite (via createRequire). Present a better-sqlite3-compatible surface: exec(sql), prepare(sql)->{run(...params)->{changes,lastInsertRowid}, get(...params), all(...params), iterate(...params)}, close(). Shim pragma(sql, {simple?}) (exec for sets; prepare(`PRAGMA ${key}`).get() for {simple:true} reads, returning the scalar). Shim transaction(fn)->callable that runs BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK at depth 0 and SAVEPOINT/RELEASE/ROLLBACK TO when nested (track depth on the connection). At module import, install a targeted process.emitWarning filter that swallows ONLY name==='ExperimentalWarning' && /SQLite/.test(message). Wrap the node:sqlite require in try/catch and, on a flag-required error, throw a clear message recommending Node 22.16+/24. The SqliteDatabase type must model BLOB results as Uint8Array. Do NOT auto-coerce booleans. This bead does NOT modify any existing call site.
- AC: openDatabase(':memory:') returns a working connection under both Node (node:sqlite) and Bun (bun:sqlite); exec/prepare/run/get/all/iterate/close all function
- AC: pragma('journal_mode = WAL') sets and pragma('user_version', {simple:true}) reads back the scalar value correctly
- AC: transaction(fn) commits on success, rolls back the whole transaction when fn throws, and a nested transaction inside an outer one uses SAVEPOINT so the inner can roll back while the outer commits — all covered by unit tests using fake timers where any delay is involved
- AC: Unit test asserts a raw boolean bind throws (documents the strict-no-coercion contract, H1)
- AC: Float32Array blob round-trips: bind a Buffer-of-Float32Array, read the column back as Uint8Array, reconstruct the Float32Array, assert values equal (covers BLOB type + alignment, H4/D6)
- AC: Importing driver.ts suppresses the SQLite ExperimentalWarning only; a non-SQLite ExperimentalWarning still emits (asserted in a unit test)
- AC: typecheck + lint pass; no import of better-sqlite3 anywhere in driver.ts
close_reason: Remediated inspection block by removing unrelated config/rule-file changes; bead diff now contains only the SQLite driver adapter and its tests.

title: pan-1101: Emit a runtime permission tripwire in auto-mode launcher scripts
description:
In src/lib/launcher-generator.ts (generateLauncherScriptSync), when the resolved permission mode is auto (permissionFlags includes the 'auto' token), emit a small bash guard immediately before the baked `exec claude ...` line. The guard inspects the baked claude invocation and, if a bypass token is present, prints a PANIC message to stderr and exits 70 (refusing to run). Build the forbidden-token pattern from the BYPASS_PERMISSION_MODE constant imported from claude-permissions.ts — do NOT write the literal 'bypassPermissions' string into launcher-generator.ts, or scripts/lint-permissions.sh will self-trip. The guard must be emitted ONLY for auto mode (bypass-mode launchers legitimately carry the token and must not be guarded). --agent launchers carry no permissionFlags and therefore correctly receive no guard. This is the only production-runtime hardening layer: it fires on every auto-mode agent start even if tests miss a future leak.
- AC: Auto-mode launcher scripts (permissionFlags include 'auto') contain a bash guard immediately before the exec line that exits 70 with a PANIC-to-stderr message if a bypass token is present in the baked claude invocation.
- AC: Bypass-mode launcher scripts and --agent launcher scripts (no permissionFlags) do NOT contain the guard.
- AC: The forbidden-token pattern is constructed from the BYPASS_PERMISSION_MODE constant (no quoted bypass literal added to launcher-generator.ts); `bash scripts/lint-permissions.sh` and `npm run lint` stay green without modifying the lint allowlist.
- AC: A unit test proves the guard fires: feeding an auto config whose baked command contains a bypass token produces a guard that exits non-zero when run in bash (or, equivalently, the test asserts the guard block greps for the token and exits 70).
- AC: Existing launcher-generator.test.ts auto-mode expectations are updated for the new guard lines; the full test file passes and bypass-mode expectations are unchanged.

title: pan-1101: Add a property/matrix test for generateLauncherScriptSync permission safety
description:
In src/lib/__tests__/launcher-generator.test.ts, add a matrix/property test that feeds generateLauncherScriptSync a grid of LauncherConfig shapes varying permission mode (auto via permissionFlags ['--permission-mode','auto'] vs bypass via ['--permission-mode','bypassPermissions']) across harness (claude-code, pi), role (work, plan, review), and spawnMode (work/resume/conversation/remote). For each combination, assert the property: when the mode is auto, the claude invocation line (the exec/command-parts portion, not the whole script) contains neither '--dangerously-skip-permissions' nor 'bypassPermissions'; when the mode is bypass, the claude invocation contains 'bypassPermissions'. Include the --agent launcher shape (no permissionFlags) as an explicitly covered case where no bypass token appears. Reuse existing config fixtures in the test file for each harness/role/spawnMode shape so configs are valid (Pi requires piExtensionPath/piFifoPath/piSessionDir; review sub-role requires reviewSignal). This closes the gap between the unit-level command builder tests and the assembled launcher script across the full spawn matrix.
- AC: A new matrix/property test exists in launcher-generator.test.ts iterating over permission mode × harness × role × spawnMode and exercising generateLauncherScriptSync for each combination.
- AC: For every auto-mode combination, the test asserts the claude invocation line contains neither '--dangerously-skip-permissions' nor 'bypassPermissions'.
- AC: For every bypass-mode combination, the test asserts the claude invocation line contains 'bypassPermissions'; the --agent (no-permissionFlags) shape is covered as a case with no bypass token.
- AC: Assertions target the claude invocation (exec line / commandParts), not a naive whole-script not.toMatch, so they remain valid alongside item 4's auto-mode guard line. `npm test` passes and `npm run lint` stays green.

title: pan-1256: Add unit tests for WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS grace window in recoverOrphanedAgents
description:
Create `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-orphan-grace.test.ts` covering the WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS startup grace window in `recoverOrphanedAgents`. Mirror the mock scaffolding from `pan-871-auto-resume.test.ts` (fs mock, agents.js Effect mocks, tmux.js sessionExistsSync mock returning false, paths.js mock pointing AGENTS_DIR at a fake path). Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` + `vi.setSystemTime()` to control `Date.now()` against each agent's `startedAt`. Write four test cases — three from the issue ACs plus one reviewer-pattern regression. Run `npm test -- deacon-orphan-grace` and ensure all four pass. Then run the full test suite (`npm test`) to confirm no regressions. NO production code changes. Verify by reading `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:5073-5088` that the WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS branch is untouched after your work.
- AC: New test file exists at `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-orphan-grace.test.ts` and uses `vi.useFakeTimers()` in beforeEach / `vi.useRealTimers()` in afterEach
- AC: Test: a work agent in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 30s in the past (inside the 120s grace) is NOT marked stopped — assert `recoverOrphanedAgents()` returns `[]` AND `saveAgentState` is never called with `status: 'stopped'` for that agent
- AC: Test: a work agent in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 200s in the past (outside the 120s grace) IS marked stopped — assert returned actions array contains the agent dir AND `saveAgentState` is called with `status: 'stopped'`
- AC: Test: a work agent in `status: 'running'` with no tmux session IS still marked stopped (no regression for the existing confirmed-dead path — `sessionExistsSync` returns false, no grace branch entered because status is 'running' not 'starting')
- AC: Test: a reviewSubRole agent in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 30s in the past and NO `reviewer-launcher.pid` file is NOT marked stopped (preserves the existing 90s REVIEWER_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS branch — `existsSync` returns false for the pid file, falls into grace check, grace holds)
- AC: PAN-1122 swarm-race regression: a swarm slot in `status: 'starting'` with `startedAt` 29s in the past (the exact failure timing from the original 2026-05-21 reproduction) is NOT marked stopped. This is the deterministic stand-in for the PAN-1122 swarm smoke evidence — it reproduces the original failing scenario and proves the fix holds.
- AC: All four+ new tests pass under `npm test -- deacon-orphan-grace`
- AC: Full test suite passes: `npm test` shows no new failures introduced by the new file
- AC: Quality gates pass: `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` are clean
- AC: Production code at `src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:5073-5088` (the WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS branch) is unchanged by this issue — verify with `git diff main -- src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts` showing no edits in that region
close_reason: Added fake-timer coverage for WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS and reviewer grace in recoverOrphanedAgents; targeted tests, typecheck, and lint pass

title: pan-1257: Add swarm dispatch regression: dispatch succeeds when specialists run for other issues
description:
Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts with a new `it(...)` case proving that a swarm dispatch is NOT rejected with 429 'No agent capacity available' when only specialists are running for other issues.

The existing test file already mocks getSystemHealthSnapshot and getResourceConfig (see lines 24-27 and 196-201). Add a new case that:
- Sets getResourceConfig to return { agentBlockCount: 4 } (the existing default in this file)
- Sets getSystemHealthSnapshot to return summary.workAgentCount = 1 (i.e. 1 active work agent, leaving 3 slots free — modelling the PAN-1257 repro state where 7 specialists were running and the original bug reported 8/10 work agents)
- Calls the swarm dispatch handler and asserts the response status is NOT 429 and the body does not include 'No agent capacity available'

The assertion deliberately does NOT depend on swarm fully succeeding — only that the workAgentCount-based capacity check at swarm.ts:988-999 lets the request through. If the call would have to traverse much more swarm code than is mocked, prefer an assertion on the dispatch outcome that proves the early-return at the capacity gate did not fire (for instance, that mocks downstream of the capacity gate were called, or that the response is one of the later success/validation responses).

Document the test with a comment referencing PAN-1257 and the original repro line: 'Failed: No agent capacity available (10/10 agents running)' with 7 of those being specialists.
- AC: New `it(...)` case exists in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts referencing PAN-1257 in the test name or a comment
- AC: Test mocks getSystemHealthSnapshot to return a summary with workAgentCount strictly less than agentBlockCount (e.g. workAgentCount=1, agentBlockCount=4)
- AC: Test asserts the swarm dispatch handler does NOT return HTTP 429 with 'No agent capacity available'
- AC: Test passes consistently (no flakes) under npm test
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass for the modified file

- AC: classifyAgentKind is exported from src/dashboard/server/services/system-health-service.ts with a JSDoc `@internal` note

- AC: `agent-pan-1228-ship` with role='ship' classifies as 'specialist'

title: pan-1577: Frontend: drag a conversation row onto a sidebar project to move it
description:
Make the conversation row (ConversationRow.tsx) a native HTML5 drag source (draggable, onDragStart carrying the conversation name; drag handle/cursor affordance). Make each sidebar project node (ProjectTree/ProjectNode.tsx) a drop target (onDragOver preventDefault + drop-target highlight, onDrop calls the shared `move` mutation with the target project's key). The optimistic re-group + rollback + toast all come from the shared mutation. Drop onto the conversation's current project is a no-op.
- AC: Dragging a conversation row onto a sidebar project moves it there via the shared mutation.
- AC: Visual affordances present: drag handle/cursor on the row and drop-target highlight on the hovered project.
- AC: Optimistic re-group with rollback + error toast on failure; drop onto the current project is a no-op.
- AC: Browser UAT in an isolated Playwright profile verifies both drag-drop and the menu picker move + toast end-to-end.

The conversation's current project is indicated and disabled. Selecting a project calls the shared `move` mutation and closes the menu. Reuse the existing portaled-submenu approach in this menu rather than introducing a new menu library.

- AC: The current project is indicated and disabled in the picker; selecting another moves the conversation via the shared mutation.

title: pan-1577: Add pan-conversations wrapper skill (Skills↔CLI convention)

Create sync-sources/skills/pan-conversations/SKILL.md documenting the `pan conversations` command group (scan, search, list, show, current, cost, enrich, embed, and the new `move <query> <projectKey>`). Every command/flag mentioned must exactly match `pan conversations --help` and each subcommand's `--help`, so `scripts/lint-skills.sh` (wired into npm run lint) passes. Keep it concise — wrapper-skill shape per docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md.

title: pan-1577: Frontend: shared optimistic `move` mutation (rollback + toast)
description:
Add a `move({ name, projectKey })` React-Query mutation to useConversationMutations.ts following the favoriteMutation pattern: onMutate cancels ['conversations'] queries and optimistically sets the moved conversation's projectKey in cache (so it re-groups instantly); onError rolls back from the returned context and fires toast.error; onSuccess fires toast.success(`Moved <title> to <project>`) using the title not the id; onSettled invalidates ['conversations']. The mutationFn PATCHes /api/conversations/:name/move. This single hook is the shared primitive both the menu action and drag-drop call.
- AC: `move` mutation optimistically re-groups the conversation, rolls back on error, and shows success/error toasts (title-not-id).
- AC: Optimistic-apply + error-rollback behavior is unit-tested.

title: pan-1577: CLI: `pan conversations move <query> <projectKey>`
description:
Create src/cli/commands/conversations/move.ts and register `move <query> <projectKey>` in registerConversationsCommands (src/cli/commands/conversations/index.ts). Resolve <query> against the live conversations table: exact getConversationByName first, then a fuzzy title match over listConversations(); error non-zero on no match or ambiguous (multiple) matches with a clear chalk.red message listing candidates. Call the move endpoint via `${getDashboardApiUrlSync()}/api/conversations/<name>/move` (PATCH, JSON body { projectKey }). On success print the resolved conversation title + old→new project. On non-2xx or fetch failure (dashboard down), print a clear chalk.red error and process.exit(1). Match the chalk/exit conventions used by show.ts and the abort-review/reset-review CLI commands.
- AC: `pan conversations move <query> <projectKey>` resolves by exact name then fuzzy title and moves via the PATCH endpoint.
- AC: Prints resolved conversation title + old→new project on success.
- AC: Unknown project, no-match, ambiguous-match, and dashboard-unreachable all exit non-zero with a clear message.
- AC: Resolver + error paths are unit-tested (using the conversations/__tests__ pattern).

title: pan-1577: Frontend: surface projectKey, prefer it in grouping, handle conversation.moved live
description:
Add `projectKey?: string | null` to the frontend Conversation interface (ConversationList.tsx). Change the project-grouping logic in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/projectsData.ts (isUnscopedConversation + wherever conversations bucket under project nodes) to read the explicit projectKey override FIRST and fall back to cwd→project-path inference only when the override is null. In EventRouter/store, handle `conversation.moved` the same way `conversation.created` is handled (bump conversationsListRevision / invalidate the ['conversations'] query) so other clients re-group without refresh.
- AC: Conversation type carries `projectKey`; grouping prefers the override and falls back to cwd inference when null (unit-tested precedence).
- AC: A `conversation.moved` event re-groups the sidebar live (invalidates/bumps the conversations list) without a manual refresh.

title: pan-1577: Backend move primitive: PATCH /api/conversations/:name/move
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts add a `PATCH /api/conversations/:name/move` route taking `{ projectKey }`. Resolve the conversation via getConversationByName; 404 if not found. Validate the target project with getProjectSync(projectKey) + existsSync(path), returning 400 `Unknown project: <key>` (mirror the guard at conversations.ts:1867). If the conversation is already associated with the target project, return 200 no-op without emitting. Otherwise call setConversationProjectKey(name, projectKey), emit `conversation.moved` via getEventStore().emitOnly({...}), and return the updated conversation. The handler must NOT touch cwd, the tmux session, or the JSONL file. Register the route in the same router-composition spot as the other conversation routes.
- AC: PATCH /api/conversations/:name/move sets the project_key override and returns the updated conversation (200).
- AC: Unknown/path-missing target project returns 400 `Unknown project: <key>`; unknown conversation name returns 404.
- AC: Already-in-target-project returns 200 no-op and does NOT emit conversation.moved.
- AC: A successful move emits `conversation.moved { conversationName, projectKey }`.
- AC: Test asserts the conversation's cwd and on-disk JSONL path are unchanged after a move (JSONL sacred).

Wire it into any shared reducer/union the same way conversation.created is wired so applyEventShared accepts it. Rebuild contracts (cd packages/contracts && npm run build) so server + frontend resolve the new type.

- AC: Contracts package builds cleanly and the new event is importable from both server and frontend.

title: pan-1577: Add `project_key` override column to conversations + thread it through reads
description:
In src/lib/database/schema.ts add migration `if (currentVersion < 49) { try { ALTER TABLE conversations ADD COLUMN project_key TEXT } catch {} }`, add the `project_key TEXT` column to the inline CREATE TABLE conversations statement (~line 406) for fresh DBs, and bump SCHEMA_VERSION to 49. In src/lib/database/conversations-db.ts add `projectKey: string | null` to the Conversation interface, map `row['project_key']` in rowToConversation, add `project_key` to EVERY SELECT column list (listConversations, listActiveConversations, getConversationByName, and any other reader), and add a `setConversationProjectKey(name: string, projectKey: string | null): void` setter. The override is the single source of truth for sidebar grouping; cwd is the fallback only.
- AC: Schema migration v49 adds project_key TEXT to conversations (idempotent, try/catch) and SCHEMA_VERSION is bumped to 49; CREATE TABLE includes the column for fresh DBs.
- AC: Conversation interface gains `projectKey`, rowToConversation maps it, and every SELECT column list in conversations-db.ts includes project_key.
- AC: `setConversationProjectKey(name, projectKey)` updates only the project_key column for the matching conversation and is unit-tested (set, overwrite, clear-to-null).
- AC: GET /api/conversations returns `projectKey` for each conversation (verified via the listConversations enrichment path).

title: pan-1610: Wire `merge` invocation in useIssueActions + dialog host (confirm, dynamic label, stuck retry)
description:

Compute a DYNAMIC label/disabled for the merge view from reviewStatus.mergeStatus: 'Queued' (queued), 'Rebasing…'/'Merging' (merging), 'Verifying…' (verifying), 'Retry Merge' when merging has elapsed >2min (PAN-490 stuck detection via reviewStatus.updatedAt), otherwise 'Merge'. Disable while queued/merging/verifying unless stuck. Add the bodyForAction case if needed (merge needs no body). Route via the existing dialog-host branch pattern (activeDialog.key === 'merge').
- AC: Invoking the merge action opens a confirm dialog; confirming POSTs to /api/issues/:id/merge and triggers refreshDashboardState (asserted with a mocked fetch)

Add any IssueActionState fields the merge gating needs that are not already present (mergeStatus/readyForMerge already arrive via reviewStatus; do NOT add redundant fields). This bead is catalog-shape only — invocation wiring is later beads.
- AC: IssueActionKey union includes 'merge' and 'viewVbrief'
