title: pan-1574: Create src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts skeleton (introspection methods + Effect wrapper) and register it
description:
Create src/lib/runtimes/codex.ts implementing AgentRuntimeSync and the Effect AgentRuntime, modeled on pi.ts. In this bead implement the introspection methods and the D-7 session model: name ('codex'); getSessionPath resolving $CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*-<threadId>.jsonl by a captured thread-id (Claude-style EXTERNAL store — NOT Pi's <agentDir>/sessions); getLastActivity (rollout mtime); listSessions (walk $CODEX_HOME/sessions); isRunning (sessionExists). Stub spawnAgent/sendMessage/killAgent/getHeartbeat/getTokenUsage/getSessionCost as not-yet-implemented (throw or passive default) so the tree compiles. Copy Pi's Effect wrapper shape (pi.ts:406-481) verbatim — hold an inner sync runtime and lift async methods via Effect.tryPromise. Register in runtimes/index.ts: export createCodexRuntimeSync/createCodexRuntime, add to getGlobalRegistry (:93-102), add a harness === 'codex' arm to getRuntimeForAgent (:74-78). This bead establishes the adapter shape and the thread-id-keyed external-session-store model every later runtime method depends on.
- AC: codex.ts compiles implementing both AgentRuntimeSync and the Effect AgentRuntime; introspection methods (name, getSessionPath, getLastActivity, listSessions, isRunning) are real
- AC: getSessionPath resolves a rollout JSONL by captured thread-id under $CODEX_HOME/sessions (external store, D-7), with a unit test against a fixture rollout tree
- AC: getRuntimeForAgent('codex'-harness agent) returns the Codex runtime; the global registry includes it
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1574: Add explicit Codex arm to harness-policy so it does not inherit Pi's Anthropic-subscription block
description:
In harness-policy.ts:69-93, canUseHarnessSync returns ALLOWED for claude-code then the implicit else (commented // harness === 'pi') applies the Pi-Anthropic-subscription ToS block to ANYTHING non-claude — so Codex would wrongly inherit it. Add an explicit `if (harness === 'codex')` arm (ALLOWED; Codex+ChatGPT-subscription is fine, it is not the Pi+Anthropic+subscription case) BEFORE the Pi block, and convert the implicit else into an explicit `=== 'pi'` check. The existing gpt-5.5+api-key block in canUseModelWithAuthSync already applies to all harnesses and is correct for codex — do not change it. Add a `codex:` key to the policy decisions map in routes/settings.ts:829-830.
- AC: canUseHarnessSync('codex', …) returns ALLOWED and never applies the Pi-Anthropic-subscription ToS block
- AC: The non-claude else branch is now an explicit harness === 'pi' check; a unit test covers the codex arm
- AC: routes/settings.ts policy map returns a codex decision cell
close_reason: Closed

Keyboard accelerators while the launcher input is focused: Cmd+Enter (intent 1), Ctrl+Enter (terminal), Opt+Enter (web), Cmd+Shift+Enter (codex). On run, persist last-used agent (feeds ordering).

- AC: Search the web opens a browser pane to a search URL; Run in terminal opens a terminal pane

title: pan-1549: Launcher intent ordering (default, URL-aware, last-used memory)
description:
Implement intent ordering as a pure, unit-testable function consumed by the Launcher. Default order: claude-code (Cmd+Enter) -> terminal (Ctrl+Enter) -> web (Opt+Enter) -> codex (Cmd+Shift+Enter). When the input looks like a URL, float 'web' to position 1. Last-used-agent memory: if the user last ran a non-Claude agent, that agent takes position 1 so Cmd+Enter stays 'repeat last action'. Persist last-used-agent (localStorage).
- AC: Default text input yields claude-code, terminal, web, codex in that order
- AC: URL-shaped input floats 'web' to position 1; last-used non-Claude agent floats to position 1 otherwise
- AC: Ordering function has unit tests covering default, URL, and last-used permutations
close_reason: launcherOrdering.ts: pure orderIntents (URL->web#1, last-used non-claude agent->#1, URL precedence), isUrlLike, per-workspace last-used localStorage. Launcher consumes it. 13 tests pass; typecheck+lint clean.

title: pan-1549: Pane model + panesStore Zustand store with localStorage persistence
description:
Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/panesStore.ts` — a sibling Zustand store (modeled on `lib/commandDeckSelection.ts`, NOT part of the event-sourced `lib/store.ts`). Define `PaneType` ('home'|'agent'|'terminal'|'files'|'commits'|'plan'|'docs'|'browser'), the `WorkspacePane` interface (paneId, paneType, label, createdAt, isPermanent, plus optional conversationId/agentType/isRunning/viewMode/terminalId/fileBrowserScopePath/fileBrowserInitialQuery/docFilePath/browserInitialUrl per the PRD data model), and per-workspace state. Actions: ensureHome(workspaceId) (synthesizes an unclosable HOME pane if absent), addPane, closePane (refuses to close HOME), setActivePane, updatePane. Persist panes to `pan-panes:{workspaceId}` and active id to `pan-active-pane:{workspaceId}` with try/catch (matching the `mc-*` localStorage convention). Export selectors for a workspace's panes and active pane. This is the data-model + store-API foundation every Stage component depends on.
- AC: `PaneType` and `WorkspacePane` match the PRD data model; HOME pane has isPermanent=true and is synthesized by ensureHome when no panes exist for a workspace
- AC: closePane refuses to remove the HOME pane and never leaves activePaneId pointing at a removed pane (falls back to HOME)
- AC: panes + activePaneId round-trip through localStorage keyed by workspaceId; corrupt/absent storage degrades gracefully to a HOME-only state
- AC: Vitest unit tests cover add/close/switch, HOME-unclosable, per-workspace isolation, and persistence round-trip
close_reason: Created panesStore.ts sibling Zustand store + 18 passing unit tests. PaneType/WorkspacePane match PRD data model; ensureHome synthesizes unclosable HOME, closePane refuses HOME + repairs active id, localStorage round-trip keyed by workspaceId with graceful corrupt-storage degradation. Typecheck + lint clean.

title: pan-1456: Post the pass-3 verdict to each audited issue (REOPEN / STAYS-CLOSED / SCOPE-AMEND)
description:

For REOPEN: gh issue reopen <num> then gh issue comment <num> --body-file <result-file-or-extracted-body>. For STAYS-CLOSED: gh issue comment <num> --body-file <…> (issue stays closed). For SCOPE-AMEND: gh issue comment <num> --body-file <…>, issue stays closed. Rewrite raw screenshot URLs in the comment body to point at the feature branch (which exists after commit-screenshots) and let the merge cycle naturally promote them to main. Write docs/audit-2026-05-24/verdicts-summary.md as a table: issue | verdict | category (AC-not-met, scope-amend, ambiguous, audit-failed) | reopened (Y/N) | comment URL.

- AC: For each result file with Verdict=STAYS-CLOSED: the issue is still CLOSED AND has a new defensive comment with per-AC evidence (and screenshot links where applicable)
- AC: For each result file with Verdict=SCOPE-AMEND: the issue is still CLOSED AND has a new amendment comment naming what shipped vs the original AC
- AC: audit-failed results (no Verdict line or <500B file) are NOT acted on — they're listed in verdicts-summary.md under 'requires operator attention' and the corresponding issue is left unchanged
- AC: docs/audit-2026-05-24/verdicts-summary.md exists with rows for every audited issue plus a totals footer (REOPEN N, STAYS-CLOSED N, SCOPE-AMEND N, audit-failed N)

title: pan-1456: Run pass-3 audits in 2-concurrent waves against the remaining list
description:
Loop through remaining-list.txt in pairs. For each pair, dispatch /tmp/audit-pass3/run-one.sh <num> as two background processes; wait for both to exit; record exit code and result-file size into docs/audit-2026-05-24/progress.log. After every wave, monitor ~/.panopticon/cliproxy/cliproxy.log for refresh_token_reused — if seen, HALT and ask the operator to re-run codex login. Any result file under 500 bytes is queued for a serial retry pass at the end. Per-audit budget is the 600s timeout baked into run-one.sh. Track elapsed wall time; if it crosses 4 hours, write a progress note and continue rather than aborting.
- AC: Every issue in remaining-list.txt has a corresponding docs/audit-2026-05-24/results/PAN-<num>.md file written by the loop
- AC: Every result file is >500 bytes and contains a 'Verdict' line; any file failing this after 2 retries is recorded as 'audit-failed' but does not block the bead
- AC: Concurrency never exceeds 2 simultaneous run-one.sh processes (verifiable via timestamps in progress.log)
- AC: If CLIProxy logs refresh_token_reused during the run, the loop halts and continue.json hazards records the timestamp + remaining list
- AC: Final per-issue tally written to docs/audit-2026-05-24/progress.log: total dispatched, successful (≥500B), audit-failed, retry count

title: pan-1488: Add scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh codifying main branch protection
description:
Create scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh as an idempotent shell script that PUTs the complete desired main branch protection state to GitHub via gh api. The script must include EVERY currently-enabled protection field (see Hazard H1 in continue.json), plus the new required_pull_request_reviews block with required_approving_review_count: 1, dismiss_stale_reviews: false, require_code_owner_reviews: false. The script header docstring must state: (1) what it does, (2) that it requires admin scope on eltmon/panopticon-cli and must be run by the repo owner, (3) that Flywheel auto-merge will now block on a human approval by design (see Hazard H2). The script must be executable (git mode 0755) and use 'set -euo pipefail'. JSON body construction should be via heredoc so reviewers can read the complete protection spec inline.
- AC: File scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh exists, is executable (git mode 0755), uses '#!/usr/bin/env bash' and 'set -euo pipefail'
- AC: Script issues a single 'gh api -X PUT repos/eltmon/panopticon-cli/branches/main/protection' call with the full desired protection config as the request body (not a delta)
- AC: PUT body includes required_status_checks with strict: false and contexts ['panopticon/review', 'panopticon/test']
- AC: PUT body includes required_pull_request_reviews with required_approving_review_count: 1, dismiss_stale_reviews: false, require_code_owner_reviews: false
- AC: PUT body includes required_conversation_resolution: true, allow_force_pushes: false, allow_deletions: false, enforce_admins: false, required_linear_history: false, block_creations: false, lock_branch: false, allow_fork_syncing: false (mirrors current state to avoid silent unset by GitHub PUT replace semantics)
- AC: Script header comment explicitly states: requires admin scope on eltmon/panopticon-cli, must be run by the repo owner (not by panopticon-agent[bot]), and notes that adding required_pull_request_reviews intentionally requires a human approver on every PR per docs/FLYWHEEL-VISION.md humans-only-merge
- AC: Script is idempotent: re-running it on a repo already at the target protection state succeeds with exit code 0 and leaves the state unchanged (verify by reading current state with 'gh api repos/eltmon/panopticon-cli/branches/main/protection' after the call — required_pull_request_reviews.required_approving_review_count == 1)
- AC: Script passes shellcheck with no errors or warnings ('shellcheck scripts/apply-branch-protection.sh' exits 0)

title: pan-1242: Vitest coverage for `POST /api/issues` endpoint

Mock the tracker factory and `updateShadowState`; do not hit real GitHub/Linear/Rally APIs. Verify the `issue.statusChanged`/`issue.created` event is appended to the mocked EventStoreService.

title: pan-1242: Build `NewIssueDialog` component
description:
Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/NewIssueDialog.tsx`. Props: `{ isOpen, onClose, defaultProjectKey, targetStatus, onCreated }`. Reuses the Radix/ConfirmationDialog primitives. Fields: project picker (auto-selected from `defaultProjectKey`; shown only when more than one project is available; GitLab-only projects are excluded), required `title`, optional `description` textarea. Submit calls `POST /api/issues` with `{ projectKey, targetStatus, title, description? }`, then calls `onCreated(newIssue)` and `refreshDashboardState(queryClient)`. Show inline error if the response is non-2xx; show a loading state on the submit button while the request is in flight. ESC and the Cancel button dismiss without submitting.
- AC: Opening the dialog with `defaultProjectKey='panopticon-cli'` and `targetStatus='todo'` renders a title input, optional description textarea, a Create button (disabled while title is empty), and a Cancel button.
- AC: When more than one project is available, the dialog shows a project picker pre-selected to `defaultProjectKey`; GitLab-only projects are filtered out of the picker.
- AC: Submitting with a valid title POSTs `/api/issues`, calls `onCreated(newIssue)` once on success, and calls `refreshDashboardState(queryClient)` before closing.
- AC: A non-2xx response surfaces the server's error message inline (e.g., 'GitLab issue creation is not yet supported'); the dialog remains open so the user can retry or cancel.
- AC: ESC and Cancel close the dialog without firing the POST.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1242: Add `POST /api/issues` endpoint
description:
In `src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts`, add a new `POST /api/issues` Effect HTTP route. Body schema: `{ projectKey: string; targetStatus: 'backlog'|'todo'; title: string; description?: string }`. Resolve the project from `loadProjectsConfig()`, build a tracker via `createTrackerFromConfig(...)`, call `tracker.createIssue({ title, description, team: project.linearTeam, ... })`, then call `updateShadowState(newIssue.id, 'open', 'dashboard-new-issue', targetStatus)` so the card lands in the column the user clicked. Invalidate the appropriate `issueDataService.invalidateTracker(...)` and append an `issue.statusChanged` (or `issue.created`) event to the EventStoreService. Return the normalized `Issue` payload. Return 400 for missing/invalid body, 404 for unknown projectKey, 501 with a clear message for projects whose tracker is `gitlab` (since `GitLabTracker.createIssue` returns `NotImplementedError`).
- AC: POST `/api/issues` with valid `{projectKey,'todo',title}` returns 200 and the created `Issue` JSON; the issue appears on the next `GET /api/issues` call in the `todo` column.
- AC: POST with missing `title`, missing `projectKey`, or invalid `targetStatus` (anything other than `backlog`/`todo`) returns 400 with a structured error.
- AC: POST with an unknown `projectKey` returns 404; POST against a project whose `tracker` is `gitlab` returns 501 with an explanatory message and does not call `createIssue`.
- AC: On success, `issueDataService.invalidateTracker(<projectTrackerType>)` is invoked and an `issue.statusChanged` (or `issue.created`) event is appended to `EventStoreService` so live dashboards refresh.
- AC: Endpoint uses `execAsync` / Effect / promise-based I/O only — no `execSync`, `readFileSync`, `writeFileSync`, or other event-loop-blocking calls (per CLAUDE.md no-blocking-calls rule).
close_reason: Added POST /api/issues endpoint with validation, project-aware tracker creation, shadow-state placement, cache invalidation, and live event emission.

title: pan-1512: Integration test: full auto-promote chain end-to-end with simulated partial failure

The test exercises the full plan-finalize → /api/issues/:id/complete-planning chain WITHOUT real bd, using a mock createBeads. Scenarios: (1) Full success — assert spec file under .pan/specs/ + workspace beads counts match. (2) Partial failure (created.length === planItemCount - 1) — assert completePlanningArtifacts throws AND no .pan/specs/ file is created AND no commit is made on main. (3) Re-run after partial failure with full-success mock — assert the second call writes the spec successfully (idempotency works through the chain, not just in createBeads). (4) Re-run after full success with no-op mock — assert nothing new is written (idempotent through the orchestration layer).
- AC: Test file exists at src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/auto-promote-chain-pan1512.integration.test.ts
- AC: Scenario 1 (full success): spec file under .pan/specs/, bead count matches plan, no warnings
- AC: Scenario 2 (partial failure): no spec file at .pan/specs/, no commit, error surfaces with both counts named
- AC: Scenario 3 (re-run after partial): second call now writes spec, chain converges to success
- AC: Scenario 4 (re-run after success): no additional spec versions, no extra beads, no extra commits
- AC: Test does NOT call real bd or git push — uses mocks/stubs per existing test patterns

title: pan-1512: agents.ts /api/agents: refuse to clear+recreate beads on a non-zero count mismatch — only repair from zero state
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts (lines 2495-2556), tighten the auto-recovery branch. Currently any time beadCount !== planItemCount, hasBeads is set false and createBeadsFromVBrief is invoked, which (today) clears existing beads and recreates them — destroying partial state if recovery itself fails partway. After this change: if beadCount === 0, attempt recovery as today (now safe because B3 makes createBeads idempotent and the actual race is reduced to a no-op). If beadCount > 0 AND beadCount !== planItemCount, do NOT call createBeadsFromVBrief — return 422 with error: 'beads_count_mismatch', a descriptive message naming the counts, and hint: `pan plan ${issueId} --auto` to rerun planning end-to-end. This makes agents.ts a read-only verifier on partial state and pushes repair through the canonical planning path.
- AC: When beadCount > 0 AND beadCount !== planItemCount, /api/agents returns 422 with error code 'beads_count_mismatch' (NOT 'beads_required')
- AC: The 422 message includes both counts and the exact repair command `pan plan <ID> --auto`
- AC: When beadCount === 0, auto-recovery still runs (path is unchanged from today, just safer via B3)
- AC: New test exercises both branches: zero-beads → recovery attempted; mismatched beads → 422 with new error code

title: pan-1512: Make completePlanningArtifacts transactional: materialize beads BEFORE writing proposed spec to main
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, completePlanningArtifacts (lines 100-150): reorder so the createBeads call and the count-match guard run BEFORE the existingSpec/writeSpec/writeSpecForIssue block. Read workspaceDoc → validatePlanIssueId → createBeads → check planItemCount === created.length → THEN do the spec write. On count mismatch throw, the spec is never written, so no orphan proposed spec ends up on main. Preserve all existing behavior for the success path: spec content, filename derivation, asPanSpecDocument normalization. Keep the existing signature and return shape — completePlanningArtifacts still returns { proposed, beadCount, beadsWarning }.
- AC: createBeads runs before writeSpec / writeSpecForIssue in completePlanningArtifacts
- AC: On count mismatch, the function throws and no file under <projectPath>/.pan/specs/ is created or modified
- AC: B1's new tests now pass (GREEN)
- AC: completePlanningArtifacts return shape is unchanged: { proposed: { path, filename }, beadCount, beadsWarning }
- AC: Existing happy-path test at complete-planning.test.ts line 44 still passes with the new ordering

Match the visual style of the existing 'To re-run planning' hint already present in the `No beads tasks found` branch (line 1008).

title: pan-1512: Raise plan-finalize fetch timeouts from 30s to 120s for promotePlanning and spawnWorkAgent
description:
In src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts, change the two `setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), 30_000)` calls (lines 166 and 202) to 120_000ms. Add a code comment near each: `// Allow up to 2 minutes — bead materialization under bd-mutex serialization can take 30-60s for 10+ items on a loaded host. Raising this is bounded by the route's own internal timeouts.`. The per-bead bd create timeout (30s in beads.ts) is intentionally NOT raised — that's runaway protection per single bd invocation.
- AC: promotePlanning AbortController timeout is 120_000ms
- AC: spawnWorkAgent AbortController timeout is 120_000ms
- AC: A comment near each timeout explains the budget and why per-bead bd-create timeout is unchanged
- AC: If a test exercises this timeout path it uses vi.useFakeTimers() and vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() per the fake-timers-for-retry-tests rule

title: pan-1512: Make createBeadsFromVBrief idempotent: skip clear+recreate when existing beads already match the plan
description:
In src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts, modify createBeadsFromVBriefPromise (lines 134-393). After reading the workspace plan and BEFORE clearBeadsForIssue (line 249), call listBeadsForIssue and check whether existing beads already satisfy the plan exactly. The match criterion: (a) existing bead count === plan.items.length, (b) for every plan item, an existing bead exists whose title equals `${plan.id}: ${item.title}`, (c) every existing bead carries the issueLabel `plan.id.toLowerCase()`. If all three hold, build the beadIds map from existing beads and return { success: true, created: existingTitles, errors: [], beadIds } without clearing. If any criterion fails (extra beads, missing items, wrong titles), fall through to the legacy clear+recreate path unchanged. Add a single log line `[beads] Existing beads already match plan for ${issueLabel} — skipping clear+recreate (idempotent fast path)`.
- AC: When existing beads exactly match (count + per-item title + label), createBeadsFromVBrief returns success without invoking bd delete or bd create
- AC: When ANY plan item is missing a matching bead, function falls through to clear+recreate (legacy path)
- AC: When existing bead count exceeds plan.items.length, function falls through to clear+recreate (orphan beads must be wiped)
- AC: Idempotency match uses exact full title `${plan.id}: ${item.title}` plus issueLabel — not substring
- AC: New unit test in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts: calling createBeadsFromVBrief twice in a row on the same workspace produces no extra beads and no extra clear operations on the second call
- AC: Existing test 'three consecutive calls leave exactly planItemCount beads' (line 412) still passes
- AC: Idempotency check is inside withBdMutexPromise so it serializes with concurrent callers; no race on list+create

title: pan-804: Verify git gc would not lose anything valuable (dry-run only)

Confirm none of those would be reaped. Do NOT run actual `git gc` — the 90-day reflog grace per CLAUDE.md is the user's recovery window.

title: pan-1491: Server: weighted-ranking service + GET /api/flywheel/substrate-bug-weights
description:
Add a service `src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-bug-weights.ts` that joins open substrate bugs (from `flywheel-substrate-bugs-db.ts`) with their issue bodies+labels (via the existing GitHub issue cache or a `gh issue view --json body,labels` call), runs `parseAffectedCriteria` + `computeSubstrateBugWeight` against the latest `FlywheelStats` for the requested window, and returns `{ window, generatedAt, weights: [{ issueId, criteria, weight, reason }] }`. Wire `GET /api/flywheel/substrate-bug-weights?window=30d` in `src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts`. Reuse the existing stats caching layer; do not add a new cache.

When a substrate bug is filed by the flywheel (via `gh issue create` per `roles/flywheel.md`) and attribution data identifies one or more affected v1.0 criteria, append a `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: <ids>` trailer to the issue body before submission. If the affected criterion cannot be inferred, do nothing (no trailer is better than a wrong trailer). Coordinate with PAN-1487's trailer convention if it has merged.

title: pan-1491: Lib: computeSubstrateBugWeight(criteria, stats) → { weight, reason }
description:
Create `src/lib/flywheel-bug-weight.ts` exporting `computeSubstrateBugWeight(criteriaIds: readonly number[], stats: FlywheelStats): { weight: number; reason: string }`. For each criterion id 1..7 look up the corresponding `FlywheelStatsCriterion` (c1_bugRate, …, c7_flake). Apply: distance per criterion shape (rate/duration → `(value - target)/target`, ratio-higher-is-better → `(target - value)/target`, absolute count → `value`), clamped at zero. Status multiplier: red=3, yellow=2, green=1, insufficient_data=0. Resolve the per-criterion shape from the criterion's `value`/`target` JSON schema (rate/duration vs ratio vs count). Sum the contributions to produce `weight` (rounded to 2 decimals). Reason is a short sentence naming the top-contributing criterion, its current value, target, and status. Empty `criteriaIds` → `{ weight: 0, reason: 'no affected criteria declared' }`. Co-locate unit tests.
- AC: Bug affecting only c1 with c1.status=red, value=0.032, target=0.02 → weight ≈ (0.032-0.02)/0.02 × 3 = 1.80
- AC: Bug affecting c3 (passRate) with status=yellow, value=0.985, target=0.995 → weight ≈ (0.995-0.985)/0.995 × 2 ≈ 0.02
- AC: Bug affecting c2 (open P0s) with status=red, value=3 → weight ≈ 3 × 3 = 9 (absolute-count path)
- AC: insufficient_data criterion contributes zero even if value/target imply distance
- AC: Empty criteriaIds → `{ weight: 0, reason: 'no affected criteria declared' }` (never throws on missing telemetry)
- AC: `reason` names the top contributor by absolute contribution, formatted like `'criterion 1 (bug rate) at 3.2% vs target <2% — red'`
close_reason: Added computeSubstrateBugWeight with criterion mappings, status multipliers, distance/count paths, reason formatting, and tests for all acceptance examples; targeted test and root typecheck pass.

title: pan-1491: Lib: parseAffectedCriteria(body, labels) → number[]
description:
Create `src/lib/flywheel-affected-criteria.ts` exporting `parseAffectedCriteria(body: string, labels: readonly string[]): number[]`. Parser recognises a `Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1,5,7` trailer (case-insensitive key, comma- or space-separated list of digits 1–7) anywhere in the body, plus any GitHub label matching `/^affects-criterion-([1-7])$/`. Result is the deduped sorted union, clamped to the 1–7 range. Empty input → `[]`. Co-locate unit tests in `src/lib/flywheel-affected-criteria.test.ts`.
- AC: `parseAffectedCriteria('Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1,5', [])` → `[1, 5]`
- AC: `parseAffectedCriteria('', ['affects-criterion-3', 'affects-criterion-5'])` → `[3, 5]`
- AC: Trailer + label union: body `'Flywheel-Affects-Criterion: 1'` with labels `['affects-criterion-5']` → `[1, 5]` (sorted, deduped)
- AC: Out-of-range or non-numeric ids are dropped; e.g., trailer `'1,8,foo'` → `[1]`
- AC: Empty body + empty labels → `[]`; never throws
close_reason: Added parseAffectedCriteria for Flywheel-Affects-Criterion trailers and affects-criterion-N labels with unit tests; parser test and root typecheck pass.

title: pan-1509: fix(plan-finalize): use server-side autoSpawn; remove second POST to /api/agents
description:
In src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts, replace the current 3-step chain (createBeadsFromVBrief locally → promotePlanning → spawnWorkAgent) with a 2-step chain: createBeadsFromVBrief locally (kept — this primes the workspace bd database for the work agent that runs in the same workspace) → POST /api/issues/:id/complete-planning with body { autoSpawn: true }. Remove the separate spawnWorkAgent function (lines 198-230) and its invocation (lines 107-111). The new HTTP response includes workAgentSpawned/workAgentSession/workAgentError/workAgentSkipReason fields — surface those in the CLI console output the same way the current code does (green ✓ on spawn success, yellow ⚠ on skip with the reason, dim hint on how to retry). Increase the fetch timeout from 30s to 90s for this call since it now does spec+beads+spawn server-side. Update src/cli/commands/__tests__/plan-finalize.test.ts to assert the new single-POST behavior and absence of any direct /api/agents call.
- AC: plan-finalize.ts no longer contains a spawnWorkAgent function or a fetch to /api/agents
- AC: promotePlanning POSTs body { autoSpawn: true } (or equivalent), and the fetch timeout is 90000ms
- AC: Console output surfaces workAgentSpawned / workAgentSkipReason / workAgentError with actionable hints
- AC: --no-promote still skips both promotion and spawn (preserved opt-out)
- AC: JSON output mode preserves the workAgentSpawned / workAgentMessage / workAgentError shape so any consumer code is unaffected
- AC: __tests__/plan-finalize.test.ts asserts only one POST is made (no fetch to /api/agents) and the body includes autoSpawn
- AC: npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass
close_reason: Replaced plan-finalize's direct /api/agents POST with complete-planning autoSpawn, preserved CLI/JSON spawn output, and added single-POST regression tests.

title: pan-1509: test: end-to-end regression for plan-finalize → autoSpawn → running work agent
description:
Add a vitest integration test (likely in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/ or a new src/lib/auto-promote-chain.integration.test.ts) that simulates the full chain: (1) set up a tmp project with a synthetic issue ID and a workspace containing a minimal valid .pan/spec.vbrief.json + matching beads; (2) invoke plan-finalize programmatically (importing planFinalizeCommand) or via execAsync against a built CLI; (3) assert that AFTER the call returns: (a) the proposed spec exists at <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/, (b) bead count matches plan items, (c) workAgentSpawned=true in the response, (d) ~/.panopticon/agents/agent-<issueLower>/state.json exists with status in {starting, running} within 60s, (e) tmux session agent-<issueLower> exists on the panopticon socket. Add a NEGATIVE test for the atomicity invariant: a plan with intentionally broken beads (e.g. a malformed bd database) must result in NO proposed spec on disk. Use vi.useFakeTimers() per CLAUDE.md for any retry delays. Mark with @slow if real-wall-clock waits are required, and exclude from default run if so.
- AC: Happy-path test: plan-finalize end-to-end produces proposed spec + matching beads + running work agent within 60s
- AC: Atomicity test: deliberate bead failure leaves NO proposed spec on disk
- AC: Reconciler test: a manually-planted orphan proposed spec (no agent, beads match) is picked up by the reconciler and a work agent is spawned on the next cycle
- AC: Stack-health-rejection test: when stack-health gate rejects, the response carries workAgentSpawned=false + workAgentSkipReason='stack-unhealthy' (not a 4xx/5xx)
- AC: All tests use vi.useFakeTimers() for retry/delay logic; no real setTimeout in test code paths (per CLAUDE.md)
- AC: npm test passes from workspace root after bun install (no @slow exclusions in default run unless documented)
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1509: fix(complete-planning): defer planning-session kill until after autoSpawn completes
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, the complete-planning route schedules setTimeout(killSession, 1500) at lines ~1276-1284 unless skipKill=true. When autoSpawn=true, this 1.5s deferred kill races against the work-agent spawn (which can take >1.5s under load). Move the killSession invocation to AFTER the spawn step has resolved (success or fail). For autoSpawn=true, the kill fires immediately on the Effect's success branch (no setTimeout needed — the response has already been generated by the handler's return value). For autoSpawn=false, keep the existing 1.5s deferred kill behavior (CLI compatibility). skipKill=true still skips the kill in both cases.
- AC: When autoSpawn=true, planning-pan-<id> tmux session is killed AFTER the spawn step has resolved (success or fail), not on a 1.5s timer
- AC: When autoSpawn=false, the existing 1.5s deferred kill behavior is preserved
- AC: When skipKill=true, no kill happens (existing behavior preserved in both branches)
- AC: If the spawn throws/rejects, the planning session is STILL killed (no leaked tmux session)
- AC: Unit test in __tests__/complete-planning.test.ts asserts kill timing in both autoSpawn=true success and failure paths
close_reason: Implemented post-autoSpawn planning-session kill timing with success, failure, delayed-default, and skipKill test coverage.

title: pan-1509: feat(pan-doctor): detect orphan proposed specs and surface remediation
description:
In src/cli/commands/doctor.ts (and/or src/lib/doctor/* if checks are factored out), add a new check 'orphan-proposed-specs': for each project in projects.yaml, scan <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/ for plan.status='proposed' that have no active agent-pan-<id> session AND no state.json showing in-flight/running. Classify each orphan by reason: beads-zero (beadCount=0 — likely planning failure or disk-full), beads-mismatch (beadCount != planItemCount — partial bead creation), no-agent-no-reason (matches plan items but no agent — likely chain failure, retryable). Print actionable hints per category (e.g. 'free disk and re-run planning' for beads-zero, 'pan start <id> --host to bypass stack-health temporarily' for no-agent). The check is informational (warns, does not fail the doctor exit code unless a --strict flag is added later).
- AC: pan doctor includes a new 'orphan-proposed-specs' check that scans .pan/specs/ across all configured projects
- AC: Each orphan is classified by reason: beads-zero, beads-mismatch, no-agent-no-reason; each category has its own actionable hint
- AC: Check is informational (warn-level) and does not change pan doctor's exit code in the default invocation
- AC: Output groups orphans by project so operators can target remediation per repo
- AC: Unit test exercises the classification logic with a tmp fixture containing one orphan of each type
- AC: Wrapper skill at skills/pan-doctor/SKILL.md mentions the new check (per the SKILLS-CONVENTION rule)
- AC: npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass (including scripts/lint-skills.sh)
close_reason: Implemented orphan-proposed-specs doctor check with grouped remediation hints; focused doctor test, typecheck, and lint passed before commit 7cf29db64.

title: pan-1509: feat(deacon): reconcile orphan proposed specs (auto-spawn safety net)
description:
In src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts (or a new src/lib/cloister/orphan-proposed-reconciler.ts wired into deacon's patrol loop), add a periodic reconciler that scans <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/ for vBRIEF files with plan.status='proposed'. For each, check (a) no active agent-pan-<id> tmux session exists on the panopticon socket, (b) no ~/.panopticon/agents/agent-pan-<id>/state.json with status='starting' or 'running' exists, (c) beads count for the issue label matches plan.items.length, (d) issue is NOT paused/troubled/closed. When all four hold, the spec is an orphan — invoke the same work-agent spawn path that POST /api/agents uses (factor out the helper from server-side-autospawn bead if cleanest). On rejection (stack-unhealthy, guardrails, provider-down), log + skip — the next cycle retries. Run no more often than every 5 minutes to avoid thrash. Add a Deacon config gate (e.g. cloister.orphanProposedReconciler.enabled, default true) so operators can disable if it misbehaves.
- AC: Reconciler patrol runs on the existing Deacon cycle at no more than 1 attempt per issue per 5 minutes
- AC: Reconciler scans <projectRoot>/.pan/specs/ for plan.status='proposed' and identifies orphans (no active session, no in-flight state.json, beads match plan items, not paused/troubled/closed)
- AC: Orphan spawn uses the SAME gate/guardrail/stack-health checks as direct POST /api/agents — no bypasses
- AC: Paused, troubled, or closed issues are skipped (respects existing gates)
- AC: Reconciler emits structured activity-log events: scan-start, orphan-detected, spawn-attempt, spawn-success, spawn-skipped (with reason)
- AC: Reconciler can be disabled via config (cloister.orphanProposedReconciler.enabled=false); default is enabled
- AC: Unit test exercises orphan-detection logic against a tmp .pan/specs/ fixture with mixed states (proposed-orphan, proposed-with-agent, proposed-paused, completed)
- AC: npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass
close_reason: Implemented Deacon orphan-proposed-spec reconciler; focused tests, typecheck, lint, and fast inspection passed for commit 6ff6ad256.

title: pan-1509: feat(activity-log): structured events across plan-finalize → complete-planning → spawn chain
description:
Add emitActivityEntrySync calls (or equivalent structured-log primitives already used by these modules) at each phase boundary so future RUN-N failures are debuggable without diffing dashboard.log: (1) src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts emits 'auto-promote.phase=createBeads', 'auto-promote.phase=completePlanning', 'auto-promote.phase=done' with success/failure/reason; (2) src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts complete-planning route emits 'complete-planning.phase=specWrite', '.phase=beadsMaterialize', '.phase=autoSpawn' with the same shape; (3) src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts /api/agents handler emits 'start-agent.phase=stackHealthGate', '.phase=guardrails', '.phase=spawn' on rejection/success. All events include issueId + ISO timestamp + reason string. Source label should be 'plan-finalize' or 'complete-planning' or 'start-agent' so dashboard log filters work cleanly.
- AC: plan-finalize CLI emits a structured event at each phase boundary (createBeads start/end, completePlanning start/end, terminal)
- AC: complete-planning route emits structured events for specWrite, beadsMaterialize, autoSpawn (when applicable), terminal
- AC: POST /api/agents emits structured events at each rejection point (stack-health, guardrails) and success
- AC: All events carry issueId, ISO timestamp, reason, and a stable source label suitable for grep/filter
- AC: No logging change introduces synchronous file I/O in dashboard server code (must use emitActivityEntrySync or equivalent already-async-safe primitive)
- AC: npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1509: feat(complete-planning): autoSpawn=true spawns work agent server-side after spec+beads succeed
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, extend the POST /api/issues/:id/complete-planning route to accept autoSpawn?: boolean in the request body (default false — preserves the kanban 'Done planning' button behavior). When autoSpawn=true AND spec+beads succeeded, invoke the same work-agent spawn logic that POST /api/agents uses today (factor out the existing logic in src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts lines ~2300-2900 into a shared helper if cleanest, OR call the existing handler via an internal in-process Effect invocation, OR call the /api/agents endpoint via localhost fetch — pick the cleanest given current architecture). The response shape gains optional fields: workAgentSpawned: boolean, workAgentSession?: string, workAgentError?: string, workAgentSkipReason?: string (e.g. 'stack-unhealthy', 'guardrails', 'paused'). All existing response fields (success, newState, gitPushed, beadsWarning, message) remain backward-compatible. When autoSpawn=true and the spawn is rejected by stack-health or guardrails, return 200 (NOT an error) with workAgentSpawned: false plus the reason — the reconciler will retry on later cycles.
- AC: Request body accepts optional autoSpawn boolean; default false; field omitted from existing callers does not change behavior
- AC: When autoSpawn=true and spec+beads succeed, work agent is spawned in the same Effect; response includes workAgentSpawned=true and workAgentSession
- AC: When autoSpawn=true and stack-health rejects the spawn, response returns 200 with workAgentSpawned=false and workAgentSkipReason='stack-unhealthy' (NOT a 4xx/5xx)
- AC: When autoSpawn=true and spec+beads fail (e.g. bead mismatch), the route returns 5xx as today; no spawn attempted
- AC: When autoSpawn=false (kanban Done button), behavior is byte-for-byte identical to current main branch (regression test covers this)
- AC: Spawn reuses the same evaluateAgentStartGate + spawnGuardrails + stack-health checks as POST /api/agents — does NOT bypass any gate
- AC: New unit test in __tests__/complete-planning.test.ts asserts autoSpawn=true happy path and stack-health-rejection path
- AC: npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass
close_reason: Implemented server-side autoSpawn for complete-planning via the existing /api/agents path; focused tests, typecheck, and lint passed.

title: pan-1509: fix(complete-planning): materialize beads before writing proposed spec
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts, completePlanningArtifacts currently writes the proposed spec to disk via writeSpec/writeSpecForIssue (lines ~122-129) BEFORE running createBeadsFromVBrief (lines ~131-138), and throws on bead count mismatch (lines ~142-147). Reverse the order: run createBeadsFromVBrief first; only on full success (beadsResult.success && created.length === planItemCount) write the proposed spec. On bead failure, throw without writing the spec. Keep the existing bd-doctor retry logic inside createBeadsFromVBrief intact. Update src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/complete-planning.test.ts to assert the new ordering: a deliberate bead-creation failure must leave the spec file untouched (or absent if it never existed) on disk.
- AC: Bead creation runs before spec write in completePlanningArtifacts
- AC: If createBeadsFromVBrief returns success=false OR created.length !== planItemCount, the proposed spec is NOT written/updated on disk
- AC: Existing successful path (beads count matches plan items) still writes the proposed spec at the same .pan/specs/ path as before
- AC: Test in __tests__/complete-planning.test.ts asserts the failure case leaves no proposed spec on disk
- AC: bd-doctor retry logic inside createBeadsFromVBrief is unchanged (no edits to src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts)
- AC: npm run typecheck && npm run lint pass
close_reason: Materialized beads before writing proposed specs and added failure tests for no spec writes

title: pan-1326: Add docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md — dashboard stop verification recipe
description:
Create docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md as a reproducible playbook the human verifier can run after merge. Capture: prerequisites (a running workspace with docker containers, e.g. start a workspace via `pan start <issueId>` and confirm `docker ps` shows the panopticon-feature-<id>-{frontend,dev} containers); steps (open dashboard, identify a ship/test specialist agent for that issue OR start one if needed, click the stop button); expected before/after evidence (`docker ps` shows containers before, absent after; dashboard logs show `[agents] ✓ Stopped Docker stack for <agentId>`); failure mode to watch for (containers still visible after stop → regression). Reference commit bd7d00787 and the PAN-1326 issue body's test plan. Keep it under ~80 lines.
- AC: File docs/manual-tests/PAN-1326.md exists, is committed, and is linked from the PR description.
- AC: Recipe includes: prerequisites, numbered steps, expected before/after `docker ps` evidence, and a 'how to spot a regression' note pointing at the original symptom (specialist kill leaves containers running).
- AC: Doc references commit bd7d00787 (the fix) and the original issue test-plan item 'Manual: kill a ship specialist via dashboard, confirm `docker ps` no longer shows that issue's stack'.
close_reason: Added PAN-1326 dashboard stop manual verification recipe with docker ps before/after evidence

title: pan-1326: Extend agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts with route-level docker teardown assertions
description:
Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts with a new describe block exercising the docker-teardown branch added in commit bd7d00787. Add vi.mock declarations for the SAME specifier strings the route uses inside its dynamic imports — '../../../lib/projects.js' and '../../../lib/lifecycle/archive-planning.js' — and for '../../../../lib/workspace-manager.js' (stopWorkspaceDocker). Add subItem assertions matching the three behaviors below. Reuse the existing runAgentStopHandler helper. The dynamic `await import(...)` inside the route's Effect.promise resolves through vitest's module registry, so static vi.mock declarations DO intercept it as long as the specifiers match exactly.
- AC: Specialist case: getAgentStateAsync resolves to { issueId: 'PAN-1052', role: 'ship', workspace: undefined }. Running the stop handler calls resolveProjectFromIssue('PAN-1052'), findWorkspacePath(projectPath, 'pan-1052'), and stopWorkspaceDocker(<findWorkspacePath's return>, 'pan-1052'). state.workspace's absence does NOT skip docker teardown.

- AC: Guard case: getAgentStateAsync resolves to { issueId: undefined, ... }. stopWorkspaceDocker is NOT called. findWorkspacePath is NOT called either.
- AC: `npm test -- agents-lifecycle-event.test.ts` passes. Existing PAN-1221 F1 lifecycle-event assertions still pass (no regression on the prior test cases).
close_reason: Extended stop handler route tests for specialist, work-agent resolver, and no-issueId docker teardown behavior

title: pan-1316: Tear down workspace Docker stack on `pan pause`
description:
In src/cli/commands/pause.ts, after the existing `if (shouldStop) { stopAgent(agentId); }` block (line 24-26), add a stopWorkspaceDocker call gated on the same `shouldStop` boolean. Mirror the exact shape from src/cli/commands/kill.ts:65-74 (the round-1 fix): try/catch, chalk.gray summary log on success when containers were found, chalk.yellow warning on failure. Import stopWorkspaceDocker from '../../lib/workspace-manager.js'. Use state.workspace and state.issueId.toLowerCase(). Read state.workspace/state.issueId from the existing `state` variable already in scope (line 13). Add a unit test in src/cli/commands/__tests__/ that mocks both '../../lib/agents.js' and '../../lib/workspace-manager.js' and asserts stopWorkspaceDocker is called when shouldStop is true and not called when shouldStop is false.

title: pan-1326: Add kill.test.ts covering specialist and work-agent docker teardown
description:
Create src/cli/commands/__tests__/kill.test.ts. Mock '../../../lib/agents.js' (stopAgent, getAgentState), '../../../lib/tmux.js' (sessionExists), '../../../lib/remote/index.js' (isRemoteAvailable -> { available: false }), '../../../lib/workspace-manager.js' (stopWorkspaceDocker), '../../../lib/projects.js' (resolveProjectFromIssue), and '../../../lib/lifecycle/archive-planning.js' (findWorkspacePath). Follow the close.test.ts vi.hoisted + vi.mock + late-import pattern so killCommand binds to the mocks. Stub process.exit to prevent test-process abort. Cover three behaviors as acceptance subItems below. Tests must run under the default `npm test` config and pass without using real timers (no retries / no backoff are exercised here, so vi.useFakeTimers is not required, but no real setTimeout/sleep calls either).
- AC: Specialist case: getAgentState returns { issueId: 'PAN-1052', role: 'ship', workspace: undefined }. killCommand('agent-pan-1052-ship', {}) calls findWorkspacePath(projectPath, 'pan-1052') and stopWorkspaceDocker(<findWorkspacePath's return value>, 'pan-1052'). state.workspace's absence does NOT skip docker teardown.

Asserts on the mock's call arguments, not on side effects.
- AC: Guard case: getAgentState returns { issueId: undefined, ... }. stopWorkspaceDocker is NOT called at all (the issueId guard short-circuits the resolver chain). findWorkspacePath also not called.
- AC: `npm test -- kill.test.ts` passes locally. No real setTimeout / sleep calls. process.exit is stubbed so failures surface as expect failures, not test-process aborts.
close_reason: Added killCommand docker teardown regression tests for specialist, work-agent resolver, and no-issueId guard

description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts (postAgentPauseRoute, around line 1664-1700), after the existing block that captures output, kills the session, and marks the runtime stopped (conditional on `hasLiveSession`), add a stopWorkspaceDocker call gated on the same `stoppedByPause` boolean already computed at line 1644. title: pan-1450: PAN-1173 follow-through: Apply resolveBareNumericIdSync to 9 remaining CLI commands
description:
PAN-1173 only fixed `pan show`. Same bug class still afflicts kill, pause, unpause, untroubled, reopen, close, inspect, open, and review-restart — each calls either resolveIssueIdSync (which doesn't resolve bare numbers) or resolveProjectFromIssueSync directly with raw input. Add a single test file (suggested: src/cli/commands/__tests__/bare-numeric-id-resolution.test.ts) that, for each of the nine commands, mocks resolveBareNumericIdSync to return 'PAN-9999' for input '9999' and asserts the command proceeds with 'PAN-9999'. title: pan-1450: PAN-1111: Add REAL DB-corruption integration test (replace mock theater)
description:
Add tests/integration/beads-real-corruption.test.ts. Skip the test if the bd binary is not on PATH (test.skipIf). Inside: (1) mkdtempSync a temp project, (2) shell out to real bd init --prefix tst, (3) corrupt the Dolt manifest — pick the lowest-effort reproducible corruption: deleting or truncating one of the .beads/dolt/*manifest* files. (4) Call createBeadsFromVBrief() against the workspace with a tiny in-memory vBRIEF, (5) assert success: result.success === true, result.created length matches plan.items length, no spurious bd init was invoked (the workspace already had a .beads/ — recovery must reuse it). DO NOT modify the existing mocked test in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts — it exercises the wrapper's retry orchestration, which has independent value. ALSO verify (separately within the same test, with a second describe block if cleaner): the AC #7 5-cycle bake. Run the create/ping/list cycle five times in a row against the same recovered workspace; assert no cycle re-triggers bd doctor --fix (i.e., corruption was actually fixed on cycle 1, not papered over). If the 5-cycle bake reveals that the wrapper still invokes bd doctor --fix on every cycle, document that finding in the test failure message and let the test fail — the bake's job is to surface that exact behavior.
- AC: New file tests/integration/beads-real-corruption.test.ts exists and is picked up by the existing vitest include glob.
- AC: Test gates on `commandExistsSync('bd')` (or equivalent) via test.skipIf — no flake when bd is absent.
- AC: Test uses the REAL bd binary (no execAsync mock) and a REAL temp .beads/ directory, then corrupts a real manifest file before exercising createBeadsFromVBrief.
- AC: Success path asserts result.success === true and result.created.length === plan.items.length.
- AC: 5-cycle bake: same workspace + same plan, run create/ping/list five times; assert bd doctor --fix is invoked AT MOST ONCE across the five cycles (recovery is sticky, not per-cycle).
- AC: Existing mocked test at src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts:219 is NOT removed — both tests coexist with non-overlapping concerns.
- AC: Temp directory is cleaned up in afterAll/afterEach so the test leaves no residue under tests/integration/.
close_reason: Added real bd corruption integration coverage with a five-cycle sticky recovery bake.

title: pan-1450: PAN-1168: Add regression test for Nous /v1/models probe path

Add src/lib/__tests__/provider-health.test.ts (new file). Mock global.fetch (vi.stubGlobal('fetch', ...)). Cases: (a) provider.name === 'nous' → fetch is called with method='GET' against `${baseUrl}/v1/models` (NOT against /v1/messages), and the response 200 yields { ok: true }, (b) provider.name === 'nous' + 401 response → returns the auth-failure classification, (c) provider.name === 'anthropic' (or any non-nous) → fetch is called with method='POST' against the messages endpoint (regression guard: the /v1/models swap MUST be Nous-only). title: pan-1450: PAN-1173: Add unit tests for resolveBareNumericIdSync
description:
Extend tests/lib/issue-id.test.ts with a new describe('resolveBareNumericIdSync (PAN-1173 regression)') block. Use mkdtempSync to create a temp dir, populate agent-state subdirectories with valid state.json files, and pass the temp dir as the panopticonHome argument. Cases: (a) no matching agent dir → returns null, (b) multiple matching agent dirs (e.g., PAN-3 and MIN-3 both present, both with valid state.json) → returns null (ambiguous), (c) exactly one matching agent dir → returns the fully-prefixed ID from state.json, (d) input is already prefixed (PAN-3, agent-pan-3, F29698) → delegates to resolveIssueIdSync and returns the canonical ID without filesystem probing, (e) input is bare digits but no AGENTS_DIR exists → returns null (no throw).
- AC: Test (a) zero-match returns null.
- AC: Test (b) multiple-match returns null (ambiguous case).
- AC: Test (c) single-match returns the fully-prefixed ID from state.json.
- AC: Test (d) already-prefixed input delegates to resolveIssueIdSync without touching the filesystem.
- AC: Test (e) missing AGENTS_DIR returns null without throwing.

title: pan-1450: PAN-1175: Add regression test for resolveParentFeatureBranch host-context resolution
description:
Extend src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm.test.ts with a focused describe('resolveParentFeatureBranch (PAN-1175 regression)') block. resolveParentFeatureBranch is currently a local async function in src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts:2016. Either (a) export it from swarm.ts for direct testing, OR (b) test it via createSlotWorktree's branch-resolution path with mocked git output. Prefer (a) for clarity. Cases: (i) localList contains 'feature/pan-123' → returns 'feature/pan-123', (ii) only remoteList contains 'origin/feature/pan-123' → returns 'feature/pan-123', (iii) localList contains numeric-suffix-only 'feature/123' → returns 'feature/123', (iv) neither list contains a candidate → throws with the documented error message naming both candidates. The point is to prove the function works against host-context (bare main, not in a workspace) — which is the entire reason for PAN-1175.
- AC: resolveParentFeatureBranch is reachable from the test (either exported or covered indirectly via createSlotWorktree with mocked git).
