title: pan-1450: PAN-1256: Add regression test for recoverOrphanedAgents starting-status grace window
description:
Add a new test file under src/lib/cloister/__tests__/ (suggested: recover-orphaned-agents-grace.test.ts) covering src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:5073-5087. Use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.setSystemTime() to control Date.now(). Construct a fixture state.json with status='starting' and stub startedAt to a known ISO timestamp; verify (a) within WORK_LAUNCHER_GRACE_MS (120s) the agent is NOT marked stopped, (b) past 120s with no tmux session the agent IS marked stopped, (c) status='running' with no tmux is still marked stopped immediately (no grace — regression guard so the fix doesn't accidentally extend grace to running). If mocking recoverOrphanedAgents end-to-end is impractical, extract a pure helper `isStartingWithinGrace(state, now, graceMs)` into a new src/lib/cloister/agent-grace.ts module and test that directly. Either approach is acceptable; choose the path with the smaller mock surface.
- AC: Test (a): status='starting' + startedAt within 120s grace window → agent state is NOT mutated to 'stopped'.
- AC: Test (b): status='starting' + startedAt past 120s grace → agent IS marked stopped.
- AC: Test (c): status='running' + no tmux session → marked stopped immediately, regardless of startedAt (regression guard against grace creeping into running status).
- AC: Tests use vi.useFakeTimers/vi.setSystemTime — zero real wall-clock waits. afterEach restores real timers.
- AC: If a pure helper was extracted, the helper's home is src/lib/cloister/ (not src/lib/) and the deacon.ts call site delegates to it (no behavioral change in production).
close_reason: Added fake-timer coverage for starting-agent orphan recovery grace.

title: pan-1450: PAN-1326: Add regression test for `pan kill` workspace Docker stack teardown
description:
Extend src/cli/commands/__tests__/operator-interventions.test.ts (or add a co-located test file) with two cases covering the PAN-1326 fix in src/cli/commands/kill.ts:76-91. (a) `pan kill <ship-specialist-agent-id>` resolves the workspace via state.issueId (NOT the agent's own state.workspace) and calls stopWorkspaceDocker against that resolved path. (b) `pan kill <work-agent-id>` (regression case for PAN-1316) also tears down the workspace stack. Both cases should mock resolveProjectFromIssueSync, findWorkspacePath, and stopWorkspaceDocker, and assert (1) stopWorkspaceDocker was called exactly once, (2) it was called with the workspace path findWorkspacePath returned for state.issueId, (3) the success branch logs the docker steps.
- AC: New test asserts that killing a ship-specialist agent invokes stopWorkspaceDocker against the workspace resolved from state.issueId (not state.workspace).
- AC: New regression test asserts that killing a work agent (the PAN-1316 case) also invokes stopWorkspaceDocker.
- AC: Tests run under the existing `npm test` invocation without changing pool config or maxForks.
- AC: `grep -rn 'PAN-1326' src/ --include='*.test.ts'` returns at least one hit after the change (the test file references the issue in a comment or describe label).
close_reason: Added PAN-1326 regression tests for pan kill Docker teardown on ship-specialist and work-agent kills

title: pan-1499: Wire deferral gate into pan close with --allow-unlinked-deferral override
description:
In src/cli/commands/close.ts, add allowUnlinkedDeferral?: string to CloseOutOptions (line ~21-24). After the existing canonical-state read at line 112-120 and BEFORE the y/N confirmation prompt at line 122-156, add the deferral gate: (1) fetch the most-recent agent-authored comment for the issue via gh issue view <number> --repo <owner>/<repo> --json comments --jq '.comments[-5:]' (last 5, then filter to those whose author login matches the panopticon-agent bot pattern). If the fetch errors, emit an activity 'warn' event and FALL THROUGH (do not hard-block — pan close is human-gated and we don't want a tracker fetch failure to break close-out); (2) load the AC list from the workspace if it still exists (findPlan() handles main-side resolution after teardown for cross-workspace cases), otherwise pass acList: null; (3) call detectDeferralLanguage({ commentBody: latestAgentComment ?? null, acList }); (4) if result.shouldBlock === true AND !options.allowUnlinkedDeferral, console.error the detected phrases and process.exit(1) with the same override instructions as pan done; (5) if options.allowUnlinkedDeferral is provided, emit warn-level activity 'Close-out deferral gate bypassed: ${reason}'. Add --allow-unlinked-deferral <reason> as a CLI option in src/cli/index.ts on the pan close command at line 493-497. The existing --force flag on close stays as-is (skip confirmation prompt only) and does NOT bypass the deferral gate. Tests: extend src/cli/commands/__tests__/close.test.ts: (a) latest agent comment contains deferral phrase, no follow-up link → exit 1; (b) deferred comment + linked follow-up → passes; (c) deferred comment + --allow-unlinked-deferral 'reason' → passes with warn activity; (d) clean comment → passes; (e) gh fetch error → warn activity, falls through to confirmation prompt (does not block); (f) --force alone → does NOT bypass the deferral gate. Mock execFileAsync for gh calls and the activity logger.
- AC: CloseOutOptions interface (close.ts) gains allowUnlinkedDeferral?: string field
- AC: src/cli/index.ts wires --allow-unlinked-deferral <reason> as a string CLI option on pan close
- AC: Deferral gate runs after canonical-state read and before y/N confirmation in closeOutCommand
- AC: Latest agent comment containing deferral phrase with no follow-up link causes exit 1
- AC: Linked follow-up issue in the agent comment passes the gate without override
- AC: --allow-unlinked-deferral "<reason>" bypasses the gate and emits a warn-level activity event containing the reason
- AC: gh comment fetch failure falls through with a warn activity (does NOT hard-block close-out)
- AC: --force alone does NOT bypass the deferral gate on pan close
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and src/cli/commands/__tests__/close.test.ts pass

title: pan-1499: Wire deferral gate into pan done with --allow-unlinked-deferral override
description:
In src/cli/commands/done.ts, add allowUnlinkedDeferral?: string to the DoneOptions interface (line ~24-35). After the existing preflight block at line 260-306, and BEFORE the rebase step at line 310, add the deferral gate: (1) skip entirely if options.strike is true (strike role short-circuits at line 192-201 anyway — the gate sits after preflight, so strike never reaches it; double-check ordering); (2) load AC list via extractAcceptanceCriteriaSync from src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts using the workspace path resolved from agent state; (3) call detectDeferralLanguage({ commentBody: options.comment ?? null, acList }); (4) if result.shouldBlock === true AND !options.allowUnlinkedDeferral, exit 1 with a chalk.red message listing the detected phrases, instructing the operator to either link a follow-up issue (PAN-NNNN or github URL), amend the AC list, or pass --allow-unlinked-deferral "<reason>"; (5) if options.allowUnlinkedDeferral is provided, emit an activity event source: 'pan-done', level: 'warn', message: `Deferral gate bypassed via --allow-unlinked-deferral: ${reason}` so the audit trail captures the override and reason; (6) if shouldBlock === false, emit a debug-level activity event noting the gate passed (so audits can also see legitimate close-outs were evaluated). Add --allow-unlinked-deferral <reason> as a CLI option in src/cli/index.ts on the pan done command at line 458-464 (description: 'Bypass deferral gate with required reason — logged to audit trail'). --force does NOT bypass the deferral gate (autoDecision 4); the existing --force preflight bypass at line 204+ stays unchanged but the deferral gate sits in its own block that only respects --allow-unlinked-deferral. Tests: extend tests/cli/commands/work/done.test.ts (or add tests/cli/commands/work/done-deferral-gate.test.ts) with: (a) deferred comment + no follow-up link → process exits 1, activity event emitted; (b) deferred comment + 'follow up in PAN-9999' → passes; (c) deferred comment + amended AC list (one item status=cancelled) → passes; (d) clean comment + clean AC list → passes; (e) --allow-unlinked-deferral 'AC amended in a way the regex missed' → passes, activity 'warn' event emitted with the reason; (f) --strike + deferred comment → passes (strike exemption); (g) --force alone does NOT bypass (must combine with --allow-unlinked-deferral or remove deferral language). Mock the activity logger and process.exit in tests.
- AC: DoneOptions interface (done.ts) gains allowUnlinkedDeferral?: string field
- AC: src/cli/index.ts wires --allow-unlinked-deferral <reason> as a string CLI option on pan done
- AC: Deferral gate runs after preflight and before rebase in doneCommand; deferred-and-unlinked invocation exits 1 with phrase list
- AC: Linked follow-up (PAN-NNNN, cross-tracker, or github URL) passes the gate without override
- AC: Amended AC list (item status cancelled/deferred or metadata.deferred or ~~title~~) passes the gate without override
- AC: --allow-unlinked-deferral "<reason>" bypasses the gate and emits a warn-level activity event containing the reason string
- AC: --force alone does NOT bypass the deferral gate — verified by a dedicated test
- AC: --strike short-circuits before the deferral gate — strike + deferred comment passes
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the relevant test file pass under vitest

title: pan-1499: Add detectDeferralLanguage helper at src/lib/close-out/deferral-detection.ts with corpus tests
description:
Create src/lib/close-out/deferral-detection.ts exporting a pure function detectDeferralLanguage(input: { commentBody: string | null | undefined; acList: VBriefSubItem[] | null | undefined }) returning DeferralDetectionResult: { suspectedPhrases: Array<{ phrase: string; context: string }>; hasFollowUpLink: boolean; followUpLinks: string[]; acAmendments: Array<{ itemId: string; reason: 'cancelled' | 'deferred-status' | 'metadata-deferred' | 'strikethrough' }>; shouldBlock: boolean }. shouldBlock is true iff suspectedPhrases.length > 0 AND hasFollowUpLink === false AND acAmendments.length === 0. Phrase corpus (case-insensitive, word-boundary): 'deferred', 'punt', 'punted', 'follow-up', 'follow up' (with hyphen optional), 'out of scope', 'did not deliver', 'didn\'t deliver', 'will come later', 'partial', 'TODO:'. Follow-up link regexes: /[A-Z]+-\d+/g (any tracker prefix) and /https?:\/\/github\.com\/[\w.-]+\/[\w.-]+\/issues\/\d+/g. AC amendment detection reuses isDeferredOrCancelledItem from src/lib/vbrief/acceptance-criteria.ts plus a strikethrough check (title matches /~~.+~~/). Also scan AC item titles for the phrase corpus (issue body: 'TODO: near an AC item'). Write unit tests in src/lib/close-out/__tests__/deferral-detection.test.ts: (a) synthetic positive cases for each phrase, (b) synthetic negative cases (e.g. 'partial credit' should match — that's how the audit catches false-honest agents — but 'partially indexed by Google' must be flagged too, since substrate is a tripwire not a semantic classifier — record this conservatism in a JSDoc note on the helper), (c) follow-up link extraction across all supported prefixes (PAN-, MIN-, KRUX-) and GitHub URL form, (d) AC amendment detection via status and metadata.deferred and ~~strikethrough~~, (e) corpus regression: include verbatim close-out comment excerpts from PAN-829, PAN-1158, PAN-1146, PAN-1249 (fetch with gh issue view --comments at implementation time) and assert shouldBlock === true on each. Helper is pure (no I/O, no fs, no exec) so it remains trivially unit-testable.
- AC: src/lib/close-out/deferral-detection.ts exists and exports detectDeferralLanguage and DeferralDetectionResult
- AC: Helper detects every phrase in the documented corpus on a synthetic comment containing that phrase
- AC: Helper extracts PAN-NNNN, MIN-NNNN, KRUX-NNNN, and https://github.com/.../issues/NNNN as follow-up links
- AC: Helper recognizes AC amendments via status in {cancelled, deferred}, metadata.deferred === true, and ~~strikethrough~~ titles
- AC: shouldBlock === true iff at least one suspected phrase AND no follow-up link AND no AC amendment
- AC: Corpus regression test: verbatim excerpts from PAN-829, PAN-1158, PAN-1146, PAN-1249 close-out comments are each detected (shouldBlock === true)
- AC: Helper is pure (no fs, no exec, no network) — verified by import-side scan in the test file
- AC: npm run typecheck and the new test file pass under vitest

title: pan-1498: End-to-end test: missing-trace requirements output produces REVIEWER_FAILED with the validator's reason
description:
Add tests/unit/cloister/review-requirements-trace-e2e.test.ts that exercises the full signal contract without spawning a real claude:

  1. Generate a requirements sub-role launcher script via buildReviewSubRoleCommand (subRole: 'requirements', synthesisAgentId: 'agent-pan-XX-review', outputPath: <tmpdir>/requirements.md, etc.).
  2. Pre-place a missing-trace fixture markdown at outputPath.
  3. Run the generated script in a real bash shell against a stubbed `claude` that immediately exits 0 (so the script proceeds to the validator step) and a stubbed `pan` that captures the message it was told.
  4. Assert the captured `pan tell` argument starts with `REVIEWER_FAILED requirements requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs:`.
  5. Repeat with a happy-path fixture and assert `REVIEWER_READY requirements <out>`.

This closes the substrate contract end-to-end. Test should use real fs / real bash but stub out `claude` and `pan` via PATH manipulation in a temp dir (no network, no real agents). Keep it under 5s wall-clock — no real timeouts, no `sleep`.
- AC: Test drives the actual generated launcher script via bash and asserts the exact `pan tell` message for both pass and fail fixtures
- AC: Test isolates `claude` and `pan` via a temp PATH directory containing executable stubs — no real Anthropic API call, no real `pan` invocation, no real tmux
- AC: Test runs in under 5 seconds wall-clock and uses no real `sleep` / no real `timeout` triggers — the stub claude exits immediately
- AC: Test additionally covers the fallback path: when the temp PATH has no `pan` binary at all, the launcher signals REVIEWER_READY and the test asserts the warning line is present on the script's stderr
close_reason: Added end-to-end test driving the generated requirements launcher script with stubbed claude/pan binaries; pass, fail, and fallback paths asserted; all tests pass.

title: pan-1498: Add 'pan review validate-trace' CLI shim and wire it into the requirements sub-role launcher
description:
Two parts, both in this bead because they only make sense shipped together:

(A) CLI shim — add src/cli/commands/review-validate-trace.ts implementing 'pan review validate-trace <outputFile>':
  - Reads outputFile from disk (fs/promises — never sync).
  - Calls validateRequirementsTrace(content).
  - On ok: process.exit(0).
  - On !ok: writes result.reason to stderr (one line, no chalk colors), process.exit(1).
  - Register the subcommand under the existing `pan review` group in src/cli/index.ts (alongside review-spawn-reviewer).
  - Unit test: invoke handler with both a passing fixture and a failing fixture, assert exit code and stderr output (use Vitest's process.exit / console mocking, not a real spawn).

(B) Launcher wiring — modify src/lib/launcher-generator.ts buildReviewSubRoleCommand so that when config.reviewSignal.subRole === 'requirements' the generated script becomes:

  echo $$ > <pidFile>
  timeout <N> <claude...>
  CLAUDE_EXIT=$?
  if [ "$CLAUDE_EXIT" = "124" ]; then
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_TIMEOUT requirements reviewer exceeded <N>s deadline" || true
  elif [ ! -s '<out>' ]; then
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_FAILED requirements reviewer exited (code $CLAUDE_EXIT) without writing report" || true
  elif command -v pan >/dev/null 2>&1 && pan review validate-trace '<out>' 2>/tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$; then
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_READY requirements <out>" || true
  elif [ -f /tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$ ]; then
    REASON=$(cat /tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$)
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_FAILED requirements $REASON" || true
  else
    # Validator CLI not installed on this worker — fallback to legacy file-present signal
    pan tell <synth> "REVIEWER_READY requirements <out>" || true
    echo '[review-requirements] WARNING: pan review validate-trace unavailable on this worker — substrate trace check skipped' >&2
  fi
  rm -f /tmp/pan-trace-reason.$$
  touch <signalMarker>
  rm -f <pidFile>

The security/correctness/performance sub-roles MUST continue to use the existing buildReviewSubRoleCommand output byte-for-byte. Use a small branch (if subRole === 'requirements' return buildRequirementsLauncher(...); else return buildLegacyLauncher(...)) so the other three sub-roles' generated scripts are demonstrably unchanged.

Tests:
  - Extend src/lib/__tests__/launcher-generator.test.ts with two new cases: 'requirements sub-role generates validator-gated signal script' (snapshot/text-contains assertions for `pan review validate-trace`) and 'non-requirements sub-roles unchanged' (snapshot of security script identical to pre-change baseline).
  - The existing reviewSignal tests at lines 173 / 615 of that file stay green.
- AC: `pan review validate-trace <file>` exists as a subcommand under the `pan review` group, exits 0 on validator pass, exits 1 with the validator's one-line reason on stderr on fail
- AC: Unit test for the CLI shim exercises both pass and fail paths and asserts both exit code and stderr content
- AC: buildReviewSubRoleCommand emits a validator-gated signal sequence ONLY when reviewSignal.subRole === 'requirements'; the generated script invokes `pan review validate-trace` and signals REVIEWER_READY only on its exit-0
- AC: On validator failure the generated script signals `REVIEWER_FAILED requirements <reason>` with the validator's stderr verbatim (no LLM in the loop)
- AC: If `pan review validate-trace` is not on PATH on the worker, the script falls back to the legacy 'file present → REVIEWER_READY' contract and writes a single-line warning to stderr — proven by a launcher-generator test asserting the fallback branch exists
- AC: Launcher-generator snapshot tests for security/correctness/performance sub-roles remain byte-identical to the pre-change baseline (proves no regression in the other three reviewers)
- AC: No use of execSync / readFileSync / writeFileSync in the new CLI shim or any code path it imports — fs/promises and async exec only (per project rule)
close_reason: Added pan review validate-trace CLI shim, wired validator into requirements sub-role launcher with fallback, added unit tests; all pass.

title: pan-1498: Implement pure-function requirements-trace validator with fixture-driven unit tests
description:
Add src/lib/cloister/review-requirements-validator.ts exporting:

  export interface RequirementsTraceValidationResult {
    ok: boolean;
    /** Titles of in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial ACs missing a usable trace */
    missingTraces: string[];
    /** Human-readable one-line reason for REVIEWER_FAILED; empty when ok */
    reason: string;
  }
  export function validateRequirementsTrace(markdown: string): RequirementsTraceValidationResult

The validator parses the reviewer's markdown:
  1. Walks the Coverage Matrix table to enumerate ACs and their (scope, status) tuple.
  2. Builds the required-trace set: ACs where status ∈ {Implemented, Partial} AND scope === 'in_pr_scope'.
  3. Locates the '## Live Code Path Traces' section (case-sensitive, anchored at line start).
  4. For each '### AC:' block under that section, extracts the **File:** line and validates that its value is a backticked token matching /^`\S+\.[A-Za-z0-9]+:\d+`$/ (path with extension and colon-line-number).
  5. Cross-references the trace blocks against the required-trace set. Any required AC that lacks a matching block, or has a block whose **File:** doesn't match the regex, goes into missingTraces.
  6. If the required-trace set is empty AND the section body is exactly the canonical 'None — no in_pr_scope ACs claimed Implemented or Partial.' sentinel, ok=true. If the required set is empty but the section is missing entirely, ok=false (because the substrate cannot distinguish 'reviewer addressed it' from 'reviewer forgot it').

The failure reason is 'requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs: <comma-separated titles>' (truncate to 240 chars to fit a `pan tell` payload comfortably).

Tests (src/lib/cloister/__tests__/review-requirements-validator.test.ts) use markdown fixtures under tests/fixtures/review-requirements/:
  - happy-path.md: two in_pr_scope Implemented ACs, both with valid traces → ok
  - whole-feature-only.md: one whole_feature_scope Implemented AC, sentinel body in traces section → ok
  - missing-section.md: section absent entirely → fail, reason names both ACs
  - missing-one-ac.md: two required, only one has a trace block → fail, reason names the missing one
  - bad-file-format.md: trace present but **File:** value is 'the dropdown' or a bare path with no extension/line → fail, reason names the AC
  - sentinel-when-not-needed.md: zero qualifying ACs, sentinel body present → ok
  - sentinel-when-needed.md: one qualifying AC but body is the sentinel → fail, reason names the AC
  - windows-path.md: trace **File:** value uses backslashes → fail (path regex rejects), reason names the AC
  - multiple-paths-one-block.md: a single block has two backticked paths, first valid → ok
  - case-variation.md: section header is '## live code path traces' lowercase → fail (case-sensitive contract)

The validator must be pure (no I/O, no process.cwd). The shim in bead 3 owns I/O.
- AC: validateRequirementsTrace exists with the signature above and is pure (no fs/exec)
- AC: All ten fixture cases above pass; each fixture lives as a separate .md file under tests/fixtures/review-requirements/ so future fixtures can be added without editing the test file
- AC: Failure reason starts with 'requirements review missing live code path trace for ACs:' and lists at least one AC title from the missingTraces array; reason is truncated to ≤240 chars
- AC: When the Coverage Matrix has zero in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial ACs AND the canonical sentinel body appears under '## Live Code Path Traces', the validator returns ok=true (this avoids blocking reviews that legitimately have nothing to trace)
- AC: When the Coverage Matrix has zero qualifying ACs but the section is missing entirely, the validator returns ok=false — the substrate cannot tell 'addressed' apart from 'forgotten' and must fail closed
- AC: The **File:** regex requires a non-empty path with at least one '.<ext>:<line>' suffix and rejects bare prose / Windows backslashes / missing line numbers (covered by bad-file-format.md and windows-path.md fixtures)
close_reason: Implemented pure-function validateRequirementsTrace with 10 fixture-driven unit tests; all pass.

title: pan-1498: Extend review-requirements.md to require a per-AC live code path trace section
description:
Edit roles/review-requirements.md to add a new mandatory output section '## Live Code Path Traces'. For every AC the reviewer marks Implemented or Partial AND classified as Scope: in_pr_scope, the reviewer MUST emit a '### AC: <ac title>' block containing exactly these bolded-field lines:

  **Scope:** in_pr_scope
  **File:** `path/to/file.ts:42`
  **Function:** `functionName`
  **Path:** <one-paragraph description of how user input reaches this function>

The section MUST appear between '## Findings' and '## Non-blocking Notes'. The reviewer MUST also include the section even when there are zero in-PR-scope Implemented/Partial ACs — with the body 'None — no in_pr_scope ACs claimed Implemented or Partial.' so the parser can distinguish 'reviewer addressed the requirement' from 'reviewer forgot the section'.

Update the existing 'Output format' template in the same file. Make the requirement contractual: 'If this section is missing or any required AC trace omits a `**File:** \`path:line\`` line, the launcher will signal REVIEWER_FAILED — the synthesis agent will not see your report as passing.' Also drop one sentence into roles/review.md §step 6 noting that the requirements reviewer's output now includes a Live Code Path Traces section (so the synthesis agent can quote it in its synthesis report). Keep the change additive — do not remove existing sections.
- AC: roles/review-requirements.md contains a 'Live Code Path Traces' subsection inside the Output format block, with a verbatim example showing **File:**, **Function:**, and **Path:** lines
- AC: The prompt explicitly states that the section is required for every in_pr_scope Implemented/Partial AC, and that whole_feature_scope / pre_existing / Missing / Not-applicable ACs do NOT require a trace
- AC: The prompt explicitly tells the reviewer that the section MUST be present even with zero qualifying ACs, with the literal body 'None — no in_pr_scope ACs claimed Implemented or Partial.'
- AC: The prompt states that omitting the section or any required `**File:** \`path:line\`` line causes the launcher to signal REVIEWER_FAILED (so the reviewer understands enforcement is mechanical, not LLM-judged)
- AC: roles/review.md §6 Output template references the new Live Code Path Traces section as something the synthesis report should quote when summarizing requirements coverage; no existing sections are removed
- AC: Snapshot test (tests/unit covering roles/) asserts the presence of the 'Live Code Path Traces' header and the required field tokens (**File:**, **Function:**, **Path:**) in roles/review-requirements.md
close_reason: Extended roles/review-requirements.md with mandatory Live Code Path Traces section, updated roles/review.md §6, added snapshot test; all tests pass.

title: pan-1500: End-to-end audit-example fixtures (PAN-1389 and PAN-1231) prove the full pipeline flags both
description:
Add a test file (src/lib/cloister/__tests__/lint-stub-ui-audit-examples.test.ts) that materializes two named git-fixture workspaces and runs buildReviewContext end-to-end against them: (1) 'pan-1389-files-tab': seeds a baseline commit then adds frontend/src/components/Inspector/FilesTab.tsx whose useFiles hook returns [] and frontend/src/components/Inspector/CommentsTab.tsx whose useComments hook returns [], asserts the resulting manifest's stubUiFindings array contains entries for both files, AND asserts the Tier-1 summary string mentions both. (2) 'pan-1231-fleet-modes': seeds a baseline then adds frontend/src/components/Fleet/FleetAgentsView.tsx wiring a SegmentedControl with TableMode and TimelineMode entries whose onChange handlers are no-op arrow functions, asserts findings for both mode entries. These fixtures lock in the PAN-1454 audit's stated examples as regression coverage — if a future edit weakens the scanner or the manifest wiring, these tests fail by name and the bisect points straight at PAN-1500. Tests are described with the relevant audit example so failures are self-explanatory.
- AC: PAN-1389 fixture test passes: a workspace adding stub FilesTab + CommentsTab produces manifest.stubUiFindings with at least 2 entries, and Tier-1 summary references both filenames
- AC: PAN-1231 fixture test passes: a workspace adding stub TableMode + TimelineMode segmented-control entries produces manifest.stubUiFindings flagging both mode entries
- AC: Each fixture test's describe/it text names the source audit issue (PAN-1389, PAN-1231) so a failure trace points back to the original pattern
- AC: Fixtures clean up their tempdir on teardown (afterAll / afterEach) to avoid leaving git scratch dirs in /tmp
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass after this bead — including the new audit fixtures

title: pan-1500: Extend roles/review-requirements.md with Stub UI BLOCKING rule
description:
Edit roles/review-requirements.md in THREE locations so the BLOCK rule fires reliably (per hazard H3): (1) add a bullet 'Stub UI scope creep — new tabs, modes, segmented-control entries, or routed views whose data hooks return [] / null, whose handlers no-op, or whose copy is `coming soon` without (a) a feature flag check gating them off, (b) removal from user-facing surface, or (c) a non-stub implementation calling real data' to the Scope list (currently lines 17-25), (2) add a new subsection under Method titled 'Stub UI BLOCKING rule' that explicitly says: 'If the shared context summary or context manifest lists `stubUiFindings`, treat each entry as `!` BLOCKING (scope: in_pr_scope) unless the PR diff also shows one of the three mitigations: a feature flag check guarding the new affordance off, removal of the affordance from the user-facing surface, or a non-stub implementation calling real data. Mitigated findings move to Non-blocking Notes with a one-line explanation of which mitigation applies.', (3) add a new row to the Coverage Matrix example output: `| Stub UI: <patternLabel> @ <file>:<line> | stubUiFindings (manifest) | in_pr_scope | Missing (BLOCKING) | Stub introduced — no feature flag, no removal, no real implementation |`. Add a snapshot/landmark test (extend src/lib/__tests__/code-review-agent-definitions.test.ts or create a sibling) that reads the prompt file and asserts all three landmarks are present: the Scope bullet ('Stub UI scope creep'), the Method subsection header ('Stub UI BLOCKING rule'), and the Coverage Matrix row template ('Stub UI:'). Test failure means a future edit accidentally regressed one of the three insertion points.
- AC: roles/review-requirements.md Scope list contains a 'Stub UI scope creep' bullet naming the three valid mitigations (feature flag, removal, non-stub implementation)
- AC: roles/review-requirements.md Method section contains a 'Stub UI BLOCKING rule' subsection that names the manifest field `stubUiFindings` and instructs the reviewer to emit `!` MUST findings (scope: in_pr_scope) for any unmitigated entry
- AC: Coverage Matrix example output in the prompt includes a stub-UI row template so the reviewer's emitted report has a natural slot for each finding
- AC: Landmark/snapshot test asserts the three textual anchors above remain present in roles/review-requirements.md — fails loudly if a future edit removes them
- AC: Prompt explicitly states that mitigated findings are downgraded to Non-blocking Notes with a one-line mitigation explanation, NOT silently dropped
- AC: The BLOCKING rule is bounded to in_pr_scope per the existing per-AC scope classification — out-of-PR stubs already present on main are not this PR's responsibility

title: pan-1500: Wire stubUiFindings into ReviewContextManifest and Tier-1 inline summary
description:
Modify src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts: (1) add `stubUiFindings: StubUiFinding[]` to the ReviewContextManifest interface, (2) in buildReviewContextPromise, await scanStubUi(workspace, diffBase) alongside the existing extractAcceptanceCriteria + readPolicyNotes Promise.all, (3) persist the field on the manifest JSON, (4) update formatTier1Summary to render a 'Stub UI findings (BLOCKING if unmitigated)' section listing up to 10 findings as `  - <patternLabel> @ <filePath>:<lineNumber>` with overflow text '... and N more (see manifest)' matching the policyNotes truncation idiom, (5) add the field to the Pick<...> on formatTier1Summary's typed parameter. Test additions to existing review-context test file (or a new one) assert: manifest contains stubUiFindings field, Tier-1 summary text includes the new section when findings are non-empty, Tier-1 summary OMITS the section entirely when findings are empty (don't add noise to clean diffs).
- AC: ReviewContextManifest type in src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts gains stubUiFindings: StubUiFinding[] field (imported from lint-stub-ui)
- AC: buildReviewContextPromise invokes scanStubUi(workspace, diffBase) once per review run and writes the result to manifest.stubUiFindings
- AC: formatTier1Summary renders a 'Stub UI findings (BLOCKING if unmitigated):' section when findings.length > 0, with up to 10 entries and a '... and N more (see manifest)' overflow line
- AC: formatTier1Summary omits the Stub UI section entirely when findings.length === 0
- AC: Integration test: a temp-workspace fixture with a PAN-1389-pattern stub produces a manifest JSON whose stubUiFindings array is non-empty and whose Tier-1 summary string contains 'Stub UI findings'
- AC: Integration test: a clean fixture (no stubs) produces a manifest with stubUiFindings: [] and a Tier-1 summary that does NOT contain 'Stub UI findings'
- AC: If scanStubUi throws (defensive belt+suspenders), buildReviewContextPromise still produces a valid manifest with stubUiFindings: [] — same posture as readPolicyNotes failure handling

title: pan-1500: Implement scanStubUi() — workspace + diff-base → StubUiFinding[]
description:
Create src/lib/cloister/lint-stub-ui.ts exporting async scanStubUi(workspace: string, diffBase: string): Promise<StubUiFinding[]>. Internally: (1) shell out to `git diff --name-status <diffBase>...HEAD` via execAsync to list changed files, (2) filter by STUB_UI_FILE_GLOBS — only added (A) files match initially; modified (M) files match only if their additions introduce new UI affordances per the pattern set, (3) for each matching file, run `git diff -U0 <diffBase>...HEAD -- <path>` and parse hunks to extract added lines only (lines starting with `+` but not `+++`), tracking the file's destination line number from hunk headers, (4) apply each STUB_UI_PATTERN regex to each added line and emit StubUiFinding records. The function MUST use execAsync (never execSync — see no-execsync-server.md). Any internal failure (git non-zero exit, malformed hunk header, filesystem error) returns [] and logs a single non-fatal warning via console.warn, matching readPolicyNotes posture (review-context.ts:251-254). Unit tests use temp directories seeded via `git init`, write files mirroring PAN-1389 (a new FilesTab.tsx whose hook returns []) and PAN-1231 (a new TableMode.tsx in FleetAgentsView with a no-op handler), commit baseline + add the stubs, then call scanStubUi and assert the expected findings. Negative test: a workspace where the same file has a real implementation calling a fetch hook must produce zero findings.
- AC: src/lib/cloister/lint-stub-ui.ts exports async scanStubUi(workspace: string, diffBase: string): Promise<StubUiFinding[]>
- AC: Scanner uses execAsync only — no execSync, no readFileSync. Verified by grep in the test or a static guard.
- AC: Scanner restricts inspection to added (+) lines from git diff -U0, never flags pre-existing lines untouched by the PR
- AC: Scanner test fixture covering PAN-1389 pattern (new *Tab.tsx with hook returning []) produces at least one StubUiFinding tagged with the empty-array-return patternId
- AC: Scanner test fixture covering PAN-1231 pattern (new segmented-control mode with no-op handler in FleetAgentsView-equivalent) produces at least one StubUiFinding tagged with the noop-handler patternId
- AC: Negative test: a workspace whose diff adds a new tab with a real fetch hook + non-empty render returns ZERO findings
- AC: Failure mode test: scanner returns [] (not throw) when git command exits non-zero or the workspace is not a git repo
- AC: No new top-level dependencies added to package.json — scanner relies on existing execAsync + micromatch (or a hand-rolled glob match if micromatch is not already a dependency)

title: pan-1500: Define STUB_UI_PATTERNS catalog and file-glob filter
description:
Create src/lib/cloister/stub-ui-patterns.ts that exports (a) STUB_UI_FILE_GLOBS — micromatch-compatible globs naming the file types in scope (frontend UI affordances under src/dashboard/frontend/), (b) STUB_UI_PATTERNS — an array of { id, label, regex, severity } records covering: 'coming soon' / 'Coming soon' / 'Not yet implemented' copy; bare `return [];` / `return null;` from a hook in an added file; `useState(false)` for a tab/mode with no other writer; new <Tab>/<TabPanel>/<Route> JSX entries; segmented-control entries with handlers that are arrow-function no-ops (`() => {}`, `() => null`); explicit onClick no-op handlers. Each record carries a stable id (e.g. 'coming-soon-copy', 'empty-array-return', 'noop-handler') so findings are linkable. (c) StubUiFinding TypeScript type: { patternId: string; patternLabel: string; filePath: string; lineNumber: number; addedLine: string; severity: 'block' | 'advisory' }. Unit tests assert each pattern matches its target line and rejects close-but-not-quite lines (e.g. `return [...prev];` must NOT match the empty-array-return pattern). Foundation bead — every downstream bead depends on these exports.
- AC: src/lib/cloister/stub-ui-patterns.ts exists and exports STUB_UI_FILE_GLOBS, STUB_UI_PATTERNS, and the StubUiFinding type
- AC: STUB_UI_PATTERNS covers at least: coming-soon copy, empty-array/null returns, no-op handlers, new Tab/TabPanel/Route JSX entries, segmented-control mode entries
- AC: STUB_UI_FILE_GLOBS restricts scope to frontend UI affordance files (*Tab.tsx, *Mode.tsx, *View.tsx, and net-new files under src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/)
- AC: Unit test file src/lib/cloister/__tests__/stub-ui-patterns.test.ts covers positive matches AND negative non-matches for every pattern (per-pattern table of cases)
- AC: Pattern records use stable ids that are exported in the type so downstream beads (scanner, prompt) can reference them by name
- AC: Module header docblock explicitly states the catalog is 'best-effort, not exhaustive — extend when new stub shapes escape post-merge audits' and references PAN-1454 pattern 4

title: pan-1495: Add regression test: workers must operate regardless of parent process cwd
description:
Add a Vitest test file (e.g. src/lib/registry/__tests__/feature-registry-storage-cwd.test.ts and src/lib/memory/__tests__/fts-db-cwd.test.ts, or a single shared spec under src/lib/__tests__/) that: (1) records the original process.cwd(); (2) calls process.chdir(os.tmpdir()) to simulate the broken-cwd condition; (3) imports and exercises the worker (initializeFeatureRegistryStorage() + listFeatureRegistryEntries() for registry; equivalent entrypoint for fts-db); (4) asserts the operation completes without throwing MODULE_NOT_FOUND; (5) restores the original cwd in afterEach/afterAll. Use a unique PANOPTICON_HOME per test (os.tmpdir()/panopticon-test-<uuid>) to avoid clobbering the user's real registry. Use a small timeout — workers should respond in well under a second. Do NOT use real-wall-clock setTimeout retries; if any waiting is needed, use vi.useFakeTimers() per project test rules.
- AC: Test file exists, runs under `npm test`, and exercises BOTH workers (feature-registry and memory FTS) with a mutated process.cwd
- AC: Test PASSES on this branch (proving the fix works) and would FAIL on main pre-fix (proving the test actually exercises the regression — verifiable by temporarily reverting the storage-file changes and re-running)
- AC: Test cleans up its temporary PANOPTICON_HOME directory and restores process.cwd in afterEach — no leaked state between test runs
- AC: Test does NOT mock better-sqlite3 — it uses the real native module to ensure the resolution path is genuinely exercised
close_reason: Added cwd regression coverage for feature-registry and memory FTS workers using temporary PANOPTICON_HOME and restored process.cwd().

title: pan-1495: Fix memory FTS worker (fts-db.ts) to resolve better-sqlite3 from parent process
description:
Apply the identical resolution-pattern fix from `fix-feature-registry-worker` to src/lib/memory/fts-db.ts: parent-side `createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve('better-sqlite3')` (with try/catch + descriptive error), pass via `workerData.betterSqlite3Path`, worker source loads via `require(workerData.betterSqlite3Path)`. Confirm fts-db.ts already uses ESM (`import.meta.url` available) — if not, fall back to `createRequire(__filename)` shaped appropriately. Make NO other changes to fts-db.ts; do not refactor the memoryFtsWorkerSource() function body beyond the require line.
- AC: Worker source no longer references process.cwd() — grep `process\.cwd` in src/lib/memory/fts-db.ts returns no matches
- AC: Calling `pan memory search <query>` (or the underlying worker entrypoint) completes successfully when invoked from a process whose cwd is outside the repo
- AC: Existing memory FTS unit tests still pass (`npm test -- fts-db` or the closest equivalent); no behavioral change to query results
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run build` all pass
close_reason: Resolved better-sqlite3 in the parent module and passed the absolute path to the memory FTS worker via workerData.

title: pan-1495: Fix feature-registry worker to resolve better-sqlite3 from parent process
description:
In src/lib/registry/feature-registry-storage.ts: (1) add `import { createRequire } from 'node:module'` and `import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'` at the top; (2) at module-load time, resolve `better-sqlite3` to an absolute path via `createRequire(import.meta.url).resolve('better-sqlite3')`, wrapped in try/catch that throws a descriptive Error if missing; (3) pass that absolute path to the worker via `new Worker(featureRegistryWorkerSource(), { eval: true, workerData: { betterSqlite3Path } })`; (4) in `featureRegistryWorkerSource()`, replace `const requireFromMain = createRequire(process.cwd() + '/package.json'); const BetterSqlite3 = requireFromMain('better-sqlite3');` with `const { workerData } = require('node:worker_threads'); const BetterSqlite3 = require(workerData.betterSqlite3Path);`. Make NO other changes to this file.
- AC: Worker source no longer references process.cwd() — grep `process\.cwd` in feature-registry-storage.ts returns no matches
- AC: Starting the dashboard server with cwd outside the repo (e.g. `cd /tmp && node /abs/path/to/dist/dashboard/server.js`) produces no '[session-context] Failed to load feature registry entries' log line and no MODULE_NOT_FOUND error in stderr
- AC: Calling `listFeatureRegistryEntries({})` returns successfully (empty array or populated rows — does not throw) regardless of the dashboard's launch cwd
- AC: If better-sqlite3 is genuinely missing from node_modules, the parent-side require.resolve throws a clear error message naming the missing module BEFORE the worker is spawned (not a cryptic MODULE_NOT_FOUND from inside the worker)
- AC: `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass with no new warnings; `npm run build` produces a dist that still boots successfully under Node 22
close_reason: Resolved better-sqlite3 in the parent module and passed the absolute path to the feature-registry worker via workerData.

title: pan-1487: End-to-end smoke: file a fake substrate bug, verify trailer + projection + stats row
description:
Add an integration test (Vitest + a Playwright check) that: (1) simulates a Flywheel-orchestrator agent invoking `gh issue create` with PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID set — verifies trailer block is appended; (2) drives the substrate-bug poller against a fixture GitHub response containing the trailer — verifies the row appears in flywheel_substrate_bugs and SubstrateBugFiledEvent is emitted; (3) calls /api/flywheel/stats — verifies criterion 1 (bug-rate) value reflects the inserted bug; (4) Playwright opens FlywheelPage Stats tab in an isolated browser instance and asserts the criterion card renders the new value.
- AC: End-to-end test runs in CI and exercises all four stages (hook → poller → stats route → UI)
- AC: Playwright uses an isolated browser instance/profile (per Playwright Isolation rule); does not share state with other agents
- AC: Test uses vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync for the poller delays — no real wall-clock waits
- AC: Provenance flows correctly: trailer says Filed-By=agent only because PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_AGENT_ROLE=flywheel was set
close_reason: Verified end-to-end substrate bug smoke test through hook, poller, stats route, and Flywheel Stats tab.

title: pan-1487: FlywheelStatsPanel React component rendering the 7 criteria cards

Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatsPanel.tsx. Fetches GET /api/flywheel/stats on mount (and on a 60s interval). Renders 7 cards in a responsive grid: each card shows label, large value, target line, status indicator (colored dot + label), trend arrow if present, sampleSize footer. Insufficient-data state shows 'collecting since <date>' instead of a value. Tooltip on each card explains how the criterion is computed (per D12 / H9 notes). Component tests with fixture FlywheelStats.

Implementation calls GET /api/flywheel/stats?window=<X> via the dashboard's internal-token client, prints a colored ASCII table with rows for each of 7 criteria: label | value | target | status (green/yellow/red glyph) | trend | sampleSize. Supports --json for raw output. Also update the pan-flywheel skill SKILL.md to document the new subcommand (per the SKILLS-CONVENTION rule).

title: pan-1487: GitHub poller projects new substrate bugs into flywheel_substrate_bugs + emits SubstrateBugFiledEvent
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/services/substrate-bug-poller.ts: poll every 60s for GitHub issues authored by panopticon-agent[bot] OR labelled 'substrate' created/updated in the last 5 minutes. For each, parse the trailer block from the body (regex over Flywheel-Run-Id, Flywheel-Filed-By, Flywheel-Discovered-In lines). Upsert into flywheel_substrate_bugs (idempotent via PRIMARY KEY). On first insert, emit SubstrateBugFiledEvent. Severity from labels (P0/P1/P2 → severity, default P2). Also watch for merged PRs with 'fix(es)? #NNNN' / 'close(s|d)? #NNNN' / 'resolve(s|d)? #NNNN' referencing a substrate bug; if found, call markFixed(issueId, commitSha, mergedAt) to populate MTTR. Register the poller in src/dashboard/server/main.ts boot sequence, respecting the rate_limits table.
- AC: Poller runs every 60s while dashboard server is up, no execSync/readFileSync (Node.js event-loop safe)
- AC: Trailer regex correctly extracts runId, filedBy, discoveredIn from the canonical trailer block; handles missing lines gracefully
- AC: Idempotent: poller re-seeing the same issue does NOT emit a duplicate SubstrateBugFiledEvent (verified by emit count after running poller twice on same fixture)
- AC: PR-merge scan correctly resolves closes/fixes/resolves references and calls markFixed only for issues present in flywheel_substrate_bugs
- AC: Severity is read from labels (P0/P1/P2); absent → default P2; tests cover all four cases
- AC: Poller respects existing rate_limits table — backs off when GitHub returns 403 with X-RateLimit-Remaining=0
- AC: Vitest tests for poller use vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — never real setTimeout-based delays (per CLAUDE.md test rules)
close_reason: Implemented GitHub substrate bug poller projection, event emission, merge fix detection, rate-limit backoff, dashboard lifecycle wiring, and fake-timer coverage.

title: pan-1487: Criterion 7: flake rate on substrate-attributable failures
description:
Implement computeCriterion7: flake = a review or test that PASSED on one cycle and FAILED on the next within the same pipeline run with no intervening commit (same head SHA). Per H9 hazard. Numerator = flake count; denominator = total substrate-attributable failures. Target: <5%. Status green <0.05, yellow 0.05-0.10, red >0.10.
- AC: Flake detection correctly identifies same-issue, same-head-SHA review/test with divergent outcomes across cycles
- AC: Numerator excludes failures where commit changed between cycles (legitimate fix attempts, not flakes)
- AC: target=0.05; green<0.05, yellow [0.05,0.10], red>0.10
- AC: Test fixture: same SHA pass-then-fail produces 1 flake; pass-fix-pass-fail across different SHAs produces 0 flakes
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: Criterion 6: time-in-pipeline consistency per complexity bucket (p95 <= 2x median)
description:
Implement computeCriterion6: for each completed run in window, sum stage durations (plan+work+review+test+ship+merge) to get totalMs. Bucket by beads count: simple=1-3, medium=4-8, complex=9+. For each bucket compute median + p95 of totalMs; check p95/median ratio. Return {simple: {medianMs, p95Ms, ratio, status}, medium: {...}, complex: {...}}. Target per bucket: ratio<=2.0. Status: green if all three buckets <=2.0, yellow if any bucket 2-3, red if any bucket >3.
- AC: Bucket assignment uses beads count from .beads/issues.jsonl OR (fallback) plan_items count from the vBRIEF spec
- AC: Per-bucket median + p95 computed correctly; ratio = p95/median
- AC: Buckets with fewer than 3 runs are marked dataSufficient=false, not red
- AC: Headline status = worst-bucket status (green if all <=2.0, yellow if any 2-3, red if any >3)
- AC: Test fixture: 6 runs across 2 buckets with known totalMs distribution produces correct ratios
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: Criterion 5: operator intervention rate per pipeline run
description:
Implement computeCriterion5: numerator = COUNT(*) operator.intervention events for issues whose pipeline run completed in window; denominator = COUNT(DISTINCT issueId) of completed runs in window. Value = numerator/denominator (interventions per run, NOT a fraction). Target: <5% — interpreted as <0.05 interventions/run on average (since 'rate per run' is the headline framing). Status green <0.05, yellow 0.05-0.20, red >0.20. UAT is explicitly NOT counted (no event emitted for UAT).
- AC: value = total interventions / completed runs in window
- AC: target=0.05; thresholds green<0.05, yellow [0.05,0.20], red>0.20
- AC: Fixture: 10 runs, 1 'tell' event total → value=0.10 → yellow
- AC: UAT actions (whatever UAT events are recorded) do NOT contribute to numerator
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: Criterion 4: MTTR for filed substrate bugs (median + p95)
description:
Implement computeCriterion4: from flywheel_substrate_bugs WHERE status='fixed' AND fix_merged_at BETWEEN since AND now, compute durations = fix_merged_at - filed_at. Report {medianMs, p95Ms}. Target: median<24h, p95<7d. Status green if both met, yellow if median<24h but p95<14d, red otherwise.
- AC: value = {medianMs, p95Ms} computed from fix_merged_at - filed_at across all fixed bugs in window
- AC: target = {medianMs: 86400000 (24h), p95Ms: 604800000 (7d)}
- AC: Status green if median<24h AND p95<7d; yellow if median<24h AND p95<14d; red otherwise
- AC: If fewer than 3 fixed bugs in window → dataSufficient=false
- AC: Test fixture: 5 fixed bugs with known fix_merged_at - filed_at yields correct median + p95
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: Criterion 3: pipeline pass success rate (substrate-attributable failures only)
description:
Implement computeCriterion3: 1 - (substrate-attributable failures / total review/test attempts). Substrate-attributable failure = pipeline.{review,test}-completed{passed:false} where within 24h after the failure event, a flywheel_substrate_bugs row was created with discovered_in_issue_id=issueId. Target: ≥99%. Status green ≥0.99, yellow 0.95-0.99, red <0.95.
- AC: Numerator counts ONLY substrate-attributable failures (per D13); legitimate code-defect failures are excluded
- AC: Denominator = total review attempts + test attempts in window
- AC: Target=0.99; green>=0.99, yellow [0.95, 0.99), red <0.95
- AC: Test fixture: 100 attempts, 1 substrate-attributable failure → 0.99 → green
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: Criterion 2: critical/P0 substrate bug count
description:
Implement computeCriterion2: COUNT(*) FROM flywheel_substrate_bugs WHERE severity='P0' AND filed_at BETWEEN since AND now. Target: 0. Status green if 0, red if >=1.
- AC: value = count of P0 substrate bugs in window
- AC: target=0; status=green when value=0, red when value>=1
- AC: Test fixture: 1 P0 → red; 0 P0 → green
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: Criterion 1: substrate-bug discovery rate (agent-filed bugs / pipeline runs)
description:
Implement computeCriterion1 in flywheel-telemetry.ts: numerator = COUNT(*) from flywheel_substrate_bugs WHERE filed_by='agent' AND filed_at BETWEEN since AND now; denominator = COUNT(DISTINCT issue_id) of completed pipeline runs in same window. Target: <2%. Status green if <2%, yellow 2-5%, red >5%.
- AC: value = agentBugsCount / completedRunsCount, returned as a fraction (e.g. 0.018 = 1.8%)
- AC: target=0.02, status thresholds: green<0.02, yellow 0.02-0.05, red>0.05
- AC: Test with fixture: 100 runs + 1 agent-filed substrate bug → value=0.01, status=green
- AC: Operator-filed bugs are EXCLUDED from numerator (only agent-discovered counts)
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1487: gh-issue-trailer-hook: PreToolUse hook that appends Flywheel-* trailers to gh issue create body
description:
Create scripts/hooks/gh-issue-trailer-hook.ts (compiled to bin/) that reads Claude Code PreToolUse JSON payload from stdin. If tool_name=='Bash' and the command is `gh issue create` (recognize flags --title/--body/--body-file), parse out the body, append '\n\n---\nFlywheel-Run-Id: <PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID>\nFlywheel-Filed-By: <agent|operator>\nFlywheel-Discovered-In: <PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID's issueId>\n' to it, and emit modifiedToolInput JSON. Filed-By=agent ONLY when PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_AGENT_ROLE=='flywheel'; everything else → operator. Install via pan admin hooks install (existing infra).
- AC: Hook handles `gh issue create --title X --body Y` and `--body-file path` and stdin-based body input; injects trailer correctly in each shape
- AC: Idempotent: if body already contains a 'Flywheel-Run-Id:' line, hook returns input unchanged

title: pan-1487: Add FlywheelStats schema to @panctl/contracts
description:

FlywheelStatsCriterion = { label, value (number | object), target (number | object), status: 'green'|'yellow'|'red'|'insufficient_data', trend?: 'up'|'down'|'flat', sampleSize: number, dataSufficient: boolean }. Export TypeScript types and the decoder. Re-export from contracts/src/index.ts.

title: pan-1487: derivePipelineRunMetrics: per-issue stage durations from events table
description:
Add src/dashboard/server/services/pipeline-run-metrics.ts exporting derivePipelineRunMetrics(issueId): {plan, work, review, test, ship, mergeMs, outcome, interventionCount, passCount, headSha?} computed from the existing `events` table. Plan: agent.created(role=plan) → agent.completed(role=plan). Work: agent.created(role=work) → first pipeline.review-started. Review: pipeline.review-started → pipeline.review-completed (sum across cycles). Test: pipeline.test-started → pipeline.test-completed (sum). Ship: last passing review/test → merge-complete-event. PassCount: count of pipeline.review-completed{passed:false} + pipeline.test-completed{passed:false} for this issue. InterventionCount: count of operator.intervention events for this issue. Outcome: merged|parked|cancelled from issue.closed/issue.status_changed.
- AC: derivePipelineRunMetrics returns correct stage durations for a happy-path single-pass issue
- AC: Multi-cycle review/test correctly sums durations across cycles
- AC: passCount counts review+test failed completions
- AC: interventionCount counts operator.intervention events scoped to this issueId
- AC: outcome resolves to merged|parked|cancelled from terminal event; in-flight issues return outcome='in_flight' and are excluded from window-bound aggregates
- AC: All SQL queries are parameterized; no string concatenation of issueId into SQL
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1486: End-to-end integration test: cooldown respected, cancel works, executor fires on expiry
description:
Add src/lib/cloister/__tests__/auto-merge-integration.test.ts that wires the scheduler → executor → cancel together end-to-end with mocked merge function and mocked GitHub PR state. Three scenarios: (1) schedule + wait 4 minutes (fake timers) + cancel → entry removed, merge function never called; (2) schedule + wait full 5 minutes + executor tick → merge function called exactly once, entry status='merged'; (3) schedule + flip CI red during cooldown + executor tick → entry status='blocked', merge function never called.
- AC: Test uses vi.useFakeTimers() and vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — no real-wall-clock delays.
- AC: Scenario 1 (cancel during cooldown): cancel returns 200, the pending list becomes empty, the mocked merge function is never invoked even after the cooldown elapses.
- AC: Scenario 2 (cooldown expires): the mocked merge function is invoked exactly once, the entry transitions pending→merging→merged.
- AC: Scenario 3 (eligibility flips during cooldown): the entry transitions pending→blocked with the eligibility reason; merge function is never invoked.
- AC: All three scenarios run in under 100ms wall-clock thanks to fake timers.
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm test pass; full suite is green.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1486: Add pending auto-merges banner with countdown + cancel button to FlywheelPage
description:
Add a PendingAutoMergesBanner component rendered above the FlywheelPage main split when GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending returns at least one entry with status='pending'. Each row shows: issueId (clickable), PR number, remaining cooldown ('auto-merging in 3:42'), and a 'Cancel' button. Cancel calls DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id; on success the row disappears via query invalidation. Countdown updates every second from the entry's absolute scheduledMergeAt timestamp.
- AC: Banner only renders when there is at least one entry with status='pending'; empty list renders nothing (no empty placeholder).
- AC: Each row's countdown updates every second based on Date.parse(scheduledMergeAt) - Date.now(); when remaining <= 0 the row shows 'merging…' (executor will pick it up).
- AC: Cancel button is disabled while the mutation is in flight and while the entry is in 'merging' state.
- AC: Cancel mutation invalidates the pending query so the row disappears from all open tabs within the next refetch.
- AC: issueId is a link that opens the issue's existing dashboard route.

title: pan-1486: Add `pan merge cancel <id>` CLI + DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id route

Add a new top-level `merge` command group with one subcommand `cancel <id>` in a new file src/cli/commands/merge.ts. The handler calls DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id (also new) which calls cancelPending(); on 200 prints 'Cancelled auto-merge for <id>' and emits TTS 'auto-merge cancelled for <id>'; on 409 (entry in 'merging' state) prints a clear message and exits non-zero; on 404 prints 'No pending auto-merge for <id>' and exits non-zero. Register the new command in src/cli/index.ts. Add a new skill sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md documenting the cancel subcommand.
- AC: `pan merge cancel PAN-123` for a pending entry exits 0 and prints the cancellation confirmation; the entry is removed from the active list.
- AC: `pan merge cancel PAN-123` when the entry is already 'merging' exits non-zero with a message explaining the cooldown has expired and the merge is in progress.
- AC: `pan merge cancel PAN-999` for a non-pending issue exits non-zero with 'No pending auto-merge for PAN-999'.
- AC: DELETE /api/flywheel/auto-merge/:id is origin-validated; non-trusted origin returns 403.
- AC: DELETE on a 'pending' entry returns 200 with the cancelled entry body; on 'merging' returns 409; on missing returns 404.
- AC: TTS announcement 'auto-merge cancelled for <issueId>' fires exactly once per cancellation.
- AC: sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md exists, documents `pan merge cancel <id>`, and `npm run lint` passes (skill ↔ CLI cross-check OK).

title: pan-1486: Add server-side executor that fires pending auto-merges after cooldown via the existing merge code path
description:
Add a small interval-based executor (src/dashboard/server/services/auto-merge-executor.ts) started from main.ts when the dashboard boots. Tick every 30s: listPendingAutoMerges() with status='pending' AND scheduledMergeAt <= now; for each entry: re-check isFlywheelGloballyPaused() (skip if true), re-check isAutoMergeEligible (if no longer eligible mark status='blocked' with reason); then transitionToMerging(id) — if it returns false (lost the race), skip. On winner: invoke the same internal merge function the dashboard MERGE button uses in src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts; on success markMerged; on throw markFailed with reason. Stop the executor cleanly on dashboard shutdown.
- AC: Executor starts when the dashboard server boots and is registered alongside the existing Deacon patrol so dev-mode and prod both pick it up; it can be disabled via env (PANOPTICON_DISABLE_AUTO_MERGE=1) for tests.
- AC: Tick processes only entries whose scheduledMergeAt <= Date.now(); future-scheduled entries remain pending.
- AC: If isFlywheelGloballyPaused() is true at tick time, the executor logs '[auto-merge] flywheel paused, skipping tick' and does NOT fire any merge; entries remain pending.
- AC: If isAutoMergeEligible returns false at fire-time (e.g. CI flipped red during cooldown), the entry is marked status='blocked' with the eligibility reason and the executor moves on without firing.
- AC: transitionToMerging is called and only the winning concurrent call proceeds; the loser logs and continues.
- AC: Successful merge: status set to 'merging' then 'merged' with mergedAt timestamp; the existing dashboard merge function (the one wired to the MERGE button) is invoked unchanged — no second merge path is added.
- AC: Failed merge: status set to 'failed' with failureReason; TTS announces 'PAN-XXX auto-merge failed: <reason>'; entry persists for the orchestrator to surface.
- AC: Vitest tests with mocked merge function cover: future-scheduled-skipped, paused-skipped, eligibility-recheck-blocked, transition-race-loser-skips, successful-merge happy path, failed-merge marks failed and announces.
- AC: Tests use vi.useFakeTimers() — no real interval delays per the CLAUDE.md retry/delay rule.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1486: Add POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule + scanner that enqueues with cooldown and TTS
description:
Add POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule (request body: {issueId}) and GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending (returns the full active list). The schedule handler checks all gates in order: isFlywheelRequireUatBeforeMerge() === false (else 412); isFlywheelGloballyPaused() === false (else 423); resolveLiveFlywheelRunId() !== null (else 412); isAutoMergeEligible(issueId).eligible === true (else 422 with reason). On gate pass: scheduleAutoMerge() with scheduledMergeAt = now + 5 minutes; emit TTS via the same path as scripts/say.sh used by the dashboard ('PAN-XXX auto-merging in 5 minutes; pan merge cancel PAN-XXX to abort'); return the entry. Constant AUTO_MERGE_COOLDOWN_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000 in a new auto-merge-config.ts module.
- AC: AUTO_MERGE_COOLDOWN_MS = 300_000 exported from src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-config.ts; not duplicated elsewhere.
- AC: POST /api/flywheel/auto-merge/schedule with valid {issueId} when require_uat_before_merge=false, flywheel running, and PR eligible: returns 200 with the persisted entry containing scheduledMergeAt = now + 300s (±2s tolerance for test wall-clock).
- AC: POST when require_uat_before_merge=true returns 412 with a body explaining UAT is still required.
- AC: POST when flywheel paused returns 423 (Locked) with a body explaining the flywheel is paused.
- AC: POST when isAutoMergeEligible returns {eligible:false} returns 422 with the eligibility reason verbatim in the body.
- AC: POST is origin-validated; missing/wrong origin returns 403.
- AC: TTS announcement fires exactly once per (issueId, 'scheduled') transition; duplicate schedule calls return 200 with the existing entry and do NOT re-announce.
- AC: GET /api/flywheel/auto-merge/pending returns the active list sorted by scheduledMergeAt ascending; entries include {id, issueId, scheduledMergeAt, scheduledAt, status, prNumber, prUrl}.
- AC: Vitest route tests cover each branch above; TTS announcement is mocked and asserted exactly-once.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1486: Add pending_auto_merges SQLite table (schema v28) + CRUD module
description:
Bump src/lib/database/schema.ts CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION from 27 to 28; add a v27→v28 migration block CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pending_auto_merges (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, issueId TEXT NOT NULL, prUrl TEXT NOT NULL, prNumber INTEGER, projectKey TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK status IN ('pending','merging','blocked','failed'), scheduledMergeAt TEXT NOT NULL, scheduledAt TEXT NOT NULL, mergedAt TEXT, failureReason TEXT, cancelledAt TEXT, cancelledBy TEXT). Add CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_pending_auto_merges_active_issue ON pending_auto_merges(issueId) WHERE status IN ('pending','merging'). Create src/lib/database/pending-auto-merges-db.ts with scheduleAutoMerge, getPendingAutoMerge(issueId), listPendingAutoMerges, transitionToMerging(id), markFailed(id, reason), markBlocked(id, reason), markMerged(id), cancelPending(id, cancelledBy), clearAllPending() — all sync and wrapped in Effect.try with DatabaseError tag like app-settings.ts.
- AC: CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION is 28 and a fresh-DB init creates the pending_auto_merges table with the schema described in the Action.
- AC: v27→v28 migration block runs on an existing v27 DB without dropping any existing data; the table appears after migration.
- AC: UNIQUE partial index prevents two simultaneously-active (pending OR merging) entries for the same issueId; a second scheduleAutoMerge for the same issue returns the existing entry rather than inserting.
- AC: transitionToMerging is atomic — uses UPDATE ... WHERE id=? AND status='pending' and returns boolean indicating whether the row transitioned; concurrent calls produce exactly one winner.
- AC: cancelPending refuses (returns false / 409-shape) when the entry is in 'merging' state; succeeds when status='pending'.
- AC: clearAllPending() removes all entries whose status='pending' but leaves 'merging' / 'failed' / 'blocked' historical entries intact.
- AC: scheduledMergeAt and scheduledAt columns are documented (jsdoc on the type) as absolute ISO timestamps that survive process sleep; failureReason free-text up to 1024 chars.
- AC: Vitest tests in src/lib/database/__tests__/pending-auto-merges-db.test.ts cover: schedule + read-back; schedule-duplicate-idempotent; transition pending→merging exactly once across concurrent calls; cancelPending honors status; clearAllPending behavior.

title: pan-1486: Add isAutoMergeEligible(issueId) predicate + BLOCKER_LABELS constant
description:
Add a new module src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-eligibility.ts exporting BLOCKER_LABELS = ['needs-design', 'needs-discussion', 'do-not-merge'] and an async isAutoMergeEligible(issueId: string): Promise<{eligible: boolean; reason?: string}>. The predicate combines: (a) read-only review status check for readyForMerge===true; (b) GitHub PR state check via getPullRequestState() with checksFailed===false and merged===false; (c) GitHub issue labels check that none of the issue's labels intersect BLOCKER_LABELS. Returns a reason string when ineligible so callers can use it in TTS / status messages.
- AC: BLOCKER_LABELS is exported as a readonly tuple from the new module; the role prompt and any future surface should import this constant rather than hard-coding the strings.
- AC: isAutoMergeEligible returns {eligible:false, reason: 'review status is not readyForMerge'} when readyForMerge is false.
- AC: Returns {eligible:false, reason: 'CI checks failing on PR HEAD <sha>'} when checksFailed===true.
- AC: Returns {eligible:false, reason: 'PR is already merged'} when prState.merged===true.
- AC: Returns {eligible:false, reason: 'issue carries blocker label: <label>'} when any BLOCKER_LABELS member is on the issue.
- AC: Returns {eligible:true} only when all checks pass.
- AC: Vitest unit tests in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/auto-merge-eligibility.test.ts cover all five branches with mocked review-status + getPullRequestState + label-fetch.
close_reason: Added auto-merge eligibility predicate, blocker labels, branch tests, typecheck, and lint.

title: pan-1501: Persist --test-waived reason to continue.json decisions[] and tracker comment
description:
When `options.testWaived` is provided AND a gate would otherwise have fired (or proactively whenever the flag is set — simpler), in doneCommand: (1) append `{ id: "D-test-waived", summary: "Test gate waived: <reason>", recordedAt: <iso> }` to the workspace's continue.json `decisions[]` via writeWorkspaceContinue. (2) If `options.comment` is set, append `\n\nTest gate waived: <reason>` to it; if unset, set it to `Test gate waived: <reason>`. The augmented comment is then passed to updateLinearToInReview / updateGitHubToInReview so the in-review tracker comment carries the waiver text for human reviewers.
- AC: When `--test-waived <reason>` is passed, a new decision `{ id: 'D-test-waived', summary: 'Test gate waived: <reason>', recordedAt: <iso> }` is appended to .pan/continue.json `decisions[]`

title: pan-1501: Orchestrator: runTestRequirementCheck composes detector + counter + fetcher and returns failure lines
description:
Add `runTestRequirementCheck(workspacePath: string, issueId: string, waivedReason?: string): Effect<string[], ProcessSpawnError>` to src/lib/work/test-requirement-gate.ts. Flow: (1) if waivedReason is non-empty, return [] immediately (waiver is the escape hatch). (2) Call fetchIssueBodyForGate(issueId); if it returns null, return a failure line directing the operator to --force or --test-waived. (3) Run detectTestRequirements on the body; if empty, return []. (4) Compute diff base via `git merge-base origin/main HEAD` (fall back to `origin/main...HEAD` three-dot syntax) and run `git diff --numstat <base>...HEAD`. (5) Run countTestDeltaInDiff on the numstat output. (6) If requirements > 0 && delta === 0, return failure lines listing the matched keywords (with line numbers) and the two escape hatches (add tests / use --test-waived). On `git merge-base` failure with no usable fallback, log a warning and return [] (soft-fail; do not block on infrastructure issues).
- AC: When waivedReason is a non-empty string, `runTestRequirementCheck` returns [] without making any tracker or git call
- AC: When the issue body has zero test-keyword matches, returns []
- AC: When the issue body has ≥1 match AND `countTestDeltaInDiff` returns 0, returns failure lines that (a) enumerate matched keywords with line numbers and (b) name both escape hatches: 'add tests covering …' and '--test-waived "<reason + sha>"'
- AC: When `fetchIssueBodyForGate` returns null (tracker fetch failed), returns failure lines directing the operator to --force or --test-waived rather than silently passing
- AC: When `git merge-base origin/main HEAD` fails AND the three-dot fallback also fails, logs a warning and returns [] (soft-fail rather than block on workspace misconfiguration)
- AC: Unit tests cover all five branches (waived, no matches, matches+zero-delta, tracker-fetch-failure, merge-base-failure)

title: pan-1501: Corpus regression test: gate must fire for each of PAN-1326, PAN-1256, PAN-1257, PAN-1175, PAN-1173, PAN-1168, PAN-1111
description:

Each contains body.md (captured from `gh issue view <num> --repo eltmon/panopticon-cli --json body --jq .body`) and diff.numstat (captured from `git diff --numstat <merge-base>..<merged-sha>` for the merged feature branch). Add src/lib/work/__tests__/test-requirement-gate-corpus.test.ts that parameterizes over the seven fixtures and asserts: `detectTestRequirements(body).length > 0 && countTestDeltaInDiff(numstat) === 0` (i.e. the gate would fire). Include a small README.md in the fixtures dir documenting the capture commands and the snapshot date.
- AC: Fixture directory exists for each of the seven issues (PAN-1326, PAN-1256, PAN-1257, PAN-1175, PAN-1173, PAN-1168, PAN-1111) and contains both body.md and diff.numstat
- AC: A parameterized vitest spec runs for each fixture and asserts the gate would fire (detector matches > 0 AND counter == 0)
- AC: The corpus test runs as part of the default `npm test` suite (not behind a tag) — it is offline and fast
- AC: Fixtures README.md documents the exact capture commands (`gh issue view ...`, `git diff --numstat ...`) and the snapshot date so future maintainers can refresh

title: pan-1501: Document the four pan-done preflight gates (including the new test-requirement gate) in docs
description:
Add a short section to docs/PANOPTICON_DEV_SOP.md (or wherever pan done is documented today — locate via `grep -rn 'pan done' docs/`) listing the four preflight gates (open beads / uncommitted changes / vBRIEF AC status / test-requirement) with one sentence each. Include the `--test-waived` escape hatch and its semantic distinction from `--force`. Cross-reference PAN-1501 and PAN-1454 pattern 8.
- AC: Docs section lists all four current pan-done preflight gates with one-sentence summaries each
- AC: Docs explain the difference between `--force` (skip all gates) and `--test-waived` (skip only the test gate, must include a reason)
