title: pan-1501: Add --test-waived <reason> flag to `pan done` CLI registration
description:
In src/cli/index.ts at the `program.command('done <id>')` block (around line 455), add `.option('--test-waived <reason>', 'Skip the test-requirement gate; reason must include rationale and SHA of an existing test that covers the requirement')`. Add `testWaived?: string` to the `DoneOptions` interface in src/cli/commands/done.ts. The flag is independent of `--force`: `--force` skips ALL preflight checks; `--test-waived` skips ONLY the test-requirement gate while leaving open beads, uncommitted changes, and AC-status checks active.
- AC: `pan done --help` lists `--test-waived <reason>` with a clear description
- AC: `DoneOptions.testWaived?: string` defined in src/cli/commands/done.ts
- AC: `--test-waived` is documented as a TARGETED skip (only the test-requirement gate), distinct from `--force` which skips ALL preflight checks
- AC: Passing `--test-waived` without a reason fails Commander's option-parsing (the flag is `<reason>` required, not `[reason]` optional)

title: pan-1501: Issue-body fetcher Effect: pulls body from GitHub (gh) or Linear (SDK)
description:
Add `fetchIssueBodyForGate(issueId: string): Effect<string | null, TrackerFetchError>` to src/lib/work/test-requirement-gate.ts. Resolve tracker via existing `resolveGitHubIssueSync` from src/lib/tracker-utils. For GitHub: `gh issue view <num> --repo <owner>/<repo> --json body --jq '.body'`. For Linear: use the existing LinearClient pattern from doneCommand (filter by team key + number, read .description). Returns null only when the tracker is unreachable / unauthenticated; otherwise throws a TrackerFetchError that the orchestrator translates to an abort message. Unit tests stub child_process.exec and the Linear SDK to exercise both paths and the failure modes.
- AC: `fetchIssueBodyForGate(issueId)` returns an Effect that resolves to the raw issue body string for GitHub issues via `gh issue view --json body --jq .body`
- AC: For Linear issues, the same function returns the LinearClient `.description` of the matching issue (resolved by team key + number)
- AC: Returns null (not throws) when the tracker is GitHub but `gh` is unauthenticated or the issue is missing — orchestrator decides whether to abort
- AC: Unit tests stub child_process.exec (gh path) and @linear/sdk (Linear path) and exercise success + failure for each

title: pan-1501: Pure helper: countTestDeltaInDiff(numstat) → sum of new lines added to test files
description:
Add `countTestDeltaInDiff(numstatOutput: string): number` to src/lib/work/test-requirement-gate.ts. Parse `git diff --numstat` output (tab-separated `<additions>\t<deletions>\t<path>`). Sum additions for paths matching `**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}`. Skip binary entries (`-\t-\t<path>`). Unit tests cover: empty input, single test file, multiple test files, non-test files ignored, .test.tsx variants included, binary entries skipped, malformed lines tolerated.
- AC: `countTestDeltaInDiff(numstatOutput: string): number` exported from src/lib/work/test-requirement-gate.ts
- AC: Sums additions for files matching **/*.test.ts, **/*.spec.ts, **/*.test.tsx, **/*.spec.tsx
- AC: Non-test files (e.g. src/lib/foo.ts) contribute 0 to the sum even if they have additions
- AC: Binary diff entries (`-\t-\tpath`) are skipped without throwing
- AC: Empty input returns 0; malformed lines do not throw

title: pan-1501: Pure helper: detectTestRequirements(text) → matched keywords with line numbers
description:
Create src/lib/work/test-requirement-gate.ts exporting `detectTestRequirements(text: string): TestRequirement[]`. Match the keyword set from the issue spec — `\btest\b`, `regression test`, `unit test`, `Test:`, `## Test plan`, `vitest`, `playwright` — case-insensitive. Each result carries { keyword, line, context }. Empty/null input returns []. Add unit tests at src/lib/work/__tests__/test-requirement-gate.test.ts covering each keyword variant, multiple matches per line, line numbering, case-insensitivity, and the negative cases 'contested' / 'latest' / 'tested' (must NOT match the bare-word `test` pattern). Export the keyword regex set so the orchestrator and tests share one source of truth.
- AC: Module src/lib/work/test-requirement-gate.ts exists and exports `detectTestRequirements(text: string): TestRequirement[]`
- AC: Each spec-listed keyword (\btest\b, regression test, unit test, Test:, ## Test plan, vitest, playwright) has at least one passing positive-match test case
- AC: Negative test cases assert that 'latest', 'contested', and 'tested' do NOT match the bare-word `test` pattern
- AC: Returns [] for empty string and null/undefined input without throwing
- AC: Each TestRequirement carries an accurate `line` number (1-indexed) matching the line in the input text

title: pan-1455: Add route-level integration test asserting GET /api/settings/codex-auth returns 'burned' when log tail contains 'refresh_token_reused'
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts (vitest). Follow the pattern of sibling route tests (e.g., issues-reopen.test.ts, request-review-nudge.test.ts). Point the cliproxy auth/log resolvers at a temp directory via env-var overrides or vi.mock. Write a fixture codex-primary.json with a future-expiring JWT and a fixture cliproxy.log whose tail contains a freshly-timestamped `refresh_token_reused` line. Invoke the GET handler for /api/settings/codex-auth (either by mounting the Effect HTTP route in a test harness or by directly calling the underlying checkCodexAuthStatus exported from src/lib/codex-auth.ts via the route's Effect — match the pattern used by neighbouring route tests). Assert the JSON response is { status: 'burned', email, expiresAt }. Cleanup the temp dir in afterEach.
- AC: Test file exists at src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts.
- AC: Test exercises the route (or its handler Effect) end-to-end with a temp cliproxy log fixture; does NOT mutate the host's real ~/.panopticon/cliproxy directory.
- AC: With log tail containing a fresh 'refresh_token_reused' line and a JWT whose exp is in the future, the response JSON has status === 'burned'.
- AC: Companion case: with no burn lines in the log, the same setup returns status === 'valid' (negative control).
- AC: Test passes under `npm test`.
close_reason: Added route-level GET /api/settings/codex-auth coverage for refresh_token_reused burned status plus a valid negative control using temp cliproxy fixtures.

title: pan-1455: Add lib-level tests for checkCodexAuthStatus covering both burn phrasings and the suppression cases
description:
Create src/lib/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts (vitest). Use vi.mock or env-var overrides for getCliproxyAuthDir / getCliproxyLogPath so the test writes a fake codex-primary.json (with a future-expiring JWT) and a fake cliproxy.log into a tmp dir. Use vi.setSystemTime so hard-coded log timestamps stay inside the 60-minute BURN_STALENESS_MS window (H5). Generate a JWT whose `exp` claim is ~1 hour in the future and whose `email` field is set. Test cases: (1) credentials missing → status: 'missing'; (2) valid JWT, empty log → status: 'valid'; (3) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh token has already been used' inside window, no later success → status: 'burned' (PAN-913 regression guard); (4) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh_token_reused' inside window, no later success → status: 'burned' (the new case for PAN-1455); (5) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh_token_reused' followed by a 200 on /v1/messages → status: 'valid' (auto-retry won, H3); (6) valid JWT, log contains 'refresh_token_reused' with timestamp older than 60 minutes → status: 'valid' (staleness suppression, H5).
- AC: Test file exists at src/lib/__tests__/codex-auth.test.ts.
- AC: Test case asserts status: 'burned' when log tail contains 'refresh_token_reused' inside the staleness window with no later 200 on /v1/messages.
- AC: Test case asserts status: 'burned' when log tail contains 'refresh token has already been used' (PAN-913 regression guard).
- AC: Test case asserts status: 'valid' when a 'refresh_token_reused' burn line is followed by a 200 on /v1/messages (suppression preserved).
- AC: Test case asserts status: 'valid' when the burn line is older than BURN_STALENESS_MS (60 min).
- AC: All tests pass under `npm test` (root vitest run).
close_reason: Added lib-level Codex auth tests for missing/valid credentials, both burn phrasings, burn-then-success suppression, and stale-burn suppression.

title: pan-1455: Widen applyBurnedTokenOverride to detect 'refresh_token_reused' in addition to 'refresh token has already been used'
description:
In src/lib/codex-auth.ts, modify the burn-line detection at line ~151. Replace the single `line.includes('refresh token has already been used')` check with a disjunction that ALSO matches `line.includes('refresh_token_reused')`. Keep the original literal — never remove it (H1: regression of PAN-913). Add a brief comment immediately above the check naming both known phrasings and citing the two issues (PAN-913 introduced the first, PAN-1455 extends to the second). Do not touch the suppression logic at lines 163-173 (burn-then-success), the staleness window (BURN_STALENESS_MS), or the ignoreBurnBefore handling. Do not introduce a regex — keep two literal `includes()` checks for clarity and speed.
- AC: Both literals are present in the source: 'refresh token has already been used' AND 'refresh_token_reused'.
- AC: A comment above the matcher names both phrasings and cites PAN-913 and PAN-1455.
- AC: The burn-then-200-success suppression at lines 163-173 is unchanged.
- AC: Typecheck (`npm run typecheck`) and lint (`npm run lint`) pass.
close_reason: Added refresh_token_reused literal detection to the Codex burned-token log matcher while preserving the PAN-913 prose matcher and suppression logic.

title: launcher-multi-append — extend LauncherGeneratorConfig to accept multiple --append-system-prompt-file

title: pan-1205: Serve raw artifacts on isolated origin
description:
Add dashboard server routes for raw artifact HTML at `/a/<slug>` that only serve from the artifact subdomain, read published snapshot bytes, reject unshared/missing artifacts, and set the CSP and cache headers required for sandbox isolation.

- AC: Missing slugs return 404 and unshared artifacts return 410 Gone without deleting metadata or copied bytes.

title: pan-1205: Implement artifact publishing lifecycle

- AC: Status compares the current source hash with `lastPublishedHash` and reports pendingChanges true after edits and false after publish.
- AC: Publish revalidates edited files, updates the copied published snapshot and published hash, preserves the slug, and records updated timestamps without deleting prior metadata.
- AC: Unshare sets `unsharedAt` and leaves source files, copied published bytes, and database metadata intact.

title: pan-1205: Implement HTML artifact validation

- AC: Validation rejects AWS, GitHub, OpenAI, Anthropic, Slack, private-key, and .env-style secrets named in the PRD, with per-line `<!-- artifact-allow-secret -->` suppressions producing warnings rather than silent passes.
- AC: Strict mode catches high-entropy strings, inline event handlers without CSP, and missing image alt text while non-strict mode leaves those as allowed or warning-only according to the PRD.
- AC: Unit tests cover positive and negative fixtures for every secret scanner, forbidden/allowed asset path class, size boundary, and strict-mode rule.

description:

Include types/schemas for `ContextLayerKind` (`global`, `project`, `workspace`), registered project summaries, workspace summaries, editable layer records (`kind`, `file`, `exists`, `content`, `editable`, optional `projectKey`/`workspacePath`), preview request/response, save request/response, and sync response. Name harness preview fields explicitly for `claude-code`, `pi`, and `fullPrompt`. Keep these contracts UI-agnostic and aligned with existing `Harness` values from `packages/contracts/src/types.ts`. Add package-level unit tests only if this package has nearby schema/type tests; otherwise ensure the root typecheck exercises the exports.
- AC: Contracts represent all three editable layers and both v1 harness previews without frontend/server-only naming drift.
- AC: The contracts distinguish preview-only operations from save/sync operations so the frontend cannot accidentally write during live preview.
- AC: `npm run typecheck` can import the new context contracts from `@panctl/contracts` in both dashboard server and frontend code.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1201: PAN-1201: Add plural `pan projects` command group
description:
Modify `src/cli/index.ts` around the existing `const project = program.command('project')` registration. Add a sibling plural command group `program.command('projects')` that registers `add`, `list`, `show`, `remove`, and `init` with the same options and action handlers (`projectAddCommand`, `projectListCommand`, `projectShowCommand`, `projectRemoveCommand`, `projectInitCommand`). Keep the singular group working unchanged for backwards compatibility. Add or update a CLI command test/fixture so `pan projects add <path>` is recognized and dispatches to the same handler shape as `pan project add <path>`. Do not change project registration semantics in `src/cli/commands/project.ts` in this bead.
- AC: `pan projects add <path>` is registered with the same options as `pan project add <path>`: `--name`, `--type`, `--linear-team`, and `--rally-project`.
- AC: The existing singular `pan project add <path>` command still works and is not removed or renamed.
- AC: CLI help/test fixtures expose the plural `projects` command so the issue's documented registration surface is discoverable.

title: pan-1203: Verify the complete docs RAG feature
description:
Run the repository quality gates and feature-specific checks after the implementation is complete. Verify that the prebuilt index is generated, materialized, queried locally, used by Claude Code prompt submission, and either used by Pi prompt submission or blocked with a clear API limitation. Confirm no prompt-time embedding/network path exists and no existing hook/memory behavior regressed.
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` pass after `bun install` in the workspace
- AC: Feature-specific tests cover chunking, indexing, retrieval ranking, budgets, CLI commands, install materialization, Claude hook injection, and Pi behavior
- AC: Manual smoke test demonstrates `pan docs query` returns relevant local snippets and hook injection emits bounded `<panopticon-docs>` context for a triggered prompt
- AC: Package verification confirms the shipped docs index is below the size budget and included in publish artifacts

title: pan-1203: Extend Claude Code UserPromptSubmit docs injection
description:
Extend `sync-sources/hooks/user-prompt-submit-hook` so it preserves waiting-state event emission and existing memory injection output, then conditionally appends docs context returned by a local query path. The hook must parse prompt/session/project fields safely, never echo the original user prompt, use short timeouts, avoid network/model calls for docs retrieval, and return no docs block on errors. Setup tests must prove the existing UserPromptSubmit registration is preserved rather than replaced or duplicated.
- AC: Hook stdout order remains existing memory context first, then optional `<panopticon-docs>` context only
- AC: Hook docs retrieval fails open quickly on missing jq, missing index, malformed payloads, disabled state, and query errors
- AC: Setup hooks tests prove UserPromptSubmit registration remains idempotent and does not clobber unrelated settings or hook arrays
- AC: Hook integration tests cover triggered prompts, non-triggered prompts, budget exhaustion, and memory-plus-docs output ordering

title: pan-1203: Document docs RAG operations and configuration
description:
Update existing user/operator documentation for the docs corpus, build/reindex workflow, `pan docs` commands, hook behavior, enable/disable scopes, telemetry, budget settings, troubleshooting, and packaging expectations. Keep documentation concise and linked from existing docs surfaces rather than creating an unreferenced island.
- AC: Docs explain query/reindex/status/enable/disable commands with examples
- AC: Docs explain hook injection behavior, budgets, telemetry, and how to disable docs context
- AC: Skill/CLI convention documentation is updated if the new `pan docs` surface affects wrapper skill expectations

title: pan-1203: Materialize the shipped docs index during install and setup
description:
Update install/setup paths so `dist/docs-index.sqlite` is copied or refreshed into `~/.panopticon/docs/index.sqlite` using atomic writes and clear freshness checks. Reuse existing hook setup safety primitives for any Claude settings mutation, and avoid overwriting user-managed state unnecessarily.
- AC: `pan install` creates the docs directory and materializes the packaged index atomically
- AC: Install detects stale or missing materialized indexes and refreshes without clobbering disable/telemetry state
- AC: Install tests cover first install, upgrade refresh, missing packaged index, and PANOPTICON_HOME overrides

title: pan-1203: Add Pi prompt-time docs injection or report API blocker
description:
Investigate the actual Pi extension prompt interception API and wire the docs query path into the best available prompt-submit/context injection mechanism with tests. If Pi exposes no way to add prompt-time context, stop and report that blocker explicitly before marking this bead complete; do not silently satisfy the feature with Claude Code only.
- AC: Pi extension implementation documents and tests the chosen prompt interception or context injection event
- AC: Pi docs injection uses the same local retrieval, trigger, budget, disable, and fail-open behavior as Claude Code
- AC: If Pi lacks prompt-time context support, the agent reports a blocker instead of marking the Pi acceptance criterion complete

title: pan-1203: Add `pan docs` commands
description:
Register a grouped `pan docs` command in the CLI with `query`, `reindex`, `status`, `disable`, and `enable` subcommands. Match the PRD command surface, support markdown/json query output, top-N and kind filters, invoke build-time embedding only in `reindex`, and make status explain index location, freshness, enabled scopes, budgets, and telemetry counters.
- AC: `pan docs query` supports quoted text, `--top`, `--kind`, and `--format markdown|json` using only the local index
- AC: `pan docs reindex` rebuilds the materialized index and reports source/chunk/embedding counts
- AC: `pan docs status`, `disable`, and `enable` reflect global, project, and session state accurately
- AC: CLI tests cover command registration, output formats, invalid options, missing index, and disabled-state messaging

title: pan-1203: Add docs trigger, budget, disable, and telemetry state
description:
Implement local state for global/project/session enablement, prompt trigger matching, token/result budget accounting, explicit `pan docs disable/enable` effects, and telemetry under `~/.panopticon/docs/`. Ensure missing hook session IDs have documented fallback behavior and that all prompt-time errors fail open without suppressing existing Panopticon hook events.
- AC: Trigger logic matches configured Panopticon/documentation patterns and avoids injection when disabled or over budget
- AC: Disable/enable state supports session, project, and global scopes with predictable precedence
- AC: Telemetry records query count, injected token estimates, hit/miss status, and disable/budget reasons without storing full prompts
- AC: Tests cover session-id present and missing hook payloads, budget exhaustion, and scope precedence

title: pan-1203: Implement local docs retrieval and formatting
description:
Create the runtime query library that opens the materialized docs SQLite index read-only, sanitizes arbitrary prompts into safe FTS5 MATCH expressions, computes BM25 and embedding result sets from stored vectors, combines rankings with reciprocal rank fusion and doc-kind priority, truncates to top-N and token budget, and formats both markdown and JSON responses. Query-time code must not call embedding models or remote APIs.
- AC: FTS query sanitization returns no results instead of throwing for malformed, empty, or punctuation-only prompts
- AC: Retrieval combines BM25 and stored-vector similarity with reciprocal rank fusion and doc_kind priority
- AC: Markdown formatter emits bounded `<panopticon-docs>` snippets with path, heading, and anchor provenance
- AC: Runtime query tests prove no embedding provider or network calls occur during normal query execution
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1203: Build and package the SQLite docs index
description:
Add a docs index build/reindex pipeline that writes a separate SQLite database with `docs_chunks`, `docs_fts`, and `docs_embeddings`, stores normalized Float32 embeddings as blobs, validates schema metadata, and produces `dist/docs-index.sqlite` as part of the build/package path. Keep embedding generation isolated to build-time and explicit reindex commands, with injectable embedding functions for tests.
- AC: Index builder creates the PRD schema with FTS5 rows synchronized to docs_chunks and embeddings keyed by chunk_id
- AC: Build scripts generate and ship `dist/docs-index.sqlite` without first-run model downloads at install or query time
- AC: Package/index size checks enforce the configured budget and fail loudly when exceeded
- AC: Builder tests cover schema creation, rebuild idempotency, embedding blob round trips, and package artifact presence
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1203: Implement docs corpus discovery and markdown chunking
description:
Create docs corpus code that discovers configured markdown sources from docs, sync-sources skills, CLAUDE.md, .claude/rules, and optional PRD locations. Chunk markdown by heading hierarchy, retain doc kind/path/heading/anchor metadata, enforce the PRD token-size target, and skip generated or irrelevant files intentionally rather than through brittle fallback chains.
- AC: Corpus discovery includes docs, skills, global/project rules, CLAUDE.md, and optional PRDs with correct doc_kind values
- AC: Markdown chunking preserves heading paths and anchors and keeps chunks within the configured budget
- AC: Corpus tests cover nested headings, empty files, large sections, rule frontmatter, and skill documents

title: pan-1203: Add docs RAG configuration and state paths
description:
Extend YAML config normalization with a `docs` section covering enablement scopes, trigger regexes, corpus toggles, token/result budgets, embedding provider/model choices for build-time/reindex work, and classifier defaults. Add or reuse path helpers for `~/.panopticon/docs/index.sqlite`, budget/disable state, and telemetry without changing dashboard-server-reachable code to blocking sync APIs.
- AC: Normalized config exposes docs enablement, trigger, corpus, budget, and classifier defaults

title: pan-1457: Browser UAT: phantom paths render plain text, real directories chip and open
description:
Drive an isolated Playwright session (fresh browser context — must not share state with any other agent's session, must not depend on a pre-existing login) against the locally running dashboard. Open a conversation in a Panopticon workspace whose cwd is a workspaces/feature-<id> path. Render a transcript that mentions: (a) 'conv/2209' inline (phantom), (b) 'notes/today.md' inline (phantom), (c) a real workspace-relative file like 'src/dashboard/server/server.ts' (positive), (d) a real workspace directory like 'src/components' (positive after dir-regex bead). Assert: (a) and (b) render as plain text — no chip border, no icon, no anchor. (c) renders as a chip and clicking it issues the editor RPC. (d) renders as a chip after the RPC resolves. Capture screenshots for the review record.
- AC: Inline 'conv/2209' (or any other phantom path) does not render as a chip regardless of regex shape (issue AC1)
- AC: Inline 'src/components/Foo' (a real directory in the workspace) renders as a chip and opens correctly (issue AC2)
- AC: Server RPC rejects path-traversal attempts and does not enumerate directory contents — verified by reading the server tests and by a Playwright-driven payload sending '../../etc/passwd' through the RPC and observing exists=false (issue AC3)
- AC: Lookup latency does not block initial transcript render — candidate-bearing paragraphs paint immediately as plain text and upgrade to chips on RPC resolution (issue AC4, observed via Playwright network throttling or by asserting paint timing precedes the RPC's response)
- AC: Cache invalidates on TTL: after the documented 60s TTL, a re-render issues a fresh RPC (asserted indirectly by inspecting the in-flight cache state via a debug hook or by waiting 60s in a slow-tagged test) (issue AC5)
- AC: Playwright session uses an isolated browser context — no shared profile, no dependency on any other agent's authenticated session

title: pan-1457: Loosen bare-text regex to admit extension-less directory candidates
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/markdown-links.ts: remove the && hasFileExtensionInLastSegment(path) gate from isLikelyPathCandidate's bare-relative branch (the gate added in 72a6bdd87) so multi-segment slash paths without an extension also qualify as candidates. Update existing tests in markdown-links.test.ts — the assertions that 'conv/2209 must not match' need to be re-expressed as 'conv/2209 reaches MarkdownFileLink as a candidate but the existence check returns missing, so it renders plain text'. Add new positive tests: 'src/components/Foo' is a candidate, 'apps/web' is a candidate. Document in the function's JSDoc that correctness is now enforced server-side by pan.resolvePathExists; the structural regex is a cheap pre-filter only.
- AC: isLikelyPathCandidate('src/components/Foo') returns true
- AC: isLikelyPathCandidate('apps/web') returns true
- AC: splitMarkdownTextFileLinks promotes 'src/components/Foo' to a link segment when cwd is supplied
- AC: Existing test for 'conv/2209' is updated to assert: regex now accepts it as a candidate, but the existence-check downgrades it to plain text in MarkdownFileLink (covered cross-bead — this AC simply requires the markdown-links test for the candidate side to be updated)
- AC: JSDoc on isLikelyPathCandidate documents that correctness is enforced by pan.resolvePathExists in MarkdownFileLink and that this function is now a cheap structural pre-filter only

title: pan-1457: MarkdownFileLink pessimistic existence-gated rendering
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/MarkdownFileLink.tsx, call usePathExists(issueId, displayPath-derived-relative-path-without-position). When state is 'exists', render the existing chip UI unchanged. When state is 'pending' or 'missing', render the original label text as a plain inline <span> (no chip border, no icon, no editor handlers). Update src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ChatMarkdown.tsx so that the bare-text link path threads the original raw text into a prop on MarkdownFileLink so the fallback renders that text verbatim (not the prettified label). Update existing MarkdownFileLink.test.tsx snapshots — they should keep the chip-rendering tests but gate them via a mock that pre-populates the cache as exists=true. Add three new tests: phantom path renders plain text, real path renders chip, pre-cached negative renders plain text on first paint.
- AC: When usePathExists returns 'exists', MarkdownFileLink renders the chip UI byte-identical to the pre-bead behaviour (snapshot test on a positive-cached path)
- AC: When usePathExists returns 'missing', MarkdownFileLink renders the original raw text as a plain <span> — no chip border, no file icon, no editor handlers (verified by querying by test id absence)
- AC: When usePathExists returns 'pending', MarkdownFileLink renders the same plain text fallback (no flicker — same DOM as 'missing' until upgrade)
- AC: Bare-text path (splitMarkdownTextFileLinks -> link node -> MarkdownFileLink) renders the original raw text on a phantom path, not a prettified workspace-relative label
- AC: Initial transcript render is unblocked by the lookup — paragraphs containing candidate paths complete their first paint with plain text before any RPC resolves

title: pan-1457: Wire resolvePathExists handler into PanRpcLayer
description:
In src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts: add a handler for WS_METHODS.resolvePathExists inside the PanRpcGroup.of({...}) block that calls readPathExistsEffect(input). Remove resolvePathExists from the @ts-expect-error 'missing handlers' list at the top of PanRpcLayer if it's listed there (it isn't yet, since the RPC is new). Add an integration test in src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/ that exercises the full RPC pipeline through the Effect handler (modelled on read-workspace-file-rpc.test.ts).
- AC: WS_METHODS.resolvePathExists handler is wired into PanRpcLayer and the existing @ts-expect-error comment compiles without referencing resolvePathExists as a missing handler
- AC: Integration test invokes the RPC through PanRpcGroup, gets a successful response for an existing file, a missing response for a phantom path, and a missing response for a traversal attempt
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass on the changed server files

title: pan-1457: Client usePathExists hook with LRU cache, in-flight dedup, and TTL
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/hooks/usePathExists.ts exporting a hook usePathExists(issueId: string | undefined, relativePath: string | undefined) -> { state: 'pending' | 'exists' | 'missing'; kind: 'file' | 'dir' | null }. Internally maintain a module-level LRU cache keyed by `${issueId}::${relativePath}` capped at 2000 entries, an in-flight Map<key, Promise> for dedup, and a TTL of 60_000 ms per entry. When issueId is undefined or relativePath empty, return state='pending' (effectively plain-text fallback). When the cache has a fresh hit, return state synchronously on first render — no useEffect tick — so chips don't flicker on re-mount. When the cache has a stale or absent entry, kick off the RPC via getTransport().request((client) => client[WS_METHODS.resolvePathExists]({ issueId, relativePath })), update the cache, and re-render. Strip ':<line>[:<col>]' from relativePath before forming the cache key and sending. Export a clearPathExistsCache() for tests. Unit tests cover: cache hit returns synchronously, two concurrent renders for the same key issue one RPC (dedup), TTL expiry triggers a re-fetch, error responses cache as 'missing' (do not retry-storm), undefined issueId short-circuits.
- AC: First render with an uncached key returns state='pending' and issues exactly one RPC call
- AC: After RPC resolves to exists=true, the next render returns state='exists' with kind='file' or 'dir'
- AC: Subsequent re-mount with the same (issueId, relativePath) within TTL returns state='exists' synchronously on the very first render — no flicker
- AC: Two simultaneous useTpathExists calls for the same key share a single in-flight RPC (dedup test counts mock invocations)
- AC: After 60s TTL elapses (via vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync), a re-mount issues a fresh RPC
- AC: Cache evicts least-recently-used entries when count exceeds 2000
- AC: Positional suffix ':12' / ':12:5' on relativePath is stripped before forming the cache key so 'src/App.tsx' and 'src/App.tsx:12' share one cache entry
- AC: Undefined issueId or empty relativePath short-circuits to state='pending' and issues no RPC
- AC: RPC error response caches as state='missing' for the TTL window — no retry storm

title: pan-1457: Server resolve-path-exists service with hardened path validation
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/services/resolve-path-exists.ts mirroring read-workspace-file.ts patterns: resolveProjectFromIssue(issueId), getWorkspacePathForIssue(...), realpath(workspaceRoot) for the sandbox root, resolve(workspaceRoot, relativePath) for the candidate, isInsidePath() guard against '..' escapes BEFORE stat, realpath() the candidate to detect symlink escape, isInsidePath() check AGAIN against the realpath result, then stat(). Return { exists: true, kind: 'file' | 'dir' } based on stat; return { exists: false, kind: null } for ENOENT and for any isInsidePath() rejection (do NOT throw an error for traversal — return missing so the client can't probe via timing). Throw PanRpcError only for invalid argument (relativePath > 4 KiB) and unexpected IO failures on entries that exist. Strip ':<line>[:<col>]' suffix from relativePath before stat. NEVER call readdir or readlink — only stat and realpath. Export both readPathExists(input) and a readPathExistsEffect(input) wrapper for the Effect RPC handler. Comprehensive tests covering: file exists → kind=file, dir exists → kind=dir, missing → exists=false, '..' escape → exists=false (NOT error), symlink-to-outside-workspace → exists=false, positional suffix stripped, oversized relativePath → invalid argument error, workspace-not-found → WORKSPACE_NOT_FOUND error.
- AC: resolvePathExists({ issueId, relativePath: 'src/dashboard/server/server.ts' }) returns { exists: true, kind: 'file' } for an existing file under the workspace root
- AC: resolvePathExists({ issueId, relativePath: 'src/dashboard' }) returns { exists: true, kind: 'dir' } for an existing directory
- AC: resolvePathExists({ issueId, relativePath: 'does/not/exist.md' }) returns { exists: false, kind: null }
- AC: Path-traversal '../../etc/passwd' returns { exists: false, kind: null } — NOT a PanRpcError, so timing cannot leak whether the target exists
- AC: Symlink inside workspace that points to /etc/hostname returns { exists: false, kind: null } (realpath escape caught by the second isInsidePath check)
- AC: Positional suffix ':12' or ':12:5' is stripped from relativePath before stat — 'src/App.tsx:12' resolves the same as 'src/App.tsx'
- AC: relativePath longer than 4096 bytes throws PanRpcError code=INVALID_ARGUMENT without touching the filesystem
- AC: Service never calls readdir/readlink — confirmed by code review and by the test suite mocking node:fs/promises to assert only stat and realpath are reached
- AC: Issue ID that maps to no project throws PanRpcError code=WORKSPACE_NOT_FOUND (mirrors read-workspace-file behaviour)

title: pan-1457: Add pan.resolvePathExists contract to @panctl/contracts
description:
In packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts: add WS_METHODS.resolvePathExists = 'pan.resolvePathExists'. Add ResolvePathExistsInput = { issueId: IssueId, relativePath: Schema.String } and ResolvePathExistsResult = { exists: Schema.Boolean, kind: Schema.Literals(['file','dir']) or Schema.Null }. Add ResolvePathExistsRpc = Rpc.make(...). Add to PanRpcGroup. Run packages/contracts build so the published .d.ts surfaces the new types for downstream beads. No server or client wiring in this bead — only the contract.
- AC: WS_METHODS.resolvePathExists is exported and equals 'pan.resolvePathExists'
- AC: ResolvePathExistsInput accepts { issueId: IssueId, relativePath: string } and ResolvePathExistsResult exposes { exists: boolean, kind: 'file' | 'dir' | null }
- AC: ResolvePathExistsRpc is included in PanRpcGroup so handlers can be wired downstream
- AC: packages/contracts builds cleanly (npm run build under packages/contracts) and the new types appear in the generated .d.ts

title: pan-1459: Frontend: MCP tool renderer — server·tool header + pretty-printed input
description:
Detect MCP tools by `entry.toolTitle?.startsWith('mcp__')` in `ToolUseRow`. Parse the title `mcp__<server>__<tool>` into `{ server, tool }` and render a small header `<server> · <tool>`, then the input as pretty-printed JSON via the shared default-branch JSON code block (wrapped in `<CollapsibleToolContent maxLines={20}>`). If the title doesn't match the `mcp__server__tool` pattern, fall through to the default unknown-tool branch.
- AC: An entry with `toolTitle: 'mcp__playwright__browser_click'` renders a header `playwright · browser_click` above the pretty-printed input.
- AC: An MCP entry's input renders as multi-line pretty-printed JSON, collapsed by default when >20 lines.
- AC: A `toolTitle` starting with `mcp__` but not matching the `mcp__server__tool` shape falls through to the default unknown-tool renderer.
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx` covers one MCP entry and asserts the server·tool header text appears in the rendered DOM.

title: pan-1459: Frontend: TodoWrite renderer — checkbox-style todo list
description:
Implement the `TodoWrite` branch in `ToolUseRow`. Narrow `toolInput.todos: Array<{ content?: string; activeForm?: string; status?: 'pending'|'in_progress'|'completed' }>` defensively. Render each item as a row with a status glyph (☐ pending, ◐ in_progress, ☑ completed) and the text (prefer `activeForm` for `in_progress`, otherwise `content`). Apply muted color + strikethrough to `completed`. Wrap the list in `<CollapsibleToolContent maxItems={8}>` so very long todo lists collapse by default. Fall through to the pretty-JSON branch if `todos` is missing or not an array.
- AC: `TodoWrite` tool_use renders each todo as a row with a status glyph and text (using `activeForm` for in_progress items, `content` otherwise).
- AC: Completed items render with strikethrough and muted color.
- AC: Todo lists longer than 8 items are collapsed by default with an expand affordance.
- AC: A TodoWrite entry with malformed `todos` (missing field, not an array) falls through to pretty-JSON without crashing.
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx` covers a TodoWrite entry with mixed pending/in_progress/completed items and asserts the rendered DOM contains text for each.

title: pan-1459: Frontend: WebFetch / WebSearch renderers — URL or query chip
description:
Implement the `WebFetch` and `WebSearch` branches in `ToolUseRow`. `WebFetch`: render `input.url` as a clickable external link (target='_blank', rel='noopener noreferrer'). `WebSearch`: render `input.query` in quotes. Truncate long URLs/queries at ~120 chars with a title-attribute showing the full value.
- AC: `WebFetch` tool_use renders `input.url` as a clickable anchor that opens in a new tab.
- AC: `WebSearch` tool_use renders `input.query` in quotes.
- AC: Long URLs or queries (>120 chars) are visually truncated with the full value available via title attribute.
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx` covers one WebFetch and one WebSearch entry.

title: pan-1459: Frontend: Grep / Glob renderers — pattern + flags + path
description:
Implement the `Grep` and `Glob` branches in `ToolUseRow`. For `Grep`: render `input.pattern` in monospace, then a compact suffix listing meaningful flags (`-i`, `-l`, `output_mode`, `glob`, `type`, `path`). For `Glob`: render `input.pattern` in monospace, then a `· in <path>` suffix if `input.path` is present. Wrap the path side in a `MarkdownFileLink` chip when it's clearly a directory.
- AC: `Grep` tool_use renders `input.pattern` in monospace text followed by a compact suffix of meaningful flags (case-insensitive, output_mode, etc.).
- AC: `Glob` tool_use renders `input.pattern` and a `· in <path>` suffix when `input.path` is set; missing path renders pattern alone.
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx` covers one Grep entry (with flags) and one Glob entry (with path), asserting pattern text appears in the rendered output.

title: pan-1459: Frontend: Read / Write / Edit / NotebookEdit / MultiEdit renderers — MarkdownFileLink chip
description:
Implement the file-tool branches in `ToolUseRow`. For `Read`/`Write`/`Edit`/`MultiEdit`, narrow `toolInput.file_path: string` and render via `<MarkdownFileLink path={file_path} cwd={cwd} issueId={issueId} />`. For `Read`, also surface `toolInput.offset` / `toolInput.limit` as a compact `· lines N-M` suffix if present. For `NotebookEdit`, narrow `toolInput.notebook_path: string` (primary path) and optional `toolInput.cell_id`. If the expected path field is missing, fall through to the default JSON pretty-print branch.
- AC: `Read` tool_use renders a `MarkdownFileLink` chip for `input.file_path`; if `input.offset`/`input.limit` are present, a `· lines N-M` suffix appears next to the chip.
- AC: `Write` / `Edit` / `MultiEdit` each render a `MarkdownFileLink` chip for `input.file_path`.
- AC: `NotebookEdit` renders a `MarkdownFileLink` chip for `input.notebook_path` (its primary path field), with optional `cell_id` shown next to it.
- AC: A file-tool entry missing its expected path field falls through to the pretty-JSON fallback rather than crashing.
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx` covers one `Read` entry and one `Edit` entry and asserts the rendered DOM contains a `MarkdownFileLink`-class anchor for each `file_path`.

title: pan-1459: Frontend: Bash renderer — description header + syntax-highlighted shell block + copy + collapse
description:
Implement the `Bash` branch in `ToolUseRow`: when `toolTitle === 'Bash'`, narrow `toolInput.command: string` and `toolInput.description?: string`. Render `input.description` (if present) as a small header line above the command and `input.command` as a syntax-highlighted shell code block — reuse the existing `ChatMarkdown` pipeline that already renders fenced code via Shiki, OR render the same `<pre>` styling used for `entry.result` in the existing terminal branch (line 841). Real newlines must be preserved (no `\n` literals visible). Add a copy-to-clipboard button on the code block. Wrap the command in `<CollapsibleToolContent maxLines={20} maxChars={800}>` so multi-hundred-line scripts collapse by default.
- AC: `Bash` tool_use with `toolInput.description = 'Insert sibling conversation row'` and a multi-line `toolInput.command` renders the description as a header line and the command as a syntax-highlighted shell code block.
- AC: No `\"` or `\n` artifacts appear in the rendered DOM for any Bash command — real quotes and newlines are present.
- AC: A copy-to-clipboard button on the Bash code block copies the raw command string to the clipboard.
- AC: Bash commands exceeding 20 lines or 800 characters are collapsed by default with an expand affordance.
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx` exercises the Bash renderer against the conv/2210 example (description + multi-line node -e command) and asserts the rendered DOM contains the description text and a `<code>` block with real newlines.

title: pan-1459: Frontend: ToolUseRow dispatcher + CollapsibleToolContent wrapper + unknown-tool JSON fallback
description:
In `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/MessagesTimeline.tsx`, refactor `SimpleWorkEntryRow` so that when `entry.tone === 'tool' || entry.tone === 'error'`, the row delegates to a new `ToolUseRow` component (co-located in the same file or a sibling file under `components/chat/tool-renderers/`). `ToolUseRow` switches on `entry.toolTitle` and dispatches to per-tool renderers (registered in subsequent beads). The default branch — and the path for entries with no `toolInput` — renders pretty-printed JSON inside a code block: if `toolInput` is present, format it; otherwise if `detail` looks like JSON, parse and pretty-print it; otherwise show `detail` as plain text. Also introduce a shared `<CollapsibleToolContent>` wrapper component (lines threshold, char threshold, item-count threshold all configurable per call site) that every renderer will use to gate long content behind an expand affordance. Preserve all existing behavior for thinking-tone entries and for the tool-call-hiding feature (`hideToolCalls`).
- AC: A new `ToolUseRow` component dispatches on `entry.toolTitle` and is invoked from `SimpleWorkEntryRow` for `tone === 'tool' || tone === 'error'` entries.
- AC: Default branch of `ToolUseRow` renders `JSON.stringify(toolInput, null, 2)` inside a code block when `toolInput` is present.
- AC: Backwards-compat: when `toolInput` is absent but `detail` is JSON-parseable, the default branch parses `detail` and renders pretty JSON; if `detail` is non-JSON it renders as plain text.
- AC: A shared `<CollapsibleToolContent>` wrapper supports `maxLines`, `maxChars`, and `maxItems` thresholds and exposes an expand/collapse affordance when any threshold is exceeded.
- AC: `hideToolCalls` collapse behavior in `WorkLogGroup` still works (n tool calls were made → click to expand).
- AC: Snapshot test in `MessagesTimeline.test.tsx`: an unknown tool (`toolTitle: 'SomeNewTool'` with `toolInput: { foo: 'bar' }`) renders as pretty-printed JSON inside a code block, NOT as a single-line escaped string.

title: pan-1459: conversation-service.ts: pass raw block.input through `toolInput`; pretty-print `detail` for fallback
description:
In `src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts` around line 519-541: when building the `WorkLogEntry` for a tool_use block, populate `toolInput: block.input as Record<string, unknown> | undefined`. For the `detail` field, switch the format from single-line `JSON.stringify(input)` to multi-line `JSON.stringify(input, null, 2)` so the legacy fallback path on the frontend is also readable. Leave the FILE_EDIT_TOOLS diff-tracking branch at line 498 untouched (it reads `block.input` directly, independent of `detail`). Leave ExitPlanMode / EnterPlanMode special-cases at line 471-495 fully unchanged. Preserve the unresolved-result error-override branch at line 528-538 (errors still set `detail` to the error string for the `tone: 'error'` chip).
- AC: Every non-ExitPlanMode/EnterPlanMode tool_use WorkLogEntry has `toolInput` populated with the raw `block.input` object, byte-for-byte equal to the source.
- AC: `detail` for tool entries is multi-line pretty-printed JSON (`JSON.stringify(input, null, 2)`), not single-line.
- AC: FILE_EDIT_TOOLS diff-tracking at line 498 still fires for Edit/Write/NotebookEdit/MultiEdit and still records `{ tool, filePath }` from `block.input.file_path` — verified by an existing or new test.
- AC: ExitPlanMode still produces a `proposedPlan` (and does NOT emit a WorkLogEntry); EnterPlanMode still produces no WorkLogEntry — covered by a regression test in `conversation-service.test.ts`.
- AC: When a tool_result with `is_error: true` arrives for a known tool_use, the resulting WorkLogEntry's `tone === 'error'` and `detail` still contains the error text — covered by a test.

title: pan-1459: Add `toolInput` field to WorkLogEntry contract (Schema + frontend mirror)
description:
Add an optional `toolInput` field to the `WorkLogEntry` Effect Schema in `packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts` and to the plain-interface mirror in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/chat-types.ts`. Field type is an unknown record (`Schema.optional(Schema.Record({ key: Schema.String, value: Schema.Unknown }))` on the contracts side; `toolInput?: Record<string, unknown>` on the frontend mirror). No consumers touched in this bead — pure contract change. Rebuild `@panctl/contracts` so the workspace dist is current.
- AC: WorkLogEntry Schema in `packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts` exposes an optional `toolInput` field whose value is an unknown record.
- AC: Frontend mirror in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/chat-types.ts` declares the matching optional `toolInput?: Record<string, unknown>` field on `WorkLogEntry`.
- AC: `npm run typecheck` passes with no implicit-any or missing-property errors against the new field across server and frontend.
- AC: `packages/contracts` is rebuilt (`cd packages/contracts && npm run build`) so the workspace dist exposes the new field.

title: pan-1430: Module-level circuit breaker after retried 401 with single actionable log
description:
In src/lib/github-app.ts, add module-level state (`appDegradedUntil: number`, `appDegradedLogged: boolean`) and helpers `markAppDegraded(reason)` / `isAppDegraded()`. When the token-retry-on-401 retry path still returns 401, call `markAppDegraded` with the actionable diagnostic message: 'GitHub App credentials invalid (persistent 401 after token re-issue). Disabling App-backed calls for 5 minutes. To recover: `node scripts/create-github-app.mjs` (PANOPTICON_DEV_WEBHOOKS=1).'. While `isAppDegraded()` returns true (5-min TTL), `githubApi` throws immediately without a network call. The error message points at the same diagnostic command. When the TTL expires, log ONCE that App calls are re-enabled and clear `appDegradedLogged`. Unit-test that the breaker opens after a retried 401, stays open for the TTL, blocks `githubApi` calls in the meantime, and re-opens after expiry.
- AC: After token-retry-on-401 fails with a second 401, `markAppDegraded` is called with the diagnostic recovery command in the message
- AC: While degraded, `githubApi` throws immediately with the actionable error message and DOES NOT make a network call
- AC: The degraded-mode entry log is emitted EXACTLY ONCE per breaker open — repeated 401s while open do not re-log the same message
- AC: Breaker auto-clears after 5 minutes; the re-enable log is emitted exactly once when the breaker closes
- AC: While the breaker is open, the deacon's false-merged patrol completes its cycle without hammering GitHub — the per-issue 401 log spam is eliminated
- AC: Unit test in `src/lib/__tests__/github-app-circuit-breaker.test.ts` covers: breaker opens after retried 401; subsequent calls fail fast; only one log per open; auto-clears after TTL (use vi.useFakeTimers / vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync per the project rule on retry tests)
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

title: pan-1430: Retry githubApi once after re-issuing installation token on HTTP 401
description:
In the private `githubApi<T>` helper (src/lib/github-app.ts:142), detect HTTP 401 responses. On 401, call `generateInstallationToken` (the existing Effect-based generator) again to obtain a fresh installation token and retry the same request exactly once with the new token. Only retry on 401 — do not retry on any other status code. If the retry also returns 401, throw the same error the function currently throws (the circuit-breaker bead handles persistent failure). Add a unit test that mocks `fetch` and verifies the retry path.
- AC: On HTTP 401 from `githubApi`, `generateInstallationToken` is called again and the request is retried exactly once with the new token
- AC: If the retry succeeds (200/204), the caller receives the parsed response — no exception
- AC: If the retry also returns 401, the original error-throwing path runs (caller sees the 401 surface area unchanged from today, modulo the breaker bead)
- AC: Non-401 errors (403, 404, 5xx) do NOT trigger a retry — the existing single-shot error path is preserved
- AC: Unit test in `src/lib/__tests__/github-app-401-retry.test.ts` covers the 401→regenerate→200 success path and the 401→regenerate→401 failure path
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

title: pan-1430: Skip closed/canceled issues in reconcileFalseMerged patrol
description:
In `reconcileFalseMerged` (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:2917), before calling `getPullRequestState`, look up the issue's canonical tracker state using the same cached `gh issue view --json state,labels` pattern as `getAutoCloseOutCanonicalState` (deacon.ts:3766). Skip issues whose canonical state is `done` or `canceled` (i.e., the GitHub issue is closed). Reuse or share the existing `autoCloseOutCache` so the canonical-state lookup is shared across patrols, not re-queried per reconciler. Cover the new filter with a unit test in src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-false-merged-scope.test.ts.
- AC: `reconcileFalseMerged` skips issues whose tracker canonical state is `done` (closed without canceled label)
- AC: `reconcileFalseMerged` skips issues whose tracker canonical state is `canceled`
- AC: Active-pipeline issues (in_progress, in_review, verifying_on_main) are still checked — behavior preserved
- AC: The canonical-state lookup is cached (10-min TTL or shared autoCloseOutCache) — closed issues do not cause a new `gh issue view` call every 60s patrol
- AC: Unit test in `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/deacon-false-merged-scope.test.ts` verifies closed/canceled issues are not polled (no GitHub App call made)
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass

title: pan-1429: Update pan-tts SKILL.md PipeWire Suspend section with the kind→prepend table
description:
In sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md under 'PipeWire Suspend', replace the single '600ms of silence is prepended on cold starts' sentence with a short paragraph plus a small table covering the kind→prepend mapping (USB DAC 600ms, HDMI/DisplayPort 1500ms, Bluetooth 1000ms, unknown 1500ms). Cite PAN-1028 (USB DAC origin) and PAN-1429 (this fix). Note the trade-off recorded in hazard H5 (Bluetooth latency is codec-dependent; 1000ms is a baseline). Do not edit the .claude/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md mirror.
- AC: SKILL.md PipeWire Suspend section documents the four sink-kind buckets with their prepend durations in a readable form (table or bullet list).
- AC: Doc references both PAN-1028 (USB DAC origin) and PAN-1429 (DisplayPort/HDMI extension) so future readers can trace the history.
- AC: Doc notes that the probe only runs on cold start (sink SUSPENDED + freshly spawned pw-play) and that the warm path stays at 50ms with no pactl invocation.
- AC: Only sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md is edited; .claude/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md is left for `pan sync` to regenerate.

title: pan-1429: Add stdlib-unittest tests for sink-kind detection and prepend mapping
description:
Create sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests/test_tts_daemon.py using stdlib `unittest` and `unittest.mock.patch`. Patch `subprocess.run` to return canned `pactl list sinks` fixtures for each sink kind (USB DAC, HDMI/GPU, DisplayPort/GPU, Bluetooth A2DP, unknown/empty output, FileNotFoundError). Assert that `_probe_default_sink()` returns the correct (state, kind) tuple AND that `SINK_PREPEND_SECS[kind]` yields the expected prepend duration for each fixture. Also add a `tests/__init__.py` if needed so `python -m unittest discover` finds the module. Do not depend on pytest or any third-party package. Tests must run cleanly from the workspace root with `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests`.
- AC: test_tts_daemon.py exists at sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests/test_tts_daemon.py and is runnable with `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests` from the workspace root, no third-party deps required.
- AC: Test covers each sink kind (usb, hdmi, bluetooth, unknown) by patching subprocess.run with a realistic `pactl list sinks` long-form fixture and asserting the returned kind.
- AC: Test covers the DisplayPort case explicitly (a fixture whose Description mentions 'DisplayPort' or 'DP' rather than only 'HDMI') and asserts kind classification still resolves to 'hdmi'.
- AC: Test covers probe-failure paths (pactl FileNotFoundError, non-zero return, timeout) and asserts they resolve to (SUSPENDED, 'unknown') → 1500ms prepend.
- AC: Test asserts the full SINK_PREPEND_SECS mapping numerically: usb=0.60, hdmi=1.50, bluetooth=1.00, unknown=1.50.
- AC: All new tests pass under `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests` — zero failures, zero skips that hide AC coverage.

title: pan-1429: Add sink-kind probe and apply kind-aware prepend in play_audio()
description:
In sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py: (a) introduce a module-level SINK_PREPEND_SECS = {'usb': 0.60, 'hdmi': 1.50, 'bluetooth': 1.00, 'unknown': 1.50} mapping; (b) replace _get_default_sink_state() with _probe_default_sink() returning a (state, kind) tuple parsed from a single `pactl list sinks` (long-form) call — classify kind by Driver, Properties (device.bus, device.class, api.bluez5.transport), and Description keywords (HDMI / DisplayPort / DP / USB / Bluetooth); fall back to (state='SUSPENDED', kind='unknown') on any subprocess or parse error; (c) in play_audio(), when `created` is True call the new probe and pick prepend_secs from SINK_PREPEND_SECS[kind] for SUSPENDED sinks, retain 0.05s for RUNNING sinks and warm pw-play reuse; (d) replace the PAN-1028 inline comment block with a short pointer to SINK_PREPEND_SECS. Do NOT edit .claude/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py — that file is the sync mirror.
- AC: First TTS utterance after sink suspend is not clipped on DisplayPort/HDMI hardware (sink kind 'hdmi' yields 1500ms prepend).
- AC: Existing USB DAC behaviour preserved: sink kind 'usb' yields 600ms prepend (the original PAN-1028 value), not 1500ms.
- AC: Bluetooth sinks get 1000ms prepend; unrecognised sinks get the safe 1500ms default.
- AC: Warm-path prepend (RUNNING sink or reused pw-play subprocess) is unchanged at 50ms and does NOT call pactl.
- AC: Probe failures (pactl missing, non-zero return, parse error, timeout) return (SUSPENDED, unknown) so the 1500ms safe default applies — the daemon never raises into the playback path.
- AC: Only sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py is edited; .claude/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py is left for `pan sync` to regenerate (or regenerated in the same commit via the sync hook).

title: pan-1419: Startup and doctor cleanup for old closed-issue orphan agent dirs
description: Add a startup sweep and pan doctor rule for old orphan agent directories whose issue has been closed for more than seven days. The rule should report and/or clean historical residue such as old test/review/ship agent dirs without touching active issue state or Claude JSONL session files.
acceptance_criteria: Startup sweep handles closed issues older than 7d safely; pan doctor reports old closed-issue orphan dirs; tests cover old closed issue, recent closed issue, and active issue cases; cleanup never deletes Claude JSONL session files.
close_reason: Implemented startup and pan doctor cleanup for old closed-issue agent dirs with seven-day grace, live tmux protection, and JSONL protection.

title: pan-1419: Read source drops agents whose state dir is gone or issue is closed
description: Make the dashboard/read-model agent source honor closed issue state and state-dir deletion. Agents whose ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/state.json no longer exists must not remain in /api/agents or snapshot-derived stopped/restart lists, and agents for closed/completed issues must be excluded from stopped restart surfaces. Add regression tests covering a cached/projection agent with no state dir and a closed-issue agent.
acceptance_criteria: Read model/bootstrap and live read source remove agents with missing state dirs; closed/completed issues do not appear in stopped restart source; regression tests fail before the fix and pass after.
close_reason: Added shared read-source pruning for missing agent state files and closed issues, applied it to RPC snapshots and /api/agents, and covered the stale cache/closed issue cases with regression tests.

title: pan-1416: Integration tests for lockfile lifecycle, port-lock guard, canonical-path guard, and pan doctor multi-dashboard detection
description:
Add Vitest integration tests covering: (1) lockfile writer atomically creates/updates ~/.panopticon/dashboard.lock with the documented schema; heartbeats refresh; SIGTERM unlinks. (2) port-lock guard exits with 75 when a live lock conflicts and cwd is non-workspace; falls back to ephemeral port when cwd is workspace. (3) canonical-path guard fires on workspace cwd without API_PORT; does not fire when API_PORT is set; does not fire on project root cwd; does not fire when PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1. (4) pan doctor reports OK/WARN/ERROR appropriately based on simulated process+lockfile state. Use PANOPTICON_HOME=tmpdir to isolate state per test (matches the pattern in src/lib/__tests__/restart-lock.test.ts). For port-binding tests, spawn the real dashboard binary in a child process with fake ports (use port 0 and read the assigned port from health output) — do NOT mock process.cwd() at the unit level; use cwd: ... in spawn options.
- AC: tests/unit/lib/dashboard-lock.test.ts covers writer, refresh, reader, unlink, stale detection (>90s), and dead-pid detection
- AC: tests/integration/dashboard-port-lock.test.ts spawns two dashboards, asserts the second exits 75 from a non-workspace cwd and falls back to an ephemeral port from a workspace cwd
- AC: tests/integration/dashboard-canonical-path.test.ts spawns the dashboard with cwd=workspaces/feature-pan-* and no API_PORT, asserts it does not bind 3011 and instead listens on an ephemeral port (visible in stdout)
- AC: tests/unit/cli/doctor-dashboard-instances.test.ts asserts OK / WARN / ERROR transitions of the pan doctor check given mocked process lists and lockfile states
- AC: All new tests use isolated PANOPTICON_HOME via tmpdir and clean up after themselves; tests pass under the project's standard `npm test` invocation

title: pan-1416: pan doctor reports multiple dashboard instances and flags non-canonical primary-port owners
description:
Extend src/cli/commands/doctor.ts with a checkDashboardInstances() function (added to the existing checks pipeline). On Linux: read /proc/*/cmdline, filter for entries containing 'dist/dashboard/server.js', resolve each pid's cwd via /proc/<pid>/cwd. On darwin/win32: parse `ps -e -o pid,command` and skip cwd resolution. Cross-reference with ~/.panopticon/dashboard.lock if present to confirm canonical identity. Return: ok if exactly one canonical instance; warn if >1 instance running; error if a non-canonical (cwd matches workspace pattern) instance is bound to the configured primary port. Include the conflicting pid + cwd in the message and recommend `pan down && pan up` to recover.
- AC: pan doctor with one canonical dashboard running reports OK ('Dashboard Instances: 1 canonical instance on port 3011')
- AC: pan doctor with two dashboards (one canonical, one workspace, different ports) reports WARN listing both with their pids/cwds
- AC: pan doctor with a non-canonical dashboard (cwd under workspaces/feature-*) bound to the configured primary port reports ERROR identifying the impostor and suggesting `pan down && pan up`
- AC: Check runs successfully on Linux with /proc-based detection; falls back to ps-based detection without crashing on darwin/win32

title: pan-1416: Add startup port-lock guard that refuses to bind the primary port when a live canonical dashboard holds the lock
description:
Before binding the HTTP server in src/dashboard/server/server.ts (the HttpServerLive layer around L71-L92, or in main.ts before runServer is awaited), read the lockfile via readDashboardLock(). If the lock exists, lock.pid is alive (process.kill(pid, 0) succeeds), lock.heartbeatAt is within the last 90s, lock.port === intendedBindPort, AND lock.pid !== process.pid, fire the guard. When firing: log a loud multi-line warning identifying the conflicting pid/cwd, then either (a) if process.cwd() matches the workspace pattern, override config.port to 0 (kernel-assigned ephemeral) and continue, then log the actual chosen port after listening, or (b) if not in a workspace cwd, process.exit(75) (EX_TEMPFAIL) so pan up surfaces the collision. Skip the guard entirely when PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1 is set.
- AC: Starting a second dashboard against an alive lockfile, from a non-workspace cwd, exits with code 75 and prints a loud warning naming the conflicting pid and cwd
- AC: Starting a second dashboard against an alive lockfile, from a workspace cwd (e.g. workspaces/feature-pan-1416), falls back to an ephemeral port, logs the chosen port, and continues to serve
- AC: Starting a dashboard with a stale lockfile (heartbeatAt > 90s old OR lock.pid not alive) proceeds normally and overwrites the stale lock
- AC: Starting a dashboard with PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1 bypasses the guard entirely (used by workspace devcontainer peers)
- AC: Canonical pan up restart cycle works without delay: pan down → pan up completes within the normal time budget (graceful shutdown unlinks lockfile in time for the new bind)

title: pan-1416: Update docs/rules/role prompts to document the new guards and mandate explicit API_PORT for workspace dashboard spawns
description:
Update the following surfaces to document the guards and prescribe API_PORT discipline: (1) .claude/rules/dashboard-node22-only.md — add a 'Canonical Port Invariant' section. (2) docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md — add a paragraph about the dashboard.lock and the PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1 exemption. (3) docs/PANOPTICON_DEV_SOP.md — note the multi-instance pan doctor check. (4) sync-sources/rules/dashboard-node22-only.md — mirror the rule update. (5) sync-sources/skills/pan-doctor/SKILL.md — document the new dashboard-instances check. (6) CONTRIBUTING.md — call out the API_PORT requirement when spawning a dashboard from a workspace for testing. Pure doc work — do not modify source code in this bead.
- AC: .claude/rules/dashboard-node22-only.md and sync-sources/rules/dashboard-node22-only.md both include a Canonical Port Invariant section describing the cwd guard, the lockfile guard, and the PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1 exemption
- AC: docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md documents the dashboard.lock file (path, schema, heartbeat) and the devcontainer exemption
- AC: sync-sources/skills/pan-doctor/SKILL.md documents the new 'Dashboard Instances' check and what its WARN/ERROR statuses mean
- AC: CONTRIBUTING.md (or equivalent) tells contributors and agents that spawning a dashboard from a workspace requires setting API_PORT explicitly
- AC: No source code under src/ is modified in this bead — diff is documentation-only

title: pan-1416: Add startup canonical-path guard that refuses primary-port bind when cwd is a workspace path and API_PORT is unset
description:
Before binding the HTTP server (alongside the port-lock guard), test process.cwd() against /\/workspaces\/feature-[^/]+(\/|$)/. If it matches AND process.env.API_PORT is undefined AND process.env.PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON is unset, fire the guard. When firing: log a loud multi-line warning explaining the policy and the cwd, then override the bind port to 0 (ephemeral). After listening, log the actual chosen port so the operator (or agent) can connect to it. The canonical dashboard (cwd = project root) and devcontainer peers (PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1) are unaffected.
- AC: Starting node dist/dashboard/server.js from workspaces/feature-pan-* without API_PORT does NOT bind 3011; it binds an ephemeral port and logs the chosen port loudly
- AC: Starting node dist/dashboard/server.js from workspaces/feature-pan-* WITH explicit API_PORT=4511 binds 4511 as requested (guard does not fire)
- AC: Starting node dist/dashboard/server.js from the project root (no /workspaces/feature-* segment) binds 3011 as normal
- AC: Starting with PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1 from a workspace cwd bypasses the guard (devcontainer peer scenario)
- AC: pan dev (npm run dev) launched from project root is unaffected — Vite (3010) and dashboard (3011) come up normally

title: pan-1416: Add dashboard heartbeat lockfile writer (~/.panopticon/dashboard.lock)
description:
Create src/lib/dashboard-lock.ts exporting writeDashboardLock(), refreshHeartbeat(), unlinkDashboardLock(), and readDashboardLock(). The writer writes ~/.panopticon/dashboard.lock atomically (tmp+rename) with schema { pid, port, cwd, startedAt, heartbeatAt, isCanonical }. isCanonical = !cwdMatchesWorkspacePattern(). Heartbeat interval 30s via setInterval; cleared on shutdown. Wire startup into src/dashboard/server/main.ts after PANOPTICON_HOME is ensured (around L47). Wire teardown into the existing handleShutdownSignal SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP handler at L400-L415 so graceful exits unlink the file before process.exit(0). Use fs/promises (async only — this is dashboard server code, blocking FS is banned per .claude/rules/no-execsync-server.md). Match conventions from src/lib/restart-lock.ts and src/lib/smee.ts.
- AC: ~/.panopticon/dashboard.lock exists after dashboard startup with valid JSON containing pid, port, cwd, startedAt, heartbeatAt, isCanonical
- AC: heartbeatAt updates every 30s while the dashboard runs (verified by checking two snapshots > 30s apart)
- AC: Graceful SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP unlinks the lockfile before process exit (verified by tailing the file across a kill cycle)
- AC: isCanonical reflects whether process.cwd() matches the workspace pattern at startup
- AC: All file I/O uses fs/promises async APIs; no execSync, readFileSync, or writeFileSync introduced in dashboard server code paths (passes the existing dashboard-server lint rules)

title: pan-1419: pan doctor reports stopped-list misclassification when state files and tmux disagree
description:
Add a new check in src/cli/commands/doctor.ts that walks ~/.panopticon/agents/*/state.json, intersects with the live tmux agent session list (via getAgentSessions / getAgentSessionsSync), and applies the same classification rule used by classifyDashboardAgent (reusing the ORPHAN_PREFIX_PATTERN and ORPHAN_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS exports). For each agent whose state.status === 'running' AND tmux is alive, the doctor verifies that the dashboard's /api/agents response would classify it 'active' (not 'stopped'). On disagreement, emit a `warn` CheckResult naming the agent and the recommended remediation. The check must run in the existing doctor pipeline — wire it into the same checks array that runs alongside the others.
- AC: `pan doctor` runs the new check by default (no opt-in flag) and reports `ok` when no misclassification is present
- AC: When an agent has state.status='running' + live tmux but the dashboard's /api/agents would classify it 'stopped', the check emits a `warn` (or `fail`) CheckResult naming the agent IDs and pointing at PAN-1419
- AC: The check shares the orphan classifier exports (no duplicated prefix regex or age threshold)
- AC: Vitest covers two scenarios: clean (no drift → ok) and dirty (one running+tmux agent missing from active → warn)
close_reason: Implemented pan doctor stopped-list liveness drift check with classifier reuse and clean/dirty coverage.

title: pan-1419: Separate visual surface for orphan_test agents with copy-to-clipboard pan wipe hint
description:
Add a new low-prominence surface (collapsed by default, expandable) below or beside the StoppedAgentsBanner that lists agents whose classifier bucket is 'orphan_test'. The visual treatment must be visually distinct from the warning-toned stopped banner (e.g. muted/secondary). Each entry shows the issueId and a 'Copy `pan wipe <id>`' button that puts the literal command on the clipboard. No new destructive HTTP endpoint — the action is informational. Add a brief inline note explaining these are residue from prior test runs and cleanup is manual via pan wipe. Mount the new surface in the same parent that today renders StoppedAgentsBanner (likely the dashboard shell or TopBar wrapper).
- AC: A new component lists orphan_test agents with visual treatment distinct from the warning-toned stopped banner
- AC: Each row has a 'Copy `pan wipe <id>`' button that writes the literal command to the clipboard; clicking does NOT issue any HTTP request
- AC: The orphan surface is hidden when no orphan_test agents are present (no empty-state chrome)
- AC: Render-level test verifies the new component appears for the PAN-AC22 / PAN-TEST-1 fixture rows and does not appear for genuinely-stopped pipeline agents
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1419: Refactor StoppedAgentsBanner to use the classifier; running-with-tmux agents never appear
description:
Rewrite the filter chain in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/StoppedAgentsBanner.tsx to call `classifyDashboardAgent(a)` and keep only agents whose bucket is 'stopped'. Remove the now-redundant inline `lifecycle?.hasLiveTmuxSession` check (the classifier owns that rule). Keep the existing exclusions for completed/done resolutions and the 7-day recency window; they remain valid for the 'stopped' bucket. Add a render-level Vitest in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/StoppedAgentsBanner.test.tsx that feeds the store a mixed fixture (running+tmux, stopped+tmux, stopped+no-tmux+recent, orphan-prefix+old) and asserts the banner's count, ID list, and that Restart All is wired only to genuinely-stopped IDs.
- AC: StoppedAgentsBanner.tsx's filter delegates to classifyDashboardAgent and keeps only the 'stopped' bucket
- AC: An agent with state.status='running' and hasLiveTmuxSession=true NEVER appears in the banner's count or ID list
- AC: The banner's Restart All button only iterates IDs whose classifier bucket is 'stopped'
- AC: Render-level integration test asserts correct count and IDs for the mixed fixture (uses the same fixture shape as the classifier unit tests)
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1419: Frontend classifier returns active | stopped | orphan_test for every agent, with unit tests
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/agent-classifier.ts that exports `classifyDashboardAgent(agent): 'active' | 'stopped' | 'orphan_test'`. Rules: (1) if `agent.hasLiveTmuxSession === true` → 'active' (overrides any stale status); (2) if `agent.status === 'running' || agent.status === 'starting'` → 'active' (tmux-undefined fallback); (3) if `agent.issueId` matches the orphan-prefix regex `/^PAN-(AC|PI(-PROMPT)?|TEST|REVIEW|SHIP|SUB(REVIEW|SIGNAL)?)-?\d*$/` AND lastActivity/startedAt older than 7d → 'orphan_test'; (4) otherwise → 'stopped'. Export the orphan-prefix regex and the age threshold as named constants so they can be reused by pan doctor. Co-locate Vitest unit tests in src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/__tests__/agent-classifier.test.ts covering every mixed-state combination from the issue body (running+tmux, stopped+tmux, stopped+no-tmux+recent, stopped+no-tmux+old+orphan-prefix, etc.).
- AC: `classifyDashboardAgent` returns 'active' whenever hasLiveTmuxSession===true, regardless of status
- AC: `classifyDashboardAgent` returns 'orphan_test' only when BOTH the prefix matches AND the age exceeds 7d
- AC: `classifyDashboardAgent` returns 'stopped' for pipeline-role agents with status='stopped', tmux dead, and recent activity (the genuinely-failed-recently case)
- AC: Unit tests cover all five named cases from the issue body (PAN-1370 running+tmux; PAN-AC22 orphan; PAN-TEST-1 orphan; recently-stopped pipeline agent; ambiguous tmux-unknown running)
- AC: ORPHAN_PREFIX_PATTERN and ORPHAN_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS are named exports reused by the pan doctor check (no duplicate regex)
close_reason: Added classifyDashboardAgent with reusable orphan prefix and age constants plus unit coverage for live tmux, stale status, recent stopped, and old orphan-prefix cases.

title: pan-1419: Add hasLiveTmuxSession to AgentSnapshot and keep it fresh from bootstrap + deacon patrol
description:
Extend the AgentSnapshot Effect Schema in packages/contracts/src/types.ts to include `hasLiveTmuxSession: Schema.optional(Schema.Boolean)`. Populate this field at read-model bootstrap from `listRunningAgents()`'s `tmuxActive` (both fast/cache and slow paths in src/dashboard/server/read-model.ts). Update buildAgentStatusChangedPayload in src/dashboard/server/main.ts to include `hasLiveTmuxSession` (sourced from the deacon's tmux check at notification time). Extend the agent.status_changed reducer in packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts to fold the new field into the snapshot. In the deacon patrol (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts: recoverOrphanedAgents and its sibling resume scan), emit a status_changed event whenever observed tmuxActive disagrees with the last-known snapshot status — including the contrapositive of the existing orphan path (state.status='stopped' but tmux now alive → emit running). Update every test fixture that constructs AgentSnapshot literals to keep the suite green.
- AC: AgentSnapshot Effect Schema exposes `hasLiveTmuxSession?: boolean`; type-check passes across packages/contracts and all consumers
- AC: Read-model bootstrap (both projection-cache fast path and slow path) sets `hasLiveTmuxSession` for every agent it inserts into agentsById, sourced from listRunningAgents().tmuxActive
- AC: The agent.status_changed event payload carries `hasLiveTmuxSession`, and the reducer folds it into the snapshot without overwriting other fields
- AC: Deacon emits status_changed when its patrol observes a tmuxActive transition that contradicts the snapshot's status (covers both stopped→running revive and the existing running→stopped orphan path)
- AC: All existing AgentSnapshot fixture literals in vitest suites compile and the full suite passes (`npm test` clean)
close_reason: Implemented hasLiveTmuxSession propagation through contracts, read model bootstrap, deacon status events, enrichment events, and reducer/tests; verified typecheck, lint, and full test suite.

title: pan-1418: Integrate auto-merge banner into issue card and InspectorPanel
description:
In the existing issue card component and the InspectorPanel, render MergeAutoMergeCountdown inline above the MergeButton when issue.autoMergeScheduled is non-null. Hide the MergeButton when the banner is shown (operator should not see two ways to merge at the same time); or render both with the banner taking primary visual weight. Decision: hide MergeButton during auto-merge cooldown — operator cancels via banner, then MergeButton returns. Pick the 1-2 spots where MergeButton is rendered today and add the conditional banner there.
- AC: Issue card renders MergeAutoMergeCountdown when autoMergeScheduled is set; MergeButton is hidden during that window
- AC: InspectorPanel renders the same banner+conditional-button pattern for the focused issue
- AC: After cancel or execute, MergeButton reappears (or is hidden if mergeStatus=merged) — verified via browser test
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1418: Add sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md wrapper for the new CLI verb
description:
Create sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md following the Skills <-> CLI convention in docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md. Documents the `pan merge cancel <id>` subcommand: what it does, when to use it (during auto-merge cooldown), example output, the relationship to the dashboard banner Cancel button. Cross-checked by scripts/lint-skills.sh.
- AC: SKILL.md exists at sync-sources/skills/pan-merge/SKILL.md with frontmatter (name, description, type=cli-wrapper)
- AC: Skill documents the `cancel <issueId>` subcommand with its actual flags and exit codes — matches `pan merge --help` output exactly
- AC: npm run lint passes (scripts/lint-skills.sh sees no drift between the skill and the CLI)
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1418: Extend issue read-model with autoMergeScheduled field
description:
Add `autoMergeScheduled: { executeAt: string; scheduledAt: string } | null` to the issue read-model shape (read-model.ts and the corresponding frontend store type). On snapshot bootstrap, populate from getPendingAutoMerges(). On merge.auto.scheduled event, set to { executeAt, scheduledAt }. On merge.auto.cancelled/executed/aborted events, set to null. The frontend issue type in packages/contracts mirrors the field.
- AC: Snapshot returned by getSnapshot RPC includes autoMergeScheduled for any issue with a pending row
- AC: Live merge.auto.* events update the in-memory read-model and broadcast to subscribed clients
- AC: TypeScript types in packages/contracts include autoMergeScheduled; typecheck passes on both server and frontend
close_reason: Projected pending auto-merge schedule state onto issue snapshots and live merge.auto event folds

title: pan-1418: HTTP integration test for the cancel endpoint
description:
