Add an integration test that boots a test dashboard (real HTTP, real SQLite, mocked triggerMerge), schedules a pending auto-merge via the scheduler API, calls POST /api/issues/PAN-XXXX/merge/cancel, verifies 200 + {cancelled:true}, re-calls and verifies {cancelled:false}, and tests the 409 path by transitioning the row to 'executing' before calling cancel.
- AC: Test boots a real HTTP server with the route and a temp SQLite file
- AC: Three test cases: pending->cancelled (200, true), no-pending (200, false), executing (409)
- AC: Test cleans up temp SQLite file in afterEach to avoid cross-test bleed
close_reason: Implemented real HTTP/SQLite cancel endpoint integration coverage

title: pan-1418: Add MergeAutoMergeCountdown banner component
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/MergeAutoMergeCountdown.tsx forking the GraceCountdown.tsx pattern. Props: { issueId: string; executeAt: string; onCancel?: () => void }. Displays 'Auto-merging in M:SS' with a Cancel button. Tick every 1000ms updating remainingSeconds from (executeAt - now). On Cancel click: POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel, show optimistic 'Cancelling…' state, rely on merge.auto.cancelled event to remove banner. Show 'Connection lost' state when WS disconnects (read from existing connection store).
- AC: Component renders 'Auto-merging in M:SS' format that updates every second
- AC: Cancel button calls POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel; loading state shows while in-flight
- AC: When the WS is disconnected, banner shows a 'connection lost' indicator and the cooldown text reads '(local view — host may be offline)'
- AC: Component unmounts cleanly when executeAt passes or autoMergeScheduled becomes null
close_reason: Implemented MergeAutoMergeCountdown banner with tests

title: pan-1418: Add `pan merge cancel <id>` CLI command
description:
Create src/cli/commands/merge.ts following the approveCommand pattern. Export a registerMergeCommands(program) function that adds `pan merge` (subgroup) with a `cancel <issueId>` subcommand. Implementation fetches the local dashboard URL (configured the same way other CLI commands do — via existing dashboard URL resolver), POSTs to /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel, and prints a status line: 'Cancelled auto-merge for PAN-XXXX' or 'No pending auto-merge for PAN-XXXX' or 'Cannot cancel — auto-merge for PAN-XXXX is already executing'. Register via src/cli/index.ts.
- AC: `pan merge cancel PAN-1234` succeeds against a pending auto-merge and prints a confirmation
- AC: `pan merge cancel PAN-1234` is idempotent — running it twice prints 'No pending auto-merge' on the second call
- AC: Exit codes: 0 on cancel success, 0 on idempotent no-op, 2 on 409 (already executing), 1 on unexpected error
- AC: `pan merge --help` displays the new `cancel` subcommand with its description
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1418: Emit unmissable startup log when auto-merge is enabled
description:
In dashboard main.ts after AutoMergeScheduler.start(), log a high-visibility banner whenever any project has merge.autoMerge.enabled=true: '[merge-auto] AUTO-MERGE ENABLED for project(s): <comma-list>'. Repeated at every boot. Mitigates hazard H5.
- AC: When at least one project has auto-merge enabled, dashboard boot prints one [merge-auto] AUTO-MERGE ENABLED line listing the project key(s)
- AC: When no project has it enabled, no line is printed (no log spam in the default case)
close_reason: Added dashboard boot banner listing projects with auto-merge enabled while staying silent for the default disabled case

title: pan-1418: Unit tests for AutoMergeScheduler with fake timers and mocked DB
description:
Tests in src/dashboard/server/services/__tests__/auto-merge-scheduler.test.ts using vi.useFakeTimers() per CLAUDE.md test rule. Cover: maybeSchedule schedules and persists; double-schedule is a no-op; cancel during cooldown clears timer and updates DB; fire after cooldown calls triggerMerge when gates pass; fire after cooldown aborts when gates fail; boot recovery re-arms pending; boot recovery aborts stale; cancel-during-execute race (executing wins); PANOPTICON_DISABLE_AUTO_MERGE makes start() a no-op.
- AC: Tests use vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — no real setTimeout delays per CLAUDE.md retry-timer rule
- AC: Coverage includes both happy path and all four gate-failure reasons (ready, label, ci, timeout)
- AC: Cancel-vs-fire race test asserts that exactly one of cancel/fire wins (DB row reflects the winner), no double triggerMerge invocation
- AC: Boot recovery test verifies stale rows (executeAt > maxStaleMinutes ago) emit merge.auto.aborted and do NOT fire
close_reason: Added fake-timer AutoMergeScheduler unit tests covering schedule/cancel/fire, gate failures, boot recovery, stale abort, race behavior, and disable env

title: pan-1418: Implement gate re-validation logic in scheduler.fire()
description:
Inside scheduler.fire(), after the atomic pending->executing transition succeeds, run gate checks in this order: (1) re-read review-status, fail if readyForMerge != true with reason='no-longer-ready'; (2) if requireNoBlockerLabels is non-empty, call getPrLabels(prUrl) and fail if any blocker label is present with reason='blocker-label:<label>'; on timeout fail with reason='label-check-timeout'; (3) if requireGitHubCiPassing OR requireAllCommitStatusChecks, call getCombinedCommitStatus(prUrl) and fail if not passing with reason='ci-failing'; on timeout fail with reason='ci-check-timeout'. On any gate fail, call markAutoMergeAborted(issueId, reason), emit merge.auto.aborted, emit TTS, and return WITHOUT calling triggerMerge.
- AC: All four gate checks (ready, labels, CI, status checks) are evaluated in sequence; first failure short-circuits
- AC: On gate failure, triggerMerge is NOT called and DB row status = 'aborted' with reason captured
- AC: Gate-check timeouts (5s) result in fail-closed behavior (abort, not merge)
- AC: On all gates passing, triggerMerge(issueId) is called and markAutoMergeExecuted is recorded after triggerMerge returns success
close_reason: Aligned AutoMergeScheduler fire-time gate revalidation with required fail-closed abort reasons and tests

title: pan-1418: Emit TTS at schedule, T-30s, and execute/cancel/abort
description:
Wire emitActivityTtsSync() from src/lib/activity-logger.ts into the scheduler. On maybeSchedule success: priority=1 'Auto merging PAN-XXXX in N minutes — say pan merge cancel PAN-XXXX to abort'. On a separate setTimeout fired at T-30s when cooldownMinutes > 1: priority=2 'Auto merge of PAN-XXXX in 30 seconds'. On cancel: priority=1 'Auto merge of PAN-XXXX cancelled'. On execute: priority=1 'Auto merging PAN-XXXX now'. On abort (gate failure): priority=1 'Auto merge of PAN-XXXX aborted — <reason>'. Each utterance limited to <140 chars per the emitActivityTtsSync contract.
- AC: Scheduling emits exactly one schedule TTS utterance with priority=1 and source='dashboard'
- AC: When cooldownMinutes > 1, exactly one T-30s reminder utterance is emitted with priority=2; not emitted when cooldownMinutes <= 1
- AC: Cancel, execute, and abort each emit exactly one priority=1 utterance with the issueId normalized to spoken form (PAN-1418 -> 'pan 1418')
- AC: All utterances < 140 chars
close_reason: Implemented bounded spoken auto-merge TTS for schedule, T-30s reminder, cancel, execute, and abort

title: pan-1418: Define and emit merge.auto.* event types
description:
Add four event types to the EventStore domain event union (packages/contracts or the dashboard server's event types): merge.auto.scheduled { issueId, executeAt, cooldownSeconds }, merge.auto.cancelled { issueId, reason, cancelledBy }, merge.auto.executed { issueId }, merge.auto.aborted { issueId, gateFailureReason }. AutoMergeScheduler emits all four via eventStore.append() at the appropriate state transitions. Update the read-model reducer (src/dashboard/server/read-model.ts) to apply these events to the autoMergeScheduled field.
- AC: All four event types are added to the typed event union and pass typecheck on producers and consumers
- AC: Scheduler appends each event at the correct transition (scheduled on maybeSchedule success, cancelled on cancel, executed after triggerMerge success, aborted on gate failure)
- AC: Frontend store (Zustand) applies events to update issue.autoMergeScheduled state; banner reflects state transitions live
close_reason: Added typed merge.auto events, scheduler payloads, and shared reducer handling for autoMergeScheduled

title: pan-1418: Add POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel HTTP route
description:
Add a new Effect HTTP route in src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts (alongside the existing /api/issues/:issueId/merge): POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel { reason?: string }. Validates issueId format. Calls AutoMergeScheduler.cancel(issueId, reason ?? 'manual', 'api'). Returns 200 with { cancelled: true } on successful cancel, 200 with { cancelled: false } if no pending row (idempotent), 409 if status is already 'executing'.
- AC: POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel exists and validates issueId format like the existing merge endpoint
- AC: Returns 200 {cancelled:true} when a pending auto-merge exists and is cancelled
- AC: Returns 200 {cancelled:false} when no pending auto-merge exists (idempotent)
- AC: Returns 409 when auto-merge is already in 'executing' state
close_reason: Added POST /api/issues/:issueId/merge/cancel with idempotent cancel and executing conflict handling

title: pan-1418: Wire scheduler.maybeSchedule() into the readyForMerge false->true transition
description:
In src/lib/review-status.ts at the existing readyForMerge false->true transition site (~line 373), call into the AutoMergeScheduler. Use a registered handler pattern (setAutoMergeOnReadyHandler) so review-status.ts does not statically import the dashboard service — avoiding a circular dep (review-status is in src/lib, scheduler is in src/dashboard/server/services). The dashboard's main.ts boot registers the handler when starting the scheduler.
- AC: review-status.ts exports setAutoMergeOnReadyHandler(fn); when readyForMerge transitions false->true and a handler is registered, it is invoked with (issueId, projectKey)
- AC: Handler invocation is fire-and-forget (Promise.resolve().then) — review-status updates do not block on scheduler decisions
- AC: Dashboard main.ts boot calls setAutoMergeOnReadyHandler(scheduler.maybeSchedule) after scheduler.start()
- AC: When no handler is registered (CLI invocations, tests), review-status updates proceed normally and no scheduling happens
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1418: Implement AutoMergeScheduler service
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/services/auto-merge-scheduler.ts exporting an AutoMergeScheduler service with: start() (initialize at dashboard boot, gated by PANOPTICON_DISABLE_AUTO_MERGE), stop() (clear all timers on shutdown), maybeSchedule(issueId, projectKey) (called when readyForMerge transitions false->true; checks config.enabled, no existing pending row, mergeStatus null/pending; on success persists row via schedulePendingAutoMerge, sets in-memory timeout, emits merge.auto.scheduled event, emits TTS), cancel(issueId, reason, cancelledBy) (clears in-memory timeout, calls cancelPendingAutoMerge, emits merge.auto.cancelled, emits TTS), and the internal fire(issueId) handler that runs at executeAt: atomically transitions to executing (returns if already cancelled), re-validates gates, calls triggerMerge or emits merge.auto.aborted. Maintains Map<issueId, NodeJS.Timeout> for live arms. Wall-clock based: timer set via setTimeout(fire, executeAt - now); if now > executeAt (stale at boot) and within maxStaleMinutes, fire immediately, otherwise abort with reason='stale'.
- AC: start() honors PANOPTICON_DISABLE_AUTO_MERGE=1 — when set, start() is a no-op and no scheduling happens
- AC: maybeSchedule returns early without scheduling when config.enabled=false for the project
- AC: maybeSchedule never schedules when mergeStatus is in ('merged','merging','queued','failed') — preventing the idempotency loop H2
- AC: Boot recovery: start() reads getPendingAutoMerges() and re-arms each one; rows with executeAt more than maxStaleMinutes in the past are aborted with reason='stale' (event + TTS) instead of fired
- AC: fire(issueId) uses markAutoMergeExecuting atomic transition — if cancel won the race, fire returns without calling triggerMerge
- AC: fire(issueId) re-validates gates (readyForMerge, no blocker labels via getPrLabels, CI passing via getCombinedCommitStatus when requireGitHubCiPassing); on any gate failure emits merge.auto.aborted with a specific reason and does NOT call triggerMerge
- AC: Logging: every state transition logs '[merge-auto] <ISSUEID> <event>' with relevant context (scheduled execute_at, cancel reason, abort reason)
- AC: stop() clears all in-memory timers but does NOT touch DB rows — pending rows survive for boot recovery
close_reason: Added AutoMergeScheduler service with boot recovery, persisted cooldown timers, gate revalidation, event/TTS emission, and merge trigger reuse

title: pan-1418: Document the merge.autoMerge config block with the solo-workflow warning
description:
Add a new Mintlify page configuration/auto-merge.mdx describing the merge.autoMerge config block with every key, defaults, and a prominent 'Solo workflows only — do not enable in shared/team Panopticon instances' warning callout. Cross-link from CLAUDE.md (Configuration section or near the merge flow notes) and from the pan-merge skill. Add the page to docs.json so it appears in the nav.
- AC: configuration/auto-merge.mdx documents every merge.autoMerge.* key with default and example
- AC: A 'Solo workflows only' warning is prominently displayed with the rationale (preserves the 'humans only merge' safety intent at the gate level, not the click level)
- AC: docs.json updated to include the new page in the nav under Configuration
- AC: CLAUDE.md gets a one-paragraph reference linking to the new page; pan-merge skill cross-links to it
close_reason: Documented merge.autoMerge config, solo-workflow warning, nav entry, CLAUDE reference, and pan-merge skill link

title: pan-1418: Add getCombinedCommitStatus(prUrl) and getPrLabels(prUrl) forge-aware helpers
description:
Add two helpers in src/lib/forge.ts (or extend the existing forge adapter): getCombinedCommitStatus(prUrl): Promise<{ passing: boolean; checks: Array<{ name; conclusion }>; queriedAt }> and getPrLabels(prUrl): Promise<string[]>. GitHub implementation uses `gh pr checks <url> --json` and `gh pr view <url> --json labels`. Both have 5s timeout and throw a typed error on timeout/failure. GitLab adapter returns { passing: true, checks: [] } as a no-op stub for v1 with a comment pointing to the v2 issue.
- AC: getCombinedCommitStatus returns passing=true only when every check has conclusion in ('success','neutral','skipped'); failing/cancelled/timed_out -> passing=false
- AC: Both helpers timeout after 5s and throw a typed TimeoutError; callers can distinguish timeout from real failures
- AC: Both helpers use execAsync (NOT execSync) per CLAUDE.md async tmux/exec rules
- AC: Unit tests with mocked exec cover: all-green, mixed-success, mixed-failure, timeout, missing-pr
close_reason: Added async forge PR checks and label helpers with typed timeouts and tests

title: pan-1418: Add auto_merge SQLite table and CRUD helpers
description:
Create src/lib/database/auto-merge-db.ts with a new auto_merge table: (issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, scheduled_at TEXT NOT NULL, execute_at TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK status IN ('pending','executing','cancelled','executed','aborted','failed'), cancel_reason TEXT, abort_reason TEXT). Export: schedulePendingAutoMerge(issueId, executeAt) (idempotent — fails if existing pending row), cancelPendingAutoMerge(issueId, reason): boolean (atomic UPDATE WHERE status='pending'), markAutoMergeExecuting(issueId): boolean (atomic transition from pending), markAutoMergeExecuted(issueId), markAutoMergeAborted(issueId, reason), markAutoMergeFailed(issueId, reason), getPendingAutoMerges(): Row[] (for boot recovery), getAutoMergeStatus(issueId): Row | null.
- AC: auto_merge table created via migration on dashboard boot; idempotent (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS)
- AC: schedulePendingAutoMerge returns false (no-op) when a pending row already exists for the same issueId
- AC: cancelPendingAutoMerge atomically updates only rows where status='pending'; returns true if 1 row updated, false otherwise
- AC: markAutoMergeExecuting atomically transitions pending->executing; returns false if not pending (cancel won the race)
- AC: Unit tests cover all helpers, including the race window between cancelPending and markAutoMergeExecuting
close_reason: Implemented auto_merge schema migration, CRUD helpers, and race coverage

title: pan-1418: Add merge.autoMerge.* config schema with defaults and project override merge
description:
Extend YamlConfig in src/lib/config-yaml.ts with a `merge` top-level slot containing `autoMerge: { enabled, cooldownMinutes, maxStaleMinutes, requireGitHubCiPassing, requireAllCommitStatusChecks, requireNoBlockerLabels }`. Add normalized defaults to DEFAULT_CONFIG. Wire per-project .pan.yaml merge so project values override global (replacing arrays, not deep-merging) the same way other config sections work. Expose a typed accessor `getAutoMergeConfig(projectKey?: string): NormalizedAutoMergeConfig` for downstream consumers.
- AC: YamlConfig.merge.autoMerge interface defined with all 6 keys and their TypeScript types
- AC: DEFAULT_CONFIG includes merge.autoMerge with enabled=false, cooldownMinutes=5, maxStaleMinutes=60, requireGitHubCiPassing=true, requireAllCommitStatusChecks=true, requireNoBlockerLabels=['needs-design','needs-discussion','do-not-merge','wip']
- AC: cooldownMinutes clamps to >= 1; maxStaleMinutes clamps to >= cooldownMinutes; both emit a console warning on clamp
- AC: Project-level .pan.yaml merge.autoMerge override fully replaces global value for the project (no deep-merge that would silently inherit a global list)
- AC: getAutoMergeConfig(projectKey) returns the normalized merged config for a project; unit tests cover global-only, project-only, and global+project-override cases
close_reason: Fixed inspection block by making getAutoMergeConfig Effect-native/server-safe and adding async accessor tests.

title: pan-1417: Make POST /api/review/:id/request non-destructive on already-passed reviews; add ?force and ?nudge flags
description:
Modify the `reviewStatus === 'passed'` branch in `src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts` (around line 3677, inside the `postWorkspaceRequestReviewRoute` handler) to add a code-drift safety gate. Parse `force` and `nudge` flags from both query string and JSON body (mirror the /trigger route's pattern at workspaces.ts:3395-3398). Read the workspace HEAD via `git rev-parse HEAD` — use `flyExecCmd` for `workspaceInfo.isRemote`, plain `execAsync` for local — and compare against `existingStatus.reviewedAtCommit`. Branch logic: (a) `force=true` → preserve existing `shouldTreatAsRerun` destructive path, log `[request-review] FORCE: full reset requested by operator for <id>`; (b) `nudge=true` AND reviewStatus=passed AND testStatus=passed → re-emit `test.passed` via the route's existing `EventStoreService` (yield* pattern, matching workspaces.ts:3987-3991), do NOT touch any status fields, log `[request-review] NUDGE: re-emitting test.passed for <id> without state reset`, return `{ success: true, nudged: true, message }`; (c) `nudge=true` AND tests have not passed → HTTP 400 with `{ success: false, error: 'Cannot nudge — tests have not passed', hint: 'Use ?force=true for a full re-review or wait for tests to complete' }`; (d) no flags AND `reviewedAtCommit` is defined AND matches current HEAD (no drift) → HTTP 200 + `{ success: false, noCodeDrift: true, error: 'No code drift since review passed', hint: 'Use ?nudge=true to re-emit lifecycle events without resetting state, or ?force=true to force a full re-review' }`, log `[request-review] REFUSED: no code drift since review passed for <id> at <short-sha>`; (e) no flags AND drift detected OR `reviewedAtCommit` undefined OR HEAD lookup failed → fall through to the existing `shouldTreatAsRerun` destructive path unchanged. Wrap the HEAD lookup in try/catch — failure falls through to (e). Add vitest coverage in `src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/request-review-nudge.test.ts` exercising every branch above with mocked review-status, mocked git-info helper, and mocked EventStoreService.
- AC: When reviewStatus === 'passed' AND reviewedAtCommit === currentHeadSha AND neither force nor nudge is set, the endpoint returns HTTP 200 + { success: false, noCodeDrift: true, error: 'No code drift since review passed', hint: <mentions both ?force=true and ?nudge=true> } and does NOT mutate review-status (verified by reading status before and after).
- AC: When `?nudge=true` is sent AND reviewStatus === 'passed' AND testStatus === 'passed', the endpoint emits exactly one `test.passed` event via EventStoreService (payload includes the normalized issueId), returns HTTP 200 + { success: true, nudged: true, message }, and does NOT write to reviewStatus, testStatus, verificationStatus, autoRequeueCount, mergeStatus, readyForMerge, or any other status field.
- AC: When `?nudge=true` is sent AND testStatus !== 'passed', the endpoint returns HTTP 400 + { success: false, error: 'Cannot nudge — tests have not passed', hint } and does NOT emit a `test.passed` event or mutate any status field.
- AC: When `?force=true` is sent AND reviewStatus === 'passed', the endpoint preserves the current destructive `shouldTreatAsRerun` behavior: reviewStatus → pending, testStatus → pending, mergeStatus → pending, readyForMerge → false, autoRequeueCount → 0, verificationCycleCount → 0, verificationStatus → pending, notes cleared; dispatches review role; logs `[request-review] FORCE: full reset requested by operator for <id>`. Existing log line `[request-review] ${issueId}: forcing full review/test rerun from passed state` is preserved verbatim.
- AC: When reviewStatus === 'passed' AND reviewedAtCommit is defined AND currentHeadSha !== reviewedAtCommit (drift detected) AND no flags are set, the endpoint falls through to the existing destructive rerun path (same as `?force=true` outcome). This preserves correct behavior for the merge-agent rebase-conflict curl hint and for any operator who legitimately rebased before re-requesting.
- AC: When reviewStatus === 'passed' AND reviewedAtCommit is undefined (legacy row) AND no flags are set, the endpoint falls through to the existing destructive rerun path. When the HEAD lookup throws (e.g. workspace path missing, git unavailable, remote VM unreachable), the endpoint also falls through to the destructive path with a warning log — never a 5xx.
- AC: Both `force` and `nudge` are accepted from either the URL query string (?force=true / ?nudge=true) OR the JSON request body ({ force: true } / { nudge: true }). When both are sent, `force` takes precedence (full reset wins over nudge). When neither is sent and the no-drift gate refuses, the response hint mentions BOTH flags so operators can pick the right one.
- AC: Remote workspaces (workspaceInfo.isRemote === true) read HEAD via `flyExecCmd(vmName, 'cd <path> && git rev-parse HEAD')` with a 30s timeout, mirroring the existing pattern at workspaces.ts:3495-3503. Local workspaces use plain `execAsync('git rev-parse HEAD', { cwd: workspacePath })`. Both are wrapped in try/catch and fall through on failure (AC6).
- AC: Vitest unit-test file at `src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/request-review-nudge.test.ts` exercises every branch (AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC7) against the postWorkspaceRequestReviewRoute handler with mocked getReviewStatusSync, mocked setReviewStatus, mocked EventStoreService, mocked getWorkspaceInfoForIssue, and mocked execAsync. Tests use `vi.useFakeTimers()` if any retry/timeout paths are exercised. `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass on the workspace after the change.
- AC: Regression: `pan review request <id>` CLI continues to work for its primary use case (reviewStatus === 'blocked' or 'failed' — agent re-requesting after fixing feedback). That path is untouched because the new gate only fires on reviewStatus === 'passed'. No CLI changes are required and no existing CLI tests need updating.
close_reason: Implemented non-destructive passed-review request handling with force/nudge flags, drift detection, and tests

title: pan-1415: Unit tests for stage 1/2/3 transitions and gate cascade

Each test asserts on (a) which agent helper was called, (b) what was persisted to state, (c) what was logged.

title: pan-1415: Implement checkStuckAgentRemediation patrol with 3-stage escalation
description:
Create `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts` exporting `async function checkStuckAgentRemediation(opts?: { now?: number }): Promise<string[]>`. The function iterates running work agents (`listRunningAgentsSync()` filtered by `state.role === 'work'` and `state.status === 'running'`), and for each one applies the gate cascade described below. Returns the same `string[]` action list shape as other deacon patrols.

Gate cascade (all must pass before stage logic runs):
  1. Config: skip if `stuck_remediation.enabled === false`.
  2. Session: skip if `!sessionExistsSync(agentId)`.
  3. Status: skip if `state.paused || state.troubled || state.completedAt`.
  4. Tracker: skip if `reviewStatus.stuck`, `reviewStatus.deaconIgnored`, `reviewStatus.mergeStatus === 'merged'`, or `reviewStatus.readyForMerge`.
  5. Dead-end overlap: skip if `reviewStatus.reviewStatus` is `'blocked'` or `'failed'`, or `reviewStatus.verificationStatus === 'failed'`, or `reviewStatus.testStatus === 'failed'` (let `checkDeadEndAgents` handle those).
  6. Idle: skip if `!isAgentIdleForNudge(agentId)` (active/waiting-on-human/uninitialized-fresh agents are not idle).
  7. Bead overlap: skip if `bd ready -l <issueLabel>` returns any rows in the agent's workspace (let `nudgeIdleWorkAgentsWithOpenBeads` handle those).

State handling:
  - Read `runtime.lastActivity` from `getAgentRuntimeStateSync(agentId)`. If missing, skip.
  - Read stored `stuckState = readStuckRemediationState(agentId)`.
  - If `stuckState` exists AND `runtime.lastActivity > stuckState.firstStuckAt` → `clearStuckRemediationState(agentId)` and continue (treat as fresh stall).
  - Compute `idleMinutes = (now - runtime.lastActivity) / 60_000`.

Stage transitions (highest applicable stage wins):
  - `idleMinutes >= stage3_minutes` AND `(stuckState?.lastStage ?? 0) < 3` → call `markAgentTroubled(agentId)`. Write `{ lastStage: 3, lastStageAt: now, firstStuckAt: stuckState?.firstStuckAt ?? runtime.lastActivity }`. Log `[deacon] stuck-remediation stage=3 issue=<id> idleMin=<n> action=marked-troubled`. Push to actions.
  - Else if `idleMinutes >= stage2_minutes` AND `(stuckState?.lastStage ?? 0) < 2` → call `resumeAgent(agentId, recoveryMessage)`. If `result.success` → write `{ lastStage: 2, lastStageAt: now, firstStuckAt }` and log/push action. If `!result.success` → DO NOT advance stored stage; log failure and let the next patrol retry.
  - Else if `idleMinutes >= stage1_minutes` AND `(stuckState?.lastStage ?? 0) < 1` → call `messageAgent(agentId, pokeMessage)`. Write `{ lastStage: 1, lastStageAt: now, firstStuckAt: stuckState?.firstStuckAt ?? new Date(runtime.lastActivity).toISOString() }`. Log/push action.
  - Otherwise no-op (waiting for the next threshold).

Messages (deterministic strings, no per-call variability):
  - Stage 1 poke: `"You appear stuck — no tool calls for ${idleMinutes} min. If your implementation is complete, run \`pan done ${issueId}\`. Otherwise reply with a one-line summary of what you're waiting on, then continue."`
  - Stage 2 resume: `"Resuming after auto-detected stall (${idleMinutes} min idle). Review your last work and decide whether to continue or signal done with \`pan done ${issueId}\`."`
  - Stage 3 has no message — it's silent gate-marking.

All exceptions wrapped per-agent in try/catch — one failing agent must not stop the loop for the others. Use `console.error` + `logDeaconEventSync` for unexpected throws.
- AC: New file `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation.ts` exports `checkStuckAgentRemediation(opts?: { now?: number }): Promise<string[]>`
- AC: Function loads config via `loadCloisterConfigSync()` and returns `[]` immediately when `stuck_remediation.enabled === false`
- AC: All seven gates (config, session, status, tracker, dead-end overlap, idle, bead overlap) implemented and skip the agent without firing a stage
- AC: Activity-reset logic clears stored state and skips the cycle when `runtime.lastActivity > stuckState.firstStuckAt`
- AC: Stage 1 calls `messageAgent` with the exact poke message and persists `lastStage=1`
- AC: Stage 2 calls `resumeAgent` with the exact recovery message; persists `lastStage=2` ONLY if the resume succeeds
- AC: Stage 3 calls `markAgentTroubled`, persists `lastStage=3`, and never calls `messageAgent`/`resumeAgent` again on subsequent cycles for the same stall
- AC: Each stage transition emits a `[deacon] stuck-remediation stage=N issue=<id> idleMin=<n> action=<verb>` log via `logDeaconEventSync` and is included in the returned actions array
- AC: Per-agent try/catch: an exception in one agent's stage logic does not stop the loop for other agents
- AC: Function accepts optional `now?: number` argument used in place of `Date.now()` everywhere — for deterministic tests
close_reason: Fixed inspection feedback by threading opts.now into the idle gate while preserving Date.now defaults for existing callers.

title: pan-1415: Add per-agent stuck-remediation state file helpers
description:
Create `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation-state.ts` with three exported helpers operating on `~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/stuck-remediation.json`: `readStuckRemediationState(agentId): StuckRemediationState | null`, `writeStuckRemediationState(agentId, state): void`, `clearStuckRemediationState(agentId): void`. The state shape is `{ lastStage: 0 | 1 | 2 | 3; lastStageAt: string /* ISO */; firstStuckAt: string /* ISO */ }`. Use `existsSync` + `readFileSync`/`writeFileSync` (CLI-process-safe — these helpers are only called from the patrol thread). On read failures (corrupt JSON, missing file), return `null` and do not throw.
- AC: New file `src/lib/cloister/stuck-remediation-state.ts` exports `StuckRemediationState` type, `readStuckRemediationState`, `writeStuckRemediationState`, `clearStuckRemediationState`
- AC: `readStuckRemediationState` returns `null` for a missing file (does not throw)
- AC: `readStuckRemediationState` returns `null` for a corrupt-JSON file (does not throw) and logs a warning via `console.warn`
- AC: `writeStuckRemediationState` creates the parent dir (`~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/`) if missing and writes pretty-printed JSON
- AC: `clearStuckRemediationState` is a no-op if the file does not exist
- AC: Unit tests in `src/lib/cloister/__tests__/stuck-remediation-state.test.ts` cover the four edge cases above using a tmp-dir override (no real `~/.panopticon` writes)

title: pan-1410: Add idempotency and dedup-failure tests in src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts
description:
Add a new describe block 'idempotency and dedup failure semantics' to src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts with four tests, all using the existing mockExecAsync vi.hoisted pattern: (1) 'three consecutive calls leave exactly planItemCount beads' — script mockExecAsync so bd list reflects the running bead set (initially [], then [N beads after first run], then [N beads before second run runs dedup, then [] after delete, then [N beads after second create]] etc.); after three invocations assert the net mock-recorded state has exactly N beads with no duplicates by title. (2) 'aborts when bd list throws during dedup' — script mockExecAsync to throw on the dedup `bd list -l <label>` call; assert result.success===false, result.errors[0] contains 'dedup failed:' and 'list failed:', and no `bd create` calls were issued (mockExecAsync recorded no create args). (3) 'aborts when any bd delete fails during dedup' — script mockExecAsync so list returns 3 beads then one of the three deletes throws; assert result.success===false, result.errors[0] contains 'dedup failed:' and 'delete <bead-id>:', and no create calls fired. (4) 'aborts when post-delete verification finds residual beads' — script mockExecAsync so list returns 2 beads, both deletes succeed, but the verification list still returns 1 bead; assert result.success===false, errors contains 'residual', and no create calls fired. Each test must complete in milliseconds — no real setTimeouts. Tests must follow the existing mocking pattern in the file (vi.hoisted, vi.mock for child_process and util.promisify).
- AC: Test 'three consecutive calls leave exactly planItemCount beads' is added and passes
- AC: Test 'aborts when bd list throws during dedup' is added and passes; asserts no bd create calls fired
- AC: Test 'aborts when any bd delete fails during dedup' is added and passes; asserts no bd create calls fired
- AC: Test 'aborts when post-delete verification finds residual beads' is added and passes; asserts no bd create calls fired
- AC: `npm test src/lib/vbrief/__tests__/create-beads.test.ts` runs green with no real-time delays
close_reason: Added idempotency and strict dedup failure tests; targeted create-beads suite passes.

title: pan-1410: Acquire bd-mutex inside createBeadsFromVBriefPromise and remove redundant outer wrappers
description:
Wrap the entire body of createBeadsFromVBriefPromise in withBdMutexPromise(async () => { ... }) imported from src/lib/bd-mutex.ts so every caller is serialized automatically. Because withBdMutexPromise is a non-reentrant Promise-queue mutex, all existing outer wrappers must be removed in the same change to avoid deadlock: (1) src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts line 130 — change `withBdMutex(() => mod.createBeadsFromVBrief(path))` to `mod.createBeadsFromVBrief(path)` (still inside Effect.runPromise); (2) src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts line 1567 — same pattern; (3) src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts line 2740 — same pattern; (4) src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts line 2406 — already calls without withBdMutex, leave as-is. Verify each call site still typechecks and returns the same error-result shape; existing error-propagation paths (plan-finalize.ts:64-72, issues.ts:134-139, agents.ts:2411-2422, start.ts:933-944/977-1011) already surface result.errors to the user so the new 'dedup failed:' messages flow through without further changes. Add a JSDoc header on createBeadsFromVBrief documenting the new contract: 'Idempotent and internally serialized via bd-mutex. Calling N times yields exactly planItemCount beads or returns success:false with errors. Do NOT wrap callers in withBdMutex — it will deadlock.'
- AC: createBeadsFromVBriefPromise's entire body runs inside withBdMutexPromise so all callers share one queue
- AC: Outer withBdMutex wrappers at issues.ts:130, issues.ts:1567, issues.ts:2740 are removed; the calls go through Effect.runPromise(createBeadsFromVBrief(...)) directly
- AC: agents.ts:2406, plan-finalize.ts:62, start.ts:932, start.ts:976 remain unchanged at their call sites but now get serialized via the internal mutex
- AC: createBeadsFromVBrief has a JSDoc header stating it is idempotent, internally mutexed, and must not be wrapped in withBdMutex by callers
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run build both pass with the outer-wrapper removal in place
close_reason: Wrapped createBeadsFromVBrief in the shared bd mutex, exported the promise mutex, removed redundant dashboard wrappers, and verified typecheck/build.

title: pan-1410: Replace silent dedup block in createBeadsFromVBriefPromise with strict clearBeadsForIssue call
description:
In src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts, replace the dedup try/catch at lines 167-189 of createBeadsFromVBriefPromise with: `const clearResult = await clearBeadsForIssue(workspacePath, issueLabel); if (clearResult.errors.length > 0) { return { success: false, created: [], errors: clearResult.errors.map(e => 'dedup failed: ' + e), beadIds: new Map() }; } if (clearResult.cleared > 0) console.log('[beads] Cleared ' + clearResult.cleared + ' existing beads for ' + issueLabel + ' (verified)');`. The function must NOT proceed to the create loop if dedup reported any error. The outer try/catch that previously swallowed all dedup errors must be removed entirely — replaced by the explicit early return.
- AC: When clearBeadsForIssue returns non-empty errors, createBeadsFromVBrief returns success:false, created:[], and errors prefixed with 'dedup failed:'
- AC: When clearBeadsForIssue succeeds with cleared > 0, the log line reads '[beads] Cleared N existing beads for <label> (verified)'
- AC: When clearBeadsForIssue succeeds (errors empty), createBeadsFromVBriefPromise proceeds to the create loop as before
- AC: The previous try/catch at lines 167-189 (silent swallow of bd list and bd delete failures) is fully removed
close_reason: Replaced silent dedup try/catch with clearBeadsForIssue early failure propagation and verified clear logging.

title: pan-1410: Add strict clearBeadsForIssue helper in src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts
description:
Add an exported helper clearBeadsForIssue(workspacePath: string, issueLabel: string): Promise<{ cleared: number; errors: string[] }> to src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts. The helper must: (1) run `bd list --json -l <label> --status all --limit 0` and return errors:['list failed: <message>'] on throw rather than swallowing; (2) iterate the parsed list, running `bd delete <id> --force` for each, collecting failures as errors:['delete <id>: <message>'] rather than swallowing; (3) after the delete loop, re-run `bd list` and if the result is non-empty, return errors:['residual <N> beads after delete: <ids>']; (4) return { cleared: <count actually deleted>, errors: [...] }. Empty errors means dedup succeeded cleanly. The helper must use execFileAsync (not exec) for argv safety. Export it from src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts and from src/lib/vbrief/index.ts. No call-site changes in this bead.
- AC: clearBeadsForIssue returns { cleared: 0, errors: [] } when initial bd list returns []
- AC: clearBeadsForIssue returns errors containing 'list failed:' when the initial bd list invocation throws
- AC: clearBeadsForIssue returns errors containing 'delete <bead-id>:' for each individual bd delete that fails
- AC: clearBeadsForIssue runs a post-delete bd list and returns errors containing 'residual' when any beads remain
- AC: clearBeadsForIssue is exported from src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts and re-exported from src/lib/vbrief/index.ts
close_reason: Added exported clearBeadsForIssue helper with strict list/delete/residual verification errors and re-exported it from vbrief index.

title: pan-1408: Add unit tests for refreshGraphify
description:
Create src/lib/graphify/__tests__/refresh.test.ts (vitest). Mock execAsync (or the exec module the helper uses) to script command outcomes per test. Test cases: (1) `which graphify` fails → returns skipped:'no-graphify-cli', no further commands run. (2) graphify present but graphify-out/ missing → returns skipped:'no-graphify-out'. (3) graphify update succeeds, `git diff --cached --quiet` returns exit 0 → returns skipped:'no-changes', no commit. (4) graphify update succeeds, diff non-empty → git add, git commit, git push all called once each in order; returns ok:true with commit SHA. (5) git push returns non-fast-forward exit code → helper calls git fetch, git pull --rebase, git push; on second-push success returns ok:true. (6) Second git push also fails → returns ok:false with 'push failed' in error. (7) graphify update times out (simulate via mock rejecting with timeout error) → returns ok:false with 'graphify update timed out'. (8) graphify update throws unexpectedly → returns ok:false, does not propagate exception. Use vi.useFakeTimers() if any timeout logic is exercised via real-clock delays (per repo rule on retry/Retry-After tests).
- AC: Test file src/lib/graphify/__tests__/refresh.test.ts exists with at least 8 test cases covering the scenarios above.
- AC: All tests pass under `npm test`.
- AC: Tests mock execAsync (or shell entry point) — no real `graphify`, `git`, or filesystem mutations on the project tree during test runs.
- AC: The 'push retry on non-fast-forward' test verifies the exact command sequence (git fetch → git pull --rebase → git push) and that the retry happens exactly once.
- AC: Any test that exercises timeout/retry timing uses vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — no real-clock setTimeout delays in the test file (per CLAUDE.md test rule).
close_reason: Added mocked Vitest coverage for refreshGraphify skip, no-change, commit/push, non-fast-forward retry, timeout, and failure paths.

title: pan-1408: Invoke refreshGraphify from postMergeLifecycle in src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts
description:
In src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts, after `transitionIssueToVerifyingOnMain(issueId, projectPath)` succeeds (around line 444) and BEFORE the `compactBeads` step (line 463), add a try/catch block that dynamically imports `../graphify/refresh.js`, awaits `refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId)`, and routes the RefreshResult to appropriate log lines: console.log + logActivity('graphify_refresh', 'Updated graphify-out and pushed commit <sha>') on ok:true; console.log on skipped (info-level, no logActivity entry needed); console.warn + logActivity('graphify_refresh_failed', '...') on ok:false. Wrap the entire block in try/catch so that any thrown error from the helper is downgraded to a warn-level log — postMergeLifecycle never fails because of graphify.
- AC: After a successful merge of any issue on a project with graphify installed and graphify-out/ present, postMergeLifecycle invokes refreshGraphify against projectPath.
- AC: Successful refresh logs both a console line and a logActivity entry tagged graphify_refresh with the commit SHA.
- AC: Skipped refresh (graphify missing or graphify-out missing) logs only a console line (info-level), no logActivity entry, no warn/error.
- AC: Failed refresh logs a console.warn AND a logActivity entry tagged graphify_refresh_failed, but postMergeLifecycle continues to the compactBeads step and ultimately marks _completedPostMerge.
- AC: Any thrown exception from refreshGraphify is caught at the call site and downgraded to a console.warn — postMergeLifecycle does not throw because of graphify.
close_reason: Wired refreshGraphify into postMergeLifecycle with success, skipped, failure, and thrown-error logging before bead compaction.

title: pan-1408: Add graphify-stale check to pan doctor
description:
Add a `checkGraphifyFreshness(projectPath: string): CheckResult` function to src/cli/commands/doctor.ts (or a small helper module imported there). For each project in projects.yaml, check if `${projectPath}/graphify-out/GRAPH_SUMMARY.md` exists. If absent, skip (no check result emitted). If present, compare its mtime against the timestamp of the local HEAD commit (`git log -1 --format=%ct`). Status: 'ok' (message: 'Fresh — updated since HEAD') if mtime ≥ HEAD commit time; 'warn' (message: 'Stale — N days older than HEAD', fix: 'Run: graphify update . from <projectPath>; or wait for the next merge to refresh automatically') otherwise. Add the call to doctorCommand alongside the other checks. Use execAsync for the git command — no execSync.
- AC: `pan doctor` emits a 'Graphify graph freshness' check result for projects that have graphify-out/GRAPH_SUMMARY.md.
- AC: Projects without graphify-out/ produce no check result at all (silent skip), so projects that don't use graphify don't see warnings.
- AC: When GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime ≥ local HEAD commit timestamp, status is 'ok' with message indicating freshness.
- AC: When GRAPH_SUMMARY.md mtime < local HEAD commit timestamp, status is 'warn' with message including age and a fix suggestion.
- AC: Git command runs via execAsync; grep for execSync in the new check function returns zero matches.
close_reason: Added pan doctor Graphify graph freshness check using async git HEAD timestamp lookup and verified it emits stale warnings.

title: pan-1408: Add src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts with refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId) helper
description:
Create src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts exporting `refreshGraphify(projectPath: string, issueId: string): Promise<RefreshResult>`. RefreshResult is a discriminated union: { skipped: 'no-graphify-cli' | 'no-graphify-out' | 'no-changes' | 'gitignored' } | { ok: true; commit: string; pushed: boolean } | { ok: false; error: string }. Implementation steps in order: (1) Use `which graphify` via execAsync to detect CLI; return skipped:'no-graphify-cli' if missing. (2) Check `existsSync(join(projectPath, 'graphify-out'))`; return skipped:'no-graphify-out' if absent. (3) Run `graphify update .` via execAsync with cwd=projectPath and 60s timeout. (4) `git add graphify-out/` in projectPath. (5) `git diff --cached --quiet` — if exit 0, return skipped:'no-changes' (or 'gitignored'). (6) `git commit -m "chore(graphify): refresh after ${issueId}"`. (7) `git push origin main`; on non-fast-forward, run `git fetch origin main && git pull --rebase origin main && git push origin main` once. (8) Return ok:true with commit SHA. Wrap each shell-out in try/catch; map failures to RefreshResult.ok=false with descriptive error strings. NO execSync anywhere — repo rule (see hazards). NO `git add -A` — only stage graphify-out/.
- AC: Module exports refreshGraphify(projectPath, issueId): Promise<RefreshResult> with the discriminated-union shape described above.
- AC: All shell-out uses execAsync from src/lib/exec.ts (or equivalent promisified exec). Grep `execSync` in src/lib/graphify/refresh.ts returns zero matches.
- AC: Returns skipped:'no-graphify-cli' when `which graphify` fails. Does not throw, does not write to git.
- AC: Returns skipped:'no-graphify-out' when `${projectPath}/graphify-out/` does not exist. Does not throw, does not write to git.
- AC: Returns skipped:'no-changes' (or 'gitignored') when `git diff --cached --quiet` exits 0 after staging graphify-out/.
- AC: On successful path: commit subject is exactly `chore(graphify): refresh after <ISSUE-ID>` (uppercase issue id passed in). Only files under graphify-out/ are staged — verified by inspecting the commit's changed-file list.
- AC: On `git push` non-fast-forward, helper retries once with fetch+rebase+push. Second push failure returns ok:false with error string including 'push failed'. Does not throw.
- AC: graphify update invocation has a 60s timeout via execAsync timeout option. On timeout, helper returns ok:false with error 'graphify update timed out'.
close_reason: Fixed inspection block by returning the final pushed graphify refresh commit SHA after any push retry rebase.

title: pan-1370: Build Quickview Shift+hover popover with Shiki-highlighted snippet
description:
In MarkdownFileLink.tsx, attach onMouseEnter / onMouseLeave handlers gated on `event.shiftKey` (or a Shift-held state via window keydown/keyup listeners scoped to the chip). When Shift+hover begins, call pan.readWorkspaceFile with the chip's relativePath (derive from displayPath), wait for the result, then render a popover anchored to the chip showing a Shiki-highlighted snippet auto-scrolled to `:line` if present. Reuse src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/usePickerPosition.ts for viewport-aware positioning. Dismiss popover on mouse-out or Shift-release. Show a loading spinner if the read takes >100ms; show an error toast if the read fails (path-traversal rejection is a system error, surface it).
- AC: Shift+hover on a file chip shows a popover with a Shiki-highlighted snippet of the file, auto-scrolled to the chip's :line (or top of file if no line specified)
- AC: Popover dismisses on mouse-out and Shift-release without leaving stale popovers
- AC: Popover positions itself inside the viewport — chips near right/bottom edges do not cause clipping (uses usePickerPosition logic)
- AC: Plain left-click on the chip still opens in editor (Quickview hover does not break the click path)
- AC: Truncated files show a 'truncated — first 256 KiB' indicator in the popover footer
close_reason: Closed

(3) Then thread `cwd` to every ChatMarkdown callsite — MessagesTimeline.tsx (8 occurrences: lines 414, 547, 585, 610, 645, 828, 832 per grep) — by reading from the parent conversation's `cwd`. Callsites where cwd is genuinely unavailable (e.g. system-summary surfaces) pass `undefined` explicitly so the type system distinguishes 'forgot to thread' from 'intentionally omitted'.

title: pan-1370: Add pan.readWorkspaceFile RPC with path-traversal validation and size cap
description:
Add a new RPC in packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts: `pan.readWorkspaceFile` with payload `{ issueId: string, relativePath: string, line?: number, contextLines?: number }` and success `{ text: string, lang: string, truncated: boolean, totalLines: number }`. Implement server side as a new service or directly in ws-rpc.ts: resolve workspace path via `getWorkspacePathForIssue`, then `path.resolve(workspaceRoot, relativePath)` and assert the resolved path starts with `workspaceRoot + sep` (or equals it). After fs.readFile, also `fs.realpath` the resolved path and re-assert containment to defeat symlink escapes. Reject with PanRpcError code `PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE` on any escape. Cap file size at 256 KiB (read fs.stat first, return `truncated: true` if exceeded — read first 256 KiB and mark). Derive `lang` from file extension via a small map (ts, tsx, js, jsx, json, md, py, go, rs, etc.). Use fs/promises ONLY (no execSync per repo rule).
- AC: RPC schema added to packages/contracts and registered in ws-rpc.ts; type-safe end-to-end
- AC: Path-traversal attempts (relativePath starting with `../`, absolute paths outside workspace, symlinks pointing outside) are rejected with PanRpcError code PATH_OUTSIDE_WORKSPACE; covered by tests
- AC: Files larger than 256 KiB return `truncated: true` with the first 256 KiB; smaller files return `truncated: false`
- AC: `lang` field is set from file extension via a documented map; unknown extensions return 'plaintext'
- AC: Server implementation uses fs/promises only (no execSync, no readFileSync) per repo rules
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1407: Document the manual cost-benchmark methodology and capture one A/B measurement
description:
Extend docs/RTK-INTEGRATION.md (created in bead 1) with a 'Cost benchmark' section describing the A/B methodology: pick a representative issue, spawn a work agent with agents.rtk.enabled=false (record total Bash output tokens from the cost event log), spawn the same agent again with agents.rtk.enabled=true (record again), compute % reduction. Capture one before/after measurement, paste it into the doc, and confirm it clears the ≥30% Bash output token reduction bar from the issue acceptance criteria. If it does NOT clear 30%, file a follow-up issue rather than silently passing.
- AC: docs/RTK-INTEGRATION.md has a Cost benchmark section with reproducible A/B steps
- AC: One captured A/B measurement is pasted into the doc with the % reduction figure
- AC: If measurement < 30%, a follow-up issue is filed referencing PAN-1407

title: pan-1407: Author the rtk-bash-filter PreToolUse wrapper script
description:
Create sync-sources/hooks/rtk-bash-filter (bash) following the tldr-read-enforcer pattern. Reads the PreToolUse JSON envelope from stdin, checks tool_name == 'Bash' (bypass otherwise), reads agents.rtk.enabled from the resolved Panopticon config gate (or a simple environment variable PANOPTICON_RTK_ENABLED that the agent launcher sets — pick whichever matches how tldr-read-enforcer reads its gate today), bypasses when disabled, bypasses when ~/.panopticon/bin/rtk is missing, bypasses when the captured command output is short enough that compression is not worth it. When active: pipes the command output through rtk and emits the rewritten output via the documented PreToolUse JSON response shape. Never break Claude Code execution — wrap rtk invocation in defensive checks and degrade to passthrough on any failure. Include verbose comments at the top documenting the bypass conditions, mirroring tldr-read-enforcer's documentation header.
- AC: sync-sources/hooks/rtk-bash-filter exists, is executable bash, and is documented at the top with all bypass conditions
- AC: Wrapper exits 0 (passthrough) on non-Bash tool_name
- AC: Wrapper exits 0 (passthrough) when agents.rtk.enabled is false (default) or when rtk binary is missing
- AC: Wrapper invokes rtk and emits the compressed output via the PreToolUse JSON response shape when enabled
- AC: Any rtk failure falls back to passthrough — agent execution never breaks
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1407: Install rtk binary from rtk-ai/rtk releases during pan admin hooks install
description:
Add an installRtk() function (in a new src/lib/rtk/setup.ts or directly in src/cli/commands/setup/hooks.ts following the existing caveman pattern). On invocation: detect the host platform, resolve the release asset URL from the pinned version (from docs/RTK-INTEGRATION.md), download into ~/.panopticon/bin/rtk, chmod 0755, validate the binary runs (rtk --version exits 0). Idempotent — re-runs skip if the pinned version is already installed. On unsupported platform OR network failure: log a warning, skip, do NOT fail the overall pan admin hooks install run (graceful degradation). Wire the call into setupHooksCommand() in src/cli/commands/setup/hooks.ts, after the existing caveman block.
- AC: pan admin hooks install downloads and installs rtk to ~/.panopticon/bin/rtk on supported platforms
- AC: Re-running pan admin hooks install does not re-download if the pinned rtk version is already installed
- AC: On unsupported platform or network failure, pan admin hooks install logs a warning and continues; exit code stays 0
- AC: Installed binary passes rtk --version smoke check before install function returns
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1407: Add a one-line CLAUDE.md note explaining compressed Bash outputs when RTK is enabled
description:
Add a short paragraph to the project CLAUDE.md (under a 'Bash Output Compression (RTK)' heading) explaining that when agents.rtk.enabled is true, Bash command outputs (git status, npm output, etc.) the agent sees are compressed versions, and the raw output can be regenerated by re-running the command with RTK disabled (PANOPTICON_RTK_ENABLED=0). Keep it under five lines.
- AC: Project CLAUDE.md has a short RTK section explaining compressed Bash outputs
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1407: Add agents.rtk config knob (enabled default false) to config-yaml.ts
description:
In src/lib/config-yaml.ts, add agents.rtk to DEFAULT_CONFIG with { enabled: false } and a NormalizedRtkConfig type. Add a mergeRtkConfig() function modeled on mergeCavemanConfig() and a getDefaultRtkConfig()/mergeRtkConfigs() pair so callers can read the resolved value. Export the resolver alongside the caveman/tts equivalents. Surface the value via the same shape Cloister already consumes (normalized config object) so the hook wrapper can be told whether to compress.
- AC: DEFAULT_CONFIG.agents.rtk = { enabled: false }
- AC: mergeRtkConfig() + mergeRtkConfigs() exist and follow the caveman pattern
- AC: Setting agents.rtk.enabled in projects.yaml is picked up by the resolver and round-trips through a unit test
close_reason: Added default-off agents.rtk.enabled normalization and config merge test

title: pan-1407: Research and document the rtk-ai/rtk release artifact pattern and pin a version
description:
Verify the rtk-ai/rtk release tag, list of prebuilt asset names per platform (linux-x64, darwin-arm64, darwin-x64, linux-arm64 if present), and choose a pinned version. Produce docs/RTK-INTEGRATION.md describing: (1) the release URL pattern (e.g. https://github.com/rtk-ai/rtk/releases/download/<tag>/rtk-<platform>), (2) the platform→asset-name mapping, (3) the pinned version constant the installer will reference, (4) the rtk CLI invocation shape (stdin → stdout, exit codes, flags) the wrapper will use, (5) any hash/checksum to validate the download. If rtk does not currently publish prebuilt binaries for the platforms we care about, document that as a blocker hazard and update the plan.
- AC: docs/RTK-INTEGRATION.md exists and identifies a pinned rtk version
- AC: Doc lists each platform→release-asset-name mapping the installer will use
- AC: Doc describes rtk CLI invocation contract (stdin/stdout shape, flags, exit codes)
- AC: If no prebuilt binary exists for any of the supported platforms, doc records that as a hazard and flags the next step
close_reason: Documented RTK v0.41.0 release assets, checksums, and Claude hook invocation contract

title: pan-1405: Playwright UAT: scroll-mode message delivery + no-MCP plain fork
description:
Add a Playwright UAT (isolated browser instance/profile — no shared session) under tests/playwright/ that: (1) creates a new Claude Code conversation via the dashboard, (2) opens the terminal panel and scrolls up to enter tmux copy-mode, (3) sends a message via the conversation input (which calls deliverAgentMessage), (4) asserts the agent transcript advances (assertion via /api/conversations history endpoint or a visible transcript element), (5) opens a plain fork of that conversation and asserts the launcher script under ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/launcher.sh contains 'pty-supervisor.js' and does NOT contain '--mcp-config'. The UAT must seed any required login/setup inside the isolated session (no dependency on another agent's playwright state).
- AC: Playwright spec uses a freshly-created browser context/profile (no shared cookies, no shared state). Login/setup is performed inside the spec.
- AC: Step: terminal panel is scrolled up by N lines so tmux is in copy-mode. Sending a message still results in the agent's transcript advancing within the timeout.
- AC: Step: plain fork created; its launcher.sh contains the supervisor exec line AND does not contain '--mcp-config' or '--dangerously-load-development-channels'.
- AC: Step: send a message to the plain fork and assert delivery log line path='supervisor' is appended to ~/.panopticon/logs/<id>.delivery.log (or equivalent existing log path).
- AC: Spec cleans up: closes its browser context, kills the spawned conversation and plain fork via /api/conversations DELETE so the host does not accumulate orphans.
close_reason: Completed

title: pan-1405: Skip dismissDevChannelsDialog when channels MCP is not wired
description:
Gate every dismissDevChannelsDialog call site on the actual channels-MCP wiring state — when channelsBridgeMcpConfig is undefined (or state.channelsEnabled is false), do NOT invoke the dialog dismissal. This is correctness, not optimization: the dialog only renders when --dangerously-load-development-channels is passed; spamming Enter at a session that never showed the dialog can confirm unrelated prompts (auto-compact, file-trust). Touch call sites in src/lib/agents.ts:spawnAgent and src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts:spawnConversationSession.
- AC: spawnAgent: when state.channelsEnabled is false, dismissDevChannelsDialog is not called (asserted via spy in agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts).
- AC: spawnConversationSession: when channelsBridgeMcpConfig is undefined, dismissDevChannelsDialog is not called.
- AC: Existing channels-MCP-on path (operator opt-in) still calls the dismissal — no regression for that flow.
close_reason: Completed

title: pan-1405: Re-apply the 'skip channels MCP on plain forks' policy and route them through the supervisor
description:
Restore the plain-fork channels-MCP skip behavior (the policy reverted by 'Revert fix(conversations): wire channels MCP on plain forks too' / 8219536cc). Plain forks now MUST NOT wire panopticon-bridge MCP and MUST NOT call dismissDevChannelsDialog — they get supervisor delivery instead. The plainFork branch in spawnConversationSession explicitly sets useSupervisor=true and channelsBridgeMcpConfig=undefined. Add a regression test asserting the launcher for a plain fork contains 'exec node … pty-supervisor.js claude' and does NOT contain '--mcp-config' or '--dangerously-load-development-channels'.
- AC: Plain-fork launcher script contains the supervisor wrap line.
- AC: Plain-fork launcher script does NOT contain '--mcp-config' or '--dangerously-load-development-channels server:panopticon-bridge'.
- AC: dismissDevChannelsDialog is NOT invoked on the plain-fork code path (asserted via spy/mock in the test).
- AC: deliverAgentMessage to a plain-fork conversation routes to path='supervisor' (asserted in an integration test that sends a message and reads the delivery log).
close_reason: Completed

title: pan-1405: Channels MCP wiring becomes off-by-default for new spawns
description:
Gate channels MCP wiring behind an explicit experimental.claudeCodeChannelsMcp flag (default false) — separate from the existing experimental.claudeCodeChannels which historically toggled the whole feature. Update decideChannelsForWorkAgent so the flag-off path is the default. The supervisor is the new preferred transport; channels-MCP code stays only as the delivery fallback for agents whose state.json still has channelsEnabled=true. Document the deprecation in the function header and in docs/. Existing tests for decideChannelsForWorkAgent are updated to match the new default. NOTE: do NOT remove the experimental.claudeCodeChannels flag — preserve backwards compatibility so operators can re-enable channels MCP per-agent for diagnostic comparison.
- AC: decideChannelsForWorkAgent returns { eligible: false, reason: 'mcp-default-off' } when experimental.claudeCodeChannelsMcp is unset/false, even when all other criteria pass.
- AC: spawnAgent on a fresh Claude Code work agent writes NO agent-mcp.json and NO bridge-token by default; verified via fs.exists assertions in the spawn-eligibility unit tests.
- AC: Operator override: setting experimental.claudeCodeChannelsMcp=true restores the previous eligibility ladder (provider=anthropic, non-Docker, non-Pi) and writes the MCP config + bridge token as today.
- AC: An already-running agent with state.channelsEnabled=true continues to receive messages via channels delivery (no resume required) — covered by an integration test that pre-seeds state.json with channelsEnabled=true and asserts deliverAgentMessage routes path='channel'.
close_reason: Completed

title: pan-1405: Enable supervisor for Claude Code conversation sessions
description:
Update spawnConversationSession in src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts to wire useSupervisor=true + supervisorScriptPath for Claude Code (non-Pi) conversations that aren't in Docker. Write pty-token before tmux create. Pi conversations stay on tmux paste-buffer. Keep channels-MCP code path untouched at this bead — channels-mcp-default-off handles its retirement.
- AC: For a Claude Code conversation (non-Pi, non-Docker), the generated launcher script contains 'exec node …/dist/pty-supervisor.js claude' and the pty-token file is written before tmux create.
- AC: Pi conversations have NO supervisor wrapping in the launcher (verified by snapshot or grep).
- AC: Docker conversations (PAN_DOCKER=1) have NO supervisor wrapping.
- AC: After spawnConversationSession returns, ~/.panopticon/sockets/pty-<tmuxSession>.sock exists at mode 0600 (asserted in an integration test).
close_reason: Completed

title: pan-1405: Extend deliver-agent-message.test.ts with supervisor routing cases
description:
Update src/lib/__tests__/deliver-agent-message.test.ts to cover: supervisor-only success, supervisor missing → channels success, both sockets missing → tmux, supervisor POST 5xx → channels fallback, supervisor POST timeout → channels fallback, explicit deliveryMethod='supervisor' strict-error on missing socket. Reuse existing fake-socket fixtures.
- AC: Test 'supervisor-only success': pty socket present, POST 200, channels socket absent → path='supervisor'.
- AC: Test 'supervisor missing, channels success': pty socket absent, channels socket present and channelsEnabled=true → path='channel'.
- AC: Test 'both missing → tmux': neither socket exists → sendKeys mock invoked, path='tmux'.
- AC: Test 'supervisor POST 500 → channels fallback': supervisor exists but returns 500; channels exists → path='channel' with reason='socket-post-failed: …' logged for the supervisor tier.
- AC: Test 'deliveryMethod="supervisor" strict': no socket → throws MessageDeliveryFailed; no fallback occurs.
- AC: Use vi.useFakeTimers / vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync for any timeout-based test — no real setTimeout waits.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1405: Wire supervisor into spawnAgent: decideSupervisorForWorkAgent + state.supervisorEnabled + useSupervisor passthrough
description:
Add decideSupervisorForWorkAgent(agentId, options, state) → { eligible, reason } to src/lib/agents.ts. Eligibility: harness === 'claude-code' AND NOT inside Docker (PAN_DOCKER / PANOPTICON_DOCKER_WORKSPACE env). Provider-agnostic — no Anthropic gate. Add supervisorEnabled?: boolean to AgentState. In spawnAgent, when eligible: writePtyTokenSync(agentId), set state.supervisorEnabled=true, pass useSupervisor=true and supervisorScriptPath=resolved-dist-path to buildAgentLaunchConfig→generateLauncherScriptSync. If dist/pty-supervisor.js is missing, raise a clear error: 'pty-supervisor build artifact missing — run `npm run build`.' Channels eligibility is now independent (and defaults off — see channels-mcp-default-off bead).
- AC: decideSupervisorForWorkAgent returns { eligible: false, reason: 'docker-not-supported-yet' } when PAN_DOCKER=1 or PANOPTICON_DOCKER_WORKSPACE=1.
- AC: decideSupervisorForWorkAgent returns { eligible: false, reason: 'harness-pi' } when state.harness !== 'claude-code'.
- AC: decideSupervisorForWorkAgent returns { eligible: true } for a Claude Code work agent on any provider (Anthropic, cliproxy-routed GPT/Kimi/MiniMax, Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry).
- AC: AgentState gains optional supervisorEnabled?: boolean; saveAgentStateSync persists it; getAgentState reads it.
- AC: When eligible, spawnAgent writes pty-token before tmux session creation and passes useSupervisor=true + supervisorScriptPath to the launcher generator.
- AC: When dist/pty-supervisor.js is absent, spawnAgent throws a clear actionable error BEFORE creating tmux/launcher artifacts (no half-spawn state).
- AC: Unit tests in agents-spawn-supervisor.test.ts cover each eligibility branch (Docker excluded, Pi excluded, every provider accepted) and assert state.supervisorEnabled persists.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1405: deliverAgentMessage: try supervisor socket → channels → tmux
description:
Update deliverAgentMessage in src/lib/agents.ts to a three-tier router: (1) if pty-supervisor socket exists AND pty-token can be read → POST via postUnixSocketJson with PTY_TOKEN_HEADER → on 2xx, log path='supervisor' and return. (2) else if channelsEnabled and channels socket exists → existing channels POST path → log path='channel'. (3) else → sendKeys(tmux) → log path='tmux'. The explicit deliveryMethod argument is honored: 'tmux' still forces tmux, 'channels' still forces channels (errors if unavailable). A new explicit deliveryMethod value 'supervisor' is added that errors if the socket is missing. Default 'auto' uses the tiered fallback. Per-tier failure reasons are appended to the existing channel-delivery log under the new key 'pty-supervisor' (and continue to fall through). Module-private helper resolves the supervisor socket path with the same panopticonHomeForChannels() function (rename it to panopticonHomeForSockets() inside this bead, with the old name kept as an alias if any external caller imports it).
- AC: When supervisor socket exists and POST returns 200, the function returns and writes a delivery-log line with path='supervisor'.
- AC: When supervisor socket is missing but channels socket exists and channelsEnabled=true, the function falls through to channels and writes path='channel'.
- AC: When neither socket exists (or both fail), the function falls through to tmux sendKeys and writes path='tmux' with the per-tier failure reasons.
- AC: deliveryMethod='supervisor' throws MessageDeliveryFailed when the supervisor socket POST fails (no silent fallback). 'channels' keeps its existing strict behavior. 'tmux' bypasses both sockets.
- AC: Bridge log and per-agent delivery log filename are unchanged so existing dashboards / log readers don't break — the new path='supervisor' line lands in the same file.
- AC: Existing call sites in agents.ts (resumeAgent, messageAgent, auto-continue) are NOT modified — they continue to call deliverAgentMessage; only its internals change.
close_reason: Updated deliverAgentMessage to try supervisor, then channels, then tmux with strict explicit methods.

title: pan-1405: Unit tests for pty-supervisor.ts using a dummy child command
description:
Add src/lib/channels/__tests__/pty-supervisor.test.ts. Tests spawn the supervisor against `cat` (or `bash -c 'cat'`) as a stand-in for claude — `cat` echoes stdin, which lets us assert that socket POSTs reach the child's input. Cover lifecycle (socket cleanup on exit), token auth (403 on missing token), and echo-to-stdout behavior. Run under Node only (skip under Bun).
- AC: Test 'socket POST reaches child stdin': spawn supervisor with `cat`, POST {content:'ping'}, assert 'ping' appears on supervisor stdout via the cat echo.
- AC: Test 'socket POST without token returns 403' using a wrong header value.
- AC: Test 'socket unlink on exit': after SIGTERM the socket file is removed.
- AC: Test 'echo behavior': socket-delivered message appears in stdout exactly once (no duplication, no truncation).
- AC: Tests skip under Bun (typeof Bun !== 'undefined') with a clear .skipIf — node-pty cannot run there.
close_reason: Added subprocess tests for PTY supervisor socket delivery, token rejection, cleanup, echo, and socket mode.

title: pan-1405: Create src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts (PTY master + unix socket)
description:
Create src/lib/channels/pty-supervisor.ts as a Node 22 script that spawns claude in a PTY (via @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch), proxies stdin↔PTY input and PTY output↔stdout, forwards SIGWINCH/resize events, and listens on ~/.panopticon/sockets/pty-<agentId>.sock for HTTP-on-unix POSTs that inject text into Claude's PTY input. Echo each socket-delivered message back to stdout (so the user sees it in the tmux pane). On EXIT/SIGTERM/SIGINT, unlink the socket and exit. Require PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID env var; reject requests without a matching pty-token header. Add a new tsdown entry to emit dist/pty-supervisor.js (node-pty marked external). Add @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch to root package.json. Header comment documents the architecture, the H1 PTY-owns-claude lifecycle, env-var requirements (TERM/COLORTERM/LANG passthrough), and the per-message echo behavior.
- AC: Spawning the supervisor with PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID set and `claude --version` as the child completes, prints 'claude --version' output to stdout, and exits when the child exits.
- AC: After spawn, ~/.panopticon/sockets/pty-<agentId>.sock exists at mode 0600 (verified via fs.stat).
- AC: POST { content: 'hello' } with a valid pty-token header writes 'hello\n' (with a trailing newline) to Claude's PTY input AND echoes it to supervisor stdout. POST without a token returns 403.
- AC: Process.stdout 'resize' events call pty.resize(cols, rows) on the spawned PTY.
- AC: On SIGTERM and SIGINT the supervisor unlinks its socket file and exits 0; the spawned child receives SIGTERM and is reaped before exit.
- AC: Per-agent log at ~/.panopticon/logs/pty-supervisor-<agentId>.log records each socket POST as one JSON line with { ts, agentId, kind: 'socket_write', contentLength, caller? }.
- AC: @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch is added to the root package.json dependencies, and tsdown emits dist/pty-supervisor.js with node-pty marked external (verified by inspecting the bundle).
- AC: Header comment in pty-supervisor.ts documents: Node-only requirement (no Bun), H1 PTY-owns-claude crash semantics, env-var passthrough, socket-path convention, per-message echo behavior, and the out-of-scope nature of permission relay.
close_reason: Added Node PTY supervisor, authenticated Unix socket injection, logging, cleanup, and dist build entry.

title: pan-1405: Document the PTY supervisor in CLAUDE.md and docs/
description:
Add a 'PTY supervisor for orchestrator delivery' section to CLAUDE.md (next to the existing 'Claude Code Channels (experimental)' block, which is now downgraded to 'legacy fallback'). Update docs/ to describe the three-tier delivery, the Node-only requirement, the dist/pty-supervisor.js build artifact, the Docker exclusion, and the H1 'supervisor owns claude' lifecycle. Update the 'Claude Code Channels' subsection to point at the supervisor as the recommended transport. No code changes.
- AC: CLAUDE.md has a new section 'PTY supervisor for orchestrator delivery' that explains the three-tier router, the Node 22 requirement, and the H1 crash semantics.
- AC: The existing 'Claude Code Channels (experimental)' section is updated with a 'now legacy fallback — see PTY supervisor section' lead-in.
- AC: Where appropriate, docs/ pages that mention delivery (anything that grep'd for 'channels MCP' or 'tmux paste-buffer') are updated to reflect supervisor-first delivery.
close_reason: Completed

title: pan-1405: Add useSupervisor + supervisorScriptPath fields to LauncherConfig
description:
Extend src/lib/launcher-generator.ts so when useSupervisor is true, the final `exec claude <args>` becomes `exec node <supervisorScriptPath> claude <args>`. Behavior is identical for everything else. Cover both the conversation path (buildCommand for spawnMode === 'conversation') and the work-agent path (buildNonConversationCommand). Add unit tests to launcher-generator.test.ts that assert the exec line composition with and without useSupervisor. Do not touch Pi or review sub-role launchers.
- AC: LauncherConfig.useSupervisor (boolean, default undefined→false) and LauncherConfig.supervisorScriptPath (string, required when useSupervisor=true) are added with JSDoc explaining the wrap-claude semantics.
- AC: When useSupervisor=true on a work-agent launcher, the emitted script contains exactly one line matching `^exec node <supervisorScriptPath> claude(?: --[\w-]+ .+)*$` — claude args are preserved verbatim from buildNonConversationCommand.
- AC: When useSupervisor=true on a conversation launcher, the final command line wraps claude in `node <supervisorScriptPath>`; the keep-alive loop and conversation-ended echoes are unchanged.
- AC: When useSupervisor=false / undefined the generated script is BYTE-FOR-BYTE identical to today — a regression snapshot test asserts this for one work-agent and one conversation fixture.
- AC: Pi launchers and review sub-role launchers ignore useSupervisor (it is a Claude-Code-only field).
- AC: supervisorScriptPath is shellQuote'd in the emitted exec line.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1405: Add src/lib/pty-token.ts mirroring bridge-token.ts
description:
Create src/lib/pty-token.ts exposing readPtyTokenSync(agentId), writePtyTokenSync(agentId), and PTY_TOKEN_HEADER. Token file lives at ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/pty-token at mode 0600. Mirror bridge-token.ts API and tests exactly so callers have a familiar shape.
- AC: writePtyTokenSync writes a 32-byte hex token to ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/pty-token at mode 0600.
- AC: readPtyTokenSync returns the token, or null when the file does not exist.
- AC: Exported header constant PTY_TOKEN_HEADER is 'x-panopticon-pty-token' (distinct from BRIDGE_TOKEN_HEADER for diagnosability).
- AC: Unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/pty-token.test.ts cover write→read round-trip, missing file returns null, and mode-0600 enforcement.
close_reason: Added pty-token helper with per-agent token path, header constant, and focused unit coverage.

title: pan-1402: Add regression tests in setup-hooks.test.ts that assert tool-event hooks register globally
description:
Extend src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts with new unit tests that exercise the helper-level surface (`addPanopticonHookIfMissing` on a fresh ClaudeSettings) for each restored entry: PreToolUse:pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse:heartbeat-hook, PostToolUse:permission-event-hook, Stop:stop-hook, Stop:permission-event-hook, PreToolUse:tldr-read-enforcer ('Read'), PostToolUse:tldr-post-edit ('Edit|Write'). Each test asserts both (a) the first call returns true and writes the expected matcher + command, and (b) a duplicate call is a no-op. The intent is a regression net: if anyone re-introduces a PAN-982-style strip in setupHooksCommand, these tests still pass at the helper layer, so add a higher-level test too that invokes the full setupHooksCommand with a temp HOME / .claude directory and asserts each of the six entries lands on disk. Use existing test patterns in the file.
- AC: setup-hooks.test.ts contains a regression test for each tool-event hook restored in restore-tool-event-hooks-in-settings, asserting addPanopticonHookIfMissing writes the entry and is idempotent on the second call.
- AC: A higher-level test exercises setupHooksCommand against an isolated temp HOME and asserts the resulting settings.json contains PreToolUse:pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse:heartbeat-hook, PostToolUse:permission-event-hook, Stop:stop-hook, and Stop:permission-event-hook (TLDR entries conditional on python3 detection).
- AC: `npm test -- src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts` passes with the new assertions.
close_reason: Added helper-level and setupHooksCommand regression tests for restored global tool-event hooks.

title: pan-1402: Remove `hooks:` blocks from sync-sources/agents/pan-*-agent.md
description:
Delete the `hooks:` YAML block from the frontmatter of all seven Panopticon pipeline agent definitions under sync-sources/agents/: pan-work-agent.md, pan-planning-agent.md, pan-review-agent.md, pan-inspect-agent.md, pan-test-agent.md, pan-uat-agent.md, pan-merge-agent.md. Keep every other frontmatter field (name, description, model, permissionMode, effort, tools, mcpServers) untouched. These files are sync sources — the workspace `.claude/agents/` directory copies them on `pan install` / `pan sync`. After this strip, the agents (if Claude Code ever discovers them by name) carry no frontmatter hooks, eliminating any future double-fire pathway.
- AC: sync-sources/agents/pan-work-agent.md, pan-planning-agent.md, pan-review-agent.md, pan-inspect-agent.md, pan-test-agent.md, pan-uat-agent.md, and pan-merge-agent.md no longer contain a `hooks:` key in their YAML frontmatter.
- AC: All other frontmatter fields (name, description, model, permissionMode, effort, tools, mcpServers) on the seven agent files are preserved exactly as before.
- AC: Any existing test that asserts `frontmatter.hooks` exists for these agent files is updated or removed; `npm test` passes.
close_reason: Removed hooks frontmatter blocks from all seven sync-source Panopticon agent definitions.

Delete the `hooks:` YAML block from the frontmatter of all five role files: roles/work.md, roles/plan.md, roles/review.md, roles/test.md, roles/ship.md. In src/lib/__tests__/role-definitions.test.ts, drop every `expect(frontmatter.hooks).toEqual(expect.any(Object))` assertion (lines 33, 57, 97, 116, 137). The role files themselves remain in roles/ — only the hooks declaration goes.
