title: pan-1402: Update docs/HOOKS.md to describe the PAN-1402 revert and remove the split-registry section
description:
Rewrite the 'Split-Registry Rule (PAN-982)' section of docs/HOOKS.md (lines 23–48). Replace it with a 'Hook Registration' section explaining all nine hook events live in global ~/.claude/settings.json and are installed by `pan install` / `pan admin hooks install`. Add a short 'History' note: PAN-982 attempted a per-agent frontmatter migration for PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop; PAN-1402 reverted it because Claude Code did not honor the frontmatter hooks when `--agent` was passed a file path. Update the 'Migration Pruning' subsection to point at the new PAN-1402 state (no pruning needed; the prune block was removed). Leave the rest of the document (hook inventory, execution flow, Pi gaps) unchanged.
- AC: docs/HOOKS.md no longer contains the 'Split-Registry Rule (PAN-982)' section claiming PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop live in per-agent frontmatter.
- AC: A new 'Hook Registration' section describes all nine hooks living in global settings.json, with a 'History' paragraph summarizing the PAN-982 attempt and PAN-1402 revert.
- AC: Users upgrading across PAN-1402 are explicitly told to re-run `pan install` and restart any in-flight work agents to pick up the restored hook registration.
close_reason: Updated HOOKS.md for PAN-1402 global hook registration and upgrade instructions.

title: pan-1402: Re-register PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop hooks in setupHooksCommand and remove the PAN-982 prune block
description:
In src/cli/commands/setup/hooks.ts, restore the pre-PAN-982 registrations: addHookIfMissing('PreToolUse', 'pre-tool-hook'), addHookIfMissing('PostToolUse', 'heartbeat-hook'), addHookIfMissing('PostToolUse', 'permission-event-hook'), addHookIfMissing('Stop', 'stop-hook'), addHookIfMissing('Stop', 'permission-event-hook'), and (inside the existing `if (python3Available)` gate) addHookIfMissing('PreToolUse', 'tldr-read-enforcer', 'Read') + addHookIfMissing('PostToolUse', 'tldr-post-edit', 'Edit|Write'). Delete the entire `removeIfPresent(...)` block (lines 384–406 of the current file) including the 'PAN-982: Atomic migration' comment. Rewrite the PAN-982 explanatory comment (lines 352–369) into a shorter PAN-1402 note explaining that tool-event hooks were briefly migrated to per-agent frontmatter and reverted because the path-form `--agent` did not honor them. Update the success-message `console.log` block (lines 452–464) to list the restored hooks alongside the existing six. The bead lands cleanly only when `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm test src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts` all pass.
- AC: After running setupHooksCommand on an empty settings.json, the result contains PreToolUse with pre-tool-hook, PostToolUse with heartbeat-hook + permission-event-hook, and Stop with stop-hook + permission-event-hook.
- AC: When python3 is detected, the post-setup settings.json also contains PreToolUse with tldr-read-enforcer (matcher 'Read') and PostToolUse with tldr-post-edit (matcher 'Edit|Write').
- AC: The `removeIfPresent(...)` PAN-982 migration block (and its console.log of removed entries) is fully deleted; setupHooksCommand never strips PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop entries that point at Panopticon's bin dir.
- AC: Running setupHooksCommand twice in a row leaves settings.json byte-identical the second time (idempotency preserved by addHookIfMissing).
- AC: `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm test -- src/cli/commands/__tests__/setup-hooks.test.ts` all pass with no new failures after the change.
close_reason: Restored global tool-event hook registrations in setupHooksCommand and removed the PAN-982 prune block.

title: pan-748: Wire <ContextUsageIndicator> into ConversationPanel.tsx header
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ConversationPanel.tsx, import <ContextUsageIndicator>, ensure the header container (.conversationTerminalHeader in the shared CSS module, or the local panel header class) has container-type: inline-size, and place the indicator between featureCost and conversationTerminalStatus. Wire conversation.contextUsage through. Verify the embedded variant (when !embedded check) also receives the indicator if appropriate — or document that embedded mode intentionally omits it (likely the better choice since embedded panels have minimal headers).
- AC: ConversationPanel header (the !embedded path) renders <ContextUsageIndicator contextUsage={conversation.contextUsage ?? null} /> in the correct position.
- AC: Header container has container-type: inline-size — verified by inspecting the rendered DOM or CSS module.
- AC: Embedded variant (when embedded prop is truthy) intentionally omits the indicator OR includes it consistently — decision documented in a code comment in the same commit.
- AC: Indicator updates live as the conversation grows — visually verified by running the dashboard and watching an active conversation accumulate messages.
- AC: When conversation.model is null or unknown, indicator hides (or only the dot in small variant is shown) — header layout unaffected.
- AC: End-to-end smoke: open a conversation in the dashboard, observe indicator present with non-zero values for a known model; check at wide, medium, and small header widths (resize the panel) and confirm responsive variants apply.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-748: Build <ContextUsageIndicator> React component with responsive variants
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ContextUsageIndicator.tsx (or a shared location both ConversationTerminal and ConversationPanel can import from — verify import paths first). Props: { contextUsage: ContextUsage | null }. Renders nothing when contextUsage is null. Otherwise renders a flex row: (1) compact size text in 'k'/'M' format from estimatedTokens (e.g., 33.04k, 1.53M, raw number for < 1024), (2) percentage text (e.g., '22%'), (3) a thin horizontal bar with width = percentUsed% and color from a threshold function (green <50%, yellow 50-80%, red >80%). Title attribute on the wrapper shows full numbers and the model's context window for tooltip context. Use a CSS module sibling file (ContextUsageIndicator.module.css) with @container queries on the indicator's container: wide variant shows all three elements, medium variant shows size+bar (no percentage text), small variant shows only a colored dot whose color matches the threshold and whose title carries the full text. The header container in both consumer headers must be marked with container-type: inline-size for the queries to fire. Format helper formatCompactCount(n) is a small pure function alongside the component.
- AC: Component renders nothing (returns null) when contextUsage prop is null.
- AC: formatCompactCount produces: '512' for 512, '33.04k' for 33041, '1.53M' for 1_530_000. Implemented as a pure helper next to the component.
- AC: Bar color is green for percentUsed < 50, yellow for 50-80, red for > 80. Driven by CSS classes (not inline color) so themes can override.
- AC: Wide variant (container > 900px) shows size text + percentage text + bar.
- AC: Medium variant (container 600-900px) shows size text + bar (no separate percentage text).
- AC: Small variant (container < 600px) shows only a colored dot. Title attribute exposes full size + percentage so hover/focus surfaces it.
- AC: Vitest/RTL tests cover: null prop, three variants render at three container widths, threshold colors at 0%, 49%, 50%, 79%, 80%, 99%, format helper edge cases.
- AC: Accessibility: bar has role='progressbar' with aria-valuenow/min/max set; the entire indicator has a title attribute summarizing the values for screen readers.
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-748: Emit contextUsage on the subscribeConversationMessages RPC stream
description:
In src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts (subscribeConversationMessages handler, around line 545-639), after each watch callback fires with new ParseResult, call computeContextUsage(sessionFile, model) and include the result in the emitted ConversationEvent. Pull conversation.model from the DB (or pass it through from the subscription open) — model is set on conversation creation and rarely changes mid-stream, so it can be fetched once at subscribe time and cached for the lifetime of the subscription. Cleanup unchanged.
- AC: subscribeConversationMessages emits contextUsage on every ConversationEvent (or as an additional event type alongside messages — pick the simpler shape).
- AC: Subscription resolves conversation.model once at open; does not re-query DB on every JSONL change.
- AC: When computeContextUsage returns null (unknown model), the event payload carries contextUsage: null — does not omit the field, does not throw.
- AC: Watcher cleanup on unsubscribe still works correctly — no leaked fs.watch handles after subscribe/unsubscribe cycles.
close_reason: Added contextUsage to subscribeConversationMessages messages events, caching conversation.model at stream open; typecheck and dashboard server build pass.

title: pan-748: Enrich REST /api/conversations and /api/conversations/:id with contextUsage
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, update the response serialization for GET /api/conversations (list, around line 1105) and GET /api/conversations/:id (single, around line 1163) to call computeContextUsage(conversation.sessionFile, conversation.model) and include the result as the contextUsage field on each Conversation object. Skip the call entirely when sessionFile is null/empty. Compute in parallel for the list endpoint (Promise.all) to avoid serializing fs.stat calls. Returns null contextUsage when the helper returns null.
- AC: GET /api/conversations returns each Conversation object with a contextUsage field (object or null).
- AC: GET /api/conversations/:id returns the Conversation with the contextUsage field.
- AC: List endpoint computes contextUsage in parallel (Promise.all), not sequentially.
- AC: Conversations with null/empty sessionFile produce contextUsage: null without throwing.
- AC: Manual curl against the running dashboard (or vitest route test) confirms the field is present with expected shape for an existing conversation.
close_reason: Added contextUsage to GET /api/conversations and GET /api/conversations/:id responses; missing or unknown session/model yields null; typecheck, route tests, and server build pass.

title: pan-748: Add ContextUsage type to contracts; extend Conversation and ConversationEvent
description:
In packages/contracts/src/ (or wherever the Conversation shape and ConversationEvent union are defined per the survey: packages/contracts/src/rpc.ts and the conversation type module), add a new ContextUsage interface: { activeBytes: number; estimatedTokens: number; contextWindow: number; percentUsed: number }. Add optional contextUsage: ContextUsage | null to the Conversation shape returned by REST endpoints, and to the ConversationEvent payload emitted by subscribeConversationMessages. Use Effect Schema (or whatever schema layer the contracts package uses) so it serializes correctly across the RPC boundary. Run packages/contracts build (cd packages/contracts && npm run build) so consumers pick up the new types.
- AC: ContextUsage type exported from packages/contracts with fields: activeBytes (number), estimatedTokens (number), contextWindow (number), percentUsed (number).
- AC: Conversation contract (REST response shape) gains optional contextUsage: ContextUsage | null field.
- AC: ConversationEvent / subscribeConversationMessages payload gains optional contextUsage: ContextUsage | null field. Schema validates correctly.
- AC: Contracts package builds cleanly: cd packages/contracts && npm run build exits 0.
- AC: Typecheck passes for the whole repo after the contract change: npm run typecheck exits 0.
close_reason: Fixed inspection feedback by importing ContextUsage from the canonical frontend chat-types mirror instead of duplicating the type in ConversationList; typecheck passes.

title: pan-748: Add computeContextUsage() helper in conversation-service.ts
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts, add an exported async function computeContextUsage(sessionFile: string, model: string | null): Promise<ContextUsage | null>. It calls findLastCompactBoundary(sessionFile) to get the byte offset, fs.stat()s the file to get total size, computes activeBytes = max(0, size - boundaryOffset), estimates tokens = Math.ceil(activeBytes / 4), resolves the model via resolveModelIdSync() and looks up contextWindow in MODEL_CAPABILITIES. Returns { activeBytes, estimatedTokens, contextWindow, percentUsed } when the model is known; returns null when model is null/empty or not in MODEL_CAPABILITIES after resolution. Clamps percentUsed to [0, 100]. Add a short code comment near the function noting the bytes/4 heuristic and the limitation. The ContextUsage type itself is defined in the contracts bead — for this bead, define a local interface and align it once contracts land (or coordinate ordering).
- AC: Helper returns null when model is null, empty string, or not in MODEL_CAPABILITIES after resolveModelIdSync.
- AC: Helper returns { activeBytes, estimatedTokens, contextWindow, percentUsed } when given a valid sessionFile and a known model.
- AC: activeBytes equals fileSize - findLastCompactBoundary(sessionFile) when a compact boundary exists; equals fileSize when no boundary exists.
- AC: estimatedTokens equals Math.ceil(activeBytes / 4); percentUsed = (estimatedTokens / contextWindow) * 100, clamped to [0, 100].
- AC: Vitest unit tests cover: known Claude model, known GPT model, deprecated model that resolves to current, unknown model returns null, null model returns null, file with no compact boundary, file with multiple compact boundaries (uses last), empty file (activeBytes = 0, percentUsed = 0).
- AC: Reuses existing compactOffsetCache via findLastCompactBoundary — does not re-scan the entire JSONL on each call.
close_reason: Added computeContextUsage helper with compact-boundary cache reuse and unit tests covering model resolution, token estimation, boundaries, empty files, and cache reuse.

title: pan-1132: End-to-end verification of all three PAN-1132 issue criteria
description:
Run the three verification commands listed in the issue body and document outcomes in the PR description (or as a comment on PAN-1132 if there is no PR). This is a verification-only bead — no code changes.
- AC: `pan admin tldr status` correctly reports the workspace daemon as running with a real PID (not the CLI's PID), and after a daemon stop reports stopped. State must survive CLI exit (run the command, exit, run it again — same result).
- AC: After `pan sync`, `~/.claude/skills/pan-tldr/SKILL.md` exists and is not deleted on a subsequent `pan sync` (i.e. pan-tldr is NOT in LEGACY_SKILL_NAMES).
- AC: All four cloister prompts (planning, review, test, resume-work) render a TLDR section when `TLDR_AVAILABLE=true` and render no TLDR section when the variable is unset. work.md already does this — confirm no regression.
- AC: All seven role files that touch code-reading workflows (roles/plan.md, roles/work.md, roles/test.md, roles/review-security.md, roles/review-correctness.md, roles/review-performance.md, roles/review-requirements.md) still contain their TLDR sections from commit ab8cd6f70 — regression check.
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` (root + frontend) all pass.
close_reason: Verified TLDR daemon status behavior, pan-tldr sync preservation, prompt/role TLDR rendering, and full quality gates.

title: pan-1389: Click on a feed entry navigates to its primary destination
description:
Wire the onSelect callback in SessionFeedSidebar: for kind='conversation' push '/conv/<name>' via window.history (matching existing /conv/:id routing in App.tsx); for kind='activity' push '/command-deck?issueId=<issueId>' (or whatever the existing command-deck entry URL is — check App.tsx tab routing) to surface the issue's command deck where the per-issue ActivityFeedSidebar lives; for kind='git' no-op with a one-time console.debug. Tests stub window.location and assert the correct URL is pushed for each kind.
- AC: Conversation entry click pushes /conv/<name> and dispatches popstate
- AC: Activity entry click pushes the existing command-deck route for the entry's issueId
- AC: Git entry click is a no-op (does not throw, does not navigate)
close_reason: Wired default SessionFeedSidebar entry navigation for conversations and activity routes, left git as a no-op, dispatched popstate, and added tests.

title: pan-1389: Mount SessionFeedSidebar in App shell with a persisted toggle (default closed)
description:
Edit src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx to add a right-hand SessionFeedSidebar slot rendered to the right of the existing main view, with width ~320px when open and zero when closed. Open/closed state lives in App component state, hydrated from localStorage key 'panopticon.ui.sessionFeedSidebarOpen' (default false). Add a toggle button (lucide History icon) in the App shell — either inline at the top-right of the main pane header or as a tiny edge-tab — that flips the state and persists. Tests cover toggle, persistence, and that the default state is closed.
- AC: Sidebar is closed by default on first load (no localStorage entry)
- AC: Clicking the toggle opens the sidebar and writes 'true' to localStorage 'panopticon.ui.sessionFeedSidebarOpen'
- AC: On reload with localStorage 'true', the sidebar is open without flicker
- AC: Clicking the in-sidebar X close button restores the closed state and persists it
close_reason: Mounted SessionFeedSidebar in the App shell behind a default-closed persisted toggle, restored open state from localStorage, persisted close, and added App tests.

title: pan-1389: Files and Comments tabs render permanent 'coming soon' empty states
description:
Add two small empty-state components inside SessionFeedSidebar (or as siblings under components/sessionFeed/) that explain the data source for Files (aggregate file_change feed) and Comments (issue comments) is not yet wired. Each empty state includes one short sentence and, when the relevant follow-up issue is filed, references it by ID. The tabs remain clickable so the strip matches the reference design. Tests assert the empty state is rendered when the tab is Files or Comments.
- AC: Selecting the Files tab renders a 'Files feed coming soon' empty state with a one-line explanation
- AC: Selecting the Comments tab renders a 'Comments feed coming soon' empty state with a one-line explanation
- AC: Neither stub tab attempts a fetch or store read (no spinner, no network)
close_reason: Added Files and Comments stub empty states with one-line explanations, bypassed wired feed hooks for stub tabs, and added tests.

title: pan-1389: SessionFeedSidebar: tab bar, tab content, empty states, live updates
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/SessionFeedSidebar.tsx as the parent component. Renders a 6-tab strip (All | Chats | Files | Git | Comments | Activity) wired to SessionFeedTab state stored in component-local useState (and synced to localStorage key 'panopticon.ui.sessionFeedSidebarTab'). For each tab: pulls the relevant feed via useMergedFeed, runs groupByContiguousLabel using formatBucketLabel, renders one BucketSection per group. Live reactivity is automatic — Zustand observation slices and react-query intervals already push updates. Header includes a Terminal/History icon and an X close button that calls onClose. Files and Comments tabs render their stub empty state (see stub-tabs bead). The 'All' tab renders the global empty state only when every wired source is empty. Each wired tab (Chats, Git, Activity) renders its own per-tab empty state when its source is empty. Tests cover tab switching, empty states per tab, group label rendering, and a 'live update' test that mutates store.observationsByIssueId and asserts the Activity tab updates without re-mount.
- AC: Tab bar contains exactly six tabs in order: All, Chats, Files, Git, Comments, Activity (covers AC1 of the issue body — sidebar panel renders time-bucketed session entries)
- AC: Entries within each label group are ordered newest-first (covers AC3 of the issue body)
- AC: When a new MemoryObservation is dispatched into the store, the Activity tab and All tab update without a remount (covers AC4 of the issue body — panel updates as new observations are written)
- AC: Each wired tab (All, Chats, Git, Activity) renders its own empty state when its source is empty; the All tab's empty state appears only when every source is empty (covers AC5 of the issue body — empty state handled gracefully)
- AC: Active tab persists across reloads via localStorage key 'panopticon.ui.sessionFeedSidebarTab'
- AC: Component header comment explicitly enumerates the other two 'activity-named' components and explains the scope distinction (mitigates H3 namespace hazard)
close_reason: Added SessionFeedSidebar with six persisted tabs, grouped feed rendering, per-tab empty states, live observation updates, close control, and tests.

title: pan-1389: Render a labeled group of feed entries
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/BucketSection.tsx that takes { label: string; items: SessionFeedEntry[]; onSelect: (entry) => void } and renders a small uppercase label header followed by the items in order, dispatching to the correct card by entry.kind. Used by SessionFeedSidebar after running groupByContiguousLabel over the tab-filtered feed. Tests cover label rendering and card-kind dispatch.
- AC: Renders the label exactly once at the top of the section
- AC: Dispatches each item to ConversationFeedCard / ActivityFeedCard / GitFeedCard based on entry.kind
- AC: Items are rendered in the order provided (caller controls sort)
close_reason: Added BucketSection with label rendering, conversation/activity/git card dispatch, order preservation, entry-select callback, and tests.

title: pan-1389: Merge conversation/observation/git feeds into a single deduplicated stream
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/useMergedFeed.ts. Consumes the three single-source hooks and returns a memoized SessionFeedEntry[] deduplicated by id and sorted newest-first by timestamp. Provides a tab-aware filter: for tab='all' the merged list; for tab='chats'|'activity'|'git' the single-source list; for 'files'|'comments' an empty array (stub tab support handled at the rendering layer).
- AC: Merges entries from all three sources, deduplicating by id (first occurrence wins)
- AC: Output is sorted strictly newest-first by timestamp
- AC: Tab filter returns only entries whose kind matches the requested tab (or all for 'all'; empty for 'files'|'comments')
close_reason: Added useMergedFeed and helpers to dedupe, sort, and tab-filter session feed entries from all wired data sources.

title: pan-1389: Render conversation entry card with reference fields
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/ConversationFeedCard.tsx rendering, in this order: (1) agent icon — reuse ProviderIcons from components/CommandDeck/ProviderIcons.tsx mapped from entry.agent; (2) status dot — small colored circle, color from a local STATUS_DOT_COLORS map keyed on agent state (active/waiting/idle), defaulting to 'idle' when not known; (3) agent display name truncated to ~120px; (4) relative timestamp using formatRelativeTime; (5) snippet rendered as plain text (no markdown in v1 — the reference comment notes markdown but this is acceptable per the v1 scope) truncated to two lines; (6) message count + bubble icon when messageCount is defined; (7) thread label badge when threadIsPrimary is false. title: pan-1389: Hook: useGitFeed wraps /api/git-activity into SessionFeedEntry[]
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/useGitFeed.ts using react-query (queryKey ['session-feed-git'], refetchInterval 15_000, staleTime 5_000) to fetch GET /api/git-activity. Maps each returned ActivityEntry to a SessionFeedEntry of kind 'git' with id=entry.id, timestamp=entry.timestamp, issueId=entry.issueId ?? null, workspaceId=null, message=entry.message, level=entry.level, source=entry.source. Surfaces loading and error states the caller can render.
- AC: Hook returns SessionFeedEntry[] of kind 'git', loading boolean, and error
- AC: refetchInterval matches the 15s cadence used by ActivityPanel for git-activity
- AC: When the endpoint returns non-OK, the hook returns an empty array and surfaces error rather than throwing
close_reason: Added useGitFeed for /api/git-activity with git SessionFeedEntry mapping, loading/error surface, and tests.

title: pan-1389: Selector: flatten observationsByIssueId into activity SessionFeedEntry[]
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/useObservationFeed.ts. It exports a closure-keyed selector (createObservationFeedSelector) following the pattern in CommandDeck/ActivityFeedSidebar.tsx createActionStatusObservationSelector — closure variables hold lastSource and lastResult so React subscribers do not re-render when the underlying slice identity is unchanged. It flattens state.observationsByIssueId across every issue, keeps only entries with actionStatus !== null, sorts newest-first by timestamp, caps the result at the most recent 500 entries, and maps each one to a SessionFeedEntry of kind 'activity' with id=observation.id, timestamp=observation.timestamp, issueId, workspaceId, headline=observation.actionStatus, summary=observation.summary.
- AC: Flattens observations from every issueId key into a single sorted list
- AC: Filters out observations where actionStatus is null
- AC: Sorted newest-first by timestamp
- AC: Caps output at 500 entries
- AC: Returns referentially identical result when the source slice identity is unchanged (memoization test)
close_reason: Added memoized observation feed selector that flattens all issue observations into activity SessionFeedEntry rows with tests.

title: pan-1389: Selector: derive conversation SessionFeedEntry[] from store + REST
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/useConversationFeed.ts. It reads the existing useQuery({ queryKey: ['conversations'] }) shape returned by GET /api/conversations and maps each Conversation row to a SessionFeedEntry of kind 'conversation', deriving lastMessageDate from lastAttachedAt || createdAt, lastMessageSnippet from title (or 'No messages yet' when null), agent from harness ('claude-code' → 'claude_code', 'pi' → 'pi', null → 'unknown'), messageCount from the enrichment field if present and undefined otherwise. Filters out conversations where archivedAt is non-null. Adds tests using a stubbed react-query result.
- AC: Returns one SessionFeedEntry per non-archived Conversation row
- AC: lastMessageDate prefers lastAttachedAt, falls back to createdAt
- AC: Snippet falls back to 'No messages yet' when title is null
- AC: agent mapping: 'claude-code' → 'claude_code', 'pi' → 'pi', null → 'unknown'
close_reason: Added useConversationFeed mapping /api/conversations rows into conversation SessionFeedEntry objects with tests.

title: pan-1389: Add M5t-style time-label formatter and contiguous-grouping helper
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/sessionFeedLabels.ts exporting (1) JUST_NOW_THRESHOLD_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000, (2) formatBucketLabel(timestamp: string|Date, now: Date): string returning 'Just Now' when (now - timestamp) ≤ JUST_NOW_THRESHOLD_MS, an hour-of-day string like '9 PM' for earlier today, 'Yesterday <Mon DD> · <H AM/PM>' for yesterday, '<Weekday> <Mon DD>' for this week, and a locale date for older, (3) groupByContiguousLabel<T>(items: T[], getLabel: (item: T) => string): Array<{ label: string; items: T[] }> that groups contiguous items sharing the same label. Add unit tests covering each label branch and a grouping test where two adjacent items have the same label and a third differs.
- AC: formatBucketLabel returns 'Just Now' when the input is within 5 minutes of now
- AC: formatBucketLabel returns an hour-of-day string (e.g. '9 PM') for earlier-today inputs older than 5 minutes
- AC: formatBucketLabel returns 'Yesterday <Mon DD> · <H AM/PM>' for inputs from the previous local day
- AC: formatBucketLabel returns '<Weekday> <Mon DD>' for inputs older than yesterday but within the past 7 days
- AC: groupByContiguousLabel collapses adjacent items with the same label and starts a new group when the label changes
- AC: JUST_NOW_THRESHOLD_MS is exported as a single named constant so the value can be changed in one place
close_reason: Added session feed bucket label formatting, contiguous grouping helper, and focused frontend unit tests.

title: pan-1389: Add SessionFeedEntry discriminated union and shared types
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/sessionFeed/types.ts exporting a SessionFeedEntry discriminated union (kind: 'conversation' | 'activity' | 'git' | 'file_change' | 'comment'), a shared SessionFeedTab type ('all' | 'chats' | 'files' | 'git' | 'comments' | 'activity'), and per-kind payload shapes that match the reference contract (ConversationEntry fields for conversation, MemoryObservation summary fields for activity, ActivityEntry shape for git). Every variant carries id, timestamp (ISO string), workspaceId (or null), and issueId (or null) so downstream code can sort, group, and filter uniformly.
- AC: types.ts compiles under tsc --strict and exports SessionFeedEntry as a discriminated union with all six kinds
- AC: Every variant carries id (string), timestamp (ISO string), workspaceId (string|null), issueId (IssueId|null)
- AC: Conversation variant matches the ConversationEntry contract in the issue comment (agent, lastMessageDate, lastMessageSnippet, messageCount, threadLabel, threadIsPrimary — with optionality where the source row may not have the field)
- AC: SessionFeedTab union has exactly six members and matches the reference tab strip
close_reason: Added shared SessionFeedEntry variants and SessionFeedTab types for the session feed sidebar.

title: pan-1394: Set .conversationTitleEditBtn default opacity to 0.4 so Sparkles + Pencil are discoverable
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/styles/command-deck.module.css, change the .conversationTitleEditBtn base rule's `opacity: 0;` to `opacity: 0.4;`. Leave the .conversationTerminalTitle:hover .conversationTitleEditBtn rule (opacity: 1) and the .conversationTitleEditBtn:hover rule unchanged. Do not modify ConversationPanel.tsx or any other CSS class. Verify in the running dashboard that (a) the Sparkles button is visible without hovering the title, (b) the Pencil button is visible without hovering, (c) both buttons still brighten to full opacity on hover, and (d) the Sparkles spinner remains fully opaque while a retitle request is in flight. Run a Playwright check in an isolated browser instance as part of verification.
- AC: The .conversationTitleEditBtn base rule in command-deck.module.css has `opacity: 0.4` (not 0). Only that single declaration changes in the file.
- AC: When viewing a conversation in the CommandDeck without hovering anything, the Sparkles (✨) regenerate-title button is visible next to the conversation title at ~40% opacity.
- AC: The Pencil (rename) button is also visible at ~40% opacity in the same resting state — no separate class change required, both buttons share the rule.
- AC: Hovering the conversation title row promotes both buttons to full opacity (opacity: 1) via the existing .conversationTerminalTitle:hover .conversationTitleEditBtn rule.
- AC: While the Sparkles regenerate-title mutation is in flight, the button's spinner (Loader2 icon) remains fully opaque — the inline style={{ opacity: 1 }} override on ConversationPanel.tsx:499 still wins over the new 0.4 base.
- AC: No other CSS rule, no other file, and no JSX className changes are included in the diff. The change is exactly one line in command-deck.module.css.
- AC: Browser UAT (Playwright in an isolated browser instance/profile, no shared session with other agents) confirms the Sparkles button is visible at rest, brightens on hover, and triggers the retitle mutation when clicked.
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass.
close_reason: Changed .conversationTitleEditBtn base opacity from 0 to 0.4 so Sparkles and Pencil are visible at rest.

Use Playwright's `test.use({ contextOptions: { ... } })` or a fresh `browser.newContext()` per spec so the test runs in an isolated browser profile (per workspace Playwright Isolation rule). Existing 'renders a live FlywheelStatus payload without browser console errors' assertion is preserved.

title: pan-1393: Rewrite docs/flywheel-brief.md tick loop to mirror the suggest-only role
description:
Rewrite docs/flywheel-brief.md so the tick loop, substrate-fix rule, and human-input invariant match the suggest-only role. Replace step 3 ('Act. Take the smallest Panopticon-native action…') with 'Emit ranked suggestions — produce a `suggestions[]` array in the FlywheelStatus snapshot, with the next-best moves for the operator.' Replace the substrate-fix-rule body so it describes filing-and-suggesting instead of fixing. Keep status/state separation and end-of-run report semantics unchanged. This bead is blocked by role-prompt-rewrite so the brief lands second and mirrors the role's final text.

description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx, add a new `<Section title="Suggestions">` rendered BEFORE the existing MetricStrip. Each row shows: priority badge (color-coded), action verb, issueId (when present) as a click-through to the existing kanban/inspector navigation, and rationale text. When suggestions[] is empty, render a placeholder: 'No suggestions yet — orchestrator will emit on next tick.' Add component unit tests in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/__tests__/FlywheelStatusDetails.test.tsx covering: rendering with multiple suggestions, priority sort order, empty state, click-through for suggestions with an issueId, click-through omission for system-level suggestions without an issueId.

title: pan-1393: Show last-tick freshness chip on the Flywheel pane header
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx (header) and/or FlywheelStatusDetails.tsx, replace the existing static `Last tick` time cell with a freshness chip computed from `Date.now() - new Date(status.lastTickAt).getTime()`: ≤30s → green 'live'; 30–90s → amber 'last tick Xs ago'; >90s → red 'stalled — last tick Xm ago'. Update the displayed elapsed time on a 5s interval so the chip transitions in real time. Add a unit test in src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/__tests__/FlywheelPage.test.tsx using vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.setSystemTime() to assert all three states render at the correct thresholds (per the workspace fake-timers rule).

- AC: Header shows a red 'stalled — last tick Xm ago' indicator when lastTickAt is >90s old
- AC: Display refreshes at least every 5s while the page is mounted (so the chip transitions without waiting for a new snapshot)
- AC: Unit test uses vi.useFakeTimers()/vi.setSystemTime() to verify all three thresholds — no real wall-clock waits
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1393: Rewrite roles/flywheel.md tick loop to suggest-only (no pipeline driving, no substrate fixing)
description:
Rewrite roles/flywheel.md so the tick loop becomes: 1. Inventory; 2. Diagnose; 3. Emit suggestions (the deliverable of every tick — a ranked list with action/issueId?/rationale/priority); 4. File substrate bugs as records when broken Panopticon behavior is discovered; 5. Respect pauses. Remove the 'Fix substrate first' step and replace it with 'Suggest substrate fixes — a substrate bug becomes an investigate/start suggestion in the suggestions list. The orchestrator never edits substrate code itself.' Expand the 'Never' list to explicitly forbid: pan start, pan plan, pan tell, pan approve, pan sync-main, pan resume, pan wake, pan kill, pan wipe, pan close, plus any direct git/PR mutation beyond `gh issue view` / `gh issue create` for substrate bug filing. Keep `pan flywheel emit-status` and `pan flywheel report` allowed. Make explicit that emit-status is the OUTPUT of every tick, not an afterthought.
- AC: Tick loop in roles/flywheel.md no longer contains a 'Drive the pipeline' step
- AC: Tick loop has an explicit 'Emit suggestions' step that requires producing a ranked suggestions[] every tick and calling `pan flywheel emit-status`
- AC: Explicit 'Never' / do-not list includes (verbatim or equivalent): pan start, pan plan, pan tell, pan approve, pan sync-main, pan resume, pan wake, pan kill, pan wipe, pan close, direct edits to feature branches, direct PR merges
- AC: Substrate handling: filing bugs via `gh issue create` is explicitly allowed; editing substrate code is explicitly forbidden; substrate bugs surface as investigate/start suggestions
- AC: Document references the new FlywheelSuggestion shape (action/issueId?/rationale/priority) so the orchestrator emits valid payloads
- AC: Existing allowlist (author=eltmon or panopticon-agent[bot]) and label exclusions (needs-design, needs-discussion) are preserved as filters for what makes it into suggestions
close_reason: Rewrote flywheel role tick loop to emit ranked suggestions, file substrate bugs only as records, preserve author/label filters, and forbid pipeline-driving or direct branch/PR mutation.

title: pan-1393: Add FlywheelSuggestion schema and suggestions field to FlywheelStatus contract
description:
In packages/contracts/src/flywheel.ts, add FlywheelSuggestionAction and FlywheelSuggestionPriority literal unions, a FlywheelSuggestion interface + Schema struct ({action, issueId?, rationale, priority}), and add `suggestions: ReadonlyArray<FlywheelSuggestion>` to both the FlywheelStatus interface (required) and the Schema (optionalWith default `[]` for backward compatibility with historical latest.json). Update packages/contracts/src/flywheel.test.ts to (a) keep the existing validPayload passing by adding a `suggestions: [...]` entry, (b) cover absent-suggestions backward compat decode, (c) cover invalid action/priority rejection.
- AC: FlywheelSuggestion exported as both TS type and Effect Schema, with action ∈ {start,resume,plan,review,merge,unblock,park,investigate,wait} and priority ∈ {urgent,high,medium,low}
- AC: FlywheelStatus TypeScript interface requires `suggestions: ReadonlyArray<FlywheelSuggestion>`
- AC: FlywheelStatus Schema decodes payloads WITHOUT a suggestions field (defaults to `[]`) — verifies historical latest.json files do not break
- AC: FlywheelStatus Schema rejects suggestions with unknown action or priority values
- AC: packages/contracts/src/flywheel.test.ts covers: valid suggestions array, empty array, missing field (default), invalid action, invalid priority — all green under `bun --filter @panctl/contracts test`
- AC: `bun --filter @panctl/contracts run build` succeeds and exports FlywheelSuggestion from packages/contracts/src/index.ts
close_reason: Added FlywheelSuggestion contract, required FlywheelStatus suggestions with decode default, and tests for valid, empty, missing, invalid action, and invalid priority cases.

title: pan-1391: RTL test for SessionDetail unarchive button
description:
Add tests to src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/__tests__/SessionDetail.test.tsx covering: (a) Unarchive button is visible only when source='managed-archived'; (b) clicking it calls POST /api/conversations/:name/unarchive with the correct conversationName; (c) on success the archived-conversations query is invalidated (verified via QueryClient spy); (d) network failure surfaces an inline error and the button re-enables. Use vi.spyOn(globalThis, 'fetch') to stub the endpoint.

title: pan-1391: Show archived timestamp and Unarchive button in SessionDetail for managed-archived rows
description:
Extend the Session interface in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/SessionDetail.tsx to include `source: 'discovered' | 'managed-archived'`, `archivedAt: string | null`, and `conversationName: string | null`. When `source === 'managed-archived'`: (1) add an 'Archived at' metadata field showing the formatted archivedAt; (2) below the existing enrichment controls (or replacing them for archived rows since enrichment/embed make less sense), render an Unarchive button. The Unarchive button calls fetch(`/api/conversations/${conversationName}/unarchive`, { method: 'POST' }), shows a loading spinner during the call, on success invalidates ['archived-conversations'] and the active conversations query (if visible elsewhere via React Query keys), and either closes the drawer or marks the row as restored. Render a small toast/inline confirmation on success. Hide enrichment controls for managed-archived rows (they reference discovered_sessions IDs that may not exist).
- AC: For source='managed-archived' rows, SessionDetail displays the Archived at timestamp in the Metadata section.
- AC: An Unarchive button is visible at the bottom of the drawer when source='managed-archived'.

- AC: Failure paths (network error, 4xx, 5xx) surface an inline error message; the button re-enables.

title: pan-1391: Add source filter chips to FacetPanel and wire the archived fetcher in ConversationsPage
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/FacetPanel.tsx, add a Source chip group at the top of the panel (above Time range) with three chips: All, Discovered, Managed-archived. Extend the Filters interface with `source?: 'all' | 'discovered' | 'managed-archived'`. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/ConversationsPage.tsx: (1) thread `source` through the filters state (default 'all'); (2) add a `fetchArchivedConversations()` helper that calls fetch('/api/conversations/archived') and parses the unified rows; (3) add a useQuery keyed on ['archived-conversations'] that runs when source === 'managed-archived' OR 'all'; (4) when source === 'all', merge the discovered list with archived results (concat, sorted by lastTs DESC); when 'discovered', behave as today; when 'managed-archived', use only archived results and hide the search bar + search-related controls; (5) tag each row with its `source` field so SessionTable/SessionDetail can render correctly; (6) selectedId becomes a composite { source, id } (or stringified key) to disambiguate across sources.

- AC: Default source is 'all'. The chip group is the topmost facet block.
- AC: Selecting 'Managed-archived' loads the archived list from GET /api/conversations/archived and shows ONLY archived rows in SessionTable.
- AC: Selecting 'Discovered' shows only discovered_sessions rows (current default behavior, no archived rows).
- AC: Selecting 'All' shows discovered + archived rows merged, sorted by lastTs DESC. React keys use composite source-id to avoid collisions.
- AC: When source === 'managed-archived' the keyword/semantic search bar is hidden (FTS does not index archived conversations).

title: pan-1391: Add GET /api/conversations/archived endpoint with discovered_sessions enrichment join
description:
Add `listArchivedConversationsWithEnrichment()` to src/lib/database/conversations-db.ts (LEFT JOIN conversations c on discovered_sessions ds where ds.session_id = c.claude_session_id, filter c.archived_at IS NOT NULL, order by c.archived_at DESC). Add `getArchivedConversationsRoute` to src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts using the same async Effect/HttpRouter pattern as `getConversationsRoute` — origin validation via validateOrigin, no sync FS, jsonResponse for output. Map each joined row to the unified shape: { id, source: 'managed-archived', conversationName, jsonlPath (derived via sessionFilePath when claude_session_id+cwd available, else null), workspacePath: cwd, primaryModel: model, messageCount (joined or 0), firstTs (joined or createdAt), lastTs (joined or archivedAt), estimatedCost (joined or totalCost), tokenInput (joined or 0), tokenOutput (joined or 0), toolsUsed (joined parsed JSON or []), filesTouched (joined parsed or []), tags (joined parsed or []), summary (joined or title), enrichmentLevel (joined or 0), enrichmentFailed (joined or false), panopticonManaged: true, panIssueId: issueId, archivedAt }. Register the route in `conversationsRouteLayer`. Add a Vitest test in src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/conversations.test.ts covering: (a) returns archived conversations sorted by archivedAt DESC, (b) excludes non-archived conversations, (c) returns rows even when no discovered_sessions row exists (LEFT JOIN null-safe), (d) merges enrichment fields when a matching discovered_sessions row is present.
- AC: GET /api/conversations/archived returns 200 with an array of archived conversation rows ordered by archivedAt DESC.
- AC: Each row contains the unified shape: id, source='managed-archived', conversationName, workspacePath, primaryModel, messageCount, lastTs, estimatedCost, summary, enrichmentLevel, panopticonManaged=true, panIssueId, archivedAt.
- AC: Conversations with no matching discovered_sessions row are still returned, with null-safe defaults (messageCount=0, toolsUsed=[], summary falls back to conversation title).
- AC: Conversations with a matching discovered_sessions row include the joined enrichment fields (messageCount, summary, toolsUsed, filesTouched, tags, estimatedCost, tokenInput/Output, enrichmentLevel).
- AC: Non-archived conversations (archived_at IS NULL) are never returned by this endpoint.
- AC: Endpoint is async-only — no execSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync, readdirSync, or statSync. Uses validateOrigin() like sibling routes.
- AC: Vitest covers all four behaviors (a)-(d) above and passes under `npm test`.
close_reason: Added archived conversations DB enrichment join, GET /api/conversations/archived route helper/registration, and backend tests for sorting, exclusion, null-safe rows, and enrichment merge.

title: pan-1358: Add /pan-handoff wrapper skill (skills/pan-handoff + sync-sources/skills/pan-handoff)
description:
Create the wrapper skill per docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md. Source-of-truth lives at skills/pan-handoff/SKILL.md (and sync-sources/skills/pan-handoff/SKILL.md mirror). Frontmatter description starts with the canonical 'pan handoff <conv> — agent-authored conversation handoff that spawns a new conversation'. Body explains: when to invoke (long-running conversations near context wall, deliberate context switch), the --focus argument, what 'fallback to summary fork' means in practice. Remove pan handoff from the docs/SKILLS-CONVENTION.md exclusion list if it appears there (it does not today; just confirming). Ensure scripts/lint-skills.sh passes against the current CLI surface.

title: pan-1358: Vitest tests for handoff pipeline fallback paths
description:
Add Vitest tests that drive runForkPipeline (or createSummaryFork) through the handoff fallback paths with mocked deliverAgentMessage and a tmp directory standing in for PANOPTICON_HOME/handoffs. Required cases: (a) source-ended → falls back to summary; (b) handshake timeout (never write doc/sentinel) → falls back to summary; (c) doc written but sentinel never appears → falls back to summary; (d) sentinel and doc both written but doc fails validation → falls back to summary; (e) happy path: doc + sentinel both written and valid → seeds the new conversation with the doc text. Use vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync for the timeout case (per the project's mandatory fake-timers rule for retry/delay code). Mock deliverAgentMessage as a no-op so the test does not actually attempt tmux/channels delivery.
- AC: Test covers source-ended → summary-fork fallback with a fallbackReason recorded
- AC: Test covers timeout fallback using vi.useFakeTimers / vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync (NOT real setTimeout)
- AC: Test covers doc-without-sentinel and sentinel-without-doc edge cases — both treated as in-progress until timeout
- AC: Test covers validation-failure fallback (doc present but lacks 'Suggested skills' header)
- AC: Test covers happy path: doc + sentinel + valid content → new conversation seeded with the doc text and handoff_doc_path persisted
close_reason: Closed

On HandoffStallError, HandoffValidationError, source-ended detection, OR source-in-workspace-container detection, log the reason and silently downgrade to forkMode: 'summary'. Update the conversation title prefix appropriately ('Handoff: ...' vs 'Summary Fork: ...'). Persist the doc path on the target conversation row (uses handoff-artifact-record's schema).

title: pan-1358: Implement requestHandoffFromAgent live-agent handshake with timeout and validation
description:
Add requestHandoffFromAgent(sourceConv, focus, options) to src/lib/conversations/summary-fork.ts (parallel in shape to generateSummaryForFork). Resolves the handoff doc and sentinel paths via handoff-paths helpers, renders the inlined roles/handoff.md template with {{focus}} and {{outputPath}} substitutions, calls deliverAgentMessage(sourceConv.tmuxSession, prompt, 'handoff-request') to deliver the prompt, then polls the filesystem with a small backoff (e.g., 1s intervals) for BOTH docPath and sentinelPath to appear. Default timeout 300_000ms (5 minutes). On timeout, throw HandoffStallError; on validation failure, throw HandoffValidationError; on successful read, return { docPath, docText }. Validation: trimmed doc length ≥200 chars AND contains a '## Suggested skills' or '## Suggested Skills' header line.
- AC: requestHandoffFromAgent exported from src/lib/conversations/summary-fork.ts (or a new sibling module); takes (sourceConv, focus?, options?)
- AC: Function delivers the prompt via deliverAgentMessage (channels + tmux fallback, same primitive used elsewhere)
- AC: Polls for BOTH doc and sentinel; requires both files present before reading the doc
- AC: Timeout defaults to 300_000ms; throws a distinct HandoffStallError on timeout (or returns a tagged failure object — implementer's choice, but distinguishable)
- AC: validateHandoffDoc(text) returns ok when text length ≥200 chars and contains an H2 'Suggested skills' header (case-insensitive); returns a failure reason otherwise
- AC: Function never blocks the event loop with sync FS or sleep calls; uses fs/promises and setTimeout-based polling
close_reason: Closed

The template must include {{focus}} and {{outputPath}} substitution placeholders. Add a brief section in docs/FORKS.md cross-referencing this template (full doc update is a separate bead).

title: pan-1331: Static no-actions-lost audit: enumerate every action exposed in the dashboard today and verify each has a registry entry — HARD GATE before any deletion
description:
Write `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/__tests__/issueActions.no-actions-lost.test.ts`. It hardcodes a list of EVERY action currently surfaced anywhere in the dashboard (the full ActionKey set from the old `commandDeckActions.ts` plus any one-off buttons elsewhere — Reset/Stop/Merge in DrawerActionBar, the BadgeBar artifact actions, the kanban card's MergeButton/RecoverButton/ResetIssueButton/StopAgentButton/RestartFromPlanButton, the StatusFlowControl buttons in `WorkspaceStatusOverview.tsx`, the project-tree right-click items in `ProjectNode.tsx` + `ContainerNode.tsx`). For each, the test asserts (a) the new `issueActions` registry contains an entry whose semantic intent matches, AND (b) the action's rendered text appears in the DOM when the menu is rendered with that action enabled. The test fails the build if any pre-existing action lacks a registry counterpart. This is the gate that BLOCKS the `deck-zone-actions` deletion bead until coverage is provably complete.
- AC: Test file enumerates every ActionKey value from the existing `commandDeckActions.ts` and asserts each has a corresponding entry in the new `issueActions` registry (either same key or an alias-mapping documented in the file)
- AC: Test enumerates the BadgeBar artifact actions (Tasks, Status, Inference, Discussions, Transcripts, Upload, Sync) and asserts each has a registry entry
- AC: Test enumerates the WorkspaceStatusOverview / StatusFlowControl buttons (compact MERGE, Review & Test, Recover, Stop, Start, Resume, Reset Session, Create Workspace, Reopen) and asserts each has a registry entry
- AC: Test enumerates the project-tree right-click context-menu items (in `ProjectNode.tsx` + `ContainerNode.tsx`) and asserts each has a registry entry
- AC: Test asserts that Zone B session-scoped actions (stopSession, viewTerminal, viewState, viewVbrief, copySessionId, copyTmuxCommand) STILL RENDER on their existing surface — they are session actions, not issue actions, and must not be lost in this refactor even though they're not in `issueActions`
- AC: Test runs in `npm test` and fails the suite if any catalogued action is missing from the registry — verified by intentionally removing a registry entry and seeing the test fail
close_reason: Added static no-actions-lost audit covering legacy Command Deck actions, BadgeBar, StatusFlowControl, project-tree context items, and Zone B session actions.
