description:
Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/IssueActionMenu/` containing: (a) `useIssueActions.ts` — hook that takes an issueId, gathers live state (agent, reviewStatus, workspace, lifecycle, planning, beads, phase) from `useDashboardStore` + React Query, runs every registry entry's `enabledWhen` predicate, partitions into `{ primary, secondary, overflow }` using `getPhasePrimaryActions()` + the kind-and-group rules, and exposes handlers (one per action) that perform the right side effect (fetch POST / open existing dialog / open PanOpenInPicker). (b) `IssueActionMenu.tsx` — component with `mode: 'inline' | 'overflow-only' | 'hybrid'` and optional `pinRight: IssueActionKey[]`. Inline renders ghost buttons; overflow renders a `<DropdownMenu>` triggered by a `⋯` button. Disabled actions render greyed with a tooltip showing the `enabledWhen` reason. Destructive actions trigger `useConfirm()` interstitial before firing. Dialog actions open existing components (`PlanDialog`, `SwitchModelModal`, `PanOpenInPicker`, etc.) — does NOT re-implement them. (c) Tests covering: every mode renders the right set; destructive prompts confirm; disabled actions are unclickable with tooltip; the open/close states of opened dialogs are tracked correctly.
- AC: `<IssueActionMenu issueId mode='inline'>` renders ghost buttons for the primary set returned by `useIssueActions()`
- AC: `<IssueActionMenu issueId mode='overflow-only'>` renders a single `⋯` button that opens a dropdown containing every enabled action
- AC: `<IssueActionMenu issueId mode='hybrid'>` renders the primary set inline plus a `⋯` button containing secondary + overflow
- AC: `pinRight={['viewPr']}` renders View PR after a `flex-1` spacer; supports any registry key in the pin array
- AC: Destructive actions (wipe, destroy, closeOut, resetIssue) open the `useConfirm()` dialog with a typed confirmation; one-click destructive is impossible to trigger
- AC: Disabled actions render greyed with a `title` tooltip (or shadcn Tooltip) showing the reason from `enabledWhen`
- AC: Dialog actions (plan, swarm, tell, switchModel, open) open the existing dialog component without re-implementing it
- AC: Component tests cover all three modes, destructive confirm flow, disabled-with-tooltip rendering, and the dialog-open side effect for at least one dialog action
close_reason: Added IssueActionMenu/useIssueActions primitive with inline, overflow-only, and hybrid modes; pinRight support; typed destructive confirmation through useConfirm; dialog mounting for existing components; and focused component coverage.

title: pan-1331: Mount `PanOpenInPicker` so the `open` action has a working dialog
description:
Wire the existing `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/PanOpenInPicker.tsx` into a portal/overlay reachable from the new `open` registry handler. The picker takes a `cwd` prop (workspace path). The handler computes the workspace path from the issueId via the existing workspace-info store/state. Add a small wrapper (`IssueOpenInDialog`) that translates `(issueId) → cwd` and mounts the picker in a modal overlay. Manual-test the chain: click Open in the menu → picker appears → selecting an editor actually launches it. If the picker is broken end-to-end, file a follow-up issue; this bead's scope is wiring only.
- AC: Clicking the `open` action from the menu opens `PanOpenInPicker` as an overlay with the correct `cwd` for the issue's workspace
- AC: Closing the picker (escape / click-outside / close button) restores focus to the menu trigger
- AC: If the issue has no workspace, the `open` action is disabled in the menu with a tooltip ('Workspace does not exist')
- AC: Manual smoke-test verifies an editor actually launches when an option is selected; result documented in PR description
close_reason: Closed

title: pan-1331: Add `POST /api/issues/:id/beads/:beadId/inspect` server route
description:
Add an Effect-based POST route at `/api/issues/:id/beads/:beadId/inspect` in `src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts`. Accept JSON body `{ deep?: boolean }`. Resolve project via `resolveProjectFromIssue()`, derive workspace path via the standard `workspaces/feature-<id-lowercase>` convention (mirror logic from `src/cli/commands/inspect.ts` lines 41-64), and call `spawnInspectAgent({ projectKey, projectPath, issueId, beadId, workspace, branch: 'feature/<id>' }, { deep })`. Return `{ success, runId, tmuxSession }` on success; HTTP 4xx with a JSON `{ error }` on missing workspace or unknown project. Use the existing `execAsync` / `fs/promises` / Effect FileSystem service — NO `execSync`, NO synchronous fs (per CLAUDE.md and the `no-execsync-server.md` rule). Integration test against a fixture workspace asserts the spawn call shape.
- AC: POST /api/issues/:id/beads/:beadId/inspect responds 200 with `{ success: true, runId, tmuxSession }` when workspace and beadId are valid
- AC: Responds 404 with `{ error }` when the issue cannot be resolved to a project, or when no workspace exists at the expected path
- AC: Optional `{ deep: true }` body forwards `deep: true` to `spawnInspectAgent()`; absence defaults to fast inspection
- AC: Route uses Effect / `execAsync` / `fs/promises` only — no `execSync` and no synchronous fs in any code path; verified by lint or grep at end of bead
- AC: Integration test in `src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/issues.inspect.test.ts` covers happy path + unknown issue + missing workspace + deep flag
close_reason: Added POST /api/issues/:id/beads/:beadId/inspect with async workspace checks, inspect agent spawning, deep flag forwarding, and route coverage.

title: pan-1331: Create canonical `issueActions` registry as a SUPERSET — PRD §4.8.1 catalog UNION every action exposed in the dashboard today
description:
Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/issueActions.ts` exporting (a) `IssueActionKey` union type containing TWO groups: (i) PRD §4.8.1 row keys — plan, autoPlan, watchPlanning, donePlanning, startAgent, startSkipPlanning, swarm, tell, doneWork, requestReview, restartReview, recoverReview, stopAgent, pause, unpause, untroubled, recoverAgent, resumeSession, switchModel, syncMain, inspectBead, reopen, closeOut, wipe, destroyWorkspace, open, resetIssue, viewPr; AND (ii) preserved-from-existing keys — beads, inference, discussions, transcripts, upload, syncDiscussions, statusReview, createWorkspace, copySettings, resetSession, restartFromPlan, restartAgent, reviewTest (the compound shortcut). (b) `IssueActionEntry` interface (`key`, `label`, `panVerb`, `endpoint`, `enabledWhen(state)`, `phasePrimary: PipelinePhase[]`, `kind: 'safe'|'dialog'|'destructive'`, `group`). (c) The full `ISSUE_ACTIONS: IssueActionEntry[]` table covering BOTH groups. (d) Pure selectors `getEnabledActions(state)` and `getPhasePrimaryActions(state, phase)`. The PRD §4.8.3 phase-primary table is encoded; the preserved-from-existing actions live in the registry but have empty `phasePrimary` (they're reachable via the `⋯` overflow but never promoted to inline). No React, no JSX, no handlers — pure declarative metadata + selector logic. Reuse the existing `derivePipelineState()` from `commandDeckActions.ts` (extract it into its own module so both the new registry AND the old one can consume it during the transition). Comprehensive unit tests for the selectors against fixture states covering each PRD §4.8.3 phase row.
- AC: `IssueActionKey` union contains one key for every row in PRD §4.8.1 (28 keys including the 'View PR' link surface but excluding 'Merge to main' per Decision D6)
- AC: Registry contains the SUPERSET — every PRD §4.8.1 key PLUS preserved-from-existing keys: beads, inference, discussions, transcripts, upload, syncDiscussions, statusReview, createWorkspace, copySettings, resetSession, restartFromPlan, restartAgent, reviewTest. Zero existing actions dropped.
- AC: Every registry entry has a non-empty `label`, a `panVerb` (or null for client-only / non-CLI actions), an `endpoint` (or null), an `enabledWhen` predicate, a `phasePrimary` array (may be empty for preserved-existing entries), a `kind`, and a `group`
- AC: `getPhasePrimaryActions(state, 'QUEUED_FOR_PLAN')` returns `[plan, startAgent]` in order; same exhaustive check for every phase row in PRD §4.8.3 (PLANNING → [watchPlanning, donePlanning], Planned-idle → [startAgent], WORK_RUNNING → [tell, doneWork], INPUT → [open, tell], REVIEW_RUNNING/SHIP_RUNNING → [tell, recoverAgent], CHANGES_REQUESTED → [open, requestReview], STUCK → [recoverAgent, tell], READY_TO_MERGE → [viewPr, merge], MERGED → [closeOut])
- AC: Unit tests cover the selector functions with fixtures for every PRD §4.8.3 phase row; tests are added to `npm test` and pass
- AC: `derivePipelineState()` is extracted into its own module if not already isolated, and is the SINGLE source of phase classification consumed by both the registry selectors and the Board card's verb badge
close_reason: Implemented canonical issueActions registry, extracted shared pipeline classifier, and added selector/phase-primary tests.

title: pan-457: Dashboard Conversations panel: search, facets, detail, enrichment controls
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/conversations/. ConversationsPanel.tsx is the top-level panel added to Mission Control navigation. ConversationSearchBar.tsx combines a free-text input (FTS), filter chips (workspace/model/managed/tag/tool/file/since), and a semantic toggle when embeddings enabled. ConversationTimeline.tsx renders grouped, faceted results (workspace/model/time/cost/enrichment-level) with click-to-filter. ConversationDetail.tsx is a drawer with messages, cost, models, duration, tools, files, summaries at each level, and EnrichmentControls.tsx buttons (quick/deep/custom-model). FacetPanel.tsx shows counts. All data flows through RPC client (WsTransport) and the Zustand store receives scan/enrich progress events.
- AC: Conversations panel appears in Mission Control nav and loads recent sessions on open
- AC: Search bar: free-text, filter chips, semantic toggle all work and compose
- AC: Faceted results group by workspace/model/time/cost/enrichment-level; clicking a facet filters
- AC: Detail drawer shows full session with visual distinction between managed and ad-hoc sessions
- AC: Enrichment buttons trigger RPC calls and show live progress via domain events
- AC: Aggregate cost view matches `pan conversations cost --by workspace` output
- AC: Manual browser test: scan + search + enrich + detail view all work end-to-end

title: pan-457: Dashboard server routes + service for conversations
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts (Effect HTTP routes: POST /scan, GET /search, GET /list, GET /:id, POST /enrich, POST /embed, GET /cost) and src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-index.ts (service layer wrapping src/lib/conversations, emitting domain events during scan/enrich/embed). All FS access must be async (fs/promises + execAsync per CLAUDE.md). Register routes in server.ts.
- AC: All seven endpoints return correct shapes and pass Effect Schema validation
- AC: Scan progress emits domain events on /ws/rpc subscribeDomainEvents stream
- AC: Enrichment progress emits per-session domain events with model + cost
- AC: Zero execSync/readFileSync/writeFileSync/readdirSync/statSync in routes or service (grep + review)

title: pan-457: CLI: pan conversations scan [dirs...] [--watched|--system|--dry-run]
description:
Register `pan conversations` parent command and `scan` subcommand in src/cli/commands/conversations/. Wires through the scanner service. Renders live progress with a simple stderr bar (cliProgressOrMinimal) including the format from spec: [████] 847/1203 dirs | 2341 sessions found | 12.3s. --dry-run only reports what would be scanned (counts by dir).
- AC: `pan conversations scan ~/Projects/foo` scans and reports inserted/updated/skipped counts
- AC: `pan conversations scan --watched` reads watchDirs from config
- AC: `pan conversations scan --system` walks entire ~/.claude/projects
- AC: `--dry-run` performs no DB writes; prints planned counts
- AC: Progress bar renders with matching format to spec

title: pan-457: CLI: pan conversations enrich with all flags
description:
Add src/cli/commands/conversations/enrich.ts. Flags per spec: --limit, --workspace, --since, --deep, --with <model>, --full, --prompt, --upgrade. Selects target session set, calls enrichment engine, renders per-session progress + final totals (sessions processed, cost actual, failures).
- AC: `pan conversations enrich --limit 50` enriches up to 50 un-enriched sessions at level 1
- AC: `--deep --upgrade` converts all level-1 sessions to level 2
- AC: `--with claude-opus-4-6 --full <id>` runs level 3 with full transcript
- AC: All flag combinations from spec CLI section parse and execute

title: pan-457: CLI: search, list, show, cost subcommands
description:
In src/cli/commands/conversations/: add search.ts (wires to search composer), list.ts (--managed/--unmanaged, defaults to recent), show.ts (full detail for a session id including messages/cost/models/duration/tools/files/summaries), cost.ts (aggregate --by workspace|model).
- AC: `pan conversations search "query"` runs FTS and prints table results
- AC: `pan conversations list --managed` lists only panopticon_managed=1 rows
- AC: `pan conversations show <id>` prints full session detail including current enrichment level
- AC: `pan conversations cost --by workspace` matches sum of discovered_sessions.estimated_cost grouped by workspace_path

title: pan-457: Scanner orchestrator with bounded parallelism work pool
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/scanner.ts and work-pool.ts. The scanner accepts { mode: 'targeted'|'watched'|'system', dirs?: string[], onProgress?: (p) => void } and emits progress events. Walks ~/.claude/projects/*/ for *.jsonl files (async readdir), filters by mode (targeted → under given dirs; watched → under config watchDirs; system → all). Uses a bounded promise work pool sized by system-probe. For each JSONL: stat for (size, mtime), skip if unchanged vs DB row, else parse async, resolve hash via hash-resolver, correlate against conversations + cost_events tables, insert/upsert into discovered_sessions. SQLite writes serialized through a batch queue. Build a correlator.ts helper that queries existing conversations.session_file and cost_events.session_id for Panopticon-managed detection.
- AC: Targeted mode scans only JSONLs whose resolved workspace is under the given dirs
- AC: Watched mode reads dirs from config.yaml conversations.watchDirs
- AC: System mode discovers all sessions under ~/.claude/projects
- AC: Change detection: re-scan of unchanged file does not re-parse JSONL (verified via parser call counter in test)
- AC: Work pool respects maxParallel from system-probe; concurrent reads never exceed limit
- AC: Progress callback fires with { dirsProcessed, dirsTotal, sessionsFound, elapsedMs }
- AC: Sessions matching existing conversations.session_file are marked panopticon_managed=1 with pan_issue_id + pan_agent_id populated
- AC: Permission-denied directories are skipped with a warning, not a crash
- AC: Cost estimate populated from token counts using model-capabilities.ts pricing

title: pan-457: Embedding providers + pure-JS cosine similarity + semantic search
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/embeddings/. providers/openai.ts, voyage.ts, ollama.ts all implement EmbeddingProvider { embed(texts: string[]): Promise<Float32Array[]> }. cosine.ts implements pure-JS cosine over Float32Array in memory (normalize once, dot product). index.ts is embed(sessionIds). Selection via config.conversations.embeddingProvider. Stored as BLOB in session_embeddings with dim + model. Search integration: search --semantic "text" embeds the query with the same provider, loads all embeddings into memory, computes topK by cosine, optionally intersects with structured filters. --similar <id> loads that session's embedding and does the same comparison.
- AC: OpenAI provider calls text-embedding-3-small and stores 1536-dim Float32 BLOB
- AC: Voyage provider calls voyage-code-3 and stores 1024-dim BLOB
- AC: Ollama provider calls nomic-embed-text via local HTTP and stores BLOB
- AC: Cosine function ranks identical vectors as 1.0 and orthogonal as 0.0 (unit test)
- AC: `pan conversations embed` embeds all enriched un-embedded sessions
- AC: `pan conversations embed --regenerate` overwrites all embeddings
- AC: `pan conversations embed --status` prints indexed/embedded counts + model name
- AC: `pan conversations search --semantic "race condition"` returns cosine-ranked results
- AC: `pan conversations search --similar <id>` returns sessions ranked by similarity to that id
- AC: Missing API key produces an actionable error (not a silent crash)

title: pan-457: Tiered enrichment engine (L1 quick / L2 deep / L3 custom) via model-fallback router
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/enrichment/. index.ts is enrich(sessionIds, level, opts). level1.ts samples first + last + 1 middle message, calls Haiku tier via existing model-fallback.ts, produces { summary, tags[] }. level2.ts samples first 3 + last 3 + 5 middle + tool_use summaries, calls Sonnet tier, produces { summary_detailed, tags[] }. level3.ts takes a user-specified model id; sample by default or full transcript if --full; optional --prompt appended to system prompt. Writes update discovered_sessions, bump enrichment_level, record enrichment_model + enriched_at, and sync FTS5. Subagent parallelism = bounded promise pool (config.conversations.enrichment.maxParallel, default 4). Cost estimation before each batch; prompts confirmation when total estimated cost exceeds costConfirmThreshold. Auto-embed on L2+ if conversations.embeddingAutoOnDeep.
- AC: Level 1 sends exactly 3 messages (first + last + 1 middle) to Haiku tier
- AC: Level 2 sends 11 messages + tool_use summaries to Sonnet tier
- AC: Level 3 with --with <model> calls the specified model; --full sends the entire transcript
- AC: --prompt "..." is appended to the enrichment system prompt
- AC: Re-enrichment preserves level-1 summary while overwriting summary_detailed and tags
- AC: Cost estimate printed and user prompted [y/N] when total exceeds costConfirmThreshold
- AC: Model API failure marks session as enrichment_failed (does not crash pool)
- AC: FTS5 rows are upserted after each successful enrichment
- AC: Enrichment goes through src/lib/model-fallback.ts (respects provider disabled state)
- AC: Cost estimate vs actual from cost_events stays within 20% on sample run

title: pan-457: Search composer: structured filters + FTS5 + semantic + output formats
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/search.ts. Parses filter flags (--workspace/--model/--managed/--unmanaged/--since/--before/--after/--min-cost/--max-cost/--min-messages/--tag/--tool/--file/--issue/--enriched/--not-enriched) into a typed ConversationFilter, composes SQL with AND logic, runs FTS5 MATCH when a free-text query is given (with BM25 ranking, column weighting summary_detailed=2/summary=1.5/tags=1/files=0.5), and intersects with semantic results when --semantic or --similar is set. Relative time parsing (7d, 24h, 30m) via existing duration util. --format table|json|brief|ids renders via a small formatter module.
- AC: All structured filters compose with AND and produce correct SQL
- AC: --since 7d, --before ISO date, --after ISO date all parse and filter correctly
- AC: --tag/--tool/--file match against JSON array columns
- AC: FTS5 query with prefix (refact*), phrase ("error handling"), boolean (redis AND cache NOT test) all return BM25-ranked results
- AC: Filters + FTS compose: FTS query runs within the filtered set
- AC: --format table|json|brief|ids all produce correct shape

title: pan-457: End-to-end integration tests: scan → enrich → embed → search
description:
Add a vitest integration suite that (1) seeds a temp ~/.claude/projects fixture with 3 sample JSONLs, (2) runs the scanner against it, (3) runs L1 enrichment with a mocked model router, (4) generates embeddings with a stub provider, (5) runs structured + FTS + semantic searches and asserts results. Also assert cost aggregation and that re-scan on unchanged files skips parsing. Mirrors the test matrix from the spec.
- AC: Full pipeline test: scan → enrich → embed → search returns expected top result
- AC: Re-scan on unchanged files does not re-parse (parser mock call count unchanged)
- AC: Cost aggregation equals sum of individual session estimated_cost
- AC: FTS stays in sync after re-enrichment (old summary text no longer matches; new text matches)
- AC: Managed session is correctly linked to an issue_id when conversations table has a matching session_file

title: pan-457: config.yaml schema additions for conversations subsystem
description:
Extend src/lib/config.ts / config-yaml.ts with the conversations block: watchDirs (default ['~/Projects']), scanMaxParallel (null = auto), embeddings (false), embeddingProvider ('openai'), embeddingModel ('text-embedding-3-small'), embeddingAutoOnDeep (true), enrichment.quickModel (null), enrichment.deepModel (null), enrichment.maxParallel (4), enrichment.costConfirmThreshold (1.00). Add migration for existing config files.
- AC: New conversations config block loads with defaults when missing from config.yaml
- AC: Config migration leaves existing user values intact and fills defaults
- AC: Typed access via getConfig() for all new fields

title: pan-457: RPC + events contracts for conversation subsystem
description:
In packages/contracts/src/: add DiscoveredSession + ConversationFilter + search result types to types.ts; add conversation RPC procs (scan, search, listDiscovered, showDiscovered, enrich, embed, cost) to rpc.ts; add domain events scan.started/scan.progress/scan.complete/enrich.progress/enrich.complete/embed.progress to events.ts. Rebuild contracts package.
- AC: All new types exported from @panopticon/contracts and typecheck passes project-wide
- AC: New RPC procs appear in PanRpcGroup with Effect Schema
- AC: Domain events are added to event-reducers.ts so the frontend store receives them

title: pan-457: SystemCapabilities probe for adaptive scan parallelism
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/system-probe.ts. Detects cpuCores (os.cpus().length), driveType (lsblk -d -o name,rota via execAsync on Linux; 'unknown' on macOS/Windows), driveReadMBps (read a 10MB sample file using fs/promises, measure elapsed), availableMemoryMB (os.freemem / 1024 / 1024). Cache result per-process. Parallelism strategy table from spec: SSD → min(cpuCores, 16); HDD → 2; unknown → 4. Respect optional config override conversations.scanMaxParallel.
- AC: Probe returns SystemCapabilities with all four fields populated on Linux (SSD case)
- AC: Gracefully falls back to 'unknown' driveType on macOS/Windows without throwing
- AC: Chosen parallelism matches spec table (SSD→min(cores,16), HDD→2, unknown→4)
- AC: conversations.scanMaxParallel config override takes precedence over probe result
- AC: Probe uses execAsync + fs/promises — zero sync FS or execSync calls

title: pan-457: Claude project hash → workspace path resolver
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/hash-resolver.ts. Primary strategy: use cwd from the first JSONL message (from async parser). Fallback strategy: build a reverse map by walking configured watchDirs + projects.yaml entries, encoding each with existing encodeClaudeProjectDir (src/lib/paths.ts), and keying by hash. On hash collision between multiple known workspaces, prefer an exact match if any JSONL row hints at it; otherwise return null and log a warning. Cache the reverse map per scan run.
- AC: JSONL-cwd primary path resolves correctly for a session with a cwd field
- AC: Reverse-map fallback resolves a hash to a known workspace when JSONL has no cwd
- AC: Returns null (not crashes) when hash is unknown and no JSONL cwd exists
- AC: Reverse map is built once per scan run (cached), not per session

title: pan-457: Async streaming JSONL parser for scanner use in dashboard server
description:
Create src/lib/conversations/jsonl-async.ts. Uses fs/promises + readline streaming to parse a JSONL and return { messageCount, firstTs, lastTs, modelsUsed, primaryModel, tokenInput, tokenOutput, toolsUsed[], filesTouched[], cwdFromFirstMessage }. Zero sync FS calls. Do NOT modify the existing sync cost-parsers/jsonl-parser.ts — the CLI cost command still uses it. Reuse its ClaudeMessage type.
- AC: Parses message_count, timestamps, models, token totals from a sample JSONL correctly (vitest fixture)
- AC: Extracts tools_used and files_touched arrays from tool_use blocks (Edit/Write/Read paths, Bash commands excluded)
- AC: Reads cwd from first JSONL message when present; returns null if absent or unparseable
- AC: Handles corrupt/empty/partial JSONLs without throwing — returns partial metadata
- AC: Uses only fs/promises + stream APIs — no sync FS calls (verified by lint rule or review)

title: pan-457: Add discovered_sessions, sessions_fts, session_embeddings tables + migration
description:
In src/lib/database/schema.ts, add idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for discovered_sessions (per spec columns), sessions_fts (FTS5 virtual table with BM25 + column weights), session_embeddings (BLOB storage). Add all indexes per spec. Add the same tables to the per-workspace DB block. Create src/lib/database/discovered-sessions-db.ts with CRUD, FTS sync, and embedding helpers. Include a vitest suite mirroring existing conversations-db tests.
- AC: All three tables created idempotently in main + per-workspace DB on startup
- AC: discovered-sessions-db.ts exposes insert/upsert, findByFilters, searchFTS, insertEmbedding, topKCosine, getByJsonlPath
- AC: Upsert is idempotent on (jsonl_path) — re-inserting same row updates file_size/file_mtime without duplicates
- AC: Unit tests cover CRUD, filter composition, FTS insert/search, and embedding storage round-trip

title: pan-1379: Implement issue
description:
## Background

PAN-1249 migrated `src/lib/` to **additive** Effect — every `async fn(): Promise<T>` got an `fnEffect()` sibling, keeping the Promise originals to avoid cascade breakage.

**PAN-1312 (Phase 2) is done** — every `src/cli/` and `src/dashboard/server/` caller now consumes the `*Effect` variants; gate-verified, `typecheck` green, on `main`. Zero `*Async()` call sites remain in production code.

## This issue — Phase 3: finish the migration

Deliver the full Effect-native end state for `src/lib/`. Two phases, in this order, with a verification gate between them:

### Phase 3a — Delete the legacy Promise variants

- **22 `*Async` exports** remain in `src/lib/`, across 8 files: `vbrief/vbrief-index.ts`, `vbrief/io.ts`, `vbrief/dag.ts`, `vbrief/continue-state.ts`, `pan-dir/specs.ts`, `pan-dir/continue.ts`, `memory/checkpoint-client.ts`, `cloister/config.ts`.
- **3 test files** still import `*Async` from `src/lib/`: `tests/lib/cloister/pan-464-container-health.test.ts`, `tests/unit/dashboard/server/routes/projects.test.ts`, `tests/unit/lib/lifecycle/teardown-workspace.test.ts` — migrate these to the `*Effect` siblings *first*, then the `*Async` exports can be deleted.
- Delete each `*Async` export and the dead `Effect.runPromise` bridges that only existed to span the Promise↔Effect boundary.
- Per-file: migrate any remaining test importers → delete `*Async` exports → `npm run typecheck`. A deletion that breaks typecheck means a caller was missed.

**Verification gate:** zero `*Async`-from-`src/lib/` imports anywhere in the repo; `npm run typecheck` + `npm run lint` + `npm test` all green.

### Phase 3b — Canonicalize naming

Once Phase 3a is verified clean (gate above passes), do the cosmetic rename:

- Rename every surviving `fooEffect` → `foo` across `src/lib/`, `src/cli/`, `src/dashboard/server/`, and the test suite. Effect is the canonical form; the `Effect` suffix was temporary scaffolding from PAN-1249's additive phase.
- Where a function has *both* an `*Effect` variant *and* a genuinely-needed sync variant of the same logical operation, the sync one becomes `*Sync` (e.g. `readPlanSync`). The Effect-returning one takes the plain name (`readPlan`).
- This is a codebase-wide mechanical rename — a single coherent commit using a scripted find-replace, then test fixups in the same commit.

**End state:** `src/lib/` exposes `foo()` (returns `Effect`) as the canonical name, `fooSync()` only where sync is genuinely needed. **No `*Async`, no `*Effect` suffix anywhere.**

## Risk control

A prior attempt to do canonicalization *bundled with the caller migration* spiraled ($82 of agent time, ~145 files of test fallout, no convergence — abandoned). The conditions now are different:

- Caller migration is **already done on `main`** — the canonicalization is a pure rename, not bundled with semantic changes.
- Phase 3a + 3b are explicitly **sequenced** — finish deletions first, *verify clean*, *then* canonicalize. Do NOT interleave.
- Use a scripted rename for 3b (one find-replace pass + test fix-ups), not 100 separate Edits.

## Reference

- `backup/pan-1312-deletion-attempt` (on origin) has 4 of the 8 Phase-3a files already cleanly deleted: `agents`, `tmux`, `config-yaml`, `vbrief-dag` async bridges. Those commits can be cherry-picked as a starting reference.

## Done = the migration is fully complete

When 3a + 3b land, `src/lib/` is Effect-native end-to-end, with clean canonical names — the migration arc (PAN-1249 → PAN-1312 → PAN-1379) closed.
- AC: **22 *Async exports** remain in src/lib/, across 8 files: vbrief/vbrief-index.ts, vbrief/io.ts, vbrief/dag.ts, vbrief/continue-state.ts, pan-dir/specs.ts, pan-dir/continue.ts, memory/checkpoint-client.ts, cloister/config.ts.

title: pan-1385: Manual smoke test on qwen3-max and conditional `tested: true` flip
description:
Two checks. **(A) qwen3.7 canonical-ID verification (open question Q1):** before or during this bead, hit `https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/compatible-mode/v1/models` (or read current Alibaba intl docs) to confirm the canonical model ID for Qwen3.7. If the canonical ID is NOT the literal `qwen3.7`, substitute the canonical literal across every artifact landed in earlier beads (settings types, providers map, capabilities, frontend catalog, label maps, routing tests, docs) — one focused diff, same bead. Record the verified ID and verification date in continue.json decisions. If Qwen3.7 is NOT yet available on the intl endpoint, REMOVE every qwen3.7 reference instead and add a deferred-scope note. **(B) Smoke test:** check whether `DASHSCOPE_API_KEY` is available in the workspace environment or in `~/.panopticon/config.yaml` under `apiKeys.dashscope`. IF present: spawn a throwaway agent on `qwen3-max` (`pan start <id> --model qwen3-max` against a benign test issue) AND on the qwen3.7 canonical literal, confirm both boot through the local proxy and emit coherent responses — then flip `tested: true` on PROVIDERS.dashscope in src/lib/providers.ts. IF NOT present: leave `tested: false`, write a short verification checklist into the bead's completion notes, and do NOT block the issue — the smoke test is a verification gate, not a blocker for shipping the wiring.
- AC: If a DASHSCOPE_API_KEY is available, a throwaway `pan start <id> --model qwen3-max` AND `pan start <id> --model <qwen3.7-canonical-ID>` both boot agents that respond to a tmux-delivered prompt with non-error output.
- AC: The canonical DashScope intl model ID for Qwen3.7 has been verified (via `/v1/models` listing or current Alibaba intl docs) and either (a) every reference in the codebase uses the canonical literal, or (b) qwen3.7 entries have been removed with a deferred-scope note recorded in continue.json.
- AC: If smoke test succeeded, PROVIDERS.dashscope.tested is flipped to `true` in src/lib/providers.ts; otherwise the bead is closed with a completion note documenting that the smoke test was deferred (and `tested: false` remains).
- AC: The decision (tested / deferred) is recorded in the bead's completion comment for downstream traceability.
close_reason: Verified canonical Qwen3.7 DashScope intl model ID as qwen3.7-max from current Qwen Cloud docs; replaced the bare qwen3.7 placeholder across code, tests, and vBRIEF artifacts. No DASHSCOPE_API_KEY was present in the workspace environment or ~/.panopticon/config.yaml, so throwaway qwen3-max/qwen3.7-max agent smoke tests were deferred and PROVIDERS.dashscope.tested remains false. Quality gates passed: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test.

title: pan-1192: Unit-test parent-aware swarm model resolution and `modelSource` response field
description:
Add a new test file `src/dashboard/server/routes/__tests__/swarm-default-model.test.ts` (or extend the existing `swarm.test.ts` if it already covers `dispatchSwarmWave` shape — prefer the latter to keep related tests colocated). Use `vi.mock('../../../../lib/agents.js', ...)` to stub `getAgentStateAsync`. Cover three resolution branches and the response shape: (1) parent state exists with `role: 'work', model: 'gpt-5.5'` → `resolveSwarmDefaultModel('PAN-1148')` returns `{ model: 'gpt-5.5', source: 'parent' }`; (2) `getAgentStateAsync` returns null → returns `{ model: <whatever roles.work.model resolves to in test config>, source: 'config' }`; (3) parent state exists but `role: 'plan'` (or empty `model`) → falls through to config branch; plus a dispatch-shape test that exercises the response body and asserts `modelSource` is present with the right value across all three branches. Tests must use fake timers wherever any retry/delay code is touched (per workspace rule on `vi.useFakeTimers()`). Tests must not depend on a real `~/.panopticon/` — the mock is the only source of state.
- AC: Test asserts `resolveSwarmDefaultModel('PAN-1148')` returns `{ model: 'gpt-5.5', source: 'parent' }` when `getAgentStateAsync` is mocked to return `{ role: 'work', model: 'gpt-5.5', ... }`.
- AC: Test asserts the helper returns `source: 'config'` when `getAgentStateAsync` returns null (no parent state).
- AC: Test asserts the helper returns `source: 'config'` when the parent state has `role !== 'work'` or an empty `model` (defensive guard exercised).
- AC: A dispatch-level test (or integration-shape test) asserts the POST /api/swarm response body carries `modelSource: 'override'` when the request had an explicit `model`, `'parent'` when no explicit model but a parent exists, and `'config'` when neither.
- AC: All new tests pass under `npm test` (root vitest run).

title: pan-1192: Migrate all `defaultSwarmModel()` callers to `resolveSwarmDefaultModel` and surface `modelSource` on the dispatch response
description:
Replace the three call sites of `defaultSwarmModel()` in `src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts` with the new helper. (1) Line ~983 in `dispatchSwarmWave`: change `const swarmModel = modelGuard.value || defaultSwarmModel();` to resolve via the helper when `modelGuard.value` is absent, and record the source: `const resolved = modelGuard.value ? { model: modelGuard.value, source: 'override' as const } : await resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueUpper); const swarmModel = resolved.model;`. (2) Line ~362 in `persistSynthesisOutput` and (3) line ~1624 in `onSlotMergeComplete`'s runtime-init branch: replace `model: defaultSwarmModel()` with `model: (await resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueId)).model` (both functions are already `async`). Add `modelSource: resolved.source` to the response body returned by `dispatchSwarmWave` (alongside `model: swarmModel` at line ~1408). Update the response interface/comment if one exists. Update the docstring at the top of `src/cli/commands/swarm.ts` and the commander `--model` description to read 'override model for work slots (default: parent agent\'s model, or config roles.work.model if no parent)'. Grep the workspace afterwards to confirm zero residual `defaultSwarmModel(` call sites (if the helper from bead 1 chose to keep `defaultSwarmModel` as a private wrapper, that single non-call definition is the only allowed survivor).
- AC: Line ~983 in `dispatchSwarmWave` resolves the model via `resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueUpper)` when `modelGuard.value` is absent and records `source` as one of 'override' | 'parent' | 'config'.
- AC: Lines ~362 and ~1624 (runtime-init fallbacks) use `resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueId)` instead of `defaultSwarmModel()`.
- AC: POST /api/swarm response body includes `modelSource: 'override' | 'parent' | 'config'` next to `model`.
- AC: `src/cli/commands/swarm.ts` docstring and commander `--model` description reflect parent-first resolution (no remaining reference to a hardcoded default or to 'config roles.work.model' as the sole default).
- AC: `grep -n 'defaultSwarmModel(' src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts` returns at most one line (the helper's own definition if it was kept as a private wrapper), and zero call sites outside `resolveSwarmDefaultModel`.
- AC: `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` both pass.

title: pan-1192: Add `resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueId)` helper
description:
Inside `src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts`, add a new async helper `resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueId: string): Promise<{ model: string; source: 'parent' | 'config' }>`. It must: (1) call `getAgentStateAsync(issueId)` from `src/lib/agents.js` — `normalizeAgentId` will map e.g. `PAN-1192` to `agent-pan-1192`; (2) when state exists AND `state.role === 'work'` AND `state.model` is a non-empty string, return `{ model: state.model, source: 'parent' }`; (3) otherwise return `{ model: resolveModel('work', undefined, loadYamlConfig().config), source: 'config' }`. The existing `defaultSwarmModel()` function should be deleted in this same bead (no callers left after bead 2) OR kept as a thin sync wrapper used only inside this helper's config branch — pick whichever leaves the diff smaller. Do not throw on missing/corrupted state.json; `getAgentStateAsync` already returns null in that case. No call sites are migrated in this bead — that is bead 2's job. This bead defines the helper's exact shape so bead 2's wiring and bead 3's tests have a stable contract to write against. Foundation flag is set because the return-shape and naming choices here propagate to two dependent beads; if the shape were wrong, both would have to be redone.
- AC: Function `resolveSwarmDefaultModel(issueId)` exists in `src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts` and is exported (or kept module-private — either is fine, since the only caller is the same file).
- AC: When `getAgentStateAsync(issueId)` returns a state with `role === 'work'` and non-empty `model`, the helper returns `{ model: state.model, source: 'parent' }`.
- AC: When `getAgentStateAsync(issueId)` returns null (state.json missing), the helper returns `{ model: <config roles.work.model>, source: 'config' }` without throwing.
- AC: When the returned state has `role !== 'work'` OR `model` is an empty string, the helper returns `{ source: 'config' }` (i.e. parent is ignored).
- AC: `npm run typecheck` passes.

title: pan-1381: Route test/ship/merge-node Restart to the generic agent restart endpoint
description:
Add test/ship/merge handling to the dashboard context-menu restart so these specialist agents restart through the generic, role-agnostic agent restart endpoint. In handleRestartSession (src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx), add a branch for sessionType of 'test', 'ship', or 'merge' that POSTs /api/agents/:id/restart with body { model, graceful: false }, using the node's sessionId as :id. Remove the fall-through to the work-agent path for these session types — that path's start-fresh branch POSTs /api/agents, which only accepts role: 'work', so it spawned a work agent when restarting a stopped test/ship agent. Extend the restartRouting.ts helper introduced by the reviewer-restart bead with the test/ship/merge mapping (and confirm the 'work' default still uses the existing resume path), and add unit-test coverage for the new cases.
- AC: Choosing restart on a test or ship node sends POST /api/agents/<sessionId>/restart with graceful: false in the body.
- AC: The selected model is forwarded as { model }; choosing 'Default' sends no model field and the agent keeps its current model.
- AC: Restarting a test/ship/merge node never issues a POST /api/agents request and never spawns a work agent.
- AC: A 404 from /api/agents/:id/restart (agent state missing) is surfaced as a clear error toast with no silent fall-through.
- AC: The restartRouting.ts helper covers 'test', 'ship', 'merge', and 'work' session types and the new cases are unit-tested.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the frontend npm test suite all pass.

title: pan-1381: Honest restart-submenu labels per specialist session type
description:
Make the restart context-menu label honest per session type in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectTree/SessionNode.tsx. The restartLabel expression is currently `session.type === 'review' ? 'Restart all' : !isLive ? 'Start' : undefined`, so a non-live sub-reviewer's submenu trigger reads 'Start' even though the action restarts the whole review convoy. Add a reviewer case that always reads 'Restart review' regardless of live state, keep 'Restart all' for review, and leave test/ship on the existing `!isLive ? 'Start' : undefined` (which renders as 'Restart' when live) — accurate once the routing beads land. Update any SessionNode/CommandDeck tests that assert the old labels.
- AC: A reviewer node's restart submenu trigger reads 'Restart review' whether or not the node is live.
- AC: A review coordinator node's restart submenu trigger still reads 'Restart all'.
- AC: A test or ship node's restart submenu trigger reads 'Start' when the node has ended and 'Restart' when it is live.
- AC: Any SessionNode/CommandDeck tests asserting the previous labels are updated; npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and frontend npm test pass.
close_reason: Labeled reviewer restarts as Restart review, preserved review Restart all, and covered test/ship live versus ended labels.

title: pan-1381: Route reviewer-node Restart to the review-convoy restart endpoint
description:
Fix the reviewer (sub-reviewer) context-menu Restart so it routes to the full review-convoy restart endpoint instead of the retired per-reviewer route. In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx, the handleRestartSession branch for sessionType === 'reviewer' currently POSTs /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/reviewer/:role/restart, which returns HTTP 410 ('per-reviewer restart route retired'). Route it instead to POST /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/review/restart (the same endpoint the review coordinator node uses), passing the optional { model } override in the body. Extract the per-session-type restart-endpoint decision into a new pure helper module (src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/restartRouting.ts) so it is unit-testable, and route the review and reviewer branches through it. Add a Vitest unit-test file for the helper.
- AC: Choosing restart on a sub-reviewer node sends POST /api/specialists/:project/:issueId/review/restart and never calls the retired /reviewer/:role/restart route.
- AC: The model selected in the restart submenu is sent as { model } in the request body; choosing 'Default' sends a body with no model field.
- AC: On a 2xx response the toast reports the review was restarted; on a non-2xx response the endpoint's error message is surfaced in an error toast.
- AC: A new pure helper module (restartRouting.ts) maps session type 'review' and 'reviewer' (with role) to a restart request descriptor and is covered by unit tests.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and the frontend npm test suite all pass.
close_reason: Routed review and reviewer restarts through the review-convoy endpoint helper with model body handling and unit tests.

title: pan-1382: Accept 'parent' sub-role model in the Settings API validator
description:
In src/lib/settings-api.ts, update validateModelRef() to accept the 'parent' sentinel (imported as PARENT_MODEL_REF from config-yaml.ts) via a new allowParentRef boolean parameter. allowParentRef is passed true only at the roles.<role>.sub.<subRole>.model call site; the workhorses.<slot> and roles.<role>.model call sites keep their existing behavior (allowParentRef false / default), so 'parent' is still rejected there. When allowParentRef is true and the ref equals PARENT_MODEL_REF, the ref is valid and no further model-capability check runs. Add settings-api.test.ts cases.
- AC: validateSettings on a payload with roles.review.sub.security.model='parent' returns no errors.
- AC: validateSettings on a payload with roles.review.model='parent' returns a validation error.
- AC: validateSettings on a payload with workhorses.mid='parent' returns a validation error.
- AC: A sub-role model saved as 'parent' round-trips unchanged through writing and re-reading settings (loadSettingsApi returns roles.<role>.sub.<subRole>.model === 'parent').
close_reason: Allowed parent sub-role model refs in Settings API validation while rejecting role-level and workhorse uses, with round-trip tests.

title: pan-1382: Add a 'Parent' option to the dashboard sub-role model picker
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx, add an optional parentModelRef prop to the inline ModelPicker component. When parentModelRef is supplied (sub-role pickers only — the role-level picker call site omits it), render a selectable 'Parent' option with value 'parent' whose label communicates the inherited model (e.g. 'Parent (inherits <Role> model)'). Selecting it routes through the existing saveRoleConfig path so it persists roles.<role>.sub.<subRole>.model = 'parent'. Special-case the 'parent' value in the picker helpers (isWorkhorseRef / modelExists / currentSpecificModelMissing, displayModelRef, modelRefTooltip, resolveModelRef, providerWarning) so 'parent' is never flagged as a missing specific model, never produces a spurious invalid-provider warning, and the 'Resolved:' line shows the parent role's effective resolved model. Define a local 'parent' literal kept identical to PARENT_MODEL_REF. Add RolesPanel.test.tsx cases.
- AC: A sub-role model picker (e.g. Review > Security) shows a selectable 'Parent' option; the role-level model picker does not show a 'Parent' option.
- AC: Selecting 'Parent' issues a save that sets roles.<role>.sub.<subRole>.model to 'parent'.
- AC: When a sub-role's saved model is 'parent', the picker shows the 'Parent' option selected and the 'Resolved:' line shows the parent role's effective model.
- AC: The 'parent' value does not trigger the 'current specific model missing' optgroup or any invalid-provider warning.
close_reason: Implemented parent option in dashboard sub-role model picker; focused tests, frontend build, and browser verification passed.

title: pan-1382: Add 'parent' sentinel to resolveModel and config-load validation
description:
In src/lib/config-yaml.ts: export a PARENT_MODEL_REF constant set to the literal 'parent'. In resolveModel(), read the raw sub-role model and, when it equals PARENT_MODEL_REF, normalize it to undefined before resolution so it falls through to roleModel ?? DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS[role] (and the fieldPath used for errors points at roles.<role>.model, exactly as for an absent sub-model). In validateRoleModelRefs(), skip the derefWorkhorse() call for any sub-role config whose model equals PARENT_MODEL_REF (so config load via mergeConfigs does not throw). Add a fail-fast guard in derefWorkhorse() that throws a clear error if it is ever called with 'parent' — it must be resolved away by resolveModel and never dereferenced. 'parent' is accepted only as a sub-role model: a role-level roles.<role>.model of 'parent' must still fail config-load validation. Cover all of this with unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/config-yaml-roles.test.ts (and src/lib/__tests__/model-validation.test.ts if a load-path case fits better there).
- AC: resolveModel('review','security',config) with roles.review.sub.security.model='parent' returns the model resolved from roles.review.model (the parent role's model).
- AC: When roles.review.model is itself absent, a sub-role model of 'parent' resolves to DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS.review.
- AC: mergeConfigs / validateRoleModelRefs accepts a config containing a sub-role model of 'parent' without throwing.
- AC: A config with a role-level roles.<role>.model of 'parent' is rejected at load with a clear error; 'parent' is never accepted for a workhorse slot.
- AC: derefWorkhorse('parent', ...) throws an explicit error identifying 'parent' as a resolve-only sub-role sentinel.
- AC: PARENT_MODEL_REF is exported from config-yaml.ts and equals the literal 'parent'.
close_reason: Added the parent sub-role sentinel to config-yaml resolution and validation with focused role-model tests.

title: pan-1235: Wire missing drawer issue-filtered subscription in useDrawerData
description:
Add a useEffect to src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/drawer/useDrawerData.ts that wires the existing drawerIssueSubscription scaffold to wsTransport. The effect depends on the open drawer issueId from useDashboardStore. On mount with an issueId: if there is no existing subscription OR the existing one is for a different issue, stop the existing one (if any) and call getTransport().subscribe((client) => client[WS_METHODS.subscribeIssueEvents]({ issueId }), (event) => useDashboardStore.getState().applyEvent(event as DomainEvent)); store the returned unsubscribe in drawerIssueSubscription = { issueId, refCount: 1, unsubscribe, releaseTimer: null }. If a subscription already exists for this issue and has a pending releaseTimer, cancel the timer via window.clearTimeout and increment refCount. On effect cleanup: decrement refCount; if refCount === 0 schedule drawerIssueSubscription.releaseTimer = window.setTimeout(stopDrawerIssueSubscription, 1000). Both failing tests (IssueDrawer.test.tsx lines 118 and 139) must pass without other code changes.
- AC: Test 'subscribes to issue-filtered drawer events and applies them to the store' passes: wsTransport.subscribe is called exactly once on mount; the connect callback invokes client[WS_METHODS.subscribeIssueEvents]({ issueId: 'PAN-1' }); the listener applied to an activity.updated event with payload events results in useDashboardStore.getState().recentActivity equaling the payload's events array.
- AC: Test 'tears down the drawer issue subscription on close and reuses quick same-issue reopens' passes: first mount, first.unmount(), advance 999ms, unsubscribe NOT called, second mount with same issue, wsTransport.subscribe still called exactly once total, second.unmount(), advance 1000ms, unsubscribe called exactly once.
- AC: All other 16 tests in IssueDrawer.test.tsx continue to pass with no regressions.
- AC: Full frontend vitest run is green: `cd src/dashboard/frontend && npx vitest run` reports 996 of 996 tests passing across 89 of 89 files (matching the post-cc81b36e8 baseline once these 2 fixes land).
- AC: The implementation reuses the existing DrawerIssueSubscription type, stopDrawerIssueSubscription() helper, and resetDrawerIssueSubscriptionForTest() export in useDrawerData.ts. No code is duplicated; the helpers stop being dead code.
- AC: Listener routes events through useDashboardStore.getState().applyEvent(event) — the same path EventRouter uses for the global stream — so all DomainEvent types (not just activity.updated) flow through the shared reducer correctly.
- AC: npm run typecheck and npm run lint pass at the repo root.
close_reason: Wired missing drawer issue-filtered subscription in useDrawerData.ts. Added useEffect with ref-counted lifecycle that subscribes via wsTransport, reuses same-issue subscriptions within 1000ms grace period, and routes events through useDashboardStore.getState().applyEvent. Fixed reuse-branch cleanup bug. All 18 IssueDrawer tests pass, full frontend suite 997/997, typecheck and lint clean.

title: pan-1234: Strengthen styleguide-conformance spec to verify visual contracts, not just data-component presence
description:
Extend tests/e2e/styleguide-conformance.spec.ts so that, in addition to the current data-component count assertions, it asserts: (1) the Pipeline issue row's grid-template-columns matches PRD §4.1 (`14px 78px 14px 1fr 220px 84px 26px`) when at least one row is rendered, via getComputedStyle on the row container, (2) at least one rendered verb-badge has both a `badge-bg-*` class and a `badge-border-*` class on its className (DOM attribute check; do NOT rely on computed colors because the test plugin empties src/index.css), and (3) the drawer title h2's computed font-family begins with 'Space Grotesk'. The font-family assertion requires Tailwind utilities to be applied — since Tailwind utilities are processed through src/index.css's @tailwind directive, the `styleguide-empty-index-css` vite plugin currently strips them. The work agent must either (a) relax the plugin to keep @tailwind directives while stripping custom @layer rules, OR (b) replace the assertion target with a DOM-attribute/class-name check that proves font-display is applied (e.g. assert the h2 has the `font-display` class). Pick (b) by default to keep the plugin's intent intact — assert the class, then leave the computed-style check as an aspirational TODO with a clear comment referencing this issue.
- AC: Spec asserts the Pipeline row's grid-template-columns matches PRD §4.1 exactly.
- AC: Spec asserts at least one verb-badge has both a `badge-bg-*` and a `badge-border-*` class in its className.
- AC: Spec asserts the drawer title h2 has the `font-display` class (or, if the vite plugin is relaxed in this bead, asserts the computed font-family starts with 'Space Grotesk').
- AC: Either the styleguide-empty-index-css vite plugin is preserved (preferred) or relaxed with a justification comment naming PAN-1234.
- AC: Spec uses isolated Playwright contexts (per the planning template's Playwright Isolation rule) — verify each route opens its own context as it does today.
- AC: `npm test` runs the spec to completion in the workspace and all assertions pass.

title: pan-1234: Add g-prefix chord shortcuts for global lens navigation; update sidebar footer chip
description:
In App.tsx (next to the existing keydown handler around line 657), add a two-key chord state machine: when the user presses `g` outside an input, set a `chordPrefix = 'g'` state with a short timeout (e.g. 1.5s) and listen for the next key. `g p` → setActiveTab('pipeline'), `g b` → setActiveTab('kanban'), `g a` → setActiveTab('agents'), `g c` → setActiveTab('command-deck'). Any other key (or timeout) clears the chord state. Update the Sidebar footer chip area (currently shows ⌘K) to also surface a discoverability hint for `g <x>` — minimal, a single chip line in the sidebar footer is fine. Test that pressing `g` then `p` switches to Pipeline; pressing `g` then `q` (unbound) silently clears the chord; pressing `g` inside an INPUT does not trigger chord state.
- AC: `g p` activates the Pipeline lens.
- AC: `g b` activates the Board lens (Tab id `kanban`).
- AC: `g a` activates the Agents lens.
- AC: `g c` activates the Command Deck lens.
- AC: Pressing `g` then an unbound key, or pausing >1.5s, clears the chord state with no side effect.
- AC: Chord handler does NOT trigger inside INPUT/TEXTAREA/contentEditable or when a palette is open.
- AC: Sidebar footer chip area surfaces the chord shortcuts as a discoverability hint (no behavior depends on this — purely informational).
- AC: A Vitest test covers `g p`, an unbound second-key cancel, and the input-focus skip.

title: pan-1234: Add j/k card navigation on Board
description:
In KanbanBoard.tsx, add j/k card navigation analogous to Pipeline. Because Board is column-based, j moves down within the current column and k moves up; if no card remains in the column, j moves to the first card of the next non-empty column to the right (and k to the last card of the prev non-empty column to the left). Apply the same gating rules (no INPUT/TEXTAREA, no open palette). Selected card gets a focus ring via the same token classes used in the Pipeline bead so they look unified. Enter opens the drawer for the selected card.
- AC: On the Board lens, pressing `j` moves selection to the next issue-card within the current column; crosses to the next non-empty column when the current column is exhausted.
- AC: Pressing `k` moves selection to the previous issue-card (or previous non-empty column).
- AC: Selected card has a visible focus indicator using the same token classes as the Pipeline focus ring (visual unification).
- AC: Pressing Enter on a selected card opens the IssueDrawer.
- AC: j/k do NOT fire inside text inputs or when a palette is open.
- AC: A Vitest test (preferably in or beside KanbanBoard.test.tsx) covers j/k progression and Enter-to-open across at least two columns.

title: pan-1234: Add j/k row navigation on Pipeline
description:
In PipelineView.tsx, add a keyboard-row-selection model: a `selectedIssueId` (or row index) state, and a keydown handler on the Pipeline container (or document-level inside an effect gated on `activeTab === 'pipeline'`) that responds to `j` (next row) and `k` (previous row) when the user is not inside an INPUT/TEXTAREA/contentEditable. Selected row gets a visible focus ring using existing token classes (e.g. `ring-2 ring-primary` on `[data-component='issue-row']`). Pressing Enter on the selected row opens that issue's drawer via `openIssue(issueId)`. Wrap-around: after the last row, j wraps to the first; before the first row, k wraps to the last. The handler must not fire when the search palette or command palette is open.
- AC: On the Pipeline lens, pressing `j` (with no modifier) moves selection to the next issue-row.
- AC: Pressing `k` moves selection to the previous issue-row, wrapping around at the boundaries.
- AC: The selected row has a visible focus indicator using existing token classes (not a custom color).
- AC: Pressing Enter on a selected row opens the IssueDrawer for that issue.
- AC: j/k do NOT fire when focus is in INPUT/TEXTAREA/contentEditable or when the search palette / command palette is open.
- AC: A Vitest test exercises j/k progression and Enter-to-open on a fixture of >=3 issue rows.

title: pan-1234: Add /issues/:id direct-link route that opens the drawer over the current (or default) surface
description:
Extend App.tsx routing so that hitting `/issues/PAN-1234` (or any issue id) (a) selects a default surface (Pipeline if no prior tab, otherwise the user's last tab), (b) calls `openIssue(<id>)` from the Zustand store (already wired at line 352), and (c) leaves the URL bar at `/issues/:id` (i.e., do NOT rewrite to ?issue=). Update `getTabFromPath` (around line 89) and the popstate handler (around line 490) so that back/forward across /issues/:id ↔ /pipeline preserves drawer state and the parent surface dim. Preserve full back-compat with `?issue=PAN-1234` (any existing call site or bookmark must still open the drawer on the same surface as today; do NOT remove the search-param branch of syncDrawerFromUrl in lib/store.ts). Add a Vitest test in __tests__/store.test.ts (or a new App.test.tsx) that asserts: navigating to /issues/PAN-1234 (a) sets activeTab to a valid Tab (default 'pipeline'), (b) opens the drawer for PAN-1234, (c) round-trips through popstate without losing the issueId.
- AC: TAB_PATHS / PATH_TO_TAB / getTabFromPath handle `/issues/:id` paths and resolve the active tab to the user's prior tab or 'pipeline' as default.
- AC: Navigating directly to `/issues/PAN-1234` opens the IssueDrawer for PAN-1234.
- AC: The URL stays at `/issues/PAN-1234` after navigation (no automatic rewrite to ?issue= form).
- AC: Back-compat: visiting `/pipeline?issue=PAN-1234` still opens the drawer on Pipeline exactly as today.
- AC: Browser back/forward across /issues/:id ↔ /pipeline restores both the active tab and the drawer state (popstate handler updated).
- AC: A Vitest test in src/dashboard/frontend/src/__tests__/ exercises the /issues/:id → openIssue path.

title: pan-1234: Emit INPUT verb badge for any non-plan agent waiting on AskUserQuestion

In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Pipeline/PipelineView.tsx, modify `verbBadgeForPhase` (line 162) to also accept the agent (or its pending-question flags) and return `<VerbBadge variant="INPUT" />` BEFORE the existing phase-based branches whenever `hasActualPendingQuestion(agent)` returns true. Add a Vitest case in PipelineView.test.tsx (or a new test file if more appropriate) that asserts: a row whose agent has hasPendingQuestion=true and pendingQuestionCount>0 on a WORK role renders the INPUT variant, not WORK RUNNING.

Apply the corresponding text-* color class to each line; remove the wrapper text-muted-foreground class so per-line colors win.

title: pan-1231: AgentCard 'Open issue' actually scrolls IssueDrawer to #active-agent (not just sets URL hash)
description:
Reproduce the audit failure in a real browser via Playwright (isolated instance): start the dashboard, navigate to /?tab=agents, click 'Open issue' on a fleet card with an active agent, and confirm whether the drawer is actually scrolled with the Active Agent section in view at the top of the scrollable region. The wiring code is in place (FleetAgentsView.openAgentIssue line 342-347; IssueDrawer.scrollToActive useEffect line 54-67; DrawerActiveAgent id='active-agent' line 57 and 95) — the most likely root cause is that scrollToActive runs in a useEffect that fires when drawer.issueId changes, but at that moment the DrawerActiveAgent element may not yet be in the DOM (async data load via useDrawerData). Fix: ensure the scroll happens AFTER the active-agent element actually exists — either via a layout effect that watches the element via MutationObserver / ref, or by retrying scroll on a short delay until the element is found (capped retries). Replace the existing FleetAgentsView 'opens the drawer from a fleet card issue action at the active-agent anchor' test with a behavioural test that spies Element.prototype.scrollIntoView and asserts it was called on the live #active-agent element after the click.
- AC: Manual Playwright UAT (isolated browser, no shared session): clicking 'Open issue' on a fleet AgentCard opens the drawer AND the Active Agent section is visible in the drawer's scroll viewport without manual scrolling. Recorded as a passing UAT step.
- AC: A new unit test mounts FleetAgentsView + IssueDrawer + DrawerActiveAgent, spies Element.prototype.scrollIntoView, clicks 'Open issue' on a card, and asserts scrollIntoView was invoked on the element with id='active-agent'. The existing URL-hash-only test is replaced (not just supplemented) — a hash-only assertion is the original closed-without-delivery pattern and must not remain as the sole proof of delivery.
- AC: The fix handles the async DrawerActiveAgent mount: if the element is not in the DOM when the effect first runs, the scroll is retried (e.g., via ref/observer/short polling with a small retry cap) until it succeeds or a clean timeout — never silently no-ops.
- AC: Opening the drawer via other entry points (e.g., kanban card click without #active-agent hash) does NOT auto-scroll to the active-agent anchor — scroll behaviour is gated on the hash being present.
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test pass
close_reason: Fixed IssueDrawer scroll to #active-agent with capped retry loop, replaced hash-only test with behavioural scrollIntoView test, and added Playwright UAT.
