- AC: Vitest assertion: with selectedFeature set, the right pane DOM does NOT contain `data-testid="issue-workbench"` at the top level; the lens is rendered instead.
- AC: Vitest assertion: when selectedConversation transitions from null to some-id, the lens's active tab becomes `'conversations'` and ConversationPanel is rendered inside the tab body.
- AC: Vitest assertion: when an issue is highlighted, the actions row containing the preserved data-testids (e.g. `zone-a-cost`) is mounted above the tab strip.
- AC: Vitest assertion: the tab strip element's `getBoundingClientRect().height` equals 48 (within ±1px tolerance for sub-pixel rounding) in the test render.
- AC: Playwright UAT in an isolated browser context performs the full flow above and asserts the same conditions in a real browser. The Playwright test does NOT share localStorage/cookies with any other agent's session; localStorage keys (e.g. `mc-project-right-tab:*`) are seeded inside the test fixture if needed.
- AC: Full test suite (`npm test` from workspace root and frontend) and `npm run typecheck` + `npm run lint` pass.
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met: lens default assertions, conversation inline rendering, ZoneA action strip, 48px height check. Typecheck+lint+tests pass.

title: pan-1230: Surface per-issue actions (start/stop/fork/plan/open-beads) within the lens shell so removing IssueWorkbench doesn't regress UX
description:
When the lens has a non-null activeIssueId (from b1's controlled prop OR from internal state), mount a compact issue-actions row above the tab strip that reuses the existing ZoneActionStrip component (or its constituent action buttons). Forward the same agent / issue / onOpenBeads / onSwitchTab props that ZoneA was passing to ZoneActionStrip. Preserve existing test ids (e.g. `data-testid="zone-a-cost"`, action-button data-testids) so existing tests and any external automation continue to find their targets. If the existing ZoneActionStrip API doesn't fit the new shell cleanly, extract the action-button JSX into a small `IssueActionsRow` component used by both ZoneA (legacy callers — none after b1) and the lens; do not modify ZoneActionStrip's behavior.
- AC: When an issue is highlighted in the lens (activeIssueId non-null), a compact actions row is visible above or beside the tab strip exposing the same actions previously surfaced by ZoneA's ZoneActionStrip (start, stop, fork, plan, open-beads, view-source, view-url, cost display).
- AC: Every existing data-testid that ZoneA / ZoneActionStrip exposed (including but not limited to `zone-a-cost`) is preserved verbatim in the new actions row OR aliased into the lens with the same id, so existing tests and tools can find them.
- AC: Action handler wiring is unchanged: e.g. clicking 'Open beads' still triggers the BeadsDialog opener, clicking 'Plan' still triggers the PlanDialog opener, etc. No behavior regressions versus the legacy IssueWorkbench surface.
- AC: When no issue is highlighted (activeIssueId null — e.g. user clicked the project header), the actions row is hidden (or shows a project-level placeholder). The lens does not render dead/disabled action buttons.
close_reason: Rendered ZoneA above the tab strip inside ProjectRightPaneTabs when an issue is selected. Mapped ZoneA onSwitchTab to lens tabs. All issue-level actions remain reachable.

title: pan-1230: Render ConversationPanel inside the Conversations tab instead of as the top-level right pane
description:
Inside ProjectRightPaneTabs, modify the `activeTab === 'conversations'` branch (currently at index.tsx ~316–332) so that when a conversation is selected (selectedConversation non-null), the tab renders ConversationPanel inline in place of the conversation list (or above it) — with a 'Back to conversations' affordance that clears selectedConversation. When no conversation is selected, the existing DiscussionsTab + conversation-button list renders as today. Wire the lens so that setting selectedConversation in CommandDeck auto-sets the lens's activeTab to `'conversations'` (an effect at the CommandDeck level or via a tab-controller prop). Forward the same `viewMode`, `onConvIdChange`, `onConversationViewModeChange`, `agentId`, and `onArchived` props that the old top-level ConversationPanel received.
- AC: Selecting a conversation (e.g. clicking a conversation row in the project tree) auto-switches the lens to the Conversations tab.
- AC: When a conversation is selected, the Conversations tab body renders ConversationPanel with the same props (viewMode, onViewModeChange, agentId, onArchived) it received from the legacy top-level branch.
- AC: A 'Back' (or equivalent) control inside the Conversations tab clears selectedConversation and restores the DiscussionsTab + conversation list view inside the tab.
- AC: The top-level `selectedConversation ? ConversationPanel : ...` branch is fully removed from the right-pane render block in CommandDeck/index.tsx; ConversationPanel is imported only by the Conversations-tab code path.
- AC: Archiving a conversation (onArchived) still clears selectedConversation and invalidates the conversations query — behavior identical to today.
close_reason: Rendered ConversationPanel inline inside the Conversations tab. Added auto-switch to conversations tab on selectedConversation change. Added back affordance to return to conversation list. Threaded conversation props through ProjectRightPaneTabs.

title: pan-1230: Right-pane tab strip height is exactly 48px per PRD §4.7.12
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx, update the ProjectRightPaneTabs tab strip element (currently at line 270: `<div className="flex shrink-0 items-center gap-1 border-b border-border bg-card px-3 py-2" role="tablist">`) to enforce a 48px height. Use Tailwind `h-[48px]` and adjust internal padding (replace `py-2` with `py-0` so the explicit height is authoritative; keep `px-3` and `items-center` so tab buttons vertically center). The Pipeline route's TopBar at 52px is unaffected — that primitive stays at h-[52px].
- AC: The right-pane tab strip element (`data-component="command-deck-right-pane-tabs"` → first child role="tablist") has class `h-[48px]` and renders at exactly 48px tall (measured via `getBoundingClientRect().height`).
- AC: Tab button vertical alignment is unchanged visually — buttons remain centered within the strip after the height change.
- AC: Pipeline route TopBar primitive remains at 52px (the Pipeline test PipelineView.test.tsx:120 asserting `h-[52px]` still passes — Pipeline = 52px per PRD §4.7.12 row 1, Command Deck = 48px per row 3, they are different surfaces).
close_reason: Set tab strip height to exactly 48px via h-[48px] px-3 py-0, matching PRD §4.7.12.

title: pan-1230: Make ProjectRightPaneTabs the unconditional right pane when a project is selected
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx, refactor the right-pane render logic at lines ~1310–1362. Remove the top-level `selectedConversation ? ConversationPanel : selectedFeature ? IssueWorkbench :` branches. Render ProjectRightPaneTabs whenever a project is resolvable (either selectedProject is set OR selectedFeature is set and the project can be derived from it). Thread selectedFeature into ProjectRightPaneTabs as a new controlled `activeIssueId` prop; inside ProjectRightPaneTabs, this prop overrides the internal `activeIssueId` state, the existing local state remains as a fallback when the prop is null (e.g. the user clicked the project header). Update the empty state to render only when no project at all is selected (no projects loaded yet).
- AC: When a project is selected in the tree (with or without a feature/conversation also selected), the right pane DOM contains `data-component="command-deck-right-pane-tabs"` as the top-level right-pane container.
- AC: When a feature is selected in the project tree, the top-level right pane does NOT mount `data-testid="issue-workbench"` (the legacy IssueWorkbench shell). The lens remains mounted instead.
- AC: ProjectRightPaneTabs accepts a new controlled `activeIssueId?: string | null` prop. When set, tabs 2–6 (Plans / Beads / Conversations / Activity / Settings) drill into that issue id. When null, the existing first-feature default applies.
- AC: Selecting a feature in the tree sets the lens's `activeTab` to `'pipeline'` (so the user sees the project-filtered Pipeline with that issue) unless the user has explicitly chosen another tab during this session; tab choice still persists per-project to localStorage `mc-project-right-tab:<projectName>`.
- AC: No regressions: existing tab keyboard navigation, tab persistence per-project, and Pipeline-tab ProjectOverview rendering all continue to work as in the current code.
- AC: Typecheck + lint + existing CommandDeck unit tests pass (some tests will be updated in b5; this bead does not break any tests that aren't touched in b5).
close_reason: Made ProjectRightPaneTabs the unconditional right pane when a project is selected. Added controlled activeIssueId prop, auto-switch to pipeline tab on feature selection, preserved project context on conversation selection. Updated tests to assert lens presence.

title: pan-1229: Rewrite Board Issue Card body to match PRD §4.7.6 (project mark + ID + verb badge / 2-line title / labels / Beads N/M / two-line agent foot)
description:
Replace the contents of the inner `
…
` block in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx (currently around lines 2725-2800) with the PRD §4.7.6 structure. Use the cross-section design tokens from docs/design/mockups/board-opus.html as the authoritative reference for sizes, fonts, and gaps. Keep the IssueCard primitive at primitives/IssueCard.tsx as the surrounding chrome (border, accent bar, gradient) — only the inner block changes. Update KanbanBoard.test.tsx in the same diff. - AC: Outer padding of the inner block is `12px 12px 10px` (replacing `p-4`). - AC: Row 1 contains in order: 8×8 project mark (radius 2px, coloured from `issue.project.color`) · SF Mono 10px muted issue identifier (e.g. `PAN-1229`) · spacer · existing VerbBadge (right-aligned). - AC: Title row: 13px foreground, line-height 1.35, `display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; -webkit-box-orient: vertical; overflow: hidden;` — the previous redundant inline ` ` group is removed. - AC: Labels row renders when `issue.labels` is non-empty: 4px gap, wrap, chip styling matches PRD §4.7.5 Issue Row labels (10px weight 500, padding `1px 6px`, radius `var(--radius-sm)`, `background: rgb(255 255 255 / 5%)`, `border: 1px solid var(--border)`, color muted). - AC: Bead progress row appears only when `issue.beadCounts` is non-null. It shows the label `Beads N/M` (10px uppercase muted, letter-spaced 0.16em) on the left and a 3px-tall track (`background: var(--accent)`, `border-radius: 2px`, `overflow: hidden`) on the right; the fill width is `(completed / total) * 100%` and the fill colour is `var(--info)` for in-progress, `var(--warning)` for in-review, `var(--signal-review)` for ship/plan, and `var(--success)` for done/merge-ready cards. - AC: Foot: 8px padding-top, `border-top: 1px solid var(--border)`. Left cell is a two-line agent stack — `card-agent-name` (SF Mono 10px foreground, ellipsis) over `card-agent-sub` (SF Mono 9px muted, ellipsis). Right side shows runtime in SF Mono 10px muted tabular-nums, then an 18×18 circle avatar with initials in a gradient fill matching the mockup palette. - AC: Empty-state agent foot: when no active agent, name renders italic DM Sans muted (`no agent`) and sub renders the tracker reference (`GitHub #1129` / `Linear MIN-816`) per the mockup; runtime cell shows `—`. - AC: Active-agent sub line content matches the mockup: work agents show `<model> · bead N/M` (using friendly model name + beadCounts when present), review convoys show `<N reviewers> · <model>`; when no specific sub data is available, fall back to model-only. - AC: REMOVED from the card body: the inline bulk-select checkbox in row 1, the redundant phase label text (`Backlog`/`Ready to start`/`Agent active`/etc), the model pill, the `N specialists` pill, the `list` (beads) icon button, and the `scrolltext` (vBRIEF) icon button. - AC: Bulk-select RELOCATED to a hover-revealed top-left corner overlay: 16×16 checkbox positioned absolutely, hidden by default, revealed on card hover OR when `isBulkSelected` is true. Same `data-testid` and bulk-toggle behaviour as before so existing bulk-select tests pass with at most label/selector tweaks. - AC: Cost button RELOCATED to a compact top-right corner overlay (absolute positioned, low z-index) — same DollarSign icon + format, same onClick → CostBreakdownModal, same `data-testid={"card-cost-${identifier}"}`. Renders only when `cost && cost.totalCost > 0`. - AC: Hover overlays (bulk-select, cost) are suppressed when the card is rendered inside DragOverlayCard so the floating ghost shows the resting card state. - AC: KanbanBoard.test.tsx is updated: existing `it('calls onViewBeads when Tasks button is clicked')` and the equivalent vBRIEF test are removed (icon buttons no longer exist on the card). Bulk-select test is updated to target the overlay checkbox if its selector changes. NEW tests cover: `Beads 7/12` renders for `beadCounts: { completed: 7, total: 12 }`; the bead row is absent when beadCounts is null; the foot row contains agent name + sub + runtime + avatar in the spec'd structure; cost overlay renders when totalCost > 0. - AC: Visual UAT: open the dashboard at `/board`, confirm cards in each column render with the new layout, the bead-progress bar shows real N/M for issues that have plans, no bar appears for pre-plan issues, hover reveals bulk-select + cost overlays, and the drag ghost matches the resting state. - AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` all pass.
close_reason: Rewrote IssueCard body to match PRD §4.7.6: project mark + ID + verb badge row, 2-line clamped title, labels chip row, conditional Beads N/M progress bar, two-line agent foot with runtime and avatar. Relocated bulk-select and cost to hover overlays. Added beadCounts to frontend Issue type. Updated tests (+5).

title: pan-1229: File a follow-up GitHub issue for the '+ New issue' column-footer button (N-B-2 from the audit)
description:
Run `gh issue create` against the `eltmon/panopticon-cli` repo to file a follow-up issue tracking N-B-2 ('+ New issue' column footer button). The body should reference PRD §4.7.6, the `board-opus.html` mockup `.add-btn` rule, the parent audit PAN-1226 and this issue PAN-1229, and note that the feature needs new tracker-write plumbing (no existing POST /api/issues, no GitHub Issues create / Linear createIssue paths in the dashboard). Record the new issue ID in `.pan/continue.json` decisions and add a single-line `// TODO(<new-issue-id>)` comment near the column body in KanbanBoard.tsx so future readers find the trail.
- AC: A new GitHub issue is created in `eltmon/panopticon-cli` titled `Board view follow-up — + New issue column footer button (deferred from PAN-1229)`.
- AC: The issue body references PRD §4.7.6 / `docs/design/mockups/board-opus.html` `.add-btn`, parent audit PAN-1226, deferral context from PAN-1229, and the missing backend plumbing (POST /api/issues + tracker createIssue flows).
- AC: A single-line `// TODO(PAN-<new-id>): + New issue column footer button — see PRD §4.7.6` comment is added at the location in KanbanBoard.tsx where the column body ends (or wherever the footer button would render in the new mockup).
- AC: The new follow-up issue ID is appended to `.pan/continue.json` decisions so subsequent agents can find the trail.

title: pan-1229: Cross-column drag (N-B-1): show a visual block on the destination column instead of silently snapping back
description:
Thread the active drag's source canonical status (already tracked at the board level as `activeDragStatus`) down into DroppableColumn so it can render an incompatible-drop affordance when hovered. handleDragEnd remains a no-op for cross-column drops. PRD non-goal stands: dragging never changes phase.
- AC: DroppableColumn accepts an `activeDragStatus: CanonicalState | null` prop (or reads it from a context provided by KanbanBoard) so it knows the source column of the active drag.
- AC: When `isOver && activeDragStatus && activeDragStatus !== status`, the column applies `cursor-not-allowed`, an opacity dim (~`opacity-60`), and DOES NOT apply the `scale-[1.02]` lift. When `isOver && activeDragStatus === status` (same column, reordering), the existing `scale-[1.02]` lift still applies.
- AC: DraggableCardWrapper updates the cursor to `cursor-not-allowed` while the active drag is over an incompatible column (best-effort — acceptable to leave the dragged card's cursor unchanged and rely on the column-level affordance if context plumbing is awkward).
- AC: handleDragEnd remains a no-op for cross-column drops: no toast, no API call, no error — the visual block during drag is the entire affordance.
- AC: Vitest: simulate `handleDragStart` from a `todo` card and assert that hovering an `in_progress` DroppableColumn renders the blocked style (opacity-60, no scale-[1.02], cursor-not-allowed). Drop is asserted to be a no-op.
- AC: Visual UAT: grab a card in `Todo` and drag it over `In Progress` — confirm the destination column dims and the cursor turns into not-allowed; releasing the drag leaves both columns unchanged.

title: pan-1229: Flip merge-ready card styling from warning tokens to success tokens in the IssueCard primitive
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/primitives/IssueCard.tsx, change the three styling layers that fire when `mergeReadyCard` is true from warning tokens to success tokens. This inverts the current incorrect amber semantic to spec-correct green per PRD §4.7.6 ('Merge-ready card | Border becomes color-mix(in srgb, var(--success) 32%, transparent)').
- AC: The mergeReadyCard branch of the outer className changes `'border-warning/60 bg-warning/10 shadow-md'` to `'border-success/60 bg-success/10 shadow-md'` (or the closest equivalent that matches `color-mix(success / 32%)`).
- AC: The `tone` computed value for mergeReadyCard changes from `'from-warning/20 via-warning/6 to-transparent'` to `'from-success/20 via-success/6 to-transparent'`.
- AC: The `accent` computed value for mergeReadyCard changes from `'bg-warning'` to `'bg-success'`.
- AC: Vitest in IssueCard.test.tsx (or KanbanBoard.test.tsx as appropriate): a card rendered with `mergeReadyCard={true}` exposes the success classes (border-success, bg-success, from-success) — no warning classes remain on the merge-ready path. Snapshot or class-assertion form, whichever fits the existing test style.
- AC: Visual UAT: open the dashboard, locate a merge-ready card (Awaiting Merge column or any card with READY TO MERGE verb), confirm the border + tint + accent are green (success token), not amber.
close_reason: Flipped merge-ready card styling from warning tokens to success tokens in IssueCard primitive: border-success/60 bg-success/10, from-success/20 gradient tone, bg-success accent bar. Added test.

title: pan-1229: Expose `beadCounts: { completed, total } | null` on each issue payload from the cached plan doc
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/issue-data-service.ts, extend PlanningStateCacheEntry.result with an optional `beadCounts: { completed: number; total: number } | null`. In refreshPlanningState(), after loading the plan doc, compute beadCounts: when `doc.plan.items` has 1+ entries, set { completed: items.filter(i => i.status === 'completed').length, total: items.length }; otherwise null. Thread the new field through getCachedPlanningState (return type) and through the issue mapper at issue-data-service.ts:411 so every issue object carries it. Add the optional field to the frontend Issue interface in src/dashboard/frontend/src/types.ts.
- AC: PlanningStateCacheEntry.result includes `beadCounts: { completed: number; total: number } | null` and is populated on every refresh.
- AC: refreshPlanningState returns beadCounts when `doc.plan.items.length > 0`, with `completed` counted from items.status === 'completed'; returns null for missing plan or empty items.
- AC: The issue mapper at issue-data-service.ts ~L411 spreads `beadCounts` onto every issue returned by IssueDataService.getIssues.
- AC: Frontend `Issue` type in src/dashboard/frontend/src/types.ts gains `beadCounts?: { completed: number; total: number } | null`; existing consumers (no required change) keep compiling.
- AC: Vitest in src/dashboard/server (issue-data-service.test.ts or a new co-located test) covers three cases: plan with 7 completed of 12 → beadCounts { completed: 7, total: 12 }; plan with 0 of 5 → { completed: 0, total: 5 }; no plan or empty items → null.
- AC: `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass for both packages (server + frontend) after the change.
close_reason: Extended PlanningStateCacheEntry.result with beadCounts, computed from doc.plan.items in refreshPlanningState, threaded through getCachedPlanningState and issue mapper. Added computeBeadCounts pure function and 5 server tests.

title: pan-1228: Pipeline drawer close restores scroll position to the row that was opened
description:
PipelineView's outer scroll container is the <div className="flex-1 overflow-auto"> wrapping the phase sections. Hold a ref to that element. When openIssue fires from a row click, capture container.scrollTop into a ref or local state. Watch drawer.issueId via useDashboardStore — when it transitions from a non-null value back to null, restore the captured scrollTop on the next animation frame. PRD §4.6 explicitly requires 'Closing returns to the exact scroll position'.
- AC: After scrolling the Pipeline list, clicking an off-screen row to open the drawer, then closing the drawer (Escape, backdrop click, or close button), the Pipeline scroll container is restored to its pre-open scrollTop.
- AC: Restoring scroll does not visibly flicker — the restored scrollTop is applied on the same frame the drawer-close animation begins (rAF-aligned).
- AC: Opening a second issue from inside the drawer (sequential replacement, not a stack) does not lose the original scroll position — closing the second drawer still returns to the first row's offset.
- AC: Scroll-restore behaviour is implemented inside PipelineView (no new global store fields, no Zustand drawer-scroll state).
close_reason: Pipeline drawer close now saves scrollTop before openIssue and restores it via requestAnimationFrame when drawer closes (drawerIssueId returns to null). ref attached to scroll container div, savedScrollTop captures position before each open.

title: pan-1228: Pipeline empty-state card when no issues match the active filter
description:
In PipelineView.tsx, after computing groupedIssues and before rendering visiblePhases, if every visible phase has zero matching issues render a single dashed-border card matching the FleetAgentsView.tsx:394 pattern with copy 'No issues match the selected filters.'. The empty card replaces the per-phase sections but the TopBar, MetricStrip, and filter row remain visible above it.
- AC: When every visible phase has zero issues after filtering, the Pipeline renders exactly one empty-state card with the prescribed copy and visual contract (dashed border, card background, muted-foreground text).
- AC: TopBar, MetricStrip, and the filter row are still rendered when the empty state appears (only the per-phase content area is replaced).
- AC: Clearing the filter from the empty state by clicking 'All phases' or the relevant sidebar/topbar control restores the row list without a hard reload.
close_reason: Added empty-state card in PipelineView when every visible phase has zero issues after filtering. Matches FleetAgentsView dashed-border pattern with copy 'No issues match the selected filters.' TopBar/MetricStrip/filter row remain visible. 1 new test covers empty state rendering.

title: pan-1228: Create PipelineSkeleton component and wire it through BootstrapGate in App.tsx
description:
Add src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/skeletons/PipelineSkeleton.tsx mirroring the shimmer pattern of KanbanSkeleton.tsx and AgentsSkeleton.tsx: TopBar bar (52px), MetricStrip row of 5 placeholder tiles, filter-row pills, and 3-4 phase-header sections each with 2-3 row placeholders shaped like the IssueRow grid. In App.tsx around line 865, wrap <PipelineView /> in <BootstrapGate fallback={<PipelineSkeleton />}> identical to the kanban/agents pattern. Add the same vitest mock the Kanban/Agents skeletons have in App.test.tsx.
- AC: src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/skeletons/PipelineSkeleton.tsx exists and exports a `PipelineSkeleton` function component with shimmer placeholders for the TopBar, 5-tile MetricStrip, filter row, and at least 3 phase sections.
- AC: App.tsx wraps the Pipeline view in <BootstrapGate fallback={<PipelineSkeleton />}> when activeTab==='pipeline'.
- AC: App.test.tsx vi.mock entry for PipelineSkeleton is added alongside the existing Kanban/Agents skeleton mocks so the test suite stays clean.
close_reason: Created PipelineSkeleton.tsx with shimmer for TopBar, 5-tile MetricStrip, filter row, and 4 phase sections. Wired through BootstrapGate in App.tsx. Added vi.mock to App.test.tsx.

title: pan-1228: Flip Pipeline 'Work running' metric tile signal from warning to info
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Pipeline/PipelineView.tsx around line 285, change `signal: 'warning' as const` on the 'Work running' tile to `signal: 'info' as const`. PRD §4.7.3 prescribes blue (info) for the machine signal — amber (warning) is reserved for human-attention metrics.
- AC: The Pipeline 'Work running' metric tile icon is rendered in the info-foreground color (blue) instead of warning-foreground (amber).
- AC: Existing PipelineView tests covering the metric strip still pass; if they snapshot signal tokens, update them in the same diff.
close_reason: Changed signal from 'warning' to 'info' in PipelineView.tsx metricTiles array and updated the test assertion to match.

title: pan-1228: Render Sidebar 'Filter phase' and 'Projects' subgroups when on Pipeline, wired to URL filter state
description:
Extend src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx so that, when activeTab==='pipeline', two extra nav groups render below 'Operations': (1) 'Filter phase' with items for 'All phases', Todo, Plan, Work, Review, Ship — each with a phase dot whose color matches the PRD §4.7.4 phase tokens; (2) 'Projects' with one item per project resolved from the same projects.yaml signal tokens already used by IssueRow. Clicks update window.location.search via history.replaceState so PipelineView's existing popstate listener picks them up. Counts come from the same selectIssues/agents the Pipeline already consumes (read from the store inside the sidebar, gated on activeTab to avoid recomputing for other surfaces).
- AC: When activeTab==='pipeline', the Sidebar renders a 'Filter phase' nav group with All / Todo / Plan / Work / Review / Ship items, phase-color dots, and per-phase counts derived from the same store selectors PipelineView uses.
- AC: When activeTab==='pipeline', the Sidebar renders a 'Projects' nav group with one item per resolved project, each labelled with its project mark and the project's issue-prefix tag (e.g. PAN, MIN) on the right.
- AC: Clicking a phase item updates the URL `?phase=<phase>` (or removes it for 'All') and PipelineView's existing popstate handler re-applies the filter; clicking a project item toggles the project in `?projects=` and PipelineView reflects the new selection without a full reload.
- AC: When activeTab is anything other than 'pipeline', neither the 'Filter phase' nor 'Projects' subgroup renders — the Board, Agents, and Settings tabs see the existing sidebar untouched.
- AC: The active phase/project items in the sidebar reflect the current filter state on first render (deep-linked URLs render with the right highlights).
close_reason: Added pipeline-gated Filter phase and Projects subgroups to Sidebar. Phase items show colored dots and per-phase counts from store. Project items toggle URL ?projects= param. Clicks dispatch popstate for PipelineView reactivity. 6 new tests cover rendering, non-pipeline tab hiding, URL updates.

title: pan-1228: Add Pipeline TopBar 'Start agent' primary button
description:
Render a primary button labelled 'Start agent' (with a play-glyph icon to match the mockup) into the TopBar `actions` slot, styled per PRD §4.7.2 primary button spec. Click handler calls the same setIsSearchOpen(true) used by the search trigger — the search modal is the existing path to pick an issue and start its agent.
- AC: Pipeline TopBar renders a 'Start agent' primary button with the prescribed visual contract (32px height, primary background, var(--radius-lg)).
- AC: Clicking the button opens the global SearchModal so the user can pick an issue to start.
close_reason: Added 32px 'Start agent' primary button to TopBar actions slot. Click calls onSearchOpen (same as ⌘K) to open SearchModal for issue selection.

title: pan-1228: Add Pipeline TopBar List/Board/Analytics segmented control
description:
Pass an `onTabChange` callback from App.tsx into PipelineView. Render a segmented control with three buttons (List | Board | Analytics) into the TopBar `segmentedControl` slot, styled per PRD §4.7.2 (32px height, var(--radius-lg), 12px weight 500, active = var(--accent)). 'List' is the active state on the Pipeline surface. 'Board' switches activeTab to 'kanban'. 'Analytics' switches activeTab to 'metrics'.
- AC: Pipeline TopBar renders a three-button segmented control labelled List / Board / Analytics with the active style applied to 'List'.
- AC: Clicking 'Board' navigates to the kanban tab; clicking 'Analytics' navigates to the metrics tab; clicking 'List' is a no-op when already active.
- AC: Segmented control matches the PRD §4.7.2 spec for height, radius, and active styling (no new design tokens introduced).
close_reason: Added segmented control to TopBar segmentedControl slot (List/Board/Analytics). List is active-styled. Board calls onTabChange('kanban'), Analytics calls onTabChange('metrics'). App.tsx passes setActiveTab.

title: pan-1228: Wire Pipeline TopBar search slot to the global SearchModal
description:
Thread an `onSearchOpen` callback from App.tsx into PipelineView (alongside the existing onSearchOpen consumed by Sidebar). title: pan-1228: Populate Pipeline IssueRow ledger (runtime + cost) from cost stream + agent.startedAt
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Pipeline/PipelineView.tsx, build a `ledger` object per IssueRow: cost = sum of `eventsByIssue[issue.identifier]` events formatted via the existing `formatCost`; runtime = `formatDuration(now - new Date(agent.startedAt))` when an agent is mapped to the issue, else undefined. Add a 30-second `now` tick to PipelineView mirroring FleetAgentsView's pattern. Pass the `ledger` prop into <IssueRow>. Do not change IssueRow itself — the prop already exists per PAN-1148.

- AC: Rows whose issue has cost events in the byIssue stream render a non-empty cost value formatted with the existing formatCost helper (e.g. '$0.42', '$12.3').

title: pan-1221: Fix DrawerActiveAgent stream excerpt panel showing 'No recent stream output' for active agents
description:
Reproduce the regression first: start the dashboard, open IssueDrawer for an issue whose work/plan/review agent is actively emitting output (the issue body cites PAN-1052), and confirm DrawerActiveAgent.tsx renders 'No recent stream output' despite the agent being live. Walk back the data flow until the broken link is identified: (1) does selectAgentOutput(activeAgent.id) return data? (2) is `agentOutputById[activeAgent.id]` populated in the store? (3) is the WS subscription to agent.output_received delivering events for that agent (see src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts:524 and packages/contracts/src/event-reducers.ts:431)? (4) is the agent key the server uses ('agent-pan-1052' style) the same key the component looks up? Fix at the broken link only — no defensive shims downstream. Document the root cause in the PR body. Add a Playwright e2e test in src/dashboard/frontend/tests/ that stubs agentOutputById and asserts the stream-excerpt panel renders the seeded lines (not the empty-state fallback).
- AC: Repro confirmed: PR body documents the exact reproduction steps and shows the diagnosed root cause (which step in server-emit → WS-subscribe → store-reduce → selector-read was dropping the data, and why)
- AC: Fix is applied at the broken link itself, not as a defensive workaround downstream. PR body explicitly addresses the CLAUDE.md 'No Bandaids' rule and explains why the chosen fix is at the root cause.
- AC: After the fix, opening the drawer for an active agent that is producing output renders the agent's last N output lines in the stream-excerpt panel (data-testid='drawer-active-agent-stream') instead of the 'No recent stream output' empty-state
- AC: New Playwright e2e test in src/dashboard/frontend/tests/ uses an isolated browser instance, seeds useDashboardStore with at least one active agent and at least 8 lines in agentOutputById for that agent's id, opens the drawer, and asserts data-testid='drawer-active-agent-stream' contains the seeded lines (the last 8) and does NOT contain the 'No recent stream output' empty-state text
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass; the new Playwright spec passes against the local dashboard build

title: pan-1221: Guard FleetAgentsView avg-runtime reduce against NaN startedAt
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Agents/FleetAgentsView.tsx:290, replace the unguarded reduce over runningAgents with a version that computes `new Date(agent.startedAt).getTime()` once per agent, skips entries where the result is not Number.isFinite (covering undefined/null/empty/malformed startedAt and the NaN return from invalid Date), accumulates only finite durations, and divides by the count of finite durations (not runningAgents.length). When zero agents have finite durations, return 0 to match the existing empty-array branch. Update FleetAgentsView.test.tsx with three new cases.
- AC: Reduce in FleetAgentsView.tsx:290 skips any running agent whose `new Date(agent.startedAt).getTime()` is not Number.isFinite
- AC: avgRuntime is the sum of finite durations divided by the count of finite-duration agents; returns 0 (not NaN, not Infinity) when no running agent has a finite startedAt
- AC: FleetAgentsView.test.tsx adds three regression cases: (a) all valid startedAt (avg matches), (b) mixed valid+invalid (avg uses only valid entries and is finite), (c) all invalid startedAt (avgRuntime tile renders the formatted 0)
- AC: npm run typecheck, npm run lint, npm test all pass from the workspace root
close_reason: Guarded FleetAgentsView avg-runtime reduce against NaN startedAt: filter to Number.isFinite timestamps, divide by finite count, return 0 when none valid. Added 3 regression tests. typecheck, lint, and full test suite pass.

title: pan-1215: Integration test: full post-review-rebase scenario end-to-end
description:
Add an integration test that simulates the PAN-1215 scenario end-to-end and proves the four code fixes compose correctly. Setup: a fixture workspace with a vBRIEF spec on a synthetic 'main' and an issue in reviewStatus='passed' with reviewedAtCommit pinned to the workspace HEAD. Act: (1) commit a tree-changing rebase to the workspace branch (simulates 'main moved + rebased'), (2) invoke checkPostReviewCommits() against a stubbed spawnReviewRoleForIssue, (3) assert spawn was called once with the right args, (4) simulate the review specialist returning passed via the done.ts CLI path, (5) assert the resulting status has verificationStatus='passed' (Gap C) and verificationSatisfied returns true, (6) assert .pan/continue.json was never tracked in any commit on the branch after the cleanup ran (Gap B.1). The test does NOT need to drive a real tmux convoy; it uses fakes for spawnReviewRoleForIssue and the bd CLI as the existing test suite already does. Place under tests/integration/post-review-rebase-scenario.test.ts or the closest existing integration directory.
- AC: An integration test file exists under tests/ (location aligned with existing integration tests) that drives the full PAN-1215 scenario
- AC: The test asserts that after a tree-changing rebase, checkPostReviewCommits invokes spawnReviewRoleForIssue exactly once with the correct issueId, workspace path, branch name, and force:true
- AC: The test asserts that after `done review <id> --status passed` runs against a status with verificationStatus='failed', the resulting status has verificationStatus='passed', verificationNotes mentions PAN-1215, and verificationSatisfied(status) returns true
- AC: The test asserts that after the cleanup step runs in a fixture where .pan/continue.json was tracked, no subsequent checkpoint commit (via captureCheckpoint) includes the file in its tree
- AC: The test runs cleanly as part of `npm test` and does not depend on a running dashboard server, tmux server, or external network
- AC: The test file header documents PAN-1215 by ID and explains which three substrate gaps it covers, so a future maintainer can identify it as scenario coverage
close_reason: Created integration test tests/integration/post-review-rebase-scenario.test.ts covering full PAN-1215 scenario: redispatch after rebase, verificationStatus override clearing, and checkpoint exclusion of .pan/ artifacts. All 4 tests pass.

title: pan-1215: Work-agent startup untracks .pan/continue.json on branches where it leaked (Gap B.1 cleanup)
description:
Add a one-shot cleanup step that runs early in the work-agent spawn flow (best site: in src/lib/agents.ts spawnRun for role==='work', or in src/lib/cloister/work-agent-prompt.ts startup, whichever runs before the first checkpoint and after workspace existence is confirmed). The cleanup checks `git ls-files --error-unmatch .pan/continue.json .pan/spec.vbrief.json` to detect tracked copies; if either is tracked AND `git status --porcelain -- .pan/` shows the workspace clean for those paths, it runs `git rm --cached .pan/continue.json .pan/spec.vbrief.json` (separately for each, ignoring missing-file errors) and commits with subject `chore: untrack workspace .pan/ artifacts (PAN-1215)`. If the workspace is dirty for those paths, log a clear warning and skip — the next clean spawn will pick it up. Cleanup runs at most once per spawn and short-circuits when nothing is tracked.
- AC: Work-agent spawn flow includes a cleanup step that runs once per spawn before the first checkpoint, after workspace existence and beads readiness are confirmed
- AC: Cleanup uses `git ls-files --error-unmatch` to detect tracked copies of .pan/continue.json and .pan/spec.vbrief.json; if neither is tracked, the step is a no-op
- AC: Cleanup runs `git rm --cached` only when `git status --porcelain -- .pan/` shows the workspace clean for those paths; if dirty, it logs a warning and skips without modifying the index
- AC: The cleanup commit subject is exactly `chore: untrack workspace .pan/ artifacts (PAN-1215)` so it is identifiable in git log on every affected branch
- AC: Cleanup uses execAsync/execFileAsync (NOT execSync) per the no-execsync-server rule
- AC: A unit test where a workspace has both files tracked and a clean working tree verifies that the cleanup produces one commit with the canonical subject and that the next `git ls-files` no longer matches either path
- AC: A unit test where the workspace is dirty for .pan/* paths verifies the cleanup logs a warning, does NOT commit, and does NOT modify the index
- AC: A unit test where neither file is tracked verifies the cleanup is a no-op (no commit, no warning) and short-circuits cheaply

title: pan-1215: `pan checkpoint` does not capture workspace-only .pan/ artifacts (Gap B.1 root)
description:
Modify src/lib/checkpoint/checkpoint-manager.ts:captureCheckpoint (lines ~88-141). After the HEAD-seed `git read-tree HEAD` and BEFORE `git add -A`, run `git update-index --force-remove .pan/continue.json` and the same for .pan/spec.vbrief.json against the temp index (best-effort; ignore errors when the file isn't in the index). Then `git add -A` will not re-stage them because they're gitignored and were never tracked in the temp index after the force-remove. This stops new checkpoint commits from carrying these files, AND naturally untracks them over time on branches where they were previously tracked: the next checkpoint commit will write a tree without them, so subsequent rebases see them as deletions. Add an inline comment explaining the workspace-only contract per CLAUDE.md's four-artifact model.
- AC: captureCheckpoint in src/lib/checkpoint/checkpoint-manager.ts force-removes .pan/continue.json and .pan/spec.vbrief.json from its temp index after the HEAD seed and before `git add -A`
- AC: The force-remove is best-effort — when the file is not present in the temp index (clean workspace, first checkpoint, etc.), captureCheckpoint does not throw or log an error
- AC: A unit test creates a workspace where .pan/continue.json is tracked in HEAD, runs captureCheckpoint, and verifies the resulting checkpoint commit's tree does NOT contain .pan/continue.json (and ditto .pan/spec.vbrief.json)
- AC: A unit test where .pan/continue.json exists on disk but is NOT in HEAD verifies captureCheckpoint still excludes it (gitignore + force-remove combine to guarantee exclusion)
- AC: An inline comment in captureCheckpoint cites CLAUDE.md's four-artifact model and explains why .pan/continue.json and .pan/spec.vbrief.json are workspace-only, so a future maintainer does not naively re-include them

title: pan-1215: `pan specialists done review --status passed` clears stale verificationStatus (Gap C)
description:
In src/cli/commands/specialists/done.ts inside the review case (lines 62-101), when options.status === 'passed', augment the update object with verificationStatus: 'passed' and verificationNotes: `Cleared by \`pan specialists done review --status passed\` override (PAN-1215)`. This unblocks verificationSatisfied(s) (src/lib/review-status.ts:115-122) and lets fixStuckReadyForMerge / clearStuckMergeStatuses restore readyForMerge for the override case. The semantic justification: the operator running the override has explicitly assumed the gate's responsibility.
- AC: In src/cli/commands/specialists/done.ts, the review case with options.status === 'passed' sets update.verificationStatus = 'passed'
- AC: The same code path sets update.verificationNotes to a string that explicitly identifies the override (mentions PAN-1215 and the override CLI) so the history audit trail distinguishes 'gate ran and passed' from 'human waived gate'
- AC: Override-clearing only happens for options.status === 'passed' — when --status blocked or --status failed is used, verificationStatus is NOT modified
- AC: A unit test in tests/ for the done command verifies that after `done review <id> --status passed` runs against a starting status with verificationStatus='failed', the resulting ReviewStatus has verificationStatus='passed' AND verificationSatisfied(status) returns true
- AC: The non-review specialist branches (test, merge, inspect, uat, ship) are unchanged — they continue to mutate only their own status fields

title: pan-1215: Deacon redispatches review convoy after post-review reset (Gap A)
description:
Modify src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:checkPostReviewCommits() so that immediately after the setReviewStatus reset block (lines 2187-2201) — and AFTER the existing tree-identical short-circuit on line 2176 — it dispatches a fresh review by calling spawnReviewRoleForIssue() from src/lib/cloister/review-agent.js, matching the pattern the /api/review/:id/request HTTP route in src/dashboard/server/routes/workspaces.ts uses. Guard the dispatch with an idempotency re-read: call getReviewStatus(issueId) immediately before spawn and only dispatch if reviewStatus is still 'pending' (no other path has already picked it up). Log dispatch outcomes through the same actions[] channel the existing patrol uses so operators can correlate the redispatch with the reset entry. Pass force:true to spawnReviewRoleForIssue so a stale tmux review-agent session for the previous cycle is reused/reset cleanly.
- AC: checkPostReviewCommits() in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts dispatches a fresh review convoy via spawnReviewRoleForIssue immediately after the setReviewStatus reset block, only on the real-drift branch (after the PAN-1213 tree-identical short-circuit)
- AC: The dispatch site re-reads getReviewStatus(issueId) and skips if reviewStatus is no longer 'pending', preventing duplicate convoys when another path (HTTP request-review, manual CLI) already picked up the work
- AC: Tree-identical rebases (the PAN-1213 path that hits `continue` on line ~2176) do NOT trigger redispatch — verified by a unit test that mocks identical tree SHAs and asserts spawnReviewRoleForIssue is not called
- AC: When dispatch is invoked, the actions[] string returned by checkPostReviewCommits() includes both the 'Reset review' line and a new line like 'Re-dispatched review for <issueId>' so the operator log shows both phases
- AC: spawnReviewRoleForIssue is called with force:true so any stale review-agent tmux session from the previous cycle is reset cleanly
- AC: A unit test in tests/ exercises checkPostReviewCommits with a fixture where old HEAD and new HEAD have different trees AND different SHAs, asserting (a) reviewStatus is reset to pending, (b) spawnReviewRoleForIssue is invoked exactly once with the correct issueId/workspace/branch arguments

title: pan-1194: Add `pan swarm recover <issueId> <slotId>` CLI subcommand wrapping the recovery endpoint
description:
Extend src/cli/commands/swarm.ts to register a `recover` subcommand: `pan swarm recover <issueId> <slotId> --action <retry|drop|handoff> [--yes]`. POSTs to the dashboard's `/api/swarm/:issueId/slot/:slotId/recover` endpoint with the internal token (same pattern as the existing `pan swarm` POST at line 167). Validate args before sending. For `--action drop`, require `--yes` OR interactive confirmation prompt (the action is destructive — drops vBRIEF task to `done` without delivering it). Surface the server's response via ora spinner: success → green checkmark + message; failure → red X + server error text. Exit code 0 on success, 1 on any failure.
- AC: `pan swarm recover --help` lists the three valid `--action` values and the destructive nature of `drop`.
- AC: `pan swarm recover PAN-1194 2 --action retry` posts to /api/swarm/PAN-1194/slot/2/recover with `{ action: 'retry' }` and the internal token. On 200, prints `Slot 2 of PAN-1194 recovered via retry.` and exits 0.
- AC: `pan swarm recover PAN-1194 2 --action drop` WITHOUT `--yes` prompts the operator for confirmation (interactive). With `--yes`, proceeds without prompt. With neither and stdin non-TTY, exits 1 with `--yes required for non-interactive drop`.
- AC: Server returns non-2xx: CLI prints the server's `error` field, exits 1.
- AC: Invalid `--action` value rejected client-side before the HTTP call with a clear message listing valid values.
- AC: Smoke test (vitest, fetch mocked) covers each action and the destructive-drop confirmation path.
close_reason: Implemented pan swarm recover CLI subcommand with retry/drop/handoff actions, destructive drop confirmation, internal-token API POST, error reporting, and focused test coverage

title: pan-1194: Wire `refreshSwarmSlotMergeability` into `pollSwarmAutoAdvance` and treat `failed-merge` as a failure for halt + error reporting
description:
In `pollSwarmAutoAdvance` (src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts ~line 1437), call `refreshSwarmSlotMergeability(state, projectPath)` AFTER the existing `refreshSwarmSlotStatuses` call and BEFORE the existing `saveSwarmState` write. Combine the `changed` flag so a single persist captures both transitions. Extend the existing failed-slot detection (`state.slots.some(slot => slot.status === 'failed')` at line 1467) to also match `'failed-merge'`. When the failure branch fires for a failed-merge slot, build a human-readable message naming the slot number, item title, branch, and PR URL (when present) and pass it to `recordAutoAdvanceFailure`. Emit an `emitActivityEntry({ source: 'ship', level: 'error', issueId, message: ... })` entry naming the slot/branch/PR and the operator action options (`pan swarm recover <issue> <slot> --action <retry|drop|handoff>`). Also mirror the change into the `/api/swarm/refresh` route (~line 2056) so manual refresh triggers the same detection.
- AC: `pollSwarmAutoAdvance` calls `refreshSwarmSlotMergeability` after `refreshSwarmSlotStatuses` and before the saveSwarmState persistence. A single saveSwarmState call writes both passes' changes.
- AC: The existing failed-slot branch at swarm.ts:1467 is extended to match `slot.status === 'failed' || slot.status === 'failed-merge'`. recordAutoAdvanceFailure is called with a slot-specific message naming the PR URL when present.
- AC: Activity log emits exactly one entry per failed-merge transition (idempotent): `{ source: 'ship', level: 'error', issueId, message: 'Swarm slot <N> PR <prUrl> unmergeable: <reason>. Recover with: pan swarm recover <ISSUE> <N> --action <retry|drop|handoff>' }`. Subsequent polls observing the same already-transitioned slot do NOT re-emit.
- AC: `/api/swarm/refresh` route (POST) calls `refreshSwarmSlotMergeability` after `refreshSwarmSlotStatuses` and persists via the same saveSwarmState path. Manual operator refresh observes the same failed-merge detection as the auto-advance poll.
- AC: Unit test (vi.useFakeTimers()): seed a SwarmState with one running implementation slot, mock gh to return CONFLICTING for two consecutive polls, run two ticks of pollSwarmAutoAdvance, assert the slot is `failed-merge`, `lastAutoAdvanceError` is populated, `autoAdvanceFailureCount >= 1`, and exactly one activity log entry was emitted.
- AC: Regression test: pre-fix scenario (slot stuck at `completed` with PR CONFLICTING, other slots `merged`, autoAdvance=true) results in the swarm being halted with a clear lastAutoAdvanceError instead of looping forever. Asserts that after N ticks the swarm has NOT dispatched a new wave AND has a recorded failure.
close_reason: Wired mergeability refresh into swarm polling and manual refresh with failed-merge halt reporting

title: pan-1194: Add `POST /api/swarm/:issueId/slot/:slotId/recover` recovery endpoint with retry/drop/handoff actions
description:
Add a new Effect route at `POST /api/swarm/:issueId/slot/:slotId/recover` in src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts. Body schema: `{ action: 'retry' | 'drop' | 'handoff' }`. Auth: require the internal token (same pattern as `/api/swarm`). Validate `issueId` via canonicalIssueId, `slotId` as positive integer. Resolve the latest matching slot record (reverse-scan, must be `failed-merge`). Reject 409 if no failed-merge slot found. `retry`: mutate the slot record's status back to a sentinel `'recovering'` (or set status to a value that causes the next dispatch to re-spawn — simplest: re-add the item to a dispatchable set by clearing the matched slot's status and clearing top-level autoAdvanceFailureCount/autoAdvanceRetryAfter/lastAutoAdvanceError; let the next pollSwarmAutoAdvance dispatch run). `drop`: apply `done` task operation to the vBRIEF for `slot.itemId` via `applyTaskOperationToPlanFileAsync` and leave the slot record at `failed-merge` (it's a terminal but advance-allowed state); clear top-level failure counters so auto-advance resumes. `handoff`: clear top-level autoAdvance flag on the SwarmState and emit a handback activity entry. All three: emit `source: 'ship', level: 'info', message: 'Operator recovered slot <N> via <action>'`. Internally use the existing `saveSwarmState` writer. Return 200 with `{ ok: true, action, slotId, issueId }`. Authoritative behavior matrix lives in a JSDoc above the route.
- AC: Route registered at exact path `POST /api/swarm/:issueId/slot/:slotId/recover` with internal-token auth. Returns 403 without the token, 400 for invalid issueId/slotId, 409 if no failed-merge slot exists, 400 for invalid action.
- AC: `action: 'retry'` clears the matched failed-merge slot (reverts to pending/recovering state) AND clears top-level autoAdvanceFailureCount, autoAdvanceRetryAfter, lastAutoAdvanceError. Next pollSwarmAutoAdvance tick is free to dispatch. Unit test verifies the next pollSwarmAutoAdvance call dispatches a fresh slot for the same itemId.
- AC: `action: 'drop'` calls applyTaskOperationToPlanFileAsync({ type: 'done', itemId: <slot.itemId>, reason: 'Operator dropped slot via failed-merge recovery', writerId: 'swarm-recover-drop-<pid>' }) AND clears top-level failure counters. The slot record stays at `failed-merge` but advance is no longer blocked. Subsequent poll dispatches the next DAG-ready item.
- AC: `action: 'handoff'` flips state.autoAdvance to false and emits `source: 'ship', level: 'warn', message: 'Swarm autoAdvance disabled for <ISSUE> after operator handoff of slot <N>. Take it from here.'`. The auto-advance poll loop drops the issue from activeSwarmIssueIds on its next tick (existing cleanup branch).
- AC: Every successful action emits a `source: 'ship', level: 'info'` activity entry recording the operator decision and naming slot, item, action.
- AC: Persistence ordering: ALL three actions write SwarmState via the existing saveSwarmState path. drop additionally writes the vBRIEF via applyTaskOperationToPlanFileAsync; if the vBRIEF write fails, saveSwarmState is NOT called and the route returns 500 with the underlying error (no half-applied state).
- AC: Idempotency: replaying the same recover request on a slot that has already been recovered (no failed-merge entry exists) returns 409 with a clear message rather than 500 or silently re-applying.
- AC: Unit tests for all three actions use vi.useFakeTimers() where the poll loop is exercised post-recovery.
close_reason: Added failed-merge swarm slot recovery endpoint

title: pan-1194: Implement `refreshSwarmSlotMergeability` helper with PR-state-driven `failed-merge` detection
description:
Add `async function refreshSwarmSlotMergeability(state: SwarmState, projectPath?: string): Promise<{ state: SwarmState; changed: boolean }>` to src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts, mirroring the contract of the existing `refreshSwarmSlotStatuses`. For each slot whose `status ∈ {'running','completed'}` AND `phase === 'implementation'`, resolve the slot branch (`feature/<issueLower>-slot-<N>`) and query `gh pr list --repo <owner/repo> --head <slotBranch> --state all --json number,mergeable,mergeableState,state,closed,url,mergedAt --limit 5` via `execAsync` with a 5 s `AbortSignal.timeout`. Decision matrix: PR `mergeableState === 'CONFLICTING'` OR (`mergeable === false` && `mergeableState` nullish) → increment `consecutiveConflictCount`; when count reaches 2, transition slot to `failed-merge` with `failureReason: 'PR #<n> not mergeable: <reason>'` and `prUrl: pr.url`. PR list empty AND slot already `completed` → transition (branch was deleted post-merge-attempt). PR `state === 'CLOSED' && !pr.mergedAt` → transition. Any successful mergeable observation resets `consecutiveConflictCount` to 0. Non-GitHub projects (`!resolveGitHubIssue(issueId).isGitHub`) → return `{ state, changed: false }` immediately. gh timeout / shell error → skip that slot for that tick, do NOT transition (transient). MUST use only the latest matching slot record per slot number (reverse-scan + prefer running > completed, same shape as `onSlotMergeComplete`).
- AC: Function signature exactly: `async function refreshSwarmSlotMergeability(state: SwarmState, projectPath?: string): Promise<{ state: SwarmState; changed: boolean }>` exported via `__testInternals` for tests.
- AC: Synthesis slots (`phase === 'synthesis'`) are never inspected (no PR exists). Unit test asserts the helper returns `changed: false` when the only slot is a completed synthesis slot.
- AC: Non-GitHub project (resolveGitHubIssue returns isGitHub=false) is a no-op. Unit test stubs the helper to non-GitHub and asserts no gh process is spawned and changed=false.
- AC: PR CONFLICTING observed once: slot stays at its current status with `consecutiveConflictCount` incremented to 1. Observed twice consecutively: slot transitions to `failed-merge` with failureReason naming the PR number and prUrl populated. Unit test covers both ticks.
- AC: PR list empty AND slot was `completed`: slot transitions to `failed-merge` with failureReason 'No open PR found for <branch>'. PR list empty AND slot was `running`: no transition (PR may not have been opened yet).
- AC: PR closed without merge (`state === 'CLOSED' && !mergedAt`): slot transitions to `failed-merge` with failureReason 'PR #<n> closed without merge'.
- AC: PR mergeable (`mergeable === true` OR `mergeableState ∈ {'CLEAN','UNSTABLE','BEHIND','HAS_HOOKS'}` per existing KNOWN_NON_BLOCKING_STATES): consecutiveConflictCount is reset to 0 if previously > 0. No status transition.
- AC: gh shell-out timeout (AbortSignal fires) OR non-zero exit: slot is skipped for the tick, no transition, no exception bubbles out of the helper. Unit test uses execAsync mock that throws AbortError.
- AC: Cumulative slot history: when state.slots has multiple records sharing a slot number, only the latest running/completed implementation record is inspected and mutated. Historical merged/failed records are untouched. Unit test seeds two records for slot 1 (one merged, one running) and asserts only the running one is mutated.
- AC: No blocking I/O: helper uses execAsync (or execFileAsync), readFile from 'fs/promises', and never imports execSync/readFileSync/readdirSync. Verified by grep in the unit test file or in CI lint.
close_reason: Implemented refreshSwarmSlotMergeability with PR-state failed-merge detection and tests

title: pan-1194: Add `failed-merge` to slot status types and runtime ↔ state mappers
description:
Extend the `SlotAssignment.status` union in src/dashboard/server/routes/swarm.ts and the `SwarmSlotRuntime.status` union in src/lib/vbrief/continue-state.ts to include the literal `'failed-merge'`. Update `runtimeFromState` and `stateFromRuntime` mappers (swarm.ts:228, 270) so a `failed-merge` slot round-trips between SwarmState and the durable SwarmRuntime without lossy folding into `failed`. Add an optional `consecutiveConflictCount?: number` field on both `SlotAssignment` and `SwarmSlotRuntime` to support the two-tick debounce (H4). Add an optional `prUrl?: string` field on `SlotAssignment` so the dashboard can render the PR link without re-querying gh. Do NOT touch the auto-advance poll loop yet — this bead lays the type foundation only.
- AC: `SlotAssignment.status` includes `'failed-merge'` and `SwarmState`/`SwarmDispatchResponseBody` type checks pass against the widened union.
- AC: `SwarmSlotRuntime.status` includes `'failed-merge'` in src/lib/vbrief/continue-state.ts. Existing readers of SwarmSlotRuntime (e.g. continue-state-swarm.test.ts) continue to typecheck without changes.
- AC: `runtimeFromState` and `stateFromRuntime` round-trip a `failed-merge` slot without changing its status. New unit test asserts the round-trip preserves status, failureReason, consecutiveConflictCount, and prUrl.
- AC: `SlotAssignment` and `SwarmSlotRuntime` gain optional `consecutiveConflictCount?: number` and `prUrl?: string` fields. The mappers preserve both fields when present and omit them when absent.
- AC: `npm run typecheck` passes with zero new errors after the union widening.
close_reason: Addressed inspection blocker by keeping workspace-local .pan files untracked and committing canonical lifecycle artifacts.

title: pan-1191: Update WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md + run end-to-end UAT against PAN-1148/1169/1189/1190 fixtures
description:
Add a 'Provisioning on Start' section to `docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md` documenting the new flow (helper, SSE endpoint, dialog, CLI behavior, the --host break-glass, the Cloister auto-resume gap as a known follow-up). Drive a Playwright UAT (isolated browser instance) against the dashboard: click Start on a test issue with an absent stack, walk through the dialog, observe the agent terminal pane after success. Confirm the four issues named in the acceptance criteria (PAN-1148, PAN-1169, PAN-1189, PAN-1190) can all start without --host fallback after this lands (verify against current main once PAN-1169 has merged).
- AC: `docs/WORKSPACE-CONTAINERS.md` has a new 'Provisioning on Start' section describing the helper, SSE endpoint, dialog, CLI behavior, and the Cloister auto-resume known gap.
- AC: Playwright UAT script (`tests/e2e/start-agent-provisioning.spec.ts` or equivalent existing location) clicks Start on a fresh issue, observes the dialog steps, and asserts the agent terminal becomes visible. Uses an isolated browser instance/profile.
- AC: Manual confirmation recorded in the PR description that PAN-1148, PAN-1169, PAN-1189, and PAN-1190 can each be started from the dashboard without the --host fallback after this lands.

title: pan-1191: Add unit + integration tests for the provisioning helper, mutex, SSE route, and dialog
description:
Unit tests in `src/lib/workspace/__tests__/provision-stack.test.ts` covering: healthy short-circuit, missing-devcontainer render, compose-up failure path, health-poll timeout, force=true compose-down. Mutex tests in `src/lib/workspace/__tests__/provisioning-lock.test.ts` covering per-issue serialization, global cap, error-path release. SSE route integration test in `src/dashboard/server/__tests__/agents-start-stream.test.ts` asserting the event sequence and pre-stream error contracts. Frontend test in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/StartAgentDialog.test.tsx` for the checklist render shape.
- AC: `npm test` passes with the new test files in place.
- AC: Provision-stack tests cover the four scenarios listed in the Action: healthy short-circuit, render-then-up, compose-up failure, health-poll timeout.
- AC: SSE route test asserts the exact event-type sequence for a success path and the error event shape for a forced failure.

title: pan-1191: Wire Retry / Force rebuild / Switch to --host (advanced) recovery actions in the dialog
description:
On failure, render three buttons under the error: (1) 'Retry' re-runs the same SSE request unchanged; (2) 'Force rebuild' re-runs with `{ force: true }` in the request body, which makes `provisionWorkspaceStack({ force: true })` do a `compose down -v` + re-render first; (3) 'Switch to --host (advanced)' triggers the existing two-step host-override flow against the non-streaming POST /api/agents endpoint. The third button is visually de-emphasized (smaller text, muted color) to discourage casual use.
- AC: Clicking 'Retry' re-opens the SSE stream with the same body (no force) and replaces the failed step list.
- AC: Clicking 'Force rebuild' sends `{ force: true }`, the helper runs `compose down -v --remove-orphans` then re-renders `.devcontainer/` then brings the stack up.
- AC: Clicking 'Switch to --host (advanced)' walks the user through the existing hostOverrideConfirmation prompt and then issues the non-streaming POST /api/agents with the host flag; the dialog reports success/failure inline.
- AC: The three buttons are visible only when the dialog is in an error state, not during normal progress.

title: pan-1191: Add `StartAgentDialog` frontend component consuming the SSE stream
description:
Add `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/StartAgentDialog.tsx` modelled on PlanDialog's `setting-up` step. It opens when KanbanBoard's Start mutation is invoked, owns a fetch-with-streaming-body call to `/api/agents/start-stream`, parses SSE events with the same `data: ` line-splitter PlanDialog uses, renders a checklist (one row per progress step with status icon, label, detail, and elapsed time), and closes itself when it receives the final `complete` event. Wire it into `KanbanBoard.tsx` so the Start button opens the dialog (replacing the silent fire-and-forget mutation for the dashboard path). The auto-start button (Start without planning) opens the same dialog with `auto: true`.
- AC: `StartAgentDialog` renders a modal with a vertical checklist of steps, status icon, label, detail line, and elapsed seconds per step.
- AC: The dialog opens when the Start button on a kanban card is clicked and stays open until the SSE stream emits `complete` or `error`.
- AC: On `complete`, the dialog closes and the dashboard transitions to the agent's terminal pane (existing behavior after start succeeds).
- AC: Pre-stream HTTP errors (non-200 response) render the error message + hint inline in the dialog with a Retry button — matching PlanDialog's error UX.
- AC: Auto-start button (existing `card-auto-start-agent-*`) opens the same dialog with `auto: true` in the request body so a fresh vBRIEF is synthesized inline.

title: pan-1191: Make `pan start <id>` provision the stack before spawn (when not --host)
description:
In `src/cli/commands/start.ts`, between workspace resolution and the spawn call, invoke `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, { force: false, onProgress: cliRenderer })` when `!options.host`. The CLI renderer prints a multi-line checklist (one `✓`/`⟳`/`✗` line per step with elapsed time), matching the issue body's expected output. `--host --yes` continues to skip provisioning entirely.
- AC: `pan start PAN-X` (no `--host`) on an issue with a missing stack prints the multi-line checklist and successfully starts the agent.
- AC: `pan start PAN-X --host --yes` skips the provisioning checklist and falls through to the existing host-override start path.
- AC: `pan start PAN-X` on an issue with an already-healthy stack prints a single `✓ Workspace stack already healthy — skipping provision` line and proceeds to spawn.

title: pan-1191: Make the existing `POST /api/agents` (non-streaming) self-heal via `provisionWorkspaceStack()`
description:
In the existing POST /api/agents flow in `src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts`, immediately before the stack-health gate (around the existing `getWorkspaceStackHealth` call), call `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, { force: false })` when `!allowHost && !isRemote`. Skip when `allowHost` is true (host override). No progress streaming on this path — just synchronous (awaited) provisioning before the gate. This keeps non-dashboard callers (host-override retries, Cloister auto-resume's manual restart trigger, CLI fallback) working without re-introducing the regression.
- AC: POSTing `/api/agents` for a non-existent-stack issue without `--host` brings the stack up and then spawns the agent (no longer returns 422 'No Docker containers found').
- AC: POSTing `/api/agents` with `host: true` + confirmation still skips provisioning entirely and falls through to the existing host-override path.
- AC: If `provisionWorkspaceStack()` throws, the route returns 422 with the same shape it already returns for unhealthy-stack failures (`error`, `hint`, `stackHealth`).

title: pan-1191: Add `POST /api/agents/start-stream` SSE route
description:
Add a new HttpRouter.add route in `src/dashboard/server/routes/agents.ts` (or a new sibling file `agents-start-stream.ts` registered from `server.ts`). The route: (1) performs the same pre-flight validation as POST /api/agents (origin, issueId parse, host-override confirmation, beads, guardrails, codex/provider health) and returns JSON 4xx errors before the stream opens; (2) opens an SSE ReadableStream with `Content-Type: text/event-stream`; (3) emits `started` event with issue + workspace metadata; (4) calls `provisionWorkspaceStack()` with an onProgress that forwards `progress` events; (5) on success, runs the existing spawn flow and emits a `spawning` and final `complete` event with `{ agentId, sessionName }`; (6) on failure, emits a single `error` event with `{ error, hint, kind }` then closes.
- AC: POSTing to `/api/agents/start-stream` with `{issueId, role: 'work', model, harness}` returns `Content-Type: text/event-stream` and streams `data: {...}\n\n` events.
- AC: Pre-stream JSON errors (origin block, codex burned, beads missing, guardrails block) are returned with the same status codes and shapes as POST /api/agents.
- AC: Successful provisioning emits at minimum: started → progress(worktree) → progress(devcontainer) → progress(compose) → progress(health) → spawning → complete.
- AC: On provisioning failure, the stream emits exactly one `error` event with `{ error, hint, kind: 'provision_failed' | 'spawn_failed' | 'unhealthy_after_timeout' }` then closes cleanly (no half-open SSE).
- AC: The route is wired into the Effect HTTP server via the existing `httpHandler` pattern and uses async I/O exclusively.

title: pan-1191: Refactor CLI `workspaceRebuildCommand` to delegate to `provisionWorkspaceStack({ force: true })`
description:
Strip the docker-compose-down/up/render duplicate logic out of `src/cli/commands/workspace-rebuild.ts` and replace with a call to `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, { force: true, onProgress: cliRenderer })`. Keep the ora spinner, but drive it from the helper's progress events. Preserve `composeProjectNameForWorkspace()` (the guard) and the existing unit tests.
- AC: `pan workspace rebuild <id>` still rebuilds the stack end-to-end on a corrupted workspace.
- AC: The COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME guard test in `src/cli/commands/__tests__/workspace-rebuild.test.ts` continues to pass.
- AC: `workspace-rebuild.ts` no longer contains its own `docker compose` exec calls — they live only in `provision-stack.ts`.

title: pan-1191: Add `provisionWorkspaceStack()` shared helper in src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts
description:
Create `src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` exporting `provisionWorkspaceStack(issueId, options)` with an `onProgress` callback. The function: (1) resolves project + workspacePath via existing `resolveProjectFromIssue`/`getProject` helpers, (2) calls `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` first — if healthy, emits a `stack-already-healthy` progress event and returns, (3) otherwise calls async `ensureDevcontainerAsync()` (new fs/promises sibling of `ensureDevcontainer`), (4) optionally runs `docker compose down -v --remove-orphans` when `options.force === true`, (5) runs `docker compose up -d --build` via execAsync with a 300s timeout, (6) polls `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` every 2s up to 120s until healthy, emitting heartbeat progress events every 10s during long phases. Returns `{ success, stackHealth, durations }`. NO sync fs calls, NO execSync.
- AC: `provisionWorkspaceStack()` exists at `src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` and exports a single async function with the documented signature.
- AC: When `getWorkspaceStackHealth()` returns healthy on entry, the function emits exactly one `stack-already-healthy` progress event and returns without invoking `docker compose`.
- AC: When the stack is missing, the function calls the canonical `renderDevcontainer()` via an async wrapper (no duplicate template rendering logic).
- AC: All filesystem and process I/O uses fs/promises + execAsync. `grep -nE 'execSync|readFileSync|writeFileSync|rmSync|readdirSync|statSync' src/lib/workspace/provision-stack.ts` returns no matches.
- AC: Heartbeat progress events fire every ~10s during compose-up and health-poll phases, so an idle SSE client doesn't see a quiet stream during long provisioning.
- AC: Health-poll terminates with a clear error after 120s of consecutive unhealthy reads; error includes the latest `stackHealth.reasons`.
- AC: `renderDevcontainer()` no-mount + `PANOPTICON_DISABLE_DEACON=1` invariants are preserved (no new mount of `~/.panopticon` is introduced anywhere in this module).

title: pan-1191: Add per-issue mutex + global concurrency cap of 2 for workspace provisioning
description:
Add `withProvisioningLock(issueId, fn)` in `src/lib/workspace/provisioning-lock.ts`. Per-issue: chain promises by normalized issueId so a duplicate Start for the same issue waits for the in-flight call. Global: a 2-slot async semaphore around the inner work. Mutex released in try/finally. Used by `provisionWorkspaceStack()`.
- AC: Two concurrent `provisionWorkspaceStack('PAN-X', ...)` calls for the same issue serialize — the second receives the first's resolved value rather than re-running.
- AC: Five concurrent `provisionWorkspaceStack()` calls for five different issues run at most 2 at a time; the other 3 queue.
- AC: When the inner function throws, the mutex slot is released (next caller can proceed without timing out).

title: pan-1190: Update Deacon reconcile paths to recognize the new non-destructive postMergeLifecycle
description:
Walk each Deacon call site of postMergeLifecycle in src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts (lines ~2481, ~3135, ~3903 today) and confirm the new non-destructive contract is acceptable. title: pan-1190: Optional auto-closeout Deacon patrol when close_out.auto=true
description:
Add a Deacon patrol that, when close_out.auto=true, finds issues in verifying_on_main older than close_out.auto_delay_minutes (measured from review-status mergeStatus=merged timestamp) and runs closeOut() against them. Default config leaves this disabled — operators opt in.
- AC: Deacon patrols include an autoCloseOut() sweep that runs on the existing patrol cadence (no new daemon, no new socket).
- AC: Sweep is gated on close_out.auto === true; when false the sweep returns immediately with zero work and zero log output.
- AC: Eligibility filter: review-status entries with mergeStatus='merged' AND canonical state === 'verifying_on_main' AND updatedAt older than (now - close_out.auto_delay_minutes).
- AC: Each eligible issue runs closeOut(ctx) with auto: true context flag so log lines / activity entries distinguish auto-close-out from operator-driven close-out.
- AC: Sweep is idempotent — a successful closeOut clears the canonical state from verifying_on_main, so the next sweep skips the issue.
- AC: If closeOut fails, the sweep does NOT retry in a tight loop — it backs off (re-eligible no sooner than auto_delay_minutes later) and surfaces the failure via emitActivityEntry so an operator can intervene.
close_reason: Implemented auto-closeout Deacon patrol in commit 3d7fe7054. Added close_out.auto-gated autoCloseOut sweep on existing runPatrol cadence, eligible verifying_on_main filtering with auto_delay_minutes, closeOut auto context, failure activity/backoff via review-status updatedAt, and focused tests. Vitest, typecheck, lint, and graphify update passed.

title: pan-1190: Refactor postMergeLifecycle into a non-destructive merge-handoff
description:
Surgically refactor postMergeLifecycle() in src/lib/cloister/merge-agent.ts so it stops being the destructive end-of-life event. After this bead the function MUST leave the workspace, agent state dir, vBRIEF, feature branch, and GitHub issue intact, transitioning the issue to canonical state verifying_on_main. The previously-destructive steps now live in closeOut (previous bead).
- AC: postMergeLifecycle still runs: merge verification, setReviewStatus({mergeStatus:'merged'}), stash drop, server deploy script handoff, Docker container/network shutdown, reviewer/synthesis session kill, specialist tmux session kill (review/test/merge), beads compaction, TLDR daemon reindex notification, slot-branch swarm handoff.
- AC: postMergeLifecycle NO LONGER runs: closeIssueWithCircuitBreaker, vBRIEF active→completed transition, agent state dir removal, workspace teardown, feature-branch deletion, PRD active→completed move, checkpoint-ref prune. closeIssueWithCircuitBreaker and its tracking state may be deleted if no other caller remains.
- AC: Work agent + planning agent are PAUSED (not deleted): setAgentPaused(agentId, 'awaiting close-out (verify on main)', true) is called for each. tmux sessions are killed (resource leak prevention), but ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/state.json survives with paused=true.
- AC: Canonical state transition: a new helper writes 'verifying_on_main' to the issue's canonical state surface (issue-lifecycle.ts label rules + tracker label edit). On GitHub: add 'verifying-on-main' label, remove 'in-review' / 'in-progress'. Do NOT close the GitHub issue. The legacy 'needs-close-out' label is no longer applied here (replaced by 'verifying-on-main').
- AC: Idempotency guards (_completedPostMerge Set, _postMergeInFlight) still work but their semantics now mean 'verify-handoff already done' rather than 'issue cleaned up'.
- AC: resetPostMergeState(issueId) is callable from the close-out flow so close-out can permit re-entry if a regression triggers a reopen.
- AC: After the handoff, the issue is observable on the dashboard as: GitHub state=open, labels include 'verifying-on-main', review-status mergeStatus='merged', work agent state.json paused=true, workspace dir present, vBRIEF still at 'active' / 'running', GitHub issue still OPEN.
- AC: Existing test src/lib/cloister/__tests__/merge-agent-stash.test.ts (and any other merge-agent tests) still pass; new test asserts the non-destructive contract above.
- AC: activity log entry on completion reads 'Merged <id>. Awaiting close-out (verify on main).' (not 'Issue moved to Done').
close_reason: Implemented non-destructive post-merge verify-on-main handoff in commit 7d3726969; focused lifecycle tests, typecheck, lint, graphify update, and required deep inspection passed.
