title: pan-1169: Fail init container loudly on UNRESOLVED_IMPORT warnings during dashboard build
description:
Modify the `init.command` shell pipeline in `infra/.devcontainer-template/docker-compose.devcontainer.yml.template` so that the `npm run build:dashboard:server` step's combined stdout+stderr is captured and scanned for the literal token `UNRESOLVED_IMPORT`. If any line matches, the init container exits non-zero so the failure surfaces as a build error inside init rather than as a runtime `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` inside the server container at startup.

Keep the change local to the existing inline shell command — do NOT introduce a separate script file. A reasonable shape:

```
npm run build:dashboard:server 2>&1 | tee /tmp/dashboard-build.log
if grep -q 'UNRESOLVED_IMPORT' /tmp/dashboard-build.log; then
  echo 'ERROR: dashboard build emitted UNRESOLVED_IMPORT warnings — top-level node_modules is incomplete (see PAN-1169).' >&2
  exit 1
fi
```

The `PIPESTATUS`/`pipefail` handling matters: a `| tee` pipeline can mask the underlying build's non-zero exit, so either set `pipefail` first or check `${PIPESTATUS[0]}` after the tee. Use whichever idiom is portable to the busybox `sh` that the alpine-based init container uses (the existing command runs under `sh -c`, not bash).

Add a comment in the template above the new block explaining that this guard exists to catch the class of failure described in PAN-1169 (top-level node_modules missing → externalized imports unresolved → server crashes at runtime), so a future engineer doesn't remove the grep as 'noise'.
- AC: The init service's `command:` in `infra/.devcontainer-template/docker-compose.devcontainer.yml.template` captures the dashboard server build's stdout+stderr, scans it for the literal token `UNRESOLVED_IMPORT`, and exits the init container with non-zero status if any match is found.
- AC: The exit-status handling is correct under busybox `sh -c`: a non-zero exit from `npm run build:dashboard:server` itself (not just the grep) must also fail init. This means either `set -o pipefail` is used (and works under the container's sh) or `${PIPESTATUS[0]}` is explicitly checked.
- AC: A comment above the new guard in the template explains why it exists, references PAN-1169, and warns against removing it as 'noise'.
- AC: On a freshly-rebuilt PAN-1169 workspace with the Bead 1 fix in place, the init container exits 0 (no UNRESOLVED_IMPORT warnings, build completes cleanly) — i.e. the guard does not produce a false positive when the system is working.
- AC: Manual injection test: run the init container once with the Bead 1 fix reverted (`--filter` restored) and a fresh empty top-level node_modules volume, and document the exact observed behavior. If that exact regression no longer emits UNRESOLVED_IMPORT in the current Bun/build environment, also run a direct missing-top-level-dependency injection that removes representative packages required by externalized dashboard imports, and require that the guard exits non-zero with a diagnostic referencing PAN-1169 and UNRESOLVED_IMPORT.
- AC: No new files are added (no separate shell script). The change is entirely inside `infra/.devcontainer-template/docker-compose.devcontainer.yml.template`'s `init.command` block.
notes:
Review clarification for PAN-1169: the exact restored --filter + empty top-level node_modules scenario no longer reproduced UNRESOLVED_IMPORT in this environment because the current dependency graph/build no longer emitted that warning. For AC5, the accepted manual injection method is synthetic top-level dependency removal: remove representative top-level packages required by externalized dashboard imports (yaml, @iarna, effect) from the named node_modules volume before the guarded build, then rerun init. That produced the intended non-zero init exit with the PAN-1169/UNRESOLVED_IMPORT diagnostic and validates the same missing-hoist failure class the guard is meant to catch.
AC12 restored-filter verification documentation (review follow-up): exact scenario was performed with a temporary compose copy where the Bead 1 fix was reverted to the original filtered install command, using a fresh compose project name pan1169filtertest so the top-level container-node-modules volume started empty. Command run: docker compose -f .devcontainer/docker-compose.pan1169-filter-test.yml -p pan1169filtertest up --build --abort-on-container-exit --exit-code-from init init. Observed result in this current Bun/build environment: init printed Init complete and exited 0; no UNRESOLVED_IMPORT warning was emitted. Because the exact restored-filter regression no longer reproduces, AC12 accepts the documented equivalent failure-class test: remove representative top-level packages required by externalized dashboard imports (yaml, @iarna, effect) from the named node_modules volume before rerunning the guarded build. That alternate injection produced init exit 1 with the diagnostic referencing PAN-1169 and UNRESOLVED_IMPORT, proving the guard catches the missing-hoist class.
close_reason: Added guarded dashboard build log capture in init. Clean guarded init exited 0. Busybox sh status handling preserves build non-zero exits. A restored --filter install no longer reproduced UNRESOLVED_IMPORT in this environment, so the failure-class injection removed top-level yaml/@iarna/effect before build; init exited 1 with the PAN-1169 UNRESOLVED_IMPORT diagnostic.

title: pan-1169: Drop --filter from workspace devcontainer templates' bun install commands
description:
Remove the --filter flag arguments from the `bun install` invocation in two template files so a plain `bun install` runs against the named volume and triggers bun's workspace-hoist phase, populating top-level node_modules/<pkg> entries that the pre-built dashboard server expects at runtime.

Files to edit:
  1. `infra/.devcontainer-template/docker-compose.devcontainer.yml.template` — `init.command` block. Change the line `bun install --filter '@panctl/cli' --filter '@panctl/contracts' --filter '@panopticon/pi-extension' --filter 'panopticon-server' --filter 'panopticon-dashboard' &&` to `bun install &&`.
  2. `infra/.devcontainer-template/devcontainer.json.template` — `postCreateCommand`. Change the value from `bun install --filter '@panctl/cli' --filter '@panctl/contracts' --filter '@panopticon/pi-extension' --filter 'panopticon-server' --filter 'panopticon-dashboard'` to `bun install`.

Do NOT change any volume mounts, environment variables, or other init steps. The `npm rebuild better-sqlite3` and dashboard build steps must remain in place and in the same order.

Add a brief comment above the changed line in the docker-compose template explaining that --filter was removed because it leaves the top-level node_modules un-hoisted on the named volume (reference PAN-1169) so a future engineer doesn't innocently re-add it.
- AC: In `infra/.devcontainer-template/docker-compose.devcontainer.yml.template`, the `init` service's `command:` block invokes `bun install` (no `--filter` flags, no workspace name arguments).
- AC: In `infra/.devcontainer-template/devcontainer.json.template`, `postCreateCommand` is `bun install` (no `--filter` flags, no workspace name arguments).
- AC: A short comment in the docker-compose template (adjacent to the changed `bun install` line) documents that `--filter` was removed because it leaves top-level node_modules un-hoisted on the named volume, and references PAN-1169.
- AC: After a freshly-rebuilt workspace (`pan workspace rebuild PAN-1169` or equivalent fresh `docker compose up`), running `docker run --rm -v <stack>_container-node-modules:/nm alpine ls /nm` lists top-level package directories (must include at least `effect`, `yaml`, and `@iarna` — the three confirmed-missing in the issue body), not just `.bun`.
- AC: After the same fresh rebuild, the `server` container reaches a steady running state (no `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` in `docker logs panopticon-feature-pan-1169-server-1`, container does not exit within 60 seconds of starting).
- AC: After the same fresh rebuild, `evaluateWorkspaceStackHealth('PAN-1169', ...)` returns `healthy: true` (no `stuck Created`, no `init exited non-zero`, no `service exited` reasons).
- AC: No other files are modified by this bead. (Specifically: `infra/.devcontainer-template/Dockerfile`, `infra/.devcontainer-template/dev.template`, `src/lib/workspace/devcontainer-renderer.ts`, and `src/lib/workspace-manager.ts` are unchanged.)
close_reason: Removed Bun --filter flags from devcontainer template installs, verified fresh docker init hoists top-level dependencies and server stack stays healthy.

title: pan-1148: Define and implement drawer subscription contract (Effect-managed, server-filtered by issueId)
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/drawer/useDrawerData.ts implementing the single Effect-managed subscription that the drawer owns and tears down on close. Per PRD §5.2, subscription filters at SERVER side by issueId — extend the WS-RPC contract in src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts (PanRpcGroup) with `subscribeIssueEvents(issueId)` returning a filtered stream of pipeline-state transitions, bead closures, agent state changes for that one issue. The hook exposes `{ issue, agents, beads, reviewSpecialists, verificationGates, activityRail }` as reactive selectors. Inspect: ensure the server-side filter is enforced (not relying on client-side filter of full feed); ensure teardown on close releases the server-side subscription; ensure the hook is the ONLY data path for the drawer's data — no per-subsection ad-hoc fetches that drift from this contract.
- AC: WS-RPC contract gains `subscribeIssueEvents(issueId)` server-filtered stream in src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts
- AC: useDrawerData hook returns `{ issue, agents, beads, reviewSpecialists, verificationGates, activityRail }` as reactive selectors keyed off the open issue id
- AC: Subscription is initiated on drawer open, torn down on drawer close (verified by network panel inspection / vitest with WS mock)
- AC: All drawer subsection components (active agent, beads, specialists, side rail) consume useDrawerData ONLY — no direct fetches or per-section subscriptions
- AC: Server-side filter implemented in src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts; client never receives events for other issues over this stream (vitest with mock server)
- AC: Re-opening the drawer for the same issue id within 1 second reuses the existing subscription (no spurious tear-down + re-subscribe)
close_reason: Implemented issue-scoped drawer subscription contract, server filtering, shared hook lifecycle, tests, and verification.

title: pan-1148: Add Issue Detail drawer shell + global store slice (single-instance, URL-addressable)
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/drawer/IssueDrawer.tsx as the slide-out shell (980px max-width, full-height pinned right, scrim with backdrop-blur(2px), shadow per PRD §4.7.8). Add `drawer` slice to the existing Zustand store (src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/store.ts) with shape `{ issueId: string | null, tab: string }` and actions `openIssue(id, tab?)`, `closeIssue()`. Wire URL sync: `?issue=<id>&tab=<tab>` is the authoritative state — opening reflects to URL, closing removes both params, opening a different issue REPLACES the prior one (sequential, never stacked). Esc, X button, click-on-scrim all close. Parent surface dims (4% accent overlay + 2px blur) behind. Inspect: ensure the single-instance invariant is enforced at the store level (opening B while A is open is a replace, not a stack); ensure subscription teardown happens on close (no leaks on rapid open→close→open burst).
- AC: IssueDrawer.tsx implements PRD §4.7.8 surface spec: 980px max-width, max-width: calc(100vw - 48px), full-height pinned right, backdrop-filter blur(2px), shadow -24px 0 64px rgb(0 0 0 / 40%)
- AC: Zustand store has new `drawer` slice with `{ issueId: string | null, tab: string }` and actions `openIssue(id, tab?)`, `closeIssue()`; opening a new issue while one is open REPLACES (not stacks)
- AC: URL sync: opening sets `?issue=<id>&tab=<tab>` via history.replaceState; closing removes both params; initial page load with `?issue=<id>` opens drawer on mount
- AC: Esc, X button, and click-on-scrim all dispatch closeIssue()
- AC: Parent surface receives a `data-drawer-open="true"` attribute and applies 4% accent overlay + 2px blur via CSS — no inline style mutation
- AC: Animation: drawer slide-in 200ms ease-in-out (scale 0.98 → 1, opacity 0 → 1) per PRD §4.7.11
- AC: Vitest integration test asserts the single-instance invariant: openIssue('A'), openIssue('B'), then store reads { issueId: 'B' } (not stacked)
- AC: Vitest integration test for rapid open→close→open burst (50 iterations) confirms no orphan listeners or subscriptions in the store
- AC: Closing the drawer restores parent surface scroll position (drawer is overlay, not navigation)

title: pan-1170: Implement issue
description:
## Problem

PAN-829 wired the dashboard's in-process TTS subscriber (\`startTtsPlayback\`) but left the **Qwen3-TTS HTTP daemon** (\`skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py\`) as a manual-start, undocumented dependency. Every TTS feature in Panopticon — activity TTS playback, voice sampling, voice preset previews, AutoPreso voice feedback — POSTs to that daemon at \`daemonHost:daemonPort/speak\`. If the daemon isn't running, every TTS path fails with \`daemon-unavailable\` / \`TTS daemon unavailable\` and the user has no path-of-least-resistance to fix it.

Tonight a user hit:
- \"daemon is not running\" in the TTS status indicator
- \`{\"spoken\":false,\"result\":\"daemon-unavailable\",\"error\":\"TTS daemon unavailable\"}\` when trying to sample a voice preset

In both cases the actual fix was \`cd ~/Projects/qwen-tts && nohup .venv/bin/python /path/to/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py &\`. That is not discoverable, and the dependency on a separate \`~/Projects/qwen-tts\` venv outside the panopticon-cli repo is even worse.

## Scope (single issue, all phases)

### 1. Install
- Move daemon Python deps under panopticon-cli (or under \`packages/qwen-tts-linux-x64/\` like the Moonshine sidecar) instead of \`~/Projects/qwen-tts/\`.
- Add \`pan install\` step that creates the daemon venv and installs \`qwen-tts\`, \`torch\` (with CUDA bindings), \`soundfile\`, \`numpy\`. Heavy — should be skippable via \`--skip-tts-daemon\` (heavy because of CUDA torch ~5GB).
- Optional: detect GPU and skip / warn if no CUDA available (daemon won't run usefully on CPU at the model's size).
- On non-Linux platforms, skip with a clear message.

### 2. Start / stop CLI
- \`pan tts start\` — launches the daemon (foreground or detached via \`--detach\`), waits for \`/health\` to return ok before exiting.
- \`pan tts stop\` — graceful SIGTERM → SIGKILL with PID tracked under \`~/.panopticon/pids/qwen-tts.pid\`.
- \`pan tts status\` — reports daemon PID, port, model, queue depth, uptime, GPU memory used.
- \`pan tts restart\` — wraps stop + start.
- All commands honor \`tts.daemonHost\` and \`tts.daemonPort\` from config.

### 3. Lifecycle integration
- \`pan up\` optionally launches the daemon (gated by \`tts.daemon.autoStart\` in config — default off because of the cold-start cost and VRAM footprint).
- Optional systemd user unit installer (\`pan tts install-systemd\`) for users who want auto-start on boot. Template lives in repo, \`pan install\` doesn't enable it by default.
- Dashboard supervisor / watchdog (extend the \`src/supervisor/watchdog.ts\` pattern from PAN-1139) detects daemon crash and restarts (with backoff and a give-up threshold so a misconfigured GPU doesn't restart-loop forever).

### 4. UI surface
- Dashboard Settings → TTS tab: \"Daemon: running on cuda:0 (4.2GB VRAM) | model loaded\" with a \"Start daemon\" button when it's down.
- Health badge in the sidebar (existing CloisterStatusBar already shows TTS — point it at the new status payload).

### 5. Docs
- Update \`skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md\` with the new CLI surface and remove the manual \`cd skills/pan-tts/scripts && python tts_daemon.py\` instruction (it survives as the lowest-level escape hatch).
- Add to \`docs/PANOPTICON_DEV_SOP.md\` (the doc PAN-1139 created) so post-restart troubleshooting includes \`pan tts status\`.

## Out of scope
- Replacing Qwen3-TTS with a different model.
- Cross-machine daemon hosting (the existing pan-tts SSE subscriber already handles that).
- Browser-side fallback synthesis (Web Speech API) for voice samples — keep the daemon as the single audio path so previews match what users hear in production.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Fresh \`pan install\` on a Linux/CUDA machine produces a working daemon (\`pan tts start\` → \`/api/tts/health\` returns ok).
- [ ] \`pan tts start\`, stop, status, restart all work and round-trip a single test phrase via \`pan tts test\`.
- [ ] Dashboard Settings TTS tab shows live daemon status and offers a one-click \"Start daemon\" when it's down.
- [ ] Crash + restart: kill the daemon process, watchdog brings it back within 30s.
- [ ] \`pan install --skip-tts-daemon\` completes without touching CUDA torch.
- [ ] Non-Linux platforms see a clear skip message during install (no failure).

## Why now

Tonight's session blocked at two separate points on the missing daemon — first \"TTS broken\" diagnosis, then \"voice sampling broken\" diagnosis — each requiring manual investigation. With \`pan tts start\` and the supervisor, both paths self-heal.

## Related
- PAN-829 (TTS lifecycle, merged) — shipped the in-process subscriber but didn't own the synth daemon.
- PAN-1139 (dashboard restart watchdog) — supervisor pattern to reuse here.
- PAN-1162 (ship-role allowHost) — example of a small substrate fix that unblocks a recurring failure mode.
- AC: Fresh \pan install\ on a Linux/CUDA machine produces a working daemon (\pan tts start\ → \/api/tts/health\ returns ok).
- AC: \pan tts start\, stop, status, restart all work and round-trip a single test phrase via \pan tts test\.
- AC: Dashboard Settings TTS tab shows live daemon status and offers a one-click \"Start daemon\" when it's down.
- AC: Crash + restart: kill the daemon process, watchdog brings it back within 30s.
- AC: \pan install --skip-tts-daemon\ completes without touching CUDA torch.
- AC: Non-Linux platforms see a clear skip message during install (no failure).
close_reason: Implementation complete

title: pan-1164: Playwright UAT — live edit propagates within ~1s, no /diffs polling
description:
Add a Playwright UAT in `src/dashboard/frontend/tests/` that exercises the acceptance criteria from the issue end-to-end using an isolated browser instance (no shared profile/session per the Panopticon isolation rule). Test flow: (a) spin up dashboard + create or attach to a test conversation with a known JSONL fixture path; (b) open the ConversationPanel and wait for initial load; (c) record network requests via `page.on('request')`; (d) append a synthetic `Edit` tool_use entry to the JSONL file from the test harness; (e) assert the new file appears in the 'Changed files' header within 2.5s (covers the 2s messages poll + event roundtrip + render); (f) over the following 6s, assert there is exactly ONE `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` GET request — the on-mount fetch — and zero periodic refetches. The test uses its own browser context (`browser.newContext()`) and does not depend on any other agent's Playwright session.
- AC: Playwright test file lives under `src/dashboard/frontend/tests/conversation-diff-push.spec.ts` and runs as part of the dashboard frontend test command
- AC: Test uses an isolated `browser.newContext()` — does not rely on any other agent's Playwright session, profile, or login state
- AC: Appending a tool_use Edit entry to the test JSONL causes the new file path to render in the 'Changed files' header within 2.5s of the JSONL write
- AC: Over a 6-second observation window after the panel opens, exactly one HTTP GET to `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` is observed (the on-mount fetch) — no periodic polling
- AC: Clicking a file in the 'Changed files' header still opens the per-turn unified diff (existing behavior unchanged)
- AC: Test passes locally and in the project's standard test command (`npm test` or whichever entry runs Playwright frontend tests)

title: pan-1164: Consume store summaries in ConversationPanel and drop refetchInterval
description:
In `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/chat/ConversationPanel.tsx`: (1) remove `refetchInterval: 5000` from the `conversation-diffs` useQuery (keep the on-mount fetch so older turns hydrate React Query). (2) Read `conversationDiffSummariesByName[conversation.name]` from the dashboard store via `useDashboardStore`. (3) Replace the current `turnDiffSummaryByAssistantMessageId` derivation so it merges store summaries (live deltas) over the React Query summaries (initial seed), then maps by `assistantMessageId` using the same fallback timestamp-matching logic. (4) Optional polish: after the initial HTTP fetch resolves, write its summaries into the store too so the merge is naturally deduped — or leave them separate and merge at read time. Pick the simpler one. Verify the diff click-through (`handleOpenTurnDiff`) is unchanged.
- AC: `refetchInterval: 5000` is removed from the `['conversation-diffs', conversation.name]` useQuery in `ConversationPanel.tsx`; the on-mount fetch still runs once
- AC: ConversationPanel subscribes to `conversationDiffSummariesByName[conversation.name]` from the dashboard store; new summaries appear in the 'Changed files' header without any additional HTTP fetch
- AC: Existing click-through behavior — opening a per-file turn diff via `handleOpenTurnDiff` and the `diffUrlPrefix` — is unchanged; the `TurnDiffPanel` still loads from `/api/conversations/:name/diffs/:turnId`
- AC: Component unit test (Vitest + RTL) renders ConversationPanel with mocked store + React Query, dispatches a `conversation.diff_summary_updated` event into the store, asserts the new file appears in the rendered DOM without invalidating the React Query cache
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` (root + frontend) all pass

title: pan-1164: Emit `conversation.diff_summary_updated` from getCachedMessages parse hook
description:
Hook the emit into `getCachedMessages` (src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts). After the parse result is computed (incremental or full), compare its `fileEditsByAssistantId` against the previous cache snapshot's edits to find new or grown entries. For each new assistantId, look up the merged assistant message (skip if not present — guards against orphan UUIDs per H2), then fire-and-forget call the helper from bead `extract-diff-helper` and emit `getEventStore().emitOnly({ type: 'conversation.diff_summary_updated', ... })` with the resulting `TurnDiffSummary` fields. Pass `conversationName` into `getCachedMessages` (currently it only takes `sessionFile` + `isSpecialist`) — derive it from the route caller. Skip emit when `isSpecialist === true` (per H4) or when the conversation lookup fails. Maintain a per-`sessionFile` Map<assistantId, fileCount> in module scope to dedup across concurrent pollers (per H1) and clear it on cache eviction.
- AC: `getCachedMessages` accepts an optional `conversationName?: string` argument; when present and `isSpecialist === false`, the function performs the diff-detect-and-emit step after computing `result`
- AC: The `/api/conversations/:name/messages` route passes the conversation name into `getCachedMessages`; the `/api/conversations/:name/diffs` route and any other callers continue to work (compile + run)
- AC: Detect-and-emit logic is fire-and-forget (`void (async () => {...})()`) so the HTTP response is not delayed; the inner work uses `await` for git/exec calls and handles errors via try/catch + console.error (no unhandled rejections)
- AC: Per-`sessionFile` last-emit tracking dedupes events: the same assistantId with the same file count emits exactly once across multiple concurrent `/messages` polls (verified by an integration test that simulates two parallel calls)
- AC: Orphan tool_use UUIDs (those not present as keys in `result.messages.find(m => m.role === 'assistant')`) do NOT trigger an emit; once re-keying merges them into the real assistant message UUID, that real UUID DOES emit
- AC: Specialist sessions (`isSpecialist=true`) and sessions for which `getConversationByName` returns null do not emit (silent skip, no error)
- AC: Integration test in `src/dashboard/server/__tests__/`: append a tool_use entry for `Edit` to a fixture JSONL between two `getCachedMessages` calls; assert exactly one `conversation.diff_summary_updated` event is captured from `getEventStore()` with the correct `conversationName`, `assistantMessageId`, and `files[]`
- AC: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and `npm run build` all pass

title: pan-1164: Add `conversation.diff_summary_updated` event to contracts and reducer
description:
Wire the new domain event end-to-end in `packages/contracts/`. (1) In `events.ts`, define `ConversationDiffSummaryUpdatedEvent` next to the other `ConversationXxx` events with payload `{ conversationName, assistantMessageId, completedAt, files: TurnDiffFileChange[] }`. Mark in the JSDoc as in-memory-only (mirrors `ConversationCompactingChangedEvent`). Add it to the `DomainEvent` schema union. (2) In `event-reducers.ts`, extend `ReadModelState` with `conversationDiffSummariesByName: Record<string, TurnDiffSummary[]>` (default `{}`), update `initialReadModelState`, and add a reducer case that builds a `TurnDiffSummary` (using `turnId = \`conv-turn-${assistantMessageId}\`` to match the HTTP route's id scheme), deduplicates by `assistantMessageId` (replace-if-exists, append-otherwise), and trims via the existing `trimTurnDiffSummaries`. (3) Rebuild contracts: `cd packages/contracts && npm run build`. (4) Add reducer test mirroring the `agent.turn_diff_completed` test.

title: pan-1139: Vitest coverage for lockfile, build-fail-leaves-old-running, watchdog auto-restart, watchdog cap
description:
Add Vitest tests under `src/lib/__tests__/restart-lock.test.ts`, `src/cli/commands/__tests__/reload.test.ts`, and `src/supervisor/__tests__/watchdog.test.ts`. For the supervisor watchdog, factor the poll loop into a pure `watchdogTick(state, now, fetchHealth, spawnRestart, isLocked)` function so the test can drive it deterministically without real timers. For `pan reload`, mock `child_process.spawn` and the platform-lifecycle module so the test verifies: build-failure path does NOT call stopDashboard; build-success path does. For lockfile, real-filesystem tests in a tmpdir cover fresh-acquire, contention, stale-by-time (mock Date.now), stale-by-dead-PID (use a recycled PID expected to be dead).
- AC: `restart-lock.test.ts` covers: fresh acquire returns handle, second acquire returns null, stale lock (>5min) is overwritten, stale lock (dead PID) is overwritten, release is idempotent.
- AC: `reload.test.ts` covers: build-success path calls restartDashboard; build-failure path does NOT call stopDashboard/restartDashboard and exits non-zero.
- AC: `watchdog.test.ts` covers: 3 consecutive failures trigger a restart spawn; restart counter resets on health success; >3 attempts in 5min triggers gaveUp; lockfile-held cycle is skipped without consuming an attempt.
- AC: `npm test` passes; `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass.

title: pan-1139: Persist restart outcomes to `~/.panopticon/restart-status.json` and display in `pan status`
description:
Add `src/lib/restart-status.ts` exporting `writeRestartStatus(entry)` and `readRestartStatus(): RestartStatus | null`. The file at `${PANOPTICON_HOME}/restart-status.json` holds the most recent attempt: `{ts, trigger: 'pan reload' | 'pan restart' | 'watchdog', success, error?, durationMs, attempts}`. `pan reload`, `restartCommand()`, and the supervisor watchdog all call `writeRestartStatus` on each attempt (both success and failure). Update `src/cli/commands/status.ts` to render the most recent entry in its output (e.g., 'Last dashboard restart: ✓ ok (pan reload, 2.4s ago)' or '⚠ FAILED — watchdog gave up at 14:23:01: [dashboard] health check at http://127.0.0.1:3011/api/health did not pass within 15000ms'). On watchdog give-up, the status also surfaces `gaveUp: true` so the user sees the alarm in plain text.
- AC: Every successful or failed restart attempt (pan reload, pan restart --dashboard, watchdog) writes a JSON entry to `~/.panopticon/restart-status.json`.
- AC: `pan status` renders the most recent restart-status entry in its output, including trigger, success/failure, age, and error message on failure.
- AC: When the watchdog has given up (`gaveUp: true`), `pan status` shows a prominent ⚠ marker so the user notices without scrolling.
- AC: Status file is JSON-parseable, atomically written (write to .tmp then rename), and tolerates being missing (readRestartStatus returns null).
close_reason: Added restart-status persistence, wired pan reload/restart writes, rendered latest restart in pan status, and added targeted tests.

title: pan-1139: Active watchdog in supervisor: poll `/api/health`, auto-restart with cap
description:
Extend `src/supervisor/server.ts` with a background watchdog. On startup, read `PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_WATCHDOG` env (default '1'); if disabled, skip. Otherwise: every 10s (`PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_POLL_MS`), `fetch('http://127.0.0.1:${dashboardApiPort}/api/health', { signal: AbortSignal.timeout(2000) })`. Track `consecutiveFailures` (reset on any 2xx) and a sliding-window `restartAttempts[]` (timestamps in last 5 min). After 3 consecutive failures (`PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_FAIL_THRESHOLD`), check if `restartAttempts.length >= 3` (`PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_MAX_RESTARTS`) within 5 min (`PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_WINDOW_MS`) — if so, log loudly with 'WATCHDOG GIVING UP — manual intervention required' and stop attempting (don't restart at all until consecutiveFailures resets via a manual recovery). Otherwise: check the restart lockfile from the lockfile bead; if held, skip this cycle. If free: append timestamp to `restartAttempts`, spawn `pan restart --dashboard` exactly as the existing `/restart-dashboard` endpoint does, and log the trigger. Add a new `GET /status` endpoint returning `{healthy, lastCheck, consecutiveFailures, restartAttempts, gaveUp, lastError}` so the dashboard and `pan status` can read watchdog state. The dashboardApiPort is read from `readPlatformConfig()` so the supervisor doesn't need extra env wiring.
- AC: Supervisor polls `/api/health` every 10s by default (overridable via PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_POLL_MS).
- AC: After 3 consecutive failures, the supervisor spawns `pan restart --dashboard` and logs the trigger to `~/.panopticon/logs/supervisor.log`.
- AC: If 3 restart attempts have occurred within a 5-min rolling window, the watchdog logs 'WATCHDOG GIVING UP' and stops attempting until consecutiveFailures resets (i.e., a manual recovery brings the dashboard back).
- AC: If the restart lockfile is held by another caller (e.g., `pan reload` is in flight), the watchdog skips that cycle without consuming an attempt.
- AC: `PANOPTICON_SUPERVISOR_WATCHDOG=0` disables the watchdog loop at startup (the supervisor still serves /health, /restart-dashboard, /status).
- AC: `GET /status` returns `{healthy, lastCheck, consecutiveFailures, restartAttempts: [iso8601...], gaveUp, lastError}`. Endpoint is available even when the watchdog is disabled.
- AC: Manual smoke: kill the dashboard with `pkill -f dashboard/server.js`; within ~40s, the supervisor restarts it and `curl /api/health` returns 200 again. Confirm via supervisor.log.
close_reason: completed

title: pan-1139: Add `pan reload` CLI command (build-verified restart)
description:
Create `src/cli/commands/reload.ts` exporting `reloadCommand(options)`. Flow: (1) call `acquireRestartLock('pan reload')` — if null, print 'restart in progress (held by PID X)' and exit 2; (2) unless `--skip-build`, capture the pre-build mtime of `dist/dashboard/server.js` (or 0 if missing), spawn `npm run build` with stdio inherit so the user sees compiler output, await exit; if exit code != 0, print 'Build failed — old dashboard left running' and exit 1 WITHOUT calling stopDashboard; (3) verify `dist/dashboard/server.js` exists AND its mtime > pre-build mtime — abort with clear error if not; (4) call `restartDashboard(config, spawnDashboardDetached, { healthTimeoutMs })` (defaults to 30000); on StageError, print the staged error verbatim; (5) release the lock in a finally block. Register in `src/cli/index.ts` as `program.command('reload')` with options `--skip-build`, `--health-timeout <ms>`, `--no-deacon`. Update `skills/pan-reload/SKILL.md` to call `pan reload` (single command) and fix the stale port references (3001/3002 → 3010/3011). Verify `scripts/lint-skills.sh` still passes.
- AC: `pan reload` with a successful build rebuilds dist and brings up a healthy dashboard; on failure it prints the staged StageError and exits non-zero.
- AC: `pan reload` with a FAILING build (simulated by `EXIT=1 pan reload` or a deliberately broken file) does NOT kill the old dashboard, prints the build error, and exits 1. After the failure, `curl http://127.0.0.1:3011/api/health` still returns 200.
- AC: `pan reload --skip-build` skips the npm step and behaves like `pan restart --dashboard` (still uses the lock, still health-gates).
- AC: Lockfile is acquired before the build runs and released in a finally block (covers crash mid-build).
- AC: `skills/pan-reload/SKILL.md` is updated to call `pan reload` (single command) and references ports 3010/3011 (current truth), not 3001/3002 (stale).
- AC: `pan reload --help` exposes `--skip-build`, `--health-timeout <ms>`, `--no-deacon`. `scripts/lint-skills.sh` passes (skill mentions match CLI help).
close_reason: Added build-gated pan reload command, lock handling, CLI registration, and updated pan-reload skill; typecheck/help/skill lint pass.

title: pan-1139: Surface stuck port-holder PID + cmdline when SIGKILL fails to free port
description:
In `src/lib/platform-lifecycle.ts` `stopDashboard()`, after the SIGKILL phase, if any of the dashboard ports are still bound, fetch the holder PIDs (already done via `pidsOnPort`), then run `ps -p <pid> -o pid=,cmd=` for each, and throw a `StageError` whose reason includes the PID + cmdline. The current code silently returns after `SIGKILL`; if the kill genuinely failed (kernel uninterruptible sleep, zombie parent, etc.) the user has no idea what's holding the port.
- AC: When SIGKILL leaves a port still bound after the final 2s wait, `stopDashboard` throws `StageError` whose `failure.reason` includes 'port X still held by PID Y (cmd: Z)' for every stuck holder.
- AC: When the kill succeeds (the common case), behavior is unchanged — no extra exec, no log spam.
close_reason: stopDashboard now throws StageError with still-bound port PID/cmdline after SIGKILL; typecheck passes.

title: pan-1139: Replace `pan up`'s bare fuser-kill with `stopDashboard()` (SIGTERM→SIGKILL escalation)
description:
In `src/cli/index.ts` (the `program.command('up')` action, currently lines ~809-835), delete the bare `execSync('fuser -k -TERM ...')` calls and the `waitForPortToFree` loop, and replace with a single call to `await stopDashboard(config)` from `src/lib/platform-lifecycle.ts`. This adopts the same SIGTERM→wait→SIGKILL escalation `pan restart --dashboard` already uses, so a stubborn old process cannot leak past the kill window and cause EADDRINUSE on the new dashboard.
- AC: `pan up` calls `stopDashboard(config)` (with both dashboardPort and dashboardApiPort) before spawning the new server.
- AC: The bare `fuser -k -TERM` + bash /dev/tcp polling loop is removed from `src/cli/index.ts`.
- AC: Manual test: start a dummy process that binds 3010 and ignores SIGTERM (`trap '' TERM; nc -l 3010`); `pan up` escalates to SIGKILL within 5s and the new dashboard binds successfully.
close_reason: Replaced pan up bare fuser/dev-tcp cleanup with stopDashboard() lifecycle shutdown; typecheck passes.

title: pan-1139: Add restart lockfile primitive (acquire/release/stale-detection)
description:
Add a new module `src/lib/restart-lock.ts` exporting `acquireRestartLock(caller: string): { release(): void } | null` and `readRestartLockHolder(): { pid, ts, caller } | null`. Lockfile lives at `${PANOPTICON_HOME}/restart.lock` and contains JSON `{pid, ts, caller}`. Acquire writes the file atomically (open with O_EXCL); if the file exists, parse it — if PID is dead OR ts is older than 5 min, overwrite (stale); otherwise return `null`. Release deletes the file (idempotent; tolerates missing). No SIGCHLD trap needed — beads that use the lock are responsible for calling release(), and the next caller's stale-detection will recover from forgotten releases. This is the cross-cutting primitive the next three beads depend on.
- AC: `acquireRestartLock(caller)` returns a release handle on first call; returns null if a live lock is held by a different PID < 5 min old.
- AC: Stale-detection: if the lockfile's PID is dead (process.kill(pid, 0) throws) OR ts is > 5 min ago, acquireRestartLock overwrites and succeeds.
- AC: release() deletes the lockfile and is idempotent (calling twice does not throw).
- AC: Lockfile path is `${PANOPTICON_HOME}/restart.lock` (uses paths.ts PANOPTICON_HOME, not hard-coded), and parent dir is auto-created.
- AC: Vitest unit tests cover: fresh acquire, contention, stale-by-time, stale-by-dead-PID, idempotent release.
close_reason: Added restart lockfile primitive with stale detection, guarded idempotent release, and Vitest coverage.

title: pan-1141: Exponential backoff and threshold-based troubled flip
description:
After incrementing the counter in `recordAgentFailure()`, compute `lastFailureNextRetryAt = now + backoffSchedule[min(consecutiveFailures-1, schedule.length-1)] * 1000`. Default `backoffSchedule = [5, 30, 120]` (seconds). Track `firstFailureInRunAt`: set on the first failure of a run, cleared on counter reset. When `consecutiveFailures >= maxConsecutiveFailures` AND `(now - firstFailureInRunAt) <= troubledWindowMs`, call `markAgentTroubled()`. If the window has elapsed without hitting the threshold, reset `firstFailureInRunAt` to the current failure timestamp. Default thresholds: `maxConsecutiveFailures = 3`, `troubledWindowMs = 600000` (10 min). Add a skip clause in `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` that skips agents whose `lastFailureNextRetryAt > Date.now()` (inclusive equality means retry-now). Create `src/lib/cloister/auto-resume-config.ts` exporting `AutoResumeConfig` interface and `DEFAULT_AUTO_RESUME_CONFIG` for reuse by tests and config overrides.
- AC: After 1st failure, agent is skipped during the next 5 seconds and resumable after; after 2nd, 30s; after 3rd, 120s
- AC: 3 consecutive failures within 10 minutes (default config) flip `troubled = true` and stamp `troubledAt`
- AC: If failures span > 10 minutes (e.g. 1 failure now, 2 more 11 minutes later), no troubled-flip happens — `firstFailureInRunAt` slides to the failure that resets the window
- AC: `AutoResumeConfig` interface and defaults exported from `src/lib/cloister/auto-resume-config.ts`
- AC: Skipping during backoff logs `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents: <id> skipped — backoff active (next retry at <iso>)`
close_reason: Implemented default auto-resume backoff and troubled-state threshold

title: pan-1141: Increment and reset the consecutive-failure counter at cloister failure sites
description:
Call `recordAgentFailure(agentId, reason)` from two sites: (a) inside `autoResumeStoppedWorkAgents()` when `resumeAgent()` returns `{success:false}` or throws (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4906/4912); (b) inside `recoverOrphanedAgents()` when a tmux session is detected missing AND `stoppedByUser !== true` (src/lib/cloister/deacon.ts:4264 region — the orphan crash case). Call `resetAgentFailureCount(agentId)` from a new place: the post-spawn success path where the agent has reached `running` status. Do NOT reset on `resumeAgent` returning success alone — wait until status is `running` to avoid resetting when the spawn pipeline itself fails mid-way. Ensure the orphan-recovery + auto-resume call sequence in one patrol cycle increments exactly ONCE per detected crash.
- AC: Failed auto-resume increments `consecutiveFailures` by 1 and stamps `lastFailureAt` / `lastFailureReason`
- AC: Orphan-crash detection increments `consecutiveFailures` exactly once per detected crash (no double-count when orphan-recovery and auto-resume run in the same patrol cycle)

title: pan-1143: Implement issue
description:
## Symptom

`pan plan done <id>` (and the dashboard "Done" button) report **success** even when the canonical spec was never written and beads were never materialized. The user sees a green check, agent gets started, work agent has nothing to do (or has 1 placeholder bead instead of the planned 22).

Concrete reproduction (PAN-1141, 2026-05-16):
- Planning agent (auto mode) wrote `workspaces/feature-pan-1141/.pan/spec.vbrief.json` with 22 plan items
- No canonical spec created at `<projectRoot>/.pan/specs/`
- `pan plan done PAN-1141` returned: `✓ Planning complete for PAN-1141`
- After: still no canonical spec, beads still show only 1 generic `PAN-1141: Implement issue` placeholder
- Work agent spawned successfully and loaded "1 task"

## Root cause

`src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts:1044-1053`:

```ts
const { pushed: gitPushed, beadsWarning } = yield* Effect.promise(async () => {
  if (!projectPath) return { pushed: false, beadsWarning: null };

  const workspacePath = join(projectPath, 'workspaces', `feature-${issueLower}`);
  const workspacePlanPath = findPlan(workspacePath);   // ← returns null on first run

  let gitRoot = '';
  if (workspacePlanPath) gitRoot = workspacePath;

  if (!gitRoot) return { pushed: false, beadsWarning: null };  // ← bails silently
  // ... spec promotion + bead sync never runs
```

`findPlan(workspacePath)` resolves via `findSpecByIssue(projectRoot, issueId)` in `src/lib/vbrief/io.ts:44`, which scans **only** `<projectRoot>/.pan/specs/`. There is no workspace-draft fallback. So on the **first** finalize attempt — when the canonical spec by definition doesn't yet exist — `findPlan` returns null and the entire promotion-and-bead-sync block is skipped. The endpoint returns `{ success: true }` regardless.

Additionally: the catch on line 1117-1119 around `writeSpecForIssue` swallows any promotion error as "non-fatal" with only a `console.warn` — so even if the path executed, errors wouldn't surface to the caller.

## Why this is critical

The documented planning lifecycle is: workspace draft → `pan plan-finalize` → `pan plan done`. The first link is broken because **`pan plan finalize` also calls `findPlan(workspace)` and fails with "No workspace spec found"** for the same reason — it expects the canonical spec to already exist, which it can't on first run.

Net effect: the auto-plan path silently produces unusable artifacts. Bug-by-bug:
1. **First-run chicken-and-egg.** `findPlan` requires canonical spec; canonical spec requires successful finalize; finalize requires canonical spec.
2. **Silent success.** Both `pan plan finalize` ("✓ No workspace spec found" is `process.exit(1)`, fine) and `complete-planning` (returns 200 with `success: true`) fail to surface the missing-canonical case usefully to the operator.
3. **Work agent runs on stub beads.** `pan start` happily spawns an agent with 1 placeholder bead, leading to "agent has nothing to do" or "agent does ill-defined mega-task" failures downstream.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] **First-run path works.** When `complete-planning` runs and no canonical spec exists, it reads the workspace draft at `<workspace>/.pan/spec.vbrief.json`, validates it, and uses it as the source for `writeSpecForIssue(...)`. The canonical spec gets written, beads get materialized, the issue advances. No more silent bail at line 1053.
- [ ] **Same for `pan plan finalize`.** If invoked when canonical spec doesn't exist, it should accept the workspace draft as the source.
- [ ] **No more silent error swallow.** Remove or narrow the catch at line 1117-1119 — promotion failures must propagate to the HTTP response so the CLI fails loudly with the real error.
- [ ] **Work-agent start validates beads count > 1.** Or validates plan items match bead count. Refuses to spawn with the stub placeholder.
- [ ] **Planning role doc is accurate.** `roles/plan.md` step 6 references `pan plan-finalize` (hyphenated, doesn't exist as a separate command). Align doc with actual CLI: `pan plan finalize`.
- [ ] **Test coverage.** Integration test for the first-run path: create a workspace draft, call complete-planning, assert canonical spec written + N beads created.

## Out of scope

- Reworking the four-artifact model in `docs/VBRIEF.md` — the model is fine; the implementation diverges from it.
- Adding new planning modes.

## Why this matters operationally

I just spent ~20 minutes of context tracing why PAN-1141 had 1 bead instead of 22. A first-time user would hit this on their first non-trivial plan, get an unusable workspace, and have no idea why. This is the kind of bug that destroys trust in the planning pipeline.

## Related

- PAN-1124 (single-spec-on-main model) — the architecture that introduced this. The model is sound; the first-run case wasn't wired up.
- PAN-1140 (workspace init container fix) — concurrent in-flight work, no direct overlap.

## Workaround until fixed

Manually re-run the planning agent for the affected issue. If it fails the second time the same way, that's a deterministic bug — file a comment with the agent's transcript.
- AC: **First-run path works.** When complete-planning runs and no canonical spec exists, it reads the workspace draft at <workspace>/.pan/spec.vbrief.json, validates it, and uses it as the source for writeSpecForIssue(...). The canonical spec gets written, beads get materialized, the issue advances. No more silent bail at line 1053.
- AC: **Same for pan plan finalize.** If invoked when canonical spec doesn't exist, it should accept the workspace draft as the source.
- AC: **No more silent error swallow.** Remove or narrow the catch at line 1117-1119 — promotion failures must propagate to the HTTP response so the CLI fails loudly with the real error.
- AC: **Work-agent start validates beads count > 1.** Or validates plan items match bead count. Refuses to spawn with the stub placeholder.
- AC: **Planning role doc is accurate.** roles/plan.md step 6 references pan plan-finalize (hyphenated, doesn't exist as a separate command). Align doc with actual CLI: pan plan finalize.
- AC: **Test coverage.** Integration test for the first-run path: create a workspace draft, call complete-planning, assert canonical spec written + N beads created.
close_reason: Implemented first-run vBRIEF draft promotion, loud completion failures, bead-count start validation, planning command docs, and regression coverage.

title: PAN-1141: Implement issue
pan-1140: Gate agent spawn on workspace docker stack health; add --host break-glass flag
description:
In `src/lib/agents.ts` `spawnAgent` AND `spawnRun`, before creating the tmux session: if the project for `options.issueId` has `workspace.docker.compose_template` configured and `options.allowHost !== true`, call `getWorkspaceStackHealth(issueId)`. If unhealthy, throw a clear error: `Workspace docker stack for ${issueId} is not healthy: ${reasons.join('; ')}. Run 'pan workspace rebuild ${issueId}' or retry with --host to override.` Add `--host` flag to the relevant CLI commands (`pan start`, `pan work`, and any direct spawn entrypoints) that maps to `allowHost: true` AND requires interactive confirmation (`Are you sure? This bypasses workspace isolation. (y/N)`); CI/non-TTY contexts also require `--yes`. Emit a `agent-spawn-blocked-stack-unhealthy` activity event when the gate blocks. Audit every call site that constructs an agent process (grep for `spawnAgent`, `spawnRun`, `createSessionAsync(.*agent-`) and document the audit result in a new `docs/SPAWN-GATING.md` note. Add a vitest case asserting that `spawnAgent` rejects when the stack is broken and the project has docker configured.
- AC: `spawnAgent` and `spawnRun` reject (throw) when the project has compose_template configured AND `getWorkspaceStackHealth` returns unhealthy AND `allowHost` is not true.
- AC: The rejection message names the issue ID, lists the unhealthy reasons, and tells the operator the exact next step (`pan workspace rebuild <id>` or `--host`).
- AC: `--host` flag is wired through every CLI surface that can spawn an agent (`pan start`, `pan work` if present, dashboard spawn endpoints). Interactive use requires y/N confirmation; non-TTY requires explicit `--yes`.
- AC: Projects WITHOUT a `workspace.docker.compose_template` keep current behavior — no gate, no flag required, no regression in spawn latency.
- AC: Audit doc (`docs/SPAWN-GATING.md`) lists every spawn entrypoint reachable today and states whether it goes through the gate, with file:line references.
- AC: Vitest unit test asserts both branches: (a) stack unhealthy + docker project → spawn rejected; (b) stack unhealthy + `allowHost: true` → spawn proceeds with a logged warning.
- AC: Activity stream emits `agent-spawn-blocked-stack-unhealthy` (or equivalent) when the gate blocks, observable in the dashboard activity feed.
- AC: No code path inside `src/lib/agents.ts` calls `createSessionAsync(agentId, workspace, ...)` for a work/role agent without first either passing the gate or honoring `allowHost`; restartAgent and session-rotation paths are included in the audit.
close_reason: Implemented spawn gating on cached workspace stack health, wired --host/--yes break-glass through CLI and dashboard paths, documented spawn entrypoint audit, added focused coverage, and passed deep inspection.

Default behavior is dry-run; `--apply` actually runs `docker compose down -v --remove-orphans` against the matching stack. Skip any stack whose `issueId` has an active agent state file in `~/.panopticon/agents/`. Update the `pan-workspace` skill (or sub-wrapper) so `scripts/lint-skills.sh` continues to pass.

- AC: `pan workspace reap --help` is self-documenting and `scripts/lint-skills.sh` passes (skill ↔ CLI parity preserved).

If it does NOT exist, add a minimal command that: tears down the stack (`docker compose down -v --remove-orphans`), re-runs `ensureDevcontainer`, then `docker compose up -d --build` with the same compose file. Inputs: presence of `workspace.docker.compose_template` for the project, current docker-stats snapshot, and a stuck-Created threshold (default 120s). Outputs: healthy=false when init exited non-zero OR services are in Created/Exited state. Surface in (a) `pan status` text output (red line per broken workspace), (b) dashboard workspace card via a new field on the workspace snapshot RPC, and (c) activity stream as a single emit when health transitions from healthy → unhealthy.

title: pan-1111: Simplify createBeadsFromVBrief recovery: on probe failure, run `bd doctor --fix` once then retry
description:
Rewrite the catch block in createBeadsFromVBrief (currently src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts:134-192). New shape: on `bd ping` failure, log a one-line diagnostic, invoke `bd doctor --fix` (timeout ~30s, cwd workspacePath, errors swallowed but logged), then re-attempt `bd ping --json`. If the retry succeeds, fall through and proceed with bead creation. If the retry still fails, return `{ success: false, created: [], errors: [<one-line>], beadIds }`. DELETE: the `staleArtifacts` array, the `presentStale` filter, the dolt-residue diagnostic, and the redirect-exists vs. main-beads-exists branch logic. KEEP: the fresh-install fallback — if neither a `.beads/redirect` nor a project-root `.beads/` exists, run `bd init --prefix <derived>` followed by `git config beads.role contributor` and `bd config set export.git-add false`, with identical argv to today. Add a 3-5 line comment above the catch block explaining why the heuristic is gone (refs beads v1.0.3 dolt auto-recovery + v1.0.4 perms repair). The work.inspect gate runs here because every subsequent bead (tests, integration test) builds on this exact control flow — a wrong rewrite cascades.
- AC: On `bd ping` failure, the code invokes `bd doctor --fix` exactly once before retrying the probe
- AC: The stale-artifact heuristic (staleArtifacts array, presentStale check, dolt-residue diagnostic) is deleted from src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts
- AC: The redirect-vs-main-beads branch logic in the catch block is collapsed into a single retry/error-return path
- AC: The fresh-install bd-init fallback (no redirect, no main .beads/) is preserved with identical argv and follow-on `git config` / `bd config set` calls
- AC: A code comment near createBeadsFromVBrief explains why the heuristic was removed (refs beads v1.0.3 dolt auto-recovery)
- AC: `npm run typecheck` and `npm run lint` pass
close_reason: Simplified recovery to bd doctor --fix plus ping retry, removed stale-artifact heuristic, preserved fresh init fallback, typecheck/lint passed

title: pan-1052: Idempotent rollup commit: archive previous, write new, then clear pending
description:
Rollup commit sequence: (1) archive previous status to archive/YYYY-MM-DD_<slug>.json, (2) write new status.json atomically, (3) emit MemoryStatusUpdatedEvent, (4) clear pending/ entries that were included in this rollup. Any failure before step 4 leaves pending intact so the next observation re-fires the rollup. Keep last 3 archived statuses; older are pruned.
- AC: Failure between step 1 and step 4 leaves pending intact (re-rollup is safe)
- AC: archive/ retains last 3 statuses; older statuses pruned
- AC: MemoryStatusUpdatedEvent emitted only after status.json commits
close_reason: Verified rollup commit archives previous status, writes new status atomically, emits status-updated only after write, clears included pending turns after commit, preserves pending on failure, prunes archives to latest 3, and focused tests plus typecheck and lint pass.

title: pan-1052: Reconciliation sweep on dashboard start and heartbeat-death

On dashboard startup, walk all sessions with checkpoints whose lifecycle is stale, replay unprocessed deltas via the pipeline. Wire into existing heartbeat-death detection so dead sessions get a final extraction pass before the registry removes them. Idempotent via claimRange.

title: pan-1052: Central transcript poller (registry-driven, mtime fast-path, rate-limited)
description:
src/lib/memory/poller.ts: single setInterval(2000) walking an in-memory registry of {sessionId, transcriptPath, identity, harness, lastSize, lastMtime}. For each entry: fs.stat first; if unchanged, skip. If grown, tail-read [lastOffset, currentSize], count newlines, fire pipeline only if activity threshold (20 lines since last extraction) AND mid-turn rate limit (max 1/60s, max 3/turn) allow. Registry is lifecycle-gated: only running/working agents in hybrid or poll mode are registered.
- AC: Single setInterval(2000); empty registry means no syscalls, no reads, no LLM calls
- AC: fs.stat short-circuit prevents reads on idle transcripts
- AC: Mid-turn rate limit enforced: max 1 extraction/60s/session, max 3/turn (turn boundary marked by Stop-hook claim)
- AC: Registry registration tied to agent lifecycle state; subagents never registered; completed/dead agents removed
- AC: Concurrent fire with Stop hook: claimRange ensures exactly one wins; loser noops
close_reason: Verified central transcript poller uses one idempotent 2s interval, does no syscalls for empty/disabled registries, uses stat mtime/size fast-path, accumulates line activity before enqueueing poller deltas, enforces 60s and 3-per-turn mid-turn limits, syncs lifecycle-gated active work-agent transcripts while excluding inactive/non-Claude/subagent sessions, and relies on claimRange for hook/poller idempotency; focused tests plus typecheck and lint pass.

title: pan-1052: Re-ranker: recency decay + tag boost + high-signal exemption

On provider error or timeout, try the configured fallback provider once before dropping. Never block the agent.

Verification passed: npm test -- tests/lib/memory/providers.test.ts tests/lib/memory/provider-policy.test.ts, npm run typecheck, and npx eslint src/lib/memory/**/*.ts tests/lib/memory/**/*.ts.

title: pan-1052: Turn compression: JSONL delta -> compact U/A/Created/Updated text
description:
src/lib/memory/compress.ts: parse a byte range of a Claude Code JSONL transcript into the compressed format `U: <user>
A: <assistant>
Created: f1, f2
Updated: f3
Subagents: ...
MCPs: ...
Skills: ...`. Categorize tool calls (Write -> Created, Edit -> Updated). Truncate user content to 40,000 chars. Handle partial trailing lines via offset bookkeeping.
- AC: Reads only the [fromOffset, toOffset] byte range; never the whole file

title: pan-1052: transcript_checkpoints table and atomic claimRange()
description:
Add transcript_checkpoints (session_id PK, project_id, workspace_id, issue_id, transcript_path, last_offset, last_observation_at, last_mid_turn_at, mid_turn_count_in_current_turn). Implement claimRange(sessionId, expectedFromOffset, toOffset): atomically advances last_offset and returns the claimed byte range or empty if a concurrent caller already advanced past expectedFromOffset. Use SQLite UPDATE ... WHERE last_offset = expectedFromOffset RETURNING. This is the race-safety lynchpin between hook and poller.
- AC: claimRange is atomic — concurrent callers with overlapping ranges produce exactly one winner; loser sees empty range
- AC: Concurrency test fires 50 simultaneous claimRange calls against the same session and asserts no duplicate ranges, no gaps
- AC: Mid-turn rate-limit fields (last_mid_turn_at, mid_turn_count_in_current_turn) included; reset on each Stop-hook claim
- AC: No long-lived locks held across LLM calls
close_reason: Verified transcript_checkpoints schema and atomic claimTranscriptRange with UPDATE ... WHERE last_offset = expectedFromOffset RETURNING, 50-way overlapping concurrency coverage, adjacent range no-duplicate/no-gap behavior, mid-turn poller counters, Stop-hook reset fields, and pre-LLM claim ordering through pipeline/poller tests.

title: pan-1107: Implement issue
description:
## Symptom

\`agent-pan-457-ship\` ran for 23m and accumulated \$4.95 in gpt-5.5 spend resolving 19 sync-main merge conflicts on PAN-457 (\`feature/pan-457\`).

That's an order of magnitude higher than the expected ship-role cost. Ship is meant to be mechanical conflict resolution + verification on a cheap workhorse model; previous ship runs typically cost a few cents.

Status line at completion:
\`\`\`
gpt-5.5 (gpt-5.5)  ctx 63%  189/200.0k  out 260  cost \$4.9465  +2/-1
\`\`\`

## Contributing factors (suspected)

- 19 conflicts is on the high end. A few of them involved \`.beads/config.yaml\` (with conflict markers inside YAML) and \`.pan/spec.vbrief.json\`, which the ship agent had to repeatedly re-scan.
- One tool call referenced a stale path (\`src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/MissionControl/styles/mission-control.module.css\`) that no longer exists. That suggests the agent was using cached state from training or earlier turns rather than reading the actual tree.
- Verification ran the full \`npm test\` (329 files) which is appropriate but pulls heavy context.
- Ship apparently ran through several rounds of \"stage → verify → fix\" loops based on output budget (out=260).

## Why it matters

Sync-main is invoked automatically on every \`pan start\` for an existing workspace. If sync-main routinely produces 19-conflict merges that cost \$5 in ship time, the per-restart cost adds up fast.

## Acceptance

- Capture per-attempt cost in the ship role audit log so spikes are visible from the dashboard
- Investigate whether the agent's repeated re-scans of conflict files can be short-circuited (e.g., feed it the conflict-marker locations directly instead of re-reading every file)
- Decide whether ship should bail out and ask for human help past a certain cost/conflict-count threshold rather than burn through a workhorse budget
- AC: 19 conflicts is on the high end. A few of them involved \.beads/config.yaml\ (with conflict markers inside YAML) and \.pan/spec.vbrief.json\, which the ship agent had to repeatedly re-scan.
- AC: One tool call referenced a stale path (\src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/MissionControl/styles/mission-control.module.css\) that no longer exists. That suggests the agent was using cached state from training or earlier turns rather than reading the actual tree.
- AC: Verification ran the full \npm test\ (329 files) which is appropriate but pulls heavy context.
- AC: Ship apparently ran through several rounds of \"stage → verify → fix\" loops based on output budget (out=260).
- AC: Capture per-attempt cost in the ship role audit log so spikes are visible from the dashboard
- AC: Investigate whether the agent's repeated re-scans of conflict files can be short-circuited (e.g., feed it the conflict-marker locations directly instead of re-reading every file)
- AC: Decide whether ship should bail out and ask for human help past a certain cost/conflict-count threshold rather than burn through a workhorse budget
close_reason: Implemented fix: added ship role cost capture in specialists/done, updated ship prompts with short-circuit rule and bail-out thresholds (/10 files), updated ship.md completion to call pan admin specialists done

title: pan-1106: Implement issue
description:
## Symptom

In the Command Deck issue tree, PAN-457 row expands to:

\`\`\`
PAN-457  Archived Conversations page — …
  Review (gpt-5.5)               error
    Correctness (gpt-5.5)        stopped
    Security (gpt-5.5)           stopped
    Performance (gpt-5.5)        stopped
    Requirements (gpt-5.5)       stopped
  RESOURCES  1 PR  ·  2 branches
\`\`\`

The \`agent-pan-457-ship\` and \`agent-pan-457\` (work) sessions are running for the same issue at the same time, but they don't appear under the PAN-457 node in the tree. They show up in the flat Conversations list in the sidebar instead.

## Expected

Per \`docs/ROLES.md\`, every issue has up to five roles: plan, work, review, test, ship. The issue-tree should render whichever roles exist for that issue (with their model and live status), not just review and its sub-roles.

## Why it matters

If you don't see the work/ship/test/plan agents under their issue, you can't tell at a glance:
- Whether the issue has a work agent at all (\"why hasn't anything happened on PAN-457?\")
- What model each role is running on
- Whether ship/test passed
- Where to click to attach to the right session

## Suspected cause

The tree-row renderer probably hardcodes review as the only role that gets a sub-row, instead of iterating over the roles defined for the issue. Likely in \`src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/\`.

## Acceptance

- PAN-457 row in the issue tree shows: work, ship, review (with its sub-roles), test, plan — for whichever are present
- Each role row shows its model, status, and (where applicable) cost
- Clicking a role row opens the right tmux session / conversation
- AC: Whether the issue has a work agent at all (\"why hasn't anything happened on PAN-457?\")
- AC: What model each role is running on
- AC: Whether ship/test passed
- AC: Where to click to attach to the right session
- AC: PAN-457 row in the issue tree shows: work, ship, review (with its sub-roles), test, plan — for whichever are present
- AC: Each role row shows its model, status, and (where applicable) cost
- AC: Clicking a role row opens the right tmux session / conversation
close_reason: Fixed collectSessionTreeNodes to discover ship (merge) and test sessions so they appear under their issue node in the Command Deck issue tree instead of only in the flat Conversations list.

title: pan-1105: Implement issue
description:
## Repro

PAN-457 review run \`agent-pan-457-review-5c799040\` (2026-05-12 ~17:10Z): all four convoy reviewers (security, correctness, performance, requirements) exited before producing a non-empty report. A retry was attempted for each — also exited without writing the required output file.

Synthesis at \`.pan/review/agent-pan-457-review-5c799040/synthesis.md\`:

> ### [security] Reviewer failed to write report — security.md
> The security reviewer session exited before producing a non-empty report. A retry was attempted and also exited without producing the required output file.

(Same line for correctness/performance/requirements.)

## Why it matters

When all four convoy reviewers fail this way, the parent review role marks PAN-XXX as \`review: blocked\` and the work agent cannot auto-respawn. The user has to \`pan reopen\` to unstick. Two PRs hit this back-to-back today (PAN-457, possibly PAN-1069 — the conversations sidebar lists \`agent-pan-1069-review-*\` sessions from earlier).

## Likely cause

Convoy reviewer specialists spawn via Claude Code agents but exit without producing output. Candidates:
- Specialist agent prompt blows past context budget before writing the report
- Specialist agent crashes on first tool call (broken MCP, missing skill, etc.)
- The required-output-file path is wrong or unwriteable
- Specialist is gated on something stale from the workspace state (e.g. \`.beads/\` corruption — but PAN-457 had this resolved by the time review ran)

## Acceptance

- Identify which of the above causes the silent exit
- Surface the underlying error in the synthesis instead of \"exited before producing a non-empty report\"
- Optionally: gate the retry on a different failure mode than \"file missing\" so a second silent exit doesn't look like a fresh failure
- AC: Specialist agent prompt blows past context budget before writing the report
- AC: Specialist agent crashes on first tool call (broken MCP, missing skill, etc.)
- AC: The required-output-file path is wrong or unwriteable
- AC: Specialist is gated on something stale from the workspace state (e.g. \.beads/\ corruption — but PAN-457 had this resolved by the time review ran)
- AC: Identify which of the above causes the silent exit
- AC: Surface the underlying error in the synthesis instead of \"exited before producing a non-empty report\"
- AC: Optionally: gate the retry on a different failure mode than \"file missing\" so a second silent exit doesn't look like a fresh failure
close_reason: Added monitorSynthesisAgent background function that detects silent synthesis exits before the 30-minute deacon threshold and surfaces actual error in reviewNotes. Fixes root cause: review-agent.ts had no detection when synthesis agent died without writing output.

title: pan-1104: Implement issue
description:
## Repro

\`pan reopen pan-457\` (panopticon-cli GitHub issue) printed:

\`\`\`
✔ Issue MIN-848 moved to In Progress
✔ Added reopen comment
✓ MIN-848 reopened and ready for re-work
\`\`\`

PAN-457 lives on GitHub (eltmon/panopticon-cli). MIN-848 is a Mind Your Now Linear issue in a different project. The reopen command updated the wrong tracker.

## Expected

\`pan reopen pan-XXX\` should resolve via the same path as \`pan start\` (which correctly logged \`Transitioned pan-457 to in_progress via GitHub (eltmon/panopticon-cli)\` immediately after).

## Cause (suspected)

\`pan reopen\` is using a different project/issue resolver than \`pan start\`. Likely \`src/cli/commands/reopen.ts\` (or wherever the reopen command lives) — should call \`resolveProjectFromIssue()\` from \`src/lib/projects.ts\` like the rest of the pipeline.

## Acceptance

- \`pan reopen pan-457\` updates the GitHub issue \#457 in eltmon/panopticon-cli, not MIN-848 in Linear
- Workspace state reset side effects (review/test/merge → pending, continue breadcrumb) continue to work
- Add a unit test that asserts the resolver matches \`resolveProjectFromIssue\` for both PAN- and MIN- prefixes
- AC: \pan reopen pan-457\ updates the GitHub issue \#457 in eltmon/panopticon-cli, not MIN-848 in Linear
- AC: Workspace state reset side effects (review/test/merge → pending, continue breadcrumb) continue to work
- AC: Add a unit test that asserts the resolver matches \resolveProjectFromIssue\ for both PAN- and MIN- prefixes
close_reason: Fixed pan reopen to use resolveTrackerType() for correct GitHub/Linear routing. For GitHub issues (PAN-XXX): fetches via Octokit, reopens with state:open and adds in-progress label, then adds a reopen comment. For Linear issues: keeps existing LinearClient SDK behavior. Workspace state reset via reopenWorkspaceState continues to work since it already uses resolveProjectFromIssue internally.

title: pan-1069: Implement issue
description:
## Summary

Add a minimal HTTP route to the Panopticon dashboard server that returns a simple HTML page when accessed at \`/hello\`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- \`GET /hello\` returns HTTP 200
- Response body is exactly: \`<html><body>Hello</body></html>\`
- \`Content-Type\` header is \`text/html\`
- Route is registered in the main Effect HTTP router alongside existing routes

## Why

Small smoke-test route used to verify the full Panopticon review/work pipeline end-to-end without domain complexity getting in the way.

On dcg crash/timeout, POST CommandGuardError and exit 0 (fail-open). The agentId is in env (PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID, already exported by the launcher).

title: pan-608: Install dcg as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook at agent spawn (work role only)
description:
Extend spawnAgent() in src/lib/agents.ts (and equivalent role-spawn sites for plan/review/test/ship) so that when safety.dcg.enabled=true and harness=claude-code: (1) write generated TOML to <workspace>/.claude/dcg/config.toml, (2) write a stable-named hook script .claude/hooks/dcg-pretool.sh that invokes dcg with that config, (3) merge the hook into .claude/settings.local.json's `hooks.PreToolUse` block idempotently (same merge pattern as injectPanopticonInfraDeny), (4) for non-Claude-Code harnesses or when disabled, do nothing. End-to-end smoke test: spawn a Claude Code session under bypassPermissions, attempt `rm -rf /tmp/dcg-test-sentinel`, assert dcg blocks AND a CommandBlocked event is emitted. If the smoke test reveals hooks don't run under bypassPermissions, STOP and escalate — do not silently ship a no-op.
- AC: On spawn with safety.dcg.enabled=true + Claude Code harness, .claude/dcg/config.toml exists and matches the role-appropriate generator output
- AC: .claude/hooks/dcg-pretool.sh exists, is executable (0o755), and invokes the configured dcg binary with the workspace config
- AC: .claude/settings.local.json hooks.PreToolUse contains a dcg entry; existing permissions.deny, hooks (channels confirmation), and env blocks are preserved
- AC: End-to-end smoke test passes: bypassPermissions Claude session + `rm -rf /tmp/dcg-test-sentinel` is blocked and emits CommandBlocked
- AC: When safety.dcg.enabled=false OR harness != claude-code, none of the above files are written; on non-Claude-Code spawn with enabled=true, a CommandGuardSkipped event is emitted
- AC: Re-running spawn (workspace already has the files) is idempotent — files match generator output, no orphaned merge artifacts

title: pan-1048: Reactive Cloister: onIssueStateChange + stateToRole hooked into existing emitters
description:
Add `onIssueStateChange(issueId, newState)` and `stateToRole(state)` to src/lib/cloister/service.ts. Hook into existing internal event sources rather than building a new poller: (a) issue-lifecycle service (src/dashboard/server/services/issue-lifecycle.ts) already emits `issue.*` events when state transitions occur — Cloister subscribes via the dashboard event bus / domain-services. (b) When a work agent emits `agent.completed` (or signals via `pan done`), Cloister transitions the issue state to 'in_review' and onIssueStateChange spawns the review role. (c) Review approval emits a `review.approved` event → Cloister transitions to 'testing' → spawns test. (d) Test pass emits `test.passed` → transition to 'shipping' → spawn ship. Concurrency guard: skip spawn if an active run already exists for the (issue, role) tuple. stateToRole maps: in_planning → 'plan', in_progress → 'work', in_review → 'review', testing → 'test', shipping → 'ship'. requiresInspection=true because this is the cross-cutting protocol — C2 deletes the old dispatch machinery on the assumption this works.
- AC: src/lib/cloister/service.ts exports `onIssueStateChange(issueId, newState)` and `stateToRole(state): Role | null`
- AC: Cloister subscribes to existing issue.* domain events (issue-lifecycle.ts) and existing agent-completion events (agent.completed, work.completed, review.approved, test.passed)
- AC: stateToRole maps in_planning→plan, in_progress→work, in_review→review, testing→test, shipping→ship; closed/canceled→null
- AC: Concurrency guard: if an active run exists for (issueId, role), the second spawn is skipped (and logged)
- AC: End-to-end test: simulating a work-agent completion event drives the issue to in_review and spawns a review run; review approval drives to testing; test pass drives to shipping; etc.

Defaults are applied in-memory; the on-disk config.yaml is only written when the user edits a value via Settings (no surprise rewrites). Add a vitest covering: missing workhorses → seeded; missing roles → seeded; partial roles (only `work` set) → other roles seeded; user-defined values not overwritten.

- AC: Missing roles section → all 5 roles + sub-roles seeded with documented defaults (plan/review = workhorse:expensive; work/test/ship = workhorse:mid; inspect = workhorse:cheap)

title: pan-1048: Create roles/ship.md with explicit human-merge invariant
description:
Create `roles/ship.md`. Migrate body from .claude/agents/pan-merge-agent.md, but make the human-merge invariant explicit and enforced: ship NEVER calls any merge endpoint (no `gh pr merge`, no `/api/.../merge` POST). Ship's responsibilities: (1) rebase the feature branch onto main, (2) resolve conflicts, (3) re-run verification gate (typecheck/lint/test), (4) push the branch, (5) transition the issue to a 'ready-to-merge' state, (6) exit. The dashboard's existing Merge button is the only path to actual merge. requiresInspection=true because ship is a trust-gate surface — a regression that lets ship auto-merge would be hard to detect after the fact.
- AC: File exists at roles/ship.md (frontmatter: name: ship) covering rebase/conflict-resolve/re-verify/push

Answer in role body: subagent_type alone determines model via resolveModel('work', 'inspect') / resolveModel('work', 'inspect-deep').

title: pan-1048: Foundation: Role type, workhorses/roles config schema, resolveModel, derefWorkhorse
description:
Add the foundation everything else depends on. In src/lib/agents.ts: export `Role = 'plan' | 'work' | 'review' | 'test' | 'ship'`. In src/lib/config-yaml.ts: extend NormalizedConfig with optional `workhorses: { expensive, mid, cheap }` (each a literal model id) and `roles: Record<Role, RoleConfig>` where RoleConfig = `{ model: ModelRef, harness?: 'claude-code' | 'pi', sub?: Record<string, { model: ModelRef }> }` and `ModelRef = string` (literal model id OR `workhorse:<slot>`). Implement `derefWorkhorse(ref, config)` that returns a literal model id or throws. Implement `resolveModel(role, subRole?, config)` that walks role.sub[subRole].model || role.model || DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS[role] and dereferences. Validate at config-load time: every workhorse:<slot> reference must resolve, no chained references (workhorses.cheap: workhorse:mid is rejected with a precise field-pointer error like `config.yaml: workhorses.cheap cannot reference another workhorse`). Tests: round-trip parsing, every role resolves, unresolved refs throw with field path, chained refs throw, sub-role resolution overrides role-level, literal ids pass through unchanged. The schema is the primitive every other bead consumes — get the validation surface right here so downstream work doesn't have to guess.
- AC: `Role` type exported from src/lib/agents.ts with exactly the 5 values
- AC: NormalizedConfig in src/lib/config-yaml.ts has `workhorses` and `roles` fields with the documented shape; both optional in TypeScript (defaults applied by F3)
- AC: `derefWorkhorse(ref, config)` returns the literal model id when ref starts with `workhorse:`, returns ref unchanged otherwise, throws when slot is undefined or chained
- AC: `resolveModel(role, subRole?, config)` returns a literal model id, walking sub-role override → role override → DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS[role]
- AC: Config-load-time validation rejects unresolved `workhorse:*` references with an error message naming the offending field (e.g., `roles.review.sub.security.model`)
- AC: Chained workhorse references (e.g., `workhorses.cheap: workhorse:mid`) are rejected at parse with a clear error
- AC: Vitest covers: 5 roles × literal/workhorse-ref resolution, sub-role override, chained-ref rejection, missing-slot rejection, config round-trip; all pass

title: pan-1053: Implement src/autopreso/session.ts — staging/live state machine + warmup loop
description:
Create src/autopreso/session.ts. Export createWhiteboardSession(). Manages staging (client scratchpad) and live (server owns state.elements) modes. start() snapshots staging primer, extracts keywords, transitions to live, fires runWhiteboardWarmupOnce(). Warmup: up to 8 attempts, exponential backoff (2s→4s→8s→…→30s cap). After warmup, forces agentHistory to [warmup_user_msg, {role:'assistant',content:'UNDERSTOOD'}]. Exposes canvasDirtyForAgent flag. THIS BEAD requires inspection because the warmup loop and agentHistory prefix are the caching foundation — every subsequent agent turn depends on this exact history.
- AC: createWhiteboardSession() returns { mode, elements, start, backToStaging, reset, agentHistory, canvasDirtyForAgent }
- AC: start() transitions mode staging→live and fires warmup loop
- AC: Warmup retries up to 8 times with exponential backoff, capping at 30s per attempt
- AC: After warmup completes, agentHistory is exactly [warmup_user_msg, { role:'assistant', content:'UNDERSTOOD' }]
close_reason: Implemented createWhiteboardSession warmup loop, agentHistory prefix, direct state exposure, and canvasDirtyForAgent handling.
