title: PAN-1031: Harden bead-close hook parsing for multiline commands
description: The inspect-on-bead-close hook can see multiline Bash tool inputs. When any later line contains bd close, the current line-oriented sed extraction leaves unrelated earlier command lines in the candidate argument list, causing false bead IDs such as 'install' to be inspected. Parse the Bash command as a single stream and extract only tokens after the actual bd close invocation so hook installation or heredoc commands cannot dispatch bogus inspections.
acceptance_criteria: A multiline Bash command that includes setup/install work before a bd close line dispatches inspection for the real bead ID only; commands without a valid bd close invocation dispatch nothing; focused hook tests cover the multiline regression while existing local-CLI and pan fallback behavior stays intact.
close_reason: Multiline hook parsing hardened and verified

title: PAN-1031: Pin inspect specialist to sentinel-safe runtime
description: Inspect specialists routed to MiniMax through claudish repeatedly exited without any assistant message or INSPECTION PASSED/BLOCKED sentinel, leaving the bead workflow stuck. Inspect is a control-plane gate whose sentinel output is parsed by Cloister, so dispatch must use a runtime that can reliably run the Claude Code agent definition and emit literal sentinels. Force inspect-agent dispatch away from non-direct provider wrappers and document/test the routing behavior.
acceptance_criteria: When work-type routing resolves specialist-inspect-agent to a non-direct provider model, spawnEphemeralSpecialist falls back to the Anthropic direct inspect runtime before launching; registry resolution trace makes the fallback visible; tests cover inspect fallback without changing other specialist routing.
close_reason: Direct inspect runtime fallback implemented and verified

title: PAN-1031: Keep bead-close inspect hook workspace-local
description: The per-agent bead-close hook still invokes the global pan binary from ~/.panopticon/bin/inspect-on-bead-close. In a feature workspace this can dispatch stale main-built code and fail before inspection, as happened when main lacked the dist/cli/prompts inspect prompt asset. Fix the hook source and installer so bead-close inspections prefer the current workspace's built CLI when available and the hook script is installed by setup even though it is no longer registered globally.
acceptance_criteria: scripts/inspect-on-bead-close prefers node ./dist/cli/index.js when run from a workspace with that built CLI and falls back to pan otherwise; setup installs inspect-on-bead-close into ~/.panopticon/bin for per-agent frontmatter hooks; automated coverage or a shellcheck-style test verifies the local CLI selection behavior without invoking a destructive request.
close_reason: Bead-close hook now prefers workspace-local built CLI, setup installs the per-agent hook script, and tests cover local/fallback dispatch.

title: PAN-1031: Stabilize inspect specialist prompt context
description: pan inspect spawned the inspect specialist but the prompt context was unusable: bead details were missing because bd show --json returns an array with acceptance_criteria, and the specialist looped over a huge repeated prompt without delivering INSPECTION PASSED or INSPECTION BLOCKED. Fix the inspect dispatch context so per-bead inspections receive concrete bead requirements and can terminate with a sentinel result.
acceptance_criteria: bd show --json array output is parsed into title/description/acceptance_criteria/labels in the inspect prompt; inspect specialist dispatch has automated coverage for the bead-description parsing shape; the fix does not manually approve or bypass inspection.
close_reason: Inspect dispatch now includes bead metadata, avoids duplicate model/permission flags, launches inspectors from the workspace, and has focused tests plus typecheck/lint/build verification.

title: PAN-1031: Repair CLI inspect prompt asset path
description: pan inspect failed in this workspace because the CLI bundle looks for inspect-agent.md under dist/cli/prompts while build-cli copies cloister prompt templates to dist/prompts. Align the CLI build asset location so pan inspect can dispatch the inspect specialist from built CLI output.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build:cli creates dist/cli/prompts/inspect-agent.md; pan inspect no longer fails with missing dist/cli/prompts/inspect-agent.md before spawning the specialist.
close_reason: Aligned CLI build prompt asset output with the built pan inspect runtime path and verified build:cli creates dist/cli/prompts/inspect-agent.md.

title: pan-829: Settings TTS VoiceDesign: Clone to Exact Voice (embedding extraction)
description:

Call POST /api/tts/extract-embedding with {design:description, text:testText}. On success: prompt for a name, call POST /api/tts/voices with kind:'clone', description, instruct, embedding:data.embedding. On error (503/timeout): show error toast. The clone button is disabled if description textarea is empty. After successful clone, refetch voice list. The key UX: a cloned voice produces byte-for-byte identical audio every time (uses the extracted speaker embedding), unlike 'Save This Design' which re-generates from the text description on each speak and may drift slightly.

title: pan-829: TtsPlaybackService — dashboard-internal TTS speaker
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/services/tts-playback.ts. The service subscribes to the event store and calls resolveAndSpeak() (from src/lib/tts-speak.ts) directly for each activity.tts event — no HTTP round-trip. Export startTtsPlayback() and stopTtsPlayback() following the same pattern as the existing tts-summarizer.ts. startTtsPlayback(): check config.tts.enabled, if false log and return; get event store, subscribe to activity.tts events, call resolveAndSpeak({text:event.payload.utterance, source:event.payload.source, eventType:event.payload.eventType, issueId:event.payload.issueId}). On 'daemon-unavailable' result: log warning, do not crash. On 'muted' or 'no-voice': silent skip. stopTtsPlayback(): unsubscribe from event store. Guard against double-start (idempotent). Note: This service replaces the pan-tts SSE consumer as the primary playback path when the dashboard is running.
- AC: Does not start when config.tts.enabled is false
- AC: Calls resolveAndSpeak() for each activity.tts event when enabled
- AC: startTtsPlayback() is idempotent — calling twice does not double-subscribe
- AC: Does not crash on 'daemon-unavailable' — logs and continues

title: pan-829: POST /api/tts/speak — voice-resolving proxy to Qwen3-TTS daemon
description:
Create src/dashboard/server/routes/tts.ts and a shared resolver src/lib/tts-speak.ts. The resolver function resolveAndSpeak({ text, source?, eventType?, issueId? }): Promise<'spoken'|'muted'|'daemon-unavailable'|'no-voice'> implements: (1) mute check — if source in config.tts.mutedSources, return 'muted'; (2) issue mute check — if issueId in config.tts.mutedIssues, return 'muted'; (3) template override — if utteranceTemplates[eventType] exists, substitute {issueId} and replace text; (4) voice resolution — look up voiceMap[eventType] first, fall back to config.tts.voice (for priority<2 events) or config.tts.statusVoice (for priority=2 routine events), if neither set return 'no-voice'; (5) load voice from tts-voices.json; (6) build speak payload: preset→{voice:presetName,instruct:instruct??'',mode:'custom'}, design→{voice:description,instruct:instruct,mode:'design'}, clone→{voice:'clone',mode:'clone',embedding:embedding}; (7) POST to http://{daemonHost}:{daemonPort}/speak; (8) on ECONNREFUSED/timeout return 'daemon-unavailable'. The HTTP route POST /api/tts/speak calls resolveAndSpeak with body fields; returns 200 with {spoken,result} or 503 if daemon unavailable. Register the route in server.ts.
- AC: POST /api/tts/speak with a preset voice sends correct {voice,instruct,mode:'custom'} to port 8787
- AC: POST /api/tts/speak with a clone voice sends {mode:'clone',embedding:[...]} to port 8787
- AC: Muted source returns 200 {result:'muted'} — no call to port 8787
- AC: utteranceTemplates substitution replaces text before speaking
- AC: Returns 503 with {error} when port 8787 is unreachable (ECONNREFUSED)

title: pan-829: Add tts section to ApiSettingsConfig + loadSettingsApi/saveSettingsApi
description:
In src/lib/settings-api.ts: add tts?: { enabled?: boolean, voice?: string, statusVoice?: string, volume?: number, rate?: number, maxChars?: number, dropInfoWhenFull?: boolean, voiceMap?: Record<string,string>, mutedSources?: string[], utteranceTemplates?: Record<string,string>, mutedIssues?: string[] } to ApiSettingsConfig. Update loadSettingsApi() to read config.tts and populate this field. Update saveSettingsApi() to write the tts section back to YAML via the existing yaml-write path. Validate that volume is 0–1, rate > 0, maxChars > 0 in validateSettingsApi().
- AC: GET /api/settings returns tts section with current values
- AC: PUT /api/settings with tts payload persists and GET returns updated values

title: pan-829: Voice library CRUD module (tts-voices.ts)
description:
Create src/lib/tts-voices.ts. Define and export: TtsVoiceKind = 'preset' | 'design' | 'clone'; TtsVoice interface with fields id (UUID), name (string), kind (TtsVoiceKind), createdAt (ISO string), presetName? (string — Qwen3 preset id like 'vivian'), description? (string — natural language voice description), instruct? (string — speaking style), embedding? (number[] — extracted speaker embedding for clone voices). Export functions: loadVoices() → TtsVoice[] (reads ~/.panopticon/tts-voices.json, returns [] if missing); saveVoices(voices: TtsVoice[]) → Promise<void>; addVoice(voice: Omit<TtsVoice,'id'|'createdAt'>) → Promise<TtsVoice>; deleteVoice(id: string) → Promise<boolean>; findVoiceById(id: string) → TtsVoice | undefined; findVoiceByName(name: string) → TtsVoice | undefined. Use fs/promises (no sync). Embeddings are number[] arrays (float32 values) stored as JSON — they can be large (thousands of floats) but stay in a separate file from config.yaml to keep YAML human-readable.
- AC: loadVoices() returns [] when tts-voices.json is missing, never throws
- AC: addVoice() persists a new voice with generated UUID and returns it
- AC: deleteVoice() removes by id and returns true; returns false for unknown id
- AC: findVoiceById and findVoiceByName work correctly after addVoice

title: pan-829: POST /api/tts/extract-embedding — proxy to Qwen3-TTS daemon
description:
In tts.ts: add POST /api/tts/extract-embedding. Accept body {design:string, text:string}, forward to http://127.0.0.1:8787/extract-embedding, return response as-is. Use a 60-second timeout (embedding extraction is slow — generates audio, extracts speaker vector). Return 503 if daemon unreachable. This endpoint is called by the VoiceDesign Clone flow in the frontend.
- AC: Proxies to port 8787 and returns {embedding:[...]} on success
- AC: Uses 60-second timeout (not the default 2s)

title: pan-829: GET /api/tts/health — proxy Qwen3-TTS daemon health
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/tts.ts: add GET /api/tts/health. Fetch GET http://127.0.0.1:8787/health with a 2-second timeout. On success: return {ok:true, queue, model} from daemon response. On any error (ECONNREFUSED, timeout): return {ok:false, error:'daemon unreachable'}. Response is always HTTP 200 (never 503) so frontend polling doesn't need special error handling.
- AC: Returns {ok:true,queue,model} when daemon is running
- AC: Returns {ok:false,error} (HTTP 200) when daemon is unreachable

title: pan-829: Extend config-yaml.ts with tts daemon config section
description:
Add a comprehensive tts section to YamlConfig in src/lib/config-yaml.ts and the corresponding NormalizedConfig fields. The new YAML shape under `tts:` (not `tts.summarizer` which already exists): enabled (boolean, default false), voice (string, default ''), statusVoice (string, optional — second voice slot for routine events), volume (number 0.0–1.0, default 1.0), rate (number, default 1.0), maxChars (number, default 140), dropInfoWhenFull (boolean, default true), daemonPort (number, default 8787), daemonHost (string, default '127.0.0.1'), voiceMap (Record<string,string> — pipeline event key to voice ID), mutedSources (string[] — ActivitySource values to silence), utteranceTemplates (Record<string,string> — event key to text template with {issueId} substitution), mutedIssues (string[] — issue IDs to silence). Normalize all into a `config.tts` accessor on NormalizedConfig with defaults applied. The existing `tts.summarizer` remains under `ttsSummarizer` — do not merge or rename it.
- AC: YamlConfig.tts accepts all listed fields without TypeScript errors
- AC: loadConfig() returns config.tts with defaults when no tts section in YAML
- AC: loadConfig() merges user-provided tts fields over defaults correctly
- AC: Existing tts.summarizer (ttsSummarizer) is unaffected — no regressions

title: Add provider default harnesses with per-role overrides in SettingsPage
description:
Settings should let users choose a default harness per model provider, e.g. OpenAI -> Codex, so models from that provider launch through the intended harness by default. Per-role/agent harness selection remains available only as an override of the provider default, not the primary defaulting mechanism.

Implementation direction:
- Add provider-level default harness controls in Settings -> Providers.
- Persist provider defaults under models.providers.<provider>.harness while keeping models.providers booleans/API shape compatible.
- Agent/planning spawn resolves harness as: explicit request override -> role override -> provider default for selected model -> claude-code.
- Role/Flywheel harness UI should offer Provider default/unset, plus explicit Claude Code/Pi/Codex overrides.

Acceptance:
- Setting OpenAI default harness to Codex causes OpenAI models such as gpt-5.5 to launch with Codex when no role/request override is set.
- Explicit role/request harness still overrides the provider default.
- Existing configs without provider harnesses continue to default to Claude Code.
- Invalid provider harness values are rejected.

title: pan-985: Add SMOKE_TEST.md documenting the manual end-to-end channel-delivery verification
description:
Add src/lib/channels/SMOKE_TEST.md with a five-step manual procedure: (1) toggle the experimental flag on in dashboard Settings; (2) start a work agent against a Claude-Code-eligible Linear/GitHub issue; (3) attach to the agent's tmux pane and observe the dev-channels dialog appears and is auto-dismissed; (4) tail ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/logs/bridge-<agent-id>.log and observe a channel-push log line for the initial prompt; (5) run pan tell <id> 'echo test' and observe a second push log line with caller=pan-tell. Document the expected log signatures, where to look on failure, and how to revert (toggle off, restart agent).
- AC: src/lib/channels/SMOKE_TEST.md exists and contains the five-step procedure with expected outputs and revert instructions
- AC: Doc references the bridge log path, the eligibility log category, and the tmux pane location for visual confirmation of the dev-channels dialog
close_reason: See commit history on feature/pan-985 for workspace-5z2n implementation.

title: pan-985: Dismiss the dev-channels confirmation dialog at agent startup with one Enter keystroke
description:
In the work-agent launch path in src/lib/agents.ts, after the tmux session is created and BEFORE the first deliverAgentMessage / sendKeysAsync of an actual prompt, add a dismissDevChannelsDialog(agentId) step that runs only when channelsEnabled is true for the agent. Implementation: poll capturePaneAsync for the literal string 'WARNING: Loading development channels' (case-sensitive) every 200ms with a 20-second timeout (cold-start claude with TLDR + Playwright MCP servers commonly takes 8-15s to render the first frame; a tighter budget will frequently false-negative and silently fall through to tmux). On match, call sendRawKeystrokeAsync(agentId, 'C-m', 'channels:dismiss-dev-dialog') ONCE and wait 500ms. If the string is not found within the timeout, log 'channels:dismiss:dialog-not-detected' and proceed (the dialog is suppressed in some auth states). Must NOT use sendKeysAsync (that would type text and submit). The dismissal step must run before any prompt is delivered, otherwise the channel listener does not register and all early channel pushes silently fail.
- AC: dismissDevChannelsDialog runs only when the agent state has channelsEnabled: true; flag-off launches skip it entirely
- AC: When the dialog text is detected within 20s, exactly one sendRawKeystrokeAsync(C-m) is fired (verified by checking the send-keys log)
- AC: When the dialog text is NOT detected within 20s, the launch continues (does not block forever) and a 'channels:dismiss:dialog-not-detected' log line is written
- AC: Implementation uses sendRawKeystrokeAsync, NOT sendKeysAsync (no load-buffer/paste-buffer machinery for a single keystroke)
- AC: Dismissal completes before any deliverAgentMessage is called for that agent (test asserts ordering with a fake clock or call-order assertion)
close_reason: See commit history on feature/pan-985 for workspace-02v7 implementation.

title: pan-985: Add Experimental section at the bottom of SettingsPage with the Claude Code Channels toggle
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/SettingsPage.tsx, append a new section at the very end (after every other existing section) titled 'Experimental' with the Beaker icon already imported. Use a visually distinct heading treatment (warning-tone color or border) and a one-paragraph blurb: 'Research-preview features that may break, change, or be removed without notice. Use at your own risk.' Inside the section, render a single labeled toggle 'Use Claude Code Channels for prompt delivery (work agents only)' wired through the existing settings query/mutation hooks to experimental.claudeCodeChannels. Sub-text under the toggle: 'Routes prompts via a stdio MCP bridge instead of tmux send-keys. Requires Claude Code with claude.ai or Console API auth. Does not apply to Codex, Cursor, Gemini, or Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry runtimes; those continue using tmux. Triggers a confirmation dialog at agent startup that Panopticon dismisses automatically.' Include a link to https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels. Add a Playwright test (or extend an existing one) that asserts the section is the last visible section on the page, the toggle defaults to off, and toggling it issues a POST to /api/settings with the expected body.
- AC: The Experimental section is rendered as the LAST section on SettingsPage (no other section appears below it visually or in DOM order)
- AC: The toggle 'Use Claude Code Channels for prompt delivery (work agents only)' defaults to off and reflects the GET /api/settings value on initial render
- AC: Sub-text under the toggle includes the runtime-eligibility caveat and a link to https://code.claude.com/docs/en/channels
- AC: Toggling the switch issues a POST to /api/settings with body { experimental: { claudeCodeChannels: true|false } } (verified by Playwright network assertion)
- AC: Section heading includes a visually distinct treatment (warning-tone color or border) and the Beaker icon

Do NOT touch sendKeysAsync calls in src/lib/cloister/ (specialists, deacon, review, merge), src/lib/runtimes/claude-code.ts, src/dashboard/server/routes/conversations.ts, src/dashboard/server/routes/misc.ts — those remain on sendKeysAsync per scope decision. description:
In src/lib/agents.ts, in the work-agent launch path that calls generateLauncherScript (around the existing const launcherContent = generateLauncherScript({ ... }) at ~line 1092), add an isEligibleForChannels(agentConfig) helper and an enableChannels = isClaudeCodeChannelsEnabled() && isEligibleForChannels(...) gate. When true: (a) write a per-agent .mcp.json at <workspace>/.pan/agent-mcp.json containing { mcpServers: { 'panopticon-bridge': { command: 'bun', args: ['<repo>/src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts'], env: { PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID: '<id>', PANOPTICON_HOME: '<home>' } } } }; (b) populate channelsBridgeMcpConfig and channelsBridgeServerName on the LauncherConfig; (c) write channelsEnabled: true to the agent state file at ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/agents/<id>/state.json. When false: do nothing new (no .mcp.json, no extra flags, channelsEnabled defaults to false in agent state). Eligibility = work agent + Claude Code runtime + Anthropic-compatible auth + non-Docker workspace. Log eligibility result once per launch with a clear category prefix 'channels:eligible' or 'channels:ineligible:<reason>'. Update existing work-agent launch tests to cover both branches.
- AC: isEligibleForChannels helper returns false for non-Claude-Code runtimes (codex, cursor, gemini), false for Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry auth, false for Docker workspaces, true otherwise
- AC: When experimental flag is off, launching a work agent produces zero new files (no .mcp.json, no state.channelsEnabled change) — regression test asserts directory contents
- AC: When experimental flag is on AND eligible, launch writes <workspace>/.pan/agent-mcp.json with the documented schema and writes channelsEnabled: true to ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/agents/<id>/state.json
- AC: When experimental flag is on AND ineligible, launch logs 'channels:ineligible:<reason>' and behaves identically to flag-off (no .mcp.json, no state mutation)
- AC: Eligibility log lines appear exactly once per launch (not per delivery) and contain the agent id and the reason

title: pan-985: Unit tests for panopticon-bridge: notification frame shape and socket protocol
description:
Add src/lib/channels/__tests__/panopticon-bridge.test.ts. Tests must run under vitest and not require a real claude binary. Mock StdioServerTransport with a stub that records JSON-RPC frames written to it. (a) Boot the bridge in-process with a temp PANOPTICON_HOME and a random agent_id; assert the socket file exists at the expected path with mode 0o600. (b) POST {content: 'hello world', meta: {agent_id: 'a-1'}} to the socket; assert exactly one stdio frame was written, method is 'notifications/claude/channel', params.content is 'hello world', params.meta.agent_id is 'a-1'. (c) POST with malformed JSON returns 400. (d) POST with missing content returns 400 and writes zero stdio frames. (e) Bridge unlinks the socket on shutdown signal. Tests must pass under `npm test` from the workspace root.
- AC: Test file exists at src/lib/channels/__tests__/panopticon-bridge.test.ts and is picked up by vitest
- AC: Test asserts the JSON-RPC notification frame method is exactly 'notifications/claude/channel' and params.content survives round-trip unchanged
- AC: Test asserts socket file mode is 0o600 (owner-only) after bind
- AC: Test asserts malformed-JSON and missing-content POSTs return 400 and emit zero notification frames
- AC: All tests pass under `npm test` with no real claude binary, no real Anthropic auth, no network access required

title: pan-985: Extend GET/POST /api/settings to read and persist the experimental.claudeCodeChannels flag
description:
In src/dashboard/server/routes/settings.ts, extend the GET /api/settings response shape with an `experimental: { claudeCodeChannels: boolean }` block populated from loadConfig(). Extend POST /api/settings to accept the same block and write it through to config.yaml using the existing config-write helpers (must use async fs/promises per server constraints). Validate that the value is a boolean; reject non-booleans with 400. Must not break existing settings consumers (additive only).
- AC: GET /api/settings returns experimental.claudeCodeChannels in its response, defaulting to false when the config has no experimental block
- AC: POST /api/settings with { experimental: { claudeCodeChannels: true } } persists the value to config.yaml such that a subsequent GET returns true and a fresh loadConfig() in another process also sees true
- AC: POST with experimental.claudeCodeChannels of a non-boolean type (string, number, object) returns 400 with a clear error and does not write to config.yaml
- AC: Implementation uses fs/promises throughout — no readFileSync/writeFileSync in the new code paths
close_reason: See commits on feature/pan-985 for workspace-mnx3.

title: pan-985: Add deliverAgentMessage primitive in src/lib/agents.ts with eligibility check and tmux fallback
description:
Export a new function deliverAgentMessage(agentId, message, caller?) from src/lib/agents.ts. The function: (a) reads the agent state file at ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/agents/<id>/state.json and looks up channelsEnabled (written at launch by the launch bead); (b) if channelsEnabled and the socket file exists, POSTs the message as JSON {content, meta: {caller}} to the Unix socket using a 2-second connect+write timeout; (c) on 200 response: returns success and logs to the existing send-keys log file with caller='channel:<original-caller>'; (d) on any failure (ENOENT, ECONNREFUSED, EPIPE, non-2xx, timeout): falls back to sendKeysAsync(agentId, message, caller) and logs the fallback reason. Pure-tmux behavior (channelsEnabled=false in agent state, or no state file) is identical to today: call sendKeysAsync directly with no socket attempt. Function must use async fs/promises (no sync IO) per dashboard server constraints.
- AC: deliverAgentMessage exported from src/lib/agents.ts with signature (agentId: string, message: string, caller?: string) => Promise<void>
- AC: When agent state.channelsEnabled is false or state file missing, deliverAgentMessage delegates to sendKeysAsync exactly once and never attempts a socket connection
- AC: When channelsEnabled is true and socket POST returns 200, sendKeysAsync is NOT called and the channel-path log line is written
- AC: When channelsEnabled is true and socket POST fails (any error mode), deliverAgentMessage falls back to sendKeysAsync and writes a fallback-reason log line; the user-visible behavior is identical to a tmux-only delivery
- AC: Implementation uses fs/promises and node:net (or fetch with unix-socket dispatcher) — no readFileSync/writeFileSync/execSync in this code path
- AC: Unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/deliver-agent-message.test.ts cover: (a) flag-off pure-tmux path, (b) flag-on socket-success path, (c) flag-on socket-ENOENT fallback, (d) flag-on socket-timeout fallback. Each test mocks both the socket and sendKeysAsync.
close_reason: See commits on feature/pan-985 for workspace-clbj.

title: pan-985: Add Unix-socket inbound listener to panopticon-bridge that pushes channel notifications
description:
In src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts, after the StdioServerTransport connect, start a minimal HTTP-over-Unix-socket server using Bun.serve({ unix: '${PANOPTICON_HOME}/sockets/agent-<id>.sock' }). On every POST whose body is a JSON object { content: string, meta?: Record<string,string> }, call mcp.notification with method 'notifications/claude/channel' and the parsed params, then return 200 'ok'. Validate the body shape; reject with 400 on missing content. On startup, ensure the sockets dir exists with mode 0o700, unlink any pre-existing socket at the target path, then bind. On SIGTERM/SIGINT, unlink the socket and exit 0. Log every push to ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/logs/bridge-<agent-id>.log with timestamp, content length, meta keys, and the Claude-side mcp.notification result.
- AC: Bridge binds to ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/sockets/agent-<PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID>.sock with file mode 0o600 (owner-only)
- AC: POST to the socket with body {"content": "hello"} returns 200 'ok' and is observable as an mcp.notification call (verified via stdio frame inspection in unit test with a fake transport)
- AC: POST with missing content field returns 400 with a JSON error body and does NOT call mcp.notification
- AC: Bridge unlinks any pre-existing socket file before bind (no EADDRINUSE on restart) and unlinks again on SIGTERM
- AC: Every push is logged to ${PANOPTICON_HOME}/logs/bridge-<agent-id>.log with ISO timestamp, content length, meta key list
close_reason: See commits on feature/pan-985 for workspace-bvzr.

title: pan-985: File two follow-up GitHub issues for reply tool and permission relay
description:
Create two follow-up GitHub issues using gh CLI: (1) 'Channels: bidirectional reply tool for orchestrator status events' — add a reply MCP tool to panopticon-bridge so Claude can call it to send structured status events back to the dashboard, replacing pane-scraping; depends on PAN-985. (2) 'Channels: route Claude permission prompts through dashboard via permission relay' — declare claude/channel/permission capability on the bridge, add notification handler for notifications/claude/channel/permission_request, build a dashboard modal to display the prompt and POST a verdict back; depends on PAN-985 and on at least Claude Code v2.1.81. Include in each issue a one-paragraph context section explaining the Channels mechanism and a link to the relevant section of channels-reference. Record the resulting issue numbers in the PR description for PAN-985.

title: pan-985: Document the Channels architecture in CLAUDE.md
description:
Add a new section to CLAUDE.md titled 'Claude Code Channels (experimental)' immediately after the existing 'tmux Message Delivery' section. The section must explain: (a) what Channels is and why we use it (reliability of prompt delivery vs tmux send-keys); (b) it is opt-in via the experimental flag in Settings, off by default; (c) it applies only to work agents on Claude Code runtimes with Anthropic auth; (d) the bridge is a Bun stdio MCP server at src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts spawned per agent; (e) the dev-channels confirmation dialog is dismissed automatically with one tmux Enter at startup; (f) deliverAgentMessage in src/lib/agents.ts is the single primitive that handles eligibility check and tmux fallback; (g) callers in src/lib/cloister, conversation routes, and runtimes other than claude-code stay on sendKeysAsync. Cross-link to the Channels reference URL.

- AC: The section describes opt-in posture, eligibility criteria (Claude Code runtime + Anthropic auth + non-Docker), and explicitly notes which call sites do NOT migrate (cloister, conversations, non-Claude runtimes)
- AC: The section references src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts, deliverAgentMessage in src/lib/agents.ts, and the Channels reference URL

title: pan-985: Extend LauncherConfig and generateLauncherScript to accept channels MCP config and dev-channels flag
description:
In src/lib/launcher-generator.ts, add two optional fields to LauncherConfig: `channelsBridgeMcpConfig?: string` (absolute path to the per-agent .mcp.json) and `channelsBridgeServerName?: string` (default 'panopticon-bridge'). When BOTH are set, buildNonConversationCommand and buildCommand for specialist-dispatch must append --mcp-config <path> --dangerously-load-development-channels server:<name> to the claude invocation, before --session-id and --model flags. Do NOT add --strict-mcp-config: --mcp-config is additive by default and we MUST keep project-level MCP servers (TLDR, Playwright) loading alongside the bridge. When either is unset, the generated script must be byte-for-byte identical to today (no new tokens, no whitespace changes). Update src/lib/__tests__/launcher-generator.test.ts: (a) regression test asserting flag-off output is unchanged for representative work-agent and specialist configs, (b) new test asserting flag-on output contains the two new flags in the documented order, (c) test asserting --strict-mcp-config is NOT present in the flag-on output.
- AC: LauncherConfig has optional fields channelsBridgeMcpConfig?: string and channelsBridgeServerName?: string with documented defaults
- AC: generateLauncherScript output is byte-for-byte identical to the current behavior when channelsBridgeMcpConfig is undefined (regression test asserts string equality against a known fixture)
- AC: When channelsBridgeMcpConfig is set, generated command includes --mcp-config <quoted-path> --dangerously-load-development-channels server:panopticon-bridge in that order, before --session-id/--model, and does NOT include --strict-mcp-config
- AC: Existing launcher-generator unit tests pass without modification; new tests added for both flag-off-regression and flag-on cases, including an explicit assertion that --strict-mcp-config is absent

title: pan-985: Scaffold panopticon-bridge as a Bun MCP server entrypoint in src/lib/channels/
description:
Create src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts as the Bun-runnable MCP server entrypoint. Add @modelcontextprotocol/sdk to root package.json. The file is a single self-contained Bun script: import Server and StdioServerTransport from the SDK, construct a Server with capabilities.experimental['claude/channel'] = {} (no tools entry), include an instructions string telling Claude how to handle incoming <channel source="panopticon-bridge"> events, and connect over StdioServerTransport. Stub the socket listener as a TODO comment for the next bead. The file's header comment must document the Bun-runtime requirement and reference the Claude Code Channels reference URL. The bridge must read PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID from env at startup (passed in via the MCP config env block); fail-fast with a clear error if missing.
- AC: src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts exists and runs cleanly under Bun (bun run src/lib/channels/panopticon-bridge.ts) without immediate exit when stdin is open
- AC: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk is added to root package.json dependencies and bun install completes successfully
- AC: Server constructor declares capabilities.experimental['claude/channel'] = {} and OMITS capabilities.tools and capabilities.experimental['claude/channel/permission'] (one-way, no reply tool, no permission relay)
- AC: Bridge process exits with non-zero code and a clear error message when PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID env var is unset
- AC: Header comment in panopticon-bridge.ts documents the Bun-runtime requirement, the Channels reference URL, and the fact that the socket-unlink-on-startup is intentional (not defensive)

title: pan-636: End-to-end smoke test: spawn Pi agent, drive one prompt, assert lifecycle signals
description:
Add a vitest under src/lib/runtimes/__tests__/pi-e2e.test.ts (gated behind `if (process.env.PAN_E2E === '1')` so it doesn't run in standard CI without Pi installed). It: (1) spawns a Pi work agent for a fixture issue against a fake provider (or `pi --provider mock` if Pi supports it; otherwise targets ollama with a tiny local model), (2) waits for ready.json, (3) sends a prompt via fifo, (4) waits for /pan-done marker after a custom command, (5) parses the session JSONL via pi-parser and asserts non-zero token totals + non-zero cost_v2, (6) kills the agent and verifies cleanup. Documents how to run the test locally in docs/HARNESSES.md.
- AC: Test gated behind PAN_E2E=1 env var; CI default skips it
- AC: Test spawns a Pi agent, observes ready.json within 30s, sends a prompt that triggers tool execution, observes a heartbeat file refresh, observes /pan-done marker
- AC: Test verifies pi-parser returns non-zero tokens and non-zero cost_v2 from the resulting session JSONL
- AC: Test verifies killAgent cleans up rpc.in/ready.json/heartbeat but preserves the session JSONL

title: pan-636: PiRuntime.killAgent: graceful RPC abort then SIGTERM then SIGKILL
description:
killAgent writes {cmd:'abort'} to the fifo, waits 2s for graceful shutdown (poll tmux pane PID exit), then SIGTERM the Pi process, waits 5s, then SIGKILL. Always unlinks the fifo and removes the per-agent dir's transient files (heartbeat, ready.json, completed marker). NEVER deletes session JSONL files (per CLAUDE.md sacred-jsonl rule).
- AC: killAgent on a healthy Pi process exits within 3s and the tmux session is gone
- AC: killAgent on a hung Pi process (ignoring abort) escalates to SIGTERM after 2s and SIGKILL after 7s total
- AC: killAgent unlinks rpc.in, ready.json, heartbeat, and completed marker but does NOT delete the session JSONL under <agentDir>/sessions/
close_reason: Replaced minimal killAgent with full ladder: writePiCommand({cmd:abort}) — graceful flush — then pollUntilSessionGone (2s @ 100ms cadence) — then tmux send-keys C-c + pkill -TERM -f agent-id=<id> — then poll (5s) — then tmux kill-session SIGKILL fallback. cleanupPiTransientFiles always runs and removes rpc.in (destroyPiFifo), ready.json, completed marker, ~/.panopticon/heartbeats/<id>.json — but NEVER touches <agentDir>/sessions/*.jsonl (sacred JSONL rule from CLAUDE.md). 2 new tests: sacred jsonl survives killAgent + exit < 3.5s when no tmux session to escalate against. 10 pi.test cases pass total. typecheck + lint clean. Force-closed (planning seed inverted depends_on).

title: pan-636: Resume a Pi agent session via stored session id
description:
After PiRuntime spawnAgent succeeds, the extension writes ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/session.id. On resume (e.g., dashboard restart, post-crash recovery), PiRuntime.spawnAgent reads session.id and adds `--session <id>` to the Pi command line. If the file is missing, it spawns a fresh session and logs a warning.
- AC: After a successful initial spawn, ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/session.id contains the Pi session id reported by the extension
- AC: Re-spawning the same agentId after a kill reuses the stored session id via `pi --session <id>`
- AC: When session.id is missing on resume, a fresh session is spawned and a warning is logged but the spawn does not fail
close_reason: session.id persisted by pi-extension; PiRuntime.spawnAgent reads + forwards as resumeSessionId. AC1/AC2/AC3 satisfied. 3 pi.test + 2 pi-extension tests pass.

title: pan-636: Single resolveSpecialistBaseCommand(role, model, harness) and replace 3 hardcoded sites
description:
Add resolveSpecialistBaseCommand(role: string, model: string, harness: HarnessName) in src/lib/cloister/router.ts (or specialists.ts). For harness='claude-code', return the existing claude/claudish-derived command (delegates to the refactored getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand). For harness='pi', return a Pi command line equivalent. Replace the three hardcoded sites: src/lib/cloister/specialists.ts:1104, src/lib/cloister/specialists.ts:2316, src/lib/cloister/review-agent.ts:577. The harness is resolved at call time via getSpecialistHarness, AND canUseHarness is consulted — if blocked, fall back to claude-code for that specialist and emit a warning log so a misconfigured Settings panel cannot crash a pipeline.
- AC: resolveSpecialistBaseCommand exists and is the only producer of specialist baseCommand strings (no remaining literal 'claude' baseCommand assignments in cloister/)
- AC: When canUseHarness returns allowed=false for the role's configured harness/model/auth combination, the helper falls back to harness='claude-code' and logs a warning with the reason
- AC: Vitest covers both harness branches AND the fallback path
close_reason: Added resolveSpecialistBaseCommand(role, model, harness?) in router.ts using dynamic imports of harness-policy + agents to avoid circular import. Helper resolves harness from getSpecialistHarness when not explicit, evaluates canUseHarness against model + getProviderAuthMode result, falls back to claude-code with console.warn on block. Replaced 3 hardcoded sites: specialists.ts:1104 (specialist-dispatch — role=specialistType), specialists.ts:2316 (specialist-init — role=name), review-agent.ts:540 (spawnSingleReviewer — role=review-agent). Removed unused getAgentRuntimeBaseCommand import. Verified AC1: grep for baseCommand:claude literals in cloister/ returns nothing. 4 tests cover claude-code branch, pi branch (ToS allows non-Anthropic), pi+Anthropic+subscription fallback with warn, explicit harness override beats config. typecheck + lint clean. Force-closed (planning seed inverted depends_on).

title: pan-636: src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts implements AgentRuntime for Pi
description:
Create src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts exporting class PiRuntime implements AgentRuntime { name: 'pi' }. Methods: spawnAgent (creates fifo via pi-fifo-ipc, writes launcher.sh via pi-launcher-support, kicks off tmux session, waits up to 30s for ready.json before returning); sendMessage (writes {cmd:'prompt',text} to fifo via writePiCommand, errors with PiNotReady if no ready.json); killAgent (writes {cmd:'abort'} to fifo, waits 2s, then kill -TERM, then kill -KILL after 5s; unlinks fifo); getHeartbeat (reads ~/.panopticon/heartbeats/<agentId>.json, falls back to JSONL mtime + tmux pane activity); getTokenUsage / getSessionCost (delegate to parsePiSession on the agent's session JSONL under <agentDir>/sessions/); getSessionPath; isRunning; listSessions. Register PiRuntime in runtimes/index.ts.
- AC: PiRuntime is registered in getGlobalRegistry() alongside ClaudeCodeRuntime and getRuntime('pi') returns it
- AC: spawnAgent({harness:'pi',...}) creates the fifo, writes the launcher script, starts a tmux session, and resolves only after ready.json is detected (with a 30s timeout that throws a PiSpawnTimeout error)
- AC: sendMessage(agentId, text) writes a single {"cmd":"prompt","text":...} JSONL line to the fifo and resolves after the write completes; sendMessage before ready.json exists rejects with PiNotReady
- AC: getHeartbeat(agentId) returns a Heartbeat sourced from the heartbeat file when fresh (<60s old), falls back to JSONL mtime when stale, falls back to tmux activity when JSONL is missing
- AC: getSessionCost(agentId) returns a CostBreakdown derived from parsePiSession on the agent's active Pi session file
close_reason: Created src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts implementing AgentRuntime (name=pi). Spawn flow: mkdir agent+sessions, createPiFifo, optional prompt file, generateLauncherScript({harness:pi,...}) -> ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/pi-launcher.sh (0755), createSessionAsync(tmux + env PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID), waitForReady (100ms poll, 30s -> PiSpawnTimeout typed error). sendMessage: throws PiNotReady when ready.json absent, otherwise writes {cmd:prompt,text} via writePiCommand. killAgent: best-effort {cmd:abort} -> killSessionAsync -> destroyPiFifo (full TERM/KILL ladder belongs to bead 8qco). Heartbeat priority: fresh active heartbeat (<60s, high) > jsonl mtime (medium) > tmux session created (low). getSessionCost: CostBreakdown from parsePiSession on latest .jsonl. PiRuntime registered in getGlobalRegistry alongside Claude Code. Widened AgentRuntime.killAgent and isRunning return types to allow async (claude keeps sync; pi async). 9 tests pass. typecheck + lint clean. Force-closed (planning seed inverted depends_on).

title: pan-636: src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts: walk Pi tree JSONL active branch into SessionUsage
description:
Add src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts. Read Pi v3 JSONL line by line. Build a Map<id, entry> and a parentId chain. Identify the latest leaf by max(timestamp) among entries with no children. Walk leaf→root via parentId, collecting message/usage/cost entries. Sum usage.input/output/cacheRead. Use entry.usage.cost.total directly as cost_v2 (Pi's inline cost). Map to existing SessionUsage interface from jsonl-parser.ts (do NOT redefine it). Handle compaction entries (subtract pre-compaction input that has been folded into a summary). Handle model_change entries (record model breakdown per segment). Emit a debug log with delta(piInlineCost - localRecomputedCost) for observability. Snapshot fixtures under src/lib/cost-parsers/__tests__/fixtures/pi/.
- AC: parsePiSession(filePath) returns a valid SessionUsage object for a linear (no-fork) Pi v3 JSONL file with correct totals
- AC: For a forked Pi session with two branches, parsePiSession returns totals for ONLY the active branch (latest leaf), not the union of all branches
- AC: For a session with one model_change entry, modelBreakdown contains both models with their respective tokens/cost
- AC: For a session with a compaction entry, input_tokens are NOT double-counted across the compaction boundary
- AC: Unknown event types are logged once per session and do not throw; parser returns whatever it could compute
- AC: cost_v2 equals the sum of entry.usage.cost.total along the active branch (Pi's inline cost is the canonical figure)
close_reason: Created src/lib/cost-parsers/pi-parser.ts: parsePiSession reads Pi v3 JSONL line-by-line, builds id->entry + parent->children index, finds latest leaf among childless entries, walks leaf->root via parentId, sums per-message usage and Pi inline cost.total along active branch into existing SessionUsage shape from jsonl-parser.ts (no redefinition). Pi reports per-call deltas (not cumulative) so summing the branch is correct without subtracting pre-compaction input. cost_v2 = sum of entry.usage.cost.total (Pi inline). Unknown event types deduped + logged via console.debug. Added 5 fixtures (linear, forked, model-change, compaction, unknown-event) and 6 tests covering AC1-5 + missing-file. typecheck + lint clean. Force-closed (planning seed inverted depends_on).

title: pan-636: Per-agent mkfifo named pipe creation and lifecycle
description:
Add helper functions in src/lib/runtimes/pi.ts (or src/lib/runtimes/pi-fifo.ts) to: create the agent dir if missing, mkfifo at ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/rpc.in (chmod 0600), and unlink it on agent kill. Use mkfifoSync from the `fs` module — node has no async mkfifo. The fifo writer side is opened lazily on first write. Adapter must wait for ready.json before opening write-side. NEVER call this from server route handlers; it's only invoked from spawnAgent and killAgent paths which run on dedicated handlers.
- AC: createPiFifo(agentId) creates ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/rpc.in with mode 0600 and is idempotent (unlinks existing fifo first)
- AC: destroyPiFifo(agentId) unlinks the fifo and is safe to call when the fifo does not exist
- AC: writePiCommand(agentId, json) opens the writer fd lazily, writes one JSONL line, and closes — covered by integration test that pre-creates a reader (cat fifo.in > out.log) and asserts the line appears
- AC: writePiCommand returns a typed error (PiNotReady) — and does NOT block — when ready.json is not present yet
close_reason: Created src/lib/runtimes/pi-fifo.ts. createPiFifo uses execAsync(mkfifo -m 600) (Node has no native mkfifo), idempotent — unlinks any existing inode at the path so a stale regular file from a crashed agent is replaced cleanly. destroyPiFifo unlinks, tolerates ENOENT. writePiCommand: checks ready.json existsSync first, throws PiNotReady without blocking when missing (AC4); opens O_WRONLY|O_NONBLOCK so reader-less fifo throws ENXIO -> typed PiNotReady letting adapter recycle the agent. Writes one JSONL line + newline. Integration test uses an O_RDWR Node reader (kept open across writer lifecycles, mirroring how Pi keeps stdin open for the whole session). 8 tests pass: mode 0600, idempotent recreate, stale regular-file replacement, destroy noop, both PiNotReady paths, JSONL round-trip through kernel pipe. typecheck + lint clean. Force-closed (planning seed inverted depends_on).

title: pan-636: Vendored @panopticon/pi-extension at packages/pi-extension/
description:
Create packages/pi-extension/ as a workspace package. TypeScript source under src/, tsdown build to dist/index.js. Entry exports default function(pi: ExtensionAPI) registering: pi.on('session_start') → write ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/ready.json and session.id; pi.on('tool_execution_end') → write ~/.panopticon/heartbeats/<agentId>.json with timestamp/tool/pid; pi.on('turn_end') → activity touch; pi.registerCommand('pan-done') → write ~/.panopticon/agents/<agentId>/completed marker. agentId comes exclusively from process.env.PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID; never default. Add tsdown.config.ts. Add to root bun workspaces. No npm publish.
- AC: packages/pi-extension/dist/index.js builds via `bun install && cd packages/pi-extension && npm run build` and exports a default function
- AC: Vitest covers all four hooks: simulating each Pi event writes the expected file under a test-specific tmp HOME, and uses agentId from PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID
- AC: Two extension instances with different PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID values do not collide on heartbeat or ready files (covered by test)
- AC: Extension does NOT publish to npm (no `npm publish` in CI; package marked `private: true`)
close_reason: Vendored @panopticon/pi-extension at packages/pi-extension/. Default export panopticonPiExtension(pi) registers session_start (writes ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/ready.json with sessionId/reason/pid/timestamp), tool_execution_end (writes ~/.panopticon/heartbeats/<id>.json with timestamp/tool/last_action/pid; last_action=tool_error when isError), turn_end (refreshes heartbeat with turn_end marker), and /pan-done command (writes ~/.panopticon/agents/<id>/completed with trimmed summary). agentId from process.env.PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID only — unset = silent no-op so parallel pi -e instances outside Panopticon cannot collide on heartbeat/ready paths (AC3). Filesystem failures swallowed in try/catch so Pi cannot crash. private:true so no npm publish (AC4). tsdown builds dist/index.js. 9 vitest tests cover all four hooks, disjoint-paths invariant, no-op path. typecheck + lint clean. Force-closed (planning seed inverted depends_on).

title: pan-969: 5-second container stats polling in CommandDeck/index.tsx
description:
In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/index.tsx, add a useEffect that polls GET /api/resources every 5 seconds when the CommandDeck is mounted AND at least one ResourcesGroup is expanded. Store containerStats as Record<string, ContainerStats> in local state (or a shallow Zustand slice if cross-component access is needed). Pass stats down through ProjectNode → FeatureItem → ResourcesGroup → ContainerNode. Guard: stop polling when no ResourcesGroups are expanded to avoid unnecessary load.

- AC: ContainerNode CPU% and memory values update visually when new stats arrive

title: pan-969: Integrate ResourcesGroup into FeatureItem.tsx
description:
Modify src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/ProjectTree/FeatureItem.tsx to render <ResourcesGroup> after the session nodes when the issue is expanded and has resources. 'Has resources' = resourceDetails.dockerContainerCount > 0 || prs.length > 0 || (localBranchCount + remoteBranchCount) > 0. The ResourcesGroup's onContainerAction handler should call the relevant API endpoints and trigger a resource-details refetch. Also trigger resource-details fetch when the resources group is first expanded (not just on popover open as it does today).

- AC: Issues with zero containers, PRs, and branches do not render a ResourcesGroup

Add CSS to src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/CommandDeck/styles/ following the exact selectors from the PAN-965 PRD.

title: pan-969: Inline diff panel toggle in summary strip

When toggled on, render the existing DiffPanel component (or pr-diff tab content) below/beside the canvas, showing the diff from GET /api/agents/:agentId/diffs/vs-main. Toggle state is local (not persisted). The panel should be collapsible and not obscure the full DAG. Use data-testid='directive-diff-panel' on the panel wrapper.

title: pan-969: Escape state overlays — BLOCKED, CANCELLED, circuit-breaker
description:
Add three escape-state overlays to the DirectiveFlow canvas. BLOCKED: when reviewStatus.stuck === true, show a yellow semi-transparent banner overlaid on the current stage gate node showing stuckReason. CANCELLED: when the issue is cancelled, gray out all nodes with reduced opacity and add a 'CANCELLED' diagonal watermark on the canvas. Circuit breaker: when autoRequeueCount >= 3, show a red banner on the REVIEW gate node reading 'Human intervention needed — circuit breaker tripped (N/3)'. Use absolute-positioned divs over the ReactFlow canvas for the global overlays; use ReactFlow's node data to pass the overlay state down to individual nodes.
- AC: When stuck=true, a yellow BLOCKED banner appears on or near the current stage gate node with stuckReason text
- AC: When autoRequeueCount >= 3, a red banner reading 'Human intervention needed' appears on the REVIEW gate node
- AC: For cancelled issues, all nodes render with 50% opacity

title: pan-969: Dashed backward feedback edges with round counts
description:
When reviewStatus.reviewStatus === 'failed' or testStatus === 'failed' and the agent has been re-queued for rework, render dashed red backward edges from the gate node (REVIEW or TEST) back to the relevant work item nodes. Since there's no explicit mapping of 'which items need rework', use a heuristic: draw backward edges to all non-completed work items, or to the last N running/blocked items. Edge rendering: use ReactFlow's custom edge API with a dashed SVG stroke (strokeDasharray='5,3'), red/orange color, and an arrow pointing back to the work item. Show the round number ('Round 2') as a label on the edge.
- AC: When reviewStatus indicates rework (failed + requeue), dashed edges render from the gate node back to work items
- AC: Rework edges use a dashed red stroke visually distinct from the solid black dependency edges
- AC: Edge label shows the round number ('Round N') derived from reviewRetryCount or testRetryCount
- AC: No rework edges are rendered when reviewStatus === 'passed' or no retry has occurred

title: pan-969: ContainerNode.tsx — container row with status dot, metrics, sparkline
description:

The bar sits above the ReactFlow canvas and does not scroll with it. Use a dark semi-transparent background.

Sections (each collapsible independently): AGENTS — tree showing work-agent and review-agent with nested sub-reviewers, model, runtime state, cost; WORKSPACE — path, branch, VS Code button, Stop button; SERVICES — frontend/API URL chips from workspaceQuery; ATTACH — tmux command with copy-to-clipboard button; ACTIONS — Review & Test, Recover, Sync, Stop buttons (moved from OverviewTab); COST — total cost + per-model breakdown. Source data: Zustand store (agentsById, reviewStatusByIssueId) + React Query hooks (useWorkspaceQuery, useIssueCostsQuery). The dock overlays the right edge of the ReactFlow canvas; the canvas pans/zooms behind it.

In the WorkItemNode component, apply state-specific CSS classes for the node border and background. pending: gray border, dim background, no animation. approved/unblocked (ready): blue border, subtle pulse animation ('anim-pulse-blue'). running: green border, glow animation ('anim-glow-green') matching agent activity pulse conventions. completed: green filled background, checkmark icon, compact one-liner layout (collapse to title + checkmark only). blocked: red border, shows the blocking dependency item IDs. cancelled: gray border, strikethrough title, dim. Use CSS custom properties from the existing design system (--primary, --success, --destructive, --muted).

Wire each item: Restart → POST /api/resources/docker/container/:id/restart, Stop → existing DELETE endpoint, Start → POST /api/resources/docker/container/:id/start, Logs → call onViewLogs prop (caller decides rendering), Inspect → onInspect prop. Populate from DockerStatsCollector.getStats() by cross-referencing container names. Use parseContainerServiceName() for the serviceName field. All FS/process calls must be async.

title: pan-969: DirectiveFlow.tsx — fork PlanDAG, replace OverviewTab, render basic nodes + gate tails

On detection, emit a structured `recovery.signature_corruption` event to the dashboard event stream with the workspace id, agent id, and a short user-facing message describing the corruption. Per the user's explicit decision: deacon performs NO automatic repair — no rename, no move, no JSONL writes. The dashboard surface tells the user the error occurred and (optionally) suggests next steps. Prevention via fork-strip-thinking-signature + specialists-no-resume removes the corruption source going forward; this detector only catches pre-existing corrupted JSONLs.
- AC: Deacon emits recovery.signature_corruption event when 'Invalid signature in thinking block' appears in agent output
- AC: The event surfaces in the dashboard workspace inspector with the corruption message
- AC: Deacon performs NO automatic JSONL writes, renames, moves, or restarts in response to the corruption

title: pan-826: Add tmux paste/submit verification helpers in src/lib/tmux.ts with capture-pane polling and busy-state detection
description:
Replace the fixed 600-3000ms delay in sendKeysAsync with a verification loop. (1) Paste via load-buffer + paste-buffer (existing pattern). (2) Poll capture-pane at 50ms intervals up to a 3s cap until the pasted text is visible in the input region. (3) Send C-m. (4) Poll capture-pane until the input region is empty / text moved out, with a bounded retry budget. (5) On failure, raise a structured MessageDeliveryFailed error with the captured pane state attached. (6) Detect Claude-busy state (e.g. 'Whirring…' indicator) before paste and route to a busy-aware path that does NOT immediately paste-then-Enter, since the TUI buffers text without submitting in that state. The new helpers must be drop-in replacements at the call-site level.
- AC: sendKeysAsync (or its replacement) verifies pasted text is visible in the input region before sending Enter
- AC: After Enter, the helper verifies the input region cleared (message submitted) within a bounded retry budget
- AC: On verification failure, raises MessageDeliveryFailed with captured pane state in the error payload
- AC: Detects Claude-busy state from the capture and routes to the busy-aware path rather than paste-then-Enter
- AC: No fixed-delay-then-fire-and-forget path remains in src/lib/tmux.ts
- AC: Integration test using a real tmux session asserts MessageDeliveryFailed is raised when the input region never clears

Replace lexicographic localeCompare/string-compare on createdAt with a comparator that compares (createdAt, sequence) where sequence is the monotonic field already present on entries. ConversationList localeCompare on conversation names is out of scope (different domain).
- AC: git grep 'localeCompare' src/dashboard/ shows no hits in timeline-adjacent code (server conversation-service or frontend chat module)
- AC: Every .sort( on conversation timeline entries uses a single shared comparator that ties on sequence
- AC: Unit test asserts deterministic ordering of three entries with identical createdAt and distinct sequence values

title: pan-826: Persist ParseState across incremental parses in conversation-service
description:
In src/dashboard/server/services/conversation-service.ts, move pendingToolUse and pendingAssistant maps from per-call ParseState into a per-conversation cache keyed by conversationId. Cache lives for the lifetime of the conversation (clear on conversation deletion). Each incremental parseConversationMessages call loads the cached state, mutates it during processing, and persists the updated state. Two-pass tool_use/tool_result pairing within a single poll continues to operate on top of this. Add a unit test that parses a JSONL split across multiple invocations and asserts no tool activities are dropped.
- AC: ParseState (pendingToolUse, pendingAssistant) survives across multiple parseConversationMessages invocations for the same conversationId
- AC: tool_result entries arriving in a later poll than their matching tool_use are correctly paired and rendered, not dropped
- AC: Cache is cleared when a conversation is deleted; no memory leak across conversation lifecycle
- AC: Vitest unit test splits a known JSONL into N chunks, parses each in turn, and asserts the final timeline has the same tool activities as a single-pass parse

title: pan-816: Implement generic copyRuntimeConfig() function

Return {copied: string[], skipped: string[], errors: string[]}. Skip missing source files (don't error).
- AC: Function expands ~ to homedir() in source paths
- AC: Function creates parent directories for target paths that don't exist
- AC: Function adds each target path to .git/info/exclude (not .gitignore)
- AC: Secret-pattern files (*.env, *secret*, *key*) are logged by name only, never content
- AC: Missing source files are skipped (added to skipped array), not treated as errors
- AC: Function is idempotent — re-running overwrites targets and doesn't duplicate exclude entries

title: pan-805: Migrate transitionIssueToInProgress to reconciler with idempotency + lazy insert
description:
In src/lib/agents.ts:~1058, replace the direct label write in transitionIssueToInProgress. New flow: (1) read issue_state.canonical_state; (2) if already in_progress, log INFO issueId=X already in-progress, skipping and return with no API call; (3) if row is missing, lazy-insert it with canonical_state=in_progress; (4) otherwise update canonical_state to in_progress and set pending_mutation so the reconciler push step writes the label.
- AC: transitionIssueToInProgress reads issue_state.canonical_state before acting
- AC: When canonical_state is already in_progress, no API call is made and an INFO log is emitted with the expected format
- AC: If the issue has no issue_state row, one is lazy-inserted (covers issues missed by boot backfill)
- AC: Actual label writes flow through the reconciler queue, not a direct fetch from this call site
close_reason: Modified transitionIssueToInProgress in agents.ts: for GitHub issues, reads getCanonicalState(), early-returns if already in_progress, lazy-inserts via ensureIssueState() if missing, otherwise setCanonicalState(). Non-GitHub issues keep existing tracker behavior.

title: pan-805: Reconciler pull step: list-issues, detect remote-ahead, update local
description:
Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/pull.ts. Each tick: one paginated call to GET /repos/:o/:r/issues with labels filter scoped to Panopticon-known labels and state!=closed (or state=all excluding terminal canonical states locally). For each issue: if remote-implied canonical state differs from local AND no pending_mutation, update issue_state.canonical_state + last_synced_at and write audit row with outcome=skipped reason=remote_ahead_pulled. If pending_mutation exists and states conflict, log WARN with both states and let the pending write proceed.
- AC: Pull uses list-issues with pagination per tick (scope narrowed from spec: active/non-terminal issues only, per planning decision recorded in STATE.md), NOT one fetch per issue
- AC: Remote-ahead detection updates local canonical_state and last_synced_at; writes audit row with reason=remote_ahead_pulled
- AC: If a pending_mutation exists and remote state conflicts, WARN log is emitted with both states and the pending local write is preserved (not overwritten)
- AC: Issues in terminal canonical states locally are excluded from pull-sync to bound API usage
close_reason: Created pull.ts: runPullStep(config, gh) lists open issues via GitHub API, maps remote labels/state to canonical_state, updates local issue_state when remote differs and no pending_mutation, records remote_ahead_pulled audit entries. Wired into loop.ts tick()

title: pan-805: Audit writer records every attempt to label_sync_audit
description:
Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/audit.ts. Exposes writeAudit({issueId, targetLabel, action, outcome, reason, retryCount, httpStatus}). Called from every terminal point in the reconciler (success, failure after max retries, rate-limited final, skipped). Integrates with issue-state-db.writeAuditRow from the schema bead.
- AC: Every push attempt (success, failure, rate_limited, skipped) writes exactly one audit row per intent
- AC: retry_count reflects the number of retries attempted before the terminal outcome
- AC: http_status is populated for success/failure/rate_limited rows; NULL for skipped (no API call made)
close_reason: Created audit.ts: recordAudit(entry) writes to label_sync_audit with issueId, targetLabel, action, outcome, reason, retryCount, httpStatus

title: pan-805: Reconciler push step: diff issue_state vs remote, write deltas
description:
Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/push.ts. Each tick: for each row in issue_state where pending_mutation IS NOT NULL (or canonical_state changed since last_synced_at), compute desired labels, diff against last-synced, and write only the add/remove deltas via the github-client. On success, clear pending_mutation and update last_synced_at. Every attempt writes an audit row via audit.ts. No-op diffs write audit with outcome=skipped reason=no_diff.
- AC: Only deltas are written — if desired == last-synced, no API call is made
- AC: On successful write, pending_mutation is cleared and last_synced_at is advanced
- AC: On final failure after max retries, pending_mutation is retained so the next tick retries
- AC: Every terminal outcome writes exactly one audit row
close_reason: Created push.ts: runPushStep(config, gh) selects issues where updated_at > last_synced_at, fetches actual labels, computes desired labels via desiredLabels(), computes deltas via computeLabelDeltas(), applies add/remove via GitHub client, records audit entries, updates last_synced_at. Wired into loop.ts tick()

title: pan-805: Boot-time backfill of issue_state from local + GitHub

This runs once per boot, is idempotent, and populates canonical_state from the labels observed.
- AC: On boot, any locally-known issue missing from issue_state gets a row whose canonical_state matches current GitHub labels
- AC: Backfill is idempotent — running it twice produces no duplicate rows and no extra API calls
- AC: Backfill runs before the first reconciler tick to avoid spurious pull-sync updates racing the backfill
close_reason: Created backfill.ts: backfillIssueState() seeds issue_state from review_status DB rows + agent directories, using reviewStatusToCanonical heuristics. Called from dashboard boot before reconciler start.

title: pan-805: Create reconciler module scaffold with loop, mutex, and config
description:

No push/pull/sweep logic in this bead — just the runner and an empty tick fn.
- AC: startReconciler() begins ticking at the configured interval; stopReconciler() clears the timer cleanly
- AC: Calling startReconciler() twice without stopping is a no-op (mutex prevents double-start) and logs a WARN
- AC: PANOPTICON_RECONCILER_INTERVAL_MS env var overrides the default 30000; invalid values fall back to default with a WARN log
- AC: A thrown error inside the tick fn is caught and logged; the next tick still runs
close_reason: Created reconciler module scaffold: types.ts, loop.ts (tick driver with mutex), index.ts (public API startReconciler/stopReconciler/enqueueLabelChange/setCanonicalState/getCanonicalState/ensureIssueState)

title: pan-805: Reconciler GitHub client with .ok check and 429/5xx retry
description:
Add src/lib/lifecycle/reconciler/github-client.ts — a thin wrapped fetch for label add/remove, issue state fetch, and list-issues. Every call checks response.ok; on 429 or 5xx retries with exponential backoff (initial 1s, cap 60s, max 5 attempts); honors Retry-After header when present. Emits structured error logs with issueId, operation, status, body snippet. Never swallows errors — throws after max retries.
- AC: Every fetch call checks response.ok; non-ok responses throw or trigger retry
- AC: On 429 or 5xx: exponential backoff 1s → 2s → 4s → ... capped at 60s, max 5 attempts
- AC: Retry-After header (seconds or HTTP-date) overrides the backoff schedule when present
- AC: Errors include issueId, operation, status, and a body snippet in the log; after max retries, the error is thrown (not swallowed)
close_reason: Created github-client.ts with createGitHubClient(config): addLabel, removeLabel, listIssueLabels, closeIssue, listIssue. Includes .ok check, 429/5xx exponential backoff (1s→60s, max 5 attempts), Retry-After header support, structured error logging.

- AC: issue_state table exists with columns issue_id (PK), canonical_state, last_synced_at, pending_mutation, updated_at
- AC: label_sync_audit table exists with id (PK AUTOINCREMENT), issue_id, attempted_at, target_label, action (CHECK add|remove), outcome (CHECK success|failure|rate_limited|skipped), reason, retry_count DEFAULT 0, http_status

title: pan-714: Command-level tests: doneCommand behavior (force, shadow, id normalization)
description:
Create tests/cli/commands/work/done.test.ts. Mock the lib-level dependencies (runPreflightChecks, updateLinearToInReview, updateGitHubToInReview, createReviewArtifactsForIssue, saveAgentState, the dashboard auto-review HTTP calls) so the test exercises the *control flow* in doneCommand without hitting real APIs. Cover: (1) --force bypasses runPreflightChecks entirely (spy asserts it is never called); (2) shadow mode (via shouldSkipTrackerUpdate returning true) skips both Linear and GitHub update paths and calls updateShadowState instead; (3) issue-id normalization: `agent-pan-714`, `pan-714`, `PAN-714` all route to the same canonical `PAN-714` / `agent-pan-714` pair; (4) GitHub branch taken when issueId.startsWith('PAN-'), Linear branch otherwise.

title: pan-714: Extend pan sync to auto-mirror project-level .claude/skills/ from local skills/

This must be a no-op for any project without a top-level skills/ directory (i.e., every non-panopticon-cli project).

- AC: After deleting a directory under skills/, `pan sync` removes the corresponding directory under .claude/skills/ and reports `1 removed`
- AC: Running `pan sync` inside a project without a top-level skills/ directory is a no-op for .claude/skills/ (the existing user-level sync behavior is unchanged)
- AC: The existing .claude/skills/.gitignore file is never deleted, overwritten, or moved by the mirror logic

Show a modal during bulk close-out execution with per-issue status rows (pending → running → done/failed/skipped). Modal is non-dismissable during execution. On completion, show summary (N succeeded, N failed, N skipped) with a Close button. Clear selection on close.
- AC: Modal appears during execution showing per-issue status (pending/running/done/failed/skipped)
- AC: Modal cannot be dismissed while operation is in progress
- AC: On completion, shows summary counts and a Close button that clears selection
close_reason: Created BulkCloseOutProgress modal with per-issue status icons and completion counter

title: pan-569: Add selection checkbox and selected styling to IssueCard
description:
Add a checkbox to the top-left corner of each IssueCard in KanbanBoard.tsx. Wire it to useBulkSelection.toggle(). Apply subtle highlight border/background when selected. Checkbox click must not trigger the existing single-select (onSelect) behavior — use stopPropagation.
- AC: Each IssueCard renders a checkbox in the top-left corner
- AC: Clicking the checkbox toggles selection without triggering the card's single-select onClick
- AC: Selected cards have a visually distinct highlight (border or background change)
close_reason: Added isBulkSelected/onBulkSelect props to IssueCard with checkbox and primary ring styling

title: pan-569: Add POST /api/issues/bulk-close-out endpoint
description:
Add new Effect HTTP route in src/dashboard/server/routes/issues.ts. Accepts { issueIds: string[] }, iterates sequentially calling closeOut() per issue via the existing lifecycle module, collects per-issue results. Emits per-issue issue.statusChanged events (already handled by closeOut). Wire route into issuesRouteLayer.
- AC: POST /api/issues/bulk-close-out accepts { issueIds: string[] } and returns { results: Array<{ issueId, success, error?, skipped? }> }
- AC: Each issue runs the full closeOut() lifecycle (verify merge, archive, teardown, close on tracker, clear review status)
- AC: If one issue fails, remaining issues still proceed (no short-circuit)
- AC: Route is wired into issuesRouteLayer and accessible at /api/issues/bulk-close-out
close_reason: Added POST /api/issues/bulk-close-out endpoint with sequential closeOut execution and per-issue event emission

title: pan-569: Active-agent warning for bulk close-out
description:
Before executing bulk close-out, check if any selected issues have running agents. If so, show a warning dialog listing those issues and their agents. User can 'Proceed (skip issues with agents)' or 'Cancel'. Skipped issues appear in results as skipped. Reuse the existing AgentWarningDialog pattern/styling.
- AC: If any selected issues have running agents, a warning dialog appears before execution
- AC: Dialog lists which issues have active agents
- AC: Proceeding skips issues with active agents; those appear as skipped in results
close_reason: Created BulkAgentWarningDialog component listing issues with active agents and offering proceed/cancel

title: pan-569: Create useBulkSelection hook for multi-select state
description:
Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/hooks/useBulkSelection.ts. Hook manages a Set<string> of selected issue IDs. Exports: selectedIds, toggle(id), selectAll(ids), deselectAll(ids), clear(), isSelected(id), count. Clear selection when issues list changes identity (prevents stale selections).
- AC: Hook exports toggle, selectAll, deselectAll, clear, isSelected, count, and selectedIds
- AC: Toggling an already-selected ID removes it; toggling an unselected ID adds it
- AC: clear() resets selection to empty set
close_reason: Created useBulkSelection hook with toggle, selectAll, clear, isSelected, and selectedCount

Test with 0, 1, 2, 4, 6, 8+ agents for layout correctness. Verify Socket.io subscription/unsubscription lifecycle.

title: PAN-341: Agent focus view — full detail page

Auto-scrolling. 3) BeadsKanban — horizontal layout: green dots (done) → highlighted bead (in progress) → dim dots (todo). Progress bar + completion estimate. Data from bd list. 4) FileActivityTree — tree view of files touched with icons (edited/new/read) and timestamps. Data from /api/agents/:id/files. 5) CostBreakdownChart — visx stacked bar over time, colored by activity type. 6) AgentTimeline — vertical timeline with colored event dots from /api/agents/:id/timeline. 7) ActionBar — Pause Agent, Send Message, View Diff, Open Terminal, Kill Agent buttons using existing API endpoints.

title: PAN-341: Socket.io hook and God View state management

Create Zustand store for God View state: per-agent terminal output buffers, activity feed entries (circular buffer, max 200), system health data. Integrates with existing React Query for /api/agents data. Provides clean API for all God View components to consume real-time data.

title: PAN-341: Right sidebar — activity feed, cost donut, infra gauges

Auto-scroll with pause on hover. 2) CostDonutChart — visx Pie/Arc showing cost breakdown by model. Total in center, average per agent, budget progress bar below. Data from /api/costs/summary. 3) InfrastructureGauges — visx Arc gauges for CPU%, Memory%, Disk%. Workspace count badge. System uptime. All glassmorphism styled.

title: PAN-341: Agent grid layout with connection lines

Uses Framer Motion layout prop for smooth card appear/disappear (AnimatePresence). Takes 75% viewport width. Also create ConnectionLines.tsx — SVG overlay positioned absolutely over the grid. Draws thin animated dashed lines between related agents (review agent ↔ implementation agent for same issue). Uses ResizeObserver to recalculate positions. CSS dash-offset animation for flow effect.

title: PAN-341: Agent card component
description: Create AgentCard.tsx — glassmorphism card (~400x280px). Features: colored left border per project (MYN=pink, PAN=blue, AUR=purple via project config). Status pill with CSS pulse animation (CODING=green, THINKING=amber, REVIEWING=blue, IDLE=dim, BLOCKED=red). Embedded CanvasTerminal mini preview. Current file breadcrumb. Active tool indicator icons (lucide). Model badge + cost + token count. Beads progress bar with fraction label. Uptime counter. Breathing glow border matching status color. Click navigates to focus view.

title: PAN-341: God View top bar
description: Create GodViewTopBar component. Left: Panopticon logo with CSS glow animation + SYSTEM NOMINAL indicator. Center: digital clock (HH:MM:SS) + date. Right section: CPU sparkline (visx LinePath, last 60 data points), memory gauge (visx Arc, circular), active agent count badge with pulse, daily cost with trend arrow. All data from /api/godview/system-health and /api/agents. Dark glassmorphism bar at top of viewport.

title: PAN-341: Canvas terminal renderer

Renders N lines of terminal output with basic syntax highlighting (ANSI color codes → canvas fillStyle). Supports two modes: mini (3-4 lines for agent cards, ~400x100px) and full (30 lines for focus view, ~800x500px). Uses requestAnimationFrame for smooth updates. IntersectionObserver to skip rendering when off-screen. Receives text via prop from Socket.io data.

title: PAN-341: Server — Socket.io events and new REST endpoints
description: Add Socket.io events: godview:agent-output (debounced tmux capture per agent, 2s, last 30 lines), godview:status-change (agent state transitions), godview:activity (global feed from cloister events, git commits, test results, errors). Add REST endpoints: GET /api/agents/:id/files (git diff for files touched), GET /api/agents/:id/timeline (health_events + activity.jsonl merged), GET /api/godview/system-health (os.cpus, os.freemem, os.totalmem, disk usage). Use room-based Socket.io so clients subscribe to specific agent outputs. All async — no execSync.
